The import RPCs (ops_import_customers/ops_import_items) remain live for future use.
  - **Zoho migration DONE via scripted one-time ingest (Session 118), not the wizard.** Cole's full Zoho export = ONE real customer (name/email/Zoho-id redacted from this public doc; live in DB), 8 invoices with line items (INV-000001..8; 7 paid, 1 open), 8 payments (modes Bank Transfer/Cash/Bank Remittance — NONE Stripe, so no Stripe-source-of-truth conflict), 1 contact. Approach: idempotent purge+ingest DO block, dry-run in a rolled-back block (8/29/8/8/1, invoiced 12038.45 / paid 8763.78 / open 3274.67) then committed for real and re-verified live. **Test data PURGED first** (3 test customers + 7 invoices + 12 lines + 1 payment + 1 estimate + 3 projects + 3 tasks + 4 time entries + 3 expenses + 8 public tokens + 2 stripe_customers — all org-scoped DELETEs child-first; config untouched). Ingest: the customer (Net 60, USD; Zoho customer id in custom_fields), its sole contact (set is_primary=true; Zoho had Is Primary=false), 8 invoices into operations.invoices keeping the original Zoho numbers (free-text, operations numbering sequence untouched; future system invoices continue as INV-2026-NNNN — no collision with INV-00000X), 29 free_form document_lines, 8 payments (mode map: Bank Transfer→ach, Cash→cash, Bank Remittance→wire; Zoho payment id in notes for traceability), 8 payment_allocations. Idempotency guard on custom_fields->>'zoho_customer_id'. Verified end-to-end: customer_account_summary outstanding 3274.67 / 1 open; ops_customer_statement opening 0 / 16 ledger rows / closing 3274.67. Invoice line items are free_form (not linked to items catalog or time entries). Project/time history (6 projects, 45 timesheet rows from the other exports) INGESTED (Session 118, scripted, idempotent on custom_fields ? 'zoho_project_id'): 6 projects (billing_method project_hours w/ rate 65/100 or task_hours; status active), 26 project_tasks (is_billable, zoho_task_id in custom_fields), 45 time_entries (all attributed to Cole's operations user, hours parsed from HH:MM = 97.00 total, 44 invoiced + 1 unbilled, is_approved true, zoho_time_entry_id in custom_fields). All 45 entries resolved to a task. CAVEAT: the Timesheet_Details export is PARTIAL vs Zoho's Project Summary logged totals — ingested 97.00h vs ~126.33h summary (3 of the 6 projects reconcile exactly; the other 3 are short ~20.33h, 3.00h, and 6.00h). Tasks exist but had no line-level timesheet rows. Does NOT affect AR (billing captured in invoices). Options if reconciliation wanted: complete timesheet re-export, or synthesize one summary entry per missing task (fabricates dates — not done without Cole's ok). Imported invoices NOT linked to imported projects/tasks/time (free_form lines; linkage available if Cole asks). **PHASE 8 effectively COMPLETE** (depth UI + contacts + statements + credit-balance view + import RPCs + real-customer migration); P9 QA/polish/deploy is the next session.
- **P9: QA, polish, deploy. Largely done (Session 123).** Backend integrity sweep clean; the statusVariant 'done'/'throttled' cosmetic fix shipped + SHA-verified; the Deals row-click and received_count carryforwards were found ALREADY FIXED in the live repo. Stripe charge.refunded dashboard subscription DONE (Cole). REMAINING: live refund test deferred until a real Stripe transaction exists to refund safely (the ops-stripe-webhook charge.refunded handler v11 + ops_handle_stripe_refund RPC are already built and verified).

**Session 115 order: P3 conversions (estimate→project, estimate→retainer) → P1 residuals (charge.refunded subscribe + refund-flow UI + first-email verification) → P5 Reports.**

The backend Phase 0–4 decomposition below is retained as build history (it tracks the original backend-first table/RPC/Edge-Function sequence, which does not map 1:1 onto the 9-phase product roadmap above).

Scope source: `BrainWise_Zoho_Replacement_Doc1_Invoice.md` (invoice) + `BrainWise_Zoho_Replacement_Doc5_CrossCutting.md` (architecture). Doc 1 v1 is a 5.5–8 month, ~25-table build; sequenced in phases, backend-first.

Scope decisions locked Session 108 (do not re-litigate):
- Single `operations` schema (Doc 5 §2.1 prose said `operations_*`; resolved to one schema, dot notation, matching every Doc 5 example).
- `operations.organizations` is the tenant root (Gap 2). `org_id` references it, never `public.organizations` (Doc 5 §2.3 Rule 1: no cross-schema FK ops→public). One v1 row: BrainWise Enterprises.
- `operations.users.id` is PK = FK to `auth.users.id`; shared Supabase auth, no `password_hash` (Gap 3). Role enum = Doc 5's four roles `admin|sales_user|sales_manager|read_only` (supersedes Doc 1 §6.5's `admin|staff|timesheet_staff`).
- Custom-fields engine is operations-local (`operations.custom_field_definitions`), NOT the shared `public` engine Doc 5 §3.4 described (Gap 4 = B). Phase 4. Rationale: the "shared" justification is speculative; `public.custom_field_definitions` does not exist; isolation discipline preferred.
- Stripe routing under the one shared account (Gap 5 = A): a SECOND Stripe webhook endpoint → new `ops-stripe-webhook`, all ops-created Stripe objects tagged `metadata.source=operations`, both handlers defensively filter on it. The existing platform `stripe-webhook` (v29) / `create-checkout` (v60) are NOT modified — keeps the corporate-invoice / zero-Stripe-exposure boundary intact. Deferred to Phase 3.

Doc 5 "reuse" claims corrected against live state (Session 108 probe): notification system is `notification_types_catalog` + `user_notifications` + `user_notification_preferences` with fns `notify_user` / `compose_notification_email` / `notification_display` (NOT Doc 5's `notification_catalog` name) — reusable for ops notification types. `public.custom_field_definitions` does NOT exist (Doc 5 §3.4 wrong). No `public.audit_log` (platform has `super_admin_audit_log` + `company_admin_audit_log`); ops audit log will be net-new `operations.audit_log`. NO Stripe mirror tables in `public` at all — confirms Stripe-as-source-of-truth; ops `stripe_customers`/`stripe_webhook_events` are net-new, no collision.

Phase plan:
- **Phase 0 — foundation: DONE (Session 108).** `operations` schema; `user_role` enum; `set_updated_at()`; `operations.organizations` (seeded BrainWise Enterprises); `operations.users` (auth-linked, seeded Cole as admin); RLS helpers `current_org_id()` / `current_user_role()` (SECURITY DEFINER, `search_path=''`, non-recursive); core tables `customers`, `contact_persons`, `items` with full RLS (canonical pattern), org_id indexes, updated_at triggers. RLS isolation proven both directions via simulated JWT. No Lovable spend.
- **Phase 1 — time/projects: DONE (Session 108).** `projects` (4 billing methods), `project_tasks`, `project_users`, `project_attachments`, `time_entries` (+ partial-unique running-timer index, minute precision). Weekly-grid backend covered by `time_entries`; UI is frontend.
- **Phase 2 — documents: DONE (Session 108).** `estimates`, `invoices` (generated `balance_due`), `retainer_invoices` (generated `available_balance`), `credit_notes` (generated `balance`), polymorphic `document_lines`, atomic numbering engine (`next_document_number` + org-explicit `next_document_number_for_org`, yearly/monthly reset). Status-lifecycle + accept-by-link public route are frontend.
- **Phase 3 — money: BACKEND DONE (Session 108, 3a + 3b).** 3a: `payments`, `payment_allocations`, `customer_credits`, `stripe_customers`, `stripe_webhook_events`. 3b: `ops-stripe-webhook` (Gap 5, signature-verified, `metadata.source=operations` isolation filter, idempotent payment RPC), `ops-invoice-checkout` (Stripe Tax `automatic_tax` + per-line `stripe_tax_code`), `ops-invoice-send` + `ops-payment-reminder-cron` (Resend-direct from `invoices@`/`reminders@`), `recurring_invoice_templates` + recurring/overdue/reminder logic, 3 crons scheduled (`ops_overdue_flag_daily`, `ops_recurring_invoice_daily`, `ops_payment_reminder_daily`). **$1 end-to-end live-Stripe test PASSED (Session 109):** checkout → `ops-stripe-webhook` (signature-verified, `source=operations` isolation confirmed) → payment $1.00 recorded → allocated → invoice flipped `paid`, `balance_due` 0, no excess/credit; `automatic_tax.status=complete` computed $0 (correct outcome: no active GA sales-tax registration — see Cole-side items). REMAINING money backend: **ops refund webhook handling** (`charge.refunded` → update `payments.refunded_amount`, reopen invoice) — currently unbuilt, so refunds reflect only in Stripe, not in our ledger. First real reminder/invoice email still unverified (will verify the Resend-direct path on first fire).
- **Phase 4 — reporting/config: PARTIAL (Session 108).** DONE: config tables (`tax_authorities`, `tax_rates`, `email_templates`, `reminder_schedules`, `late_fee_rules`, `currencies`, `custom_field_definitions` ops-local) + 5 of ~16 report views (`ar_aging_detail`, `ar_aging_summary`, `unbilled_hours`, `unbilled_expenses`, `customer_balance_summary`). REMAINING backend: ~11 more report views, salesperson/commission, Zoho-CSV migration tooling, PTP-own-PDF generation engine (Doc 1 D5, not started). Settings UI is frontend.

Cole-side external dependencies — status after Session 108: **DONE** — Stripe Tax configured in the live account (preset category "Digital products > Business and web services", automatic behavior; state nexus registrations being added by Cole); second Stripe webhook endpoint registered in LIVE mode → `ops-stripe-webhook` URL, `OPS_STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` set in Supabase; Resend needs **no new setup** (the `mail.brainwiseenterprises.com` domain is already verified for the platform `noreply@`, so ops sends from `invoices@`/`reminders@` on the same domain with zero new DNS — the per-sender-subdomain idea in Doc 5 §3.6 is an optional future reputation-isolation step, not required). **Both pre-frontend gates DONE (Session 109):** `operations` schema exposed (verified via REST probe — anon correctly denied; all 5 report views confirmed `security_invoker=true`, so no cross-tenant view leak); $1 live-Stripe test passed. **Cole-side action item (NOT engineering, gated on CPA):** confirm with a CPA whether BrainWise's SaaS / digital-assessment / coaching offerings are taxable in Georgia and whether a collection obligation exists. Note: physical-presence nexus applies because the business is located in GA, independent of the 100k-USD / 200-transaction economic threshold (that threshold is for remote sellers and excludes services). If taxable: register for a **Sales and Use Tax** account (NOT Corporate Income) via the Georgia Tax Center, then add that registration in Stripe → Tax → Registrations — the verified integration picks it up with zero code change.

**Frontend enablement DONE (Session 109): operations TypeScript types.** The Supabase MCP `generate_typescript_types` tool emits ONLY the `public` schema (verified — zero `operations` mentions), and Lovable auto-regenerates `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` from `public` on sync, so operations types must live in a SEPARATE hand-maintained file or a Lovable sync clobbers them. Generated `src/integrations/supabase/operations-types.ts` by introspecting the live schema (all 32 tables + 5 views + `user_role` enum; generated columns omitted from Insert/Update; `tsc --noEmit --strict` clean). It exports `OperationsDatabase` and a dedicated client `opsSupabase = createClient<OperationsDatabase>(url, key, { db:{ schema:'operations' }, auth:{ storageKey:'sb-svprhtzawnbzmumxnhsq-auth-token', persistSession:true, autoRefreshToken:false }})`. PATTERN: ops frontend uses `opsSupabase.from(...)`; it shares the primary client's session via the same storageKey and disables its own refresh (only the primary client refreshes), so RLS applies as normal. Emits a harmless "Multiple GoTrueClient instances" console warning. **Regenerate this file (re-run the introspection generator) whenever the operations schema changes — it is NOT covered by Lovable's type sync.** Cole commits this file manually like the other repo files.

**Operations frontend build IN PROGRESS (Session 109).** Slice 1 (read + Pay Now) live: `/operations/customers`, `/operations/customers/:id`, `/operations/invoices`, `/operations/invoices/:id`, gated by `RoleGuard allowedRoles={["brainwise_super_admin"]}` inside the existing `ProtectedRoute`+`AppLayout`; Pay Now calls `supabase.functions.invoke("ops-invoice-checkout")` then `window.location.href = data.url`; `?paid=1`/`?canceled=1` handled. Slice 2 (customer create/edit) live: `CustomerFormDialog`, plain useState pattern (app does NOT use react-hook-form in pages). Backend for inserts: `customers.org_id` now DEFAULT `operations.current_org_id()` and `created_by` DEFAULT `auth.uid()` — form omits both. NOTE: `customers` Insert type in `operations-types.ts` was hand-edited to make `org_id` optional after the default was added (any future table that gets an org_id default needs the same Insert-type tweak). Slice 3 (invoice create/edit) backend DONE + verified: `public.ops_create_invoice(p_header jsonb, p_lines jsonb)` and `public.ops_update_invoice(p_id uuid, p_header jsonb, p_lines jsonb)`, SECURITY DEFINER, search_path '', granted to `authenticated` only. They resolve org via `current_org_id()`, validate the customer belongs to the org, allocate the number via `next_document_number_for_org(org,'invoice')`, compute per-line `line_total` and invoice subtotal/total server-side (tax_amount 0; Stripe computes tax at checkout), insert invoice (status `draft`) + `document_lines`. Update guards status IN ('draft','sent') and replaces lines. Frontend calls them via the DEFAULT `supabase` client (`supabase.rpc('ops_create_invoice', { p_header, p_lines })`), NOT direct table inserts, so invoices/document_lines need no org_id default and no Insert-type edit. Verified end to end with a simulated authenticated call (INV-2026-0001, totals correct), then cleaned up and reset the invoice sequence to 1. Lovable prompt for the invoice form is next. **UPDATE (Session 109 continued): target locked = A2 (all 14 Doc 1 v1 modules); product model locked (customer to named projects with a billing_method to tasks/time to invoice; tasks != items; in-context multi-entry UX); Phase 1 money path COMPLETE — ops_record_payment / ops_set_invoice_status / ops_clone_invoice / ops_delete_draft_invoice / ops_handle_stripe_refund all verified, ops-stripe-webhook v3 (charge.refunded), ops-payment-receipt v1, payment_mode += ach, org/created_by defaults on projects+project_tasks+time_entries; UI slices live = customer dialog, items, invoice form, projects+tasks spine, with Record-payment + lifecycle-Actions + Send-invoice/Send-receipt prompts delivered. Numbering corrected: next_number=2 + last_reset_at=now (the earlier reset-to-1 was wrong once a real INV-2026-0001 existed; never reset to 1 again). Full detail in v117 below + session-109-to-110.md. Session 110 opens on Phase 2 (time + expenses into billing).** **UPDATE (Session 110 close): Phase 2 COMPLETE — composable billing (billing_method = hourly scheme only: project_hours|task_hours|staff_hours|none; fixed fees are project_charges; expenses independent), project_time_rollup view, operations-receipts bucket + 6 seeded categories, project_charges table, ops_create_invoice_from_project RPC (composes charges + time-by-method + marked-up expenses into a draft, flips sources to invoiced), and delete/void source-release patch all shipped + verified; four Lovable prompts built (untested, batched to one pass). Customer-pay gap found (branded invoice email pay button links to a super-admin-gated route; external customers cannot pay) — Option A public /pay/:token page slotted into Phase 3. Full detail in v118 below + session-110-to-111.md. Session 111 opens on Cole's Phase 2 test pass, then Phase 3 (estimates + accept-by-link + convert + public pay page).** **UPDATE (Session 111 close): Phase 2 confirmed GREEN by Cole, then invoicing depth shipped + verified — (a) edit/delete for time/expenses/tasks (uninvoiced-only, FK-aware task delete, receipt cleanup); (b) Way B customer-level "invoice from work" (invoiceable_candidates view + ops_create_invoice_from_selection RPC + InvoiceFromWork page + entry buttons); (c) invoice detail levels (Itemized / Summary by project) with project-name line prefixes (shared _ops_emit_from_sel helper + p_detail on both composers). P6 receipt-attachment scope note recorded. Full detail in v119 below + session-111-to-112.md. Session 112 opens on Phase 3 (estimates + accept-by-link + convert + Option A public /pay/:token customer pay page).**

### Operations Platform: CRM (Wave 2, Doc 2): COMPLETE (Sessions 119-123; 11-phase roadmap; Phases 1-10 COMPLETE end-to-end and verified; Phase 9 QA done with Zoho CRM import dropped [no Zoho CRM history], Phase 10 owner/team RLS hardening done Session 123)

CRM layer of the operations platform (Doc 2). Greenfield inside the `operations` schema; no lead/account/deal/pipeline/activity tables existed before this session. Reuses the live substrate: tenant root `operations.organizations`, `operations.current_org_id()` + `operations.current_user_role()` (SECDEF) for org default + RLS, `operations.log_activity(...)` writing `operations.audit_log` (the change-log; distinct from Doc 2's user-facing activities entity), the `operations.user_role` enum (already carries admin/sales_user/sales_manager/read_only), and the `operations.custom_field_definitions` engine. Doc 5 honored: all CRM tables stay intra-schema, FK to `operations.organizations`, no cross-schema FK.

**Locked modeling decisions (Session 119, Cole-approved):**
- **Accounts = NEW `operations.accounts` table with a nullable `customer_id` FK to `operations.customers` (option B).** Not reusing `customers` as the account (its type CHECK is individual|business and it carries no owner/hierarchy/industry/domain; reusing it would pollute the live billing table with prospects/partners). The Won bridge creates/links the customers row, matching the Q10 5-minute-delay design.
- **Contacts = EXTEND `operations.contact_persons` (option A).** Make `customer_id` nullable, add `account_id` FK to accounts, `owner_user_id`, and the CRM columns (marketing/transactional opt-out pairs, linkedin/twitter, created_from, lead_source_id, relationship_score); change the customers FK from ON DELETE CASCADE to ON DELETE SET NULL so deleting a billing customer never erases CRM relationship history. Keeps one unified contact identity (timeline UX + v2 portal). MUST verify before the Phase-1 migration: no live Doc-1 RPC/Lovable insert path assumes customer_id is always present.
- **RLS = org-scoped now, owner column present, owner/team differentiation deferred (option B).** `owner_user_id` on every CRM table from day one; RLS mirrors the live four-policy org-scoped pattern (select/insert/update on current_org_id(); delete also gated current_user_role()='admin'). Owner/team read/write differentiation (Doc 2 §5.14) is Phase 10, built before the second CRM user is onboarded, not before.

**11-phase roadmap (covers all of Doc 2 §5 v1 scope; excludes Doc 2 §8 v2/v3 items):**
- **Phase 0 — Foundation config. COMPLETE + verified (Session 119).** 3 migrations. (1) Four config tables `operations.pipelines`, `operations.deal_stages`, `operations.lead_statuses`, generic `operations.picklist_values` (all org-scoped default current_org_id(), set_updated_at trigger, four-policy RLS, authenticated+service_role grants, idempotency unique keys). (2) Extended `custom_field_definitions_entity_type_check` to add lead/account/contact/deal/activity (drop+re-add; Doc 5 §3.4). (3) Added `operations.users.is_salesperson` boolean default false. (4) Idempotent SECDEF seeder `operations.seed_crm_defaults(p_org uuid)` (search_path '', §156-locked: anon/PUBLIC/authenticated revoked, service_role only; verified authenticated cannot execute), reusable at org-creation time; seeded the BrainWise org: 1 default pipeline (BrainWise Corporate Sales) with 7 stages (Inquiry 10 / Discovery 25 / Demo 40 / Proposal 60 / Negotiation 80 / Closed Won 100 is_won+requires_reason / Closed Lost 0 is_lost+requires_reason), 5 lead statuses (Qualified carries promote_to_status_at_score=50), 10 lead_source + 4 won_reason + 9 lost_reason + 10 industry picklists. Idempotency re-verified (re-run delta=0, rolled back). NOTE for Phase 3 UI: stage colors include teal (Discovery) and green (Closed Won); honor the brand rule that green and teal must never be paired in pastel/muted card contexts.
- **Phase 1 — Core entities. COMPLETE + verified (Session 119).** Schema (5 migrations): `operations.accounts` (owner, type CHECK customer|prospect|partner|vendor|reseller, parent self-FK, industry_id→picklist, domain with partial-unique on lower(domain) per org, nullable customer_id FK→customers ON DELETE SET NULL, custom_fields); `contact_persons` EXTENDED (customer_id now nullable + FK→customers switched CASCADE→SET NULL; added account_id FK, owner_user_id, title/department/mobile/alternate_phone, linkedin/twitter, date_of_birth, lead_source_id, reports_to self-FK, marketing+transactional opt-out triples, mailing/other address, description, tags, custom_fields, relationship_score, created_from CHECK, source_lead_id, enrichment_data/last_enriched_at, last_activity_at); `operations.leads` (status_id→lead_statuses, source_id/industry_id→picklist, company_name_text + last_name NOT NULL, score, converted_* FKs, archived_at, utm_*, source_webhook_id [FK in Phase 6]); `operations.deals` (pipeline/stage/account NOT NULL FKs, primary_contact SET NULL, amount/probability with 0–100 CHECK, expected_revenue GENERATED, type CHECK, won/lost reason, parent_deal self-FK, renewal fields, rot fields) + `deal_stage_history` (append-only) + `deal_team_members` (role CHECK, unique per deal+user) + `deal_stakeholders` (role CHECK, unique per deal+contact) + `deal_line_items` (FK→items SET NULL). Cross-cycle FKs (contact_persons.source_lead_id→leads, leads.converted_deal_id→deals) attached in M5 after all tables existed. Verified no live Doc-1 caller depends on contact_persons.customer_id NOT NULL (only ops_add_contact/ops_update_contact touch it, both pass it; no direct client insert in the repo). NEXT (M6+): stage-history + auto-probability triggers on deals; ops_convert_lead + ops_bulk_convert_leads (Account+Contact+Deal, domain-dedup, archive lead); Won→customer bridge RPC (plain RPC now; the Q10 5-min-delay auto-trigger is Phase 7). **Behavior (M6 triggers + M7 RPCs, all verified by one rolled-back DO block):** Two deals triggers — BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE `deals_set_probability_close` (probability defaults from the stage on insert and follows the stage on change unless the caller set it explicitly; stamps actual_close_date when moved to a won/lost stage) and AFTER INSERT/UPDATE `deals_record_stage_history` (writes deal_stage_history on creation and on every stage change, computing time_in_prior_stage_seconds; transitioned_by resolved from auth.uid() looked up in operations.users so it is null for non-ops/cron actors). Three public SECDEF RPCs (search_path '', anon/PUBLIC revoked, authenticated+service_role): `ops_convert_lead(p_lead_id,p_options)` (resolves account by forced id > email-domain match > new prospect; creates a contact created_from='lead_conversion' set primary when the account has none; optionally creates a deal in the org default pipeline's first open stage; archives the lead with converted_* links; logs lead_converted + account_created_from_lead + deal_created), `ops_bulk_convert_leads(uuid[],p_options)` (loops with per-lead EXCEPTION isolation; returns converted/errors + counts), `ops_create_customer_from_deal(p_deal_id)` (Won bridge: idempotent if account.customer_id set; else creates customers row from the account, sets account.customer_id + type='customer', links the account's unlinked contacts to the new customer; NO automatic on-Won trigger here, that is Phase 7's 5-min-delay queue). DO-block test asserted: new account, initial probability 10 (Inquiry) + 1 history row on convert, probability 25 + 2 history rows + non-null time-in-prior on stage move to Discovery, and customer created + account linked/flipped + contact linked by the bridge; rolled back, zero residue.
- **Phase 2 — Activities + timeline. COMPLETE + verified (Session 119).** 3 migrations. `operations.activities` (single table, type CHECK task|call|meeting|email|note; status/priority/call_type/call_result CHECKs allow-null; polymorphic related_to_type CHECK lead|account|contact|deal + related_to_id with a both-or-neither CHECK; meeting + email_* columns inline for manually-logged emails ahead of Phase 4's full Sync-A; attachment_count, custom_fields) + `operations.activity_attendees` (attendee_type CHECK contact|user|external) + `operations.activity_attachments` (metadata only; the operations-attachments storage bucket is a Phase-3/later dependency). Triggers: set_updated_at; `activities_validate_related` (BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE — the polymorphic target must exist in the same org, else raise); `activity_attachments_count` (AFTER INSERT/DELETE maintains activities.attachment_count). All three RLS four-policy org-scoped + authenticated/service_role grants. `public.ops_entity_timeline(p_entity_type,p_entity_id,p_limit)` (SECDEF, anon/PUBLIC revoked) returns a jsonb array unioning activities (related_to match) with audit_log events (entity match) for a lead/account/contact/deal, ordered desc by time with the limit applied inside the union before aggregation. `operations.deal_pipeline_health` view (security_invoker) per deal: is_open, days_in_current_stage (off the latest deal_stage_history transition), rot_threshold_days, is_rotting, next_activity_at, has_next_activity, is_stale_no_activity (the activity-based-selling warn flag; false for terminal stages) — drives the Kanban red-glow. Verified by one rolled-back DO block: related-to bad-ref raised, attachment_count 1→0, timeline returned both the activity and an audit event, health view showed has_next=true/stale=false/open=true/rotting=false; zero residue. (Doc 2 §5.5.)
- **Phase 3 — Pipeline Kanban + saved lists + detail pages (Lovable). COMPLETE (Session 119: all 6 prompts shipped + verified by API SHA; saved_lists backend built + verified).** RECON (live operations frontend): pages in `src/pages/operations/`; routes in `src/App.tsx` (pattern `RoleGuard allowedRoles={["brainwise_super_admin"]}` + `SuperAdminSessionProvider`, super-admin-gated for now per the in-file note "refine later via operations.users membership"); nav in `src/components/AppSidebar.tsx` (typed NavItem array, sectionHeader groups; "Operations" group starts at Customers). Client split: `opsSupabase` (from `@/integrations/supabase/operations-types`) for direct ops table reads/writes under RLS; default `supabase.rpc(... as any)` for public RPCs; operations-types.ts is NOT regenerated (use `as any`/embeds). List template = OperationsCustomers.tsx (useQuery key `["ops",entity,"list"]`, Card+Table, row→detail). Shared helpers in `_shared.tsx`. Access model: leads/accounts/contacts/deals basic CRUD via direct opsSupabase writes under RLS (no CRUD RPCs); only convert/bulk-convert/Won-bridge + ops_entity_timeline are RPCs. Decomposition (Cole runs each; verify by API SHA after): **P3.1** AppSidebar CRM section + App.tsx routes + 4 list pages (Leads/Accounts/Contacts/Deals) + 4 create/edit dialogs (direct inserts; deals dialog resolves default pipeline + first open stage to satisfy NOT NULL; contacts dialog is account-scoped, distinct from the customer-scoped ContactFormDialog) — SHIPPED + verified against the repo by API SHA (commit 5a8e29b), Session 119. New files: OperationsLeads/Accounts/Contacts/Deals.tsx + LeadFormDialog/AccountFormDialog/ContactCrmFormDialog/DealFormDialog.tsx; edited AppSidebar.tsx (CRM nav group) + App.tsx (4 routes); ContactFormDialog.tsx untouched. Verified embeds (status:lead_statuses, source:picklist_values!leads_source_id_fkey [required disambiguation], account/stage single-FK) and DealFormDialog default-pipeline+first-open-stage resolution. PostgREST schema cache reloaded (NOTIFY pgrst) before the run so the new tables/relationships were queryable. Row-click opens the edit dialog (interim until detail pages in P3.3–P3.5). **P3.2** Pipeline Kanban at /operations/pipeline (columns=deal_stages, native HTML5 drag→update deals.stage_id [triggers handle history+probability], cards + deal_pipeline_health glow, sidebar Pipeline as first CRM item) — SHIPPED + verified (OperationsPipeline.tsx). **P3.3** Deal detail /operations/deals/:id (header stats + stage Select + Edit + Won→customer via supabase.rpc ops_create_customer_from_deal; tabs Overview/Activities[ops_entity_timeline + log activity]/Team[deal_team_members]/Stakeholders[deal_stakeholders]/Line items[deal_line_items, line_total computed client-side]) — SHIPPED + verified (OperationsDealDetail.tsx). RPC client split confirmed: public RPCs via default `supabase` client, tables via `opsSupabase`. **P3.4** Lead detail + Convert (ops_convert_lead) + bulk convert (ops_bulk_convert_leads); extracted reusable EntityTimeline.tsx; ConvertLeadDialog options {create_deal, account_id, pipeline_id, deal_name, deal_amount}; leads list gained multi-select + row→detail — SHIPPED + verified (OperationsLeadDetail.tsx, EntityTimeline.tsx, ConvertLeadDialog.tsx, OperationsLeads.tsx). **P3.5** Account detail (parent self-embed !accounts_parent_account_id_fkey, linked-customer chip, tabs Contacts/Deals/Activity) + Contact detail (opt-out toggles writing _at timestamps, linked-customer chip) — SHIPPED + verified (OperationsAccountDetail.tsx, OperationsContactDetail.tsx); accounts/contacts lists switched to row→detail. **P3.6a** activity dashboard /operations/activities (Overdue/Today/This week buckets from activities.scheduled_start_at, excludes done, related-entity links; sidebar Activity item) — SHIPPED + verified (OperationsActivities.tsx). **P3.6b** saved views: generic SavedViewsBar.tsx (saved_lists insert {entity_type,name,filters}; org/owner/created_by default server-side; load/delete) wired into all 4 list pages with minimal filters (search .or ilike + one entity select, empty filters omitted) — SHIPPED + verified (SavedViewsBar.tsx + 4 list pages). saved_lists backend (operations.saved_lists, 4-policy org-scoped RLS, set_updated_at, grants) built + verified this session (rolled-back JWT-sim insert confirmed org default; cache reloaded). Rich query-builder (multi-condition/column chooser) deferred to v2 (Doc 2 §8). **POLISH CARRYFORWARD:** Deals LIST row-click still opens the edit dialog (P3.1 interim) instead of navigating to /operations/deals/:id like the other three lists — one-line fix outstanding. Config editors for Phase-0 picklists/stages are Phase 8, not Phase 3. (Doc 2 §3.7, §5, §6.11.)
- **Phase 4 (Email, Sync-A). COMPLETE + verified (Session 120).** operations.crm_email_templates (named separately because operations.email_templates already exists for Doc-1 invoice/reminder templates), operations.email_activities + operations.email_tracking_events + operations.email_inbound_ingestion (all audit-log posture: SELECT-only RLS, writes via service_role SECDEF recorders only). inbox_token routing (operations.users.inbox_token, address token@inbox.brainwiseenterprises.com). RPCs ops_crm_email_prepare (two-tier opt-out: transactional hard-block, marketing block on templated/bulk/marketing, marketing warn on one_off; recipient-to-contact match; render_merge) / ops_crm_email_record_sent / ops_crm_email_record_tracking (service_role-only, dedup, open/click counters) / ops_get_my_inbox_address / ops_crm_email_record_inbound (always-store-raw, idempotent, best-effort contact match) / ops_crm_email_link_inbound (manual link to lead/account/contact/deal). Edge fns crm-email-send v1 + crm-email-inbound v1 + resend-webhook v12. Clicks-on, open-tracking-off. Sent and received emails surface in ops_entity_timeline with no RPC change. Deferred to a later pass: attachments (inbound and outbound), send retry queue, multi-entity inbound-link UI. (Doc 2 §5.6.)
- **Phase 5: Calendar. COMPLETE (backend + frontend), Session 121.** Frontend shipped + verified by GitHub API SHA: AddToCalendarButton.tsx (Google/Outlook-web/.ics, fire-and-forget ops_record_calendar_link) wired into EntityTimeline meeting rows; Calendar-reminders settings card in OperationsSettings (Templates & Reminders tab) saving via ops_update_reminder_settings (added this session, admin-gated, validates tz + 0-23 hour). Backend: organizations += timezone/day_of_digest_hour/reminders_enabled; meeting_calendar_links (audit of Add-to-Calendar invocations) + meeting_reminders (send-log + dedup); RPCs ops_record_calendar_link (authenticated), ops_record_meeting_reminder_sent / ops_record_day_of_digest_sent / ops_due_meeting_reminders / ops_due_day_of_digest (service_role only, §156); ops-meeting-reminder-cron v1 (jobid 28, 5,20,35,50) + ops-day-of-digest-cron v1 (jobid 29, hourly :10, org-local-hour gate), both reuse reminders@mail.brainwiseenterprises.com. Decisions: email-only (1hr-before + day-of digest, 15min in-app deferred, no ops notification surface), owner-only recipients, compute-in-cron dedup. (Doc 2 §5.7.)
- **Phase 6: Lead capture + enrichment. COMPLETE (backend + frontend), Session 121; live providers verified.** Frontend shipped + verified by GitHub API SHA: OperationsLeadCapture.tsx (new /operations/lead-capture page + route + nav; RPC-driven webhook CRUD, one-time secret reveal on create/rotate, signing-info reveal, toggle/delete, HMAC-vs-token help) and enrichment UI on OperationsLeadDetail.tsx ("Captured via web form" badge, Enrichment card reading enrichment_log + "Verify email"/"Enrich company" via ops_enqueue_enrichment). LIVE PROVIDER SMOKE TEST PASSED (Apollo org-enrich on stripe.com returned full firmographics; Hunter verify on a public address returned status=valid; test rows cleaned up); Apollo key confirmed working for organizations/enrich on the current plan. TWO trivial cosmetic frontend follow-ups (non-blocking): OperationsLeadCapture reads received_count but the RPC returns total_received (count always shows 0); OperationsLeadDetail statusVariant does not map 'done' to the success color. Backend: lead_capture_webhooks (RPC-mediated, REVOKE ALL FROM authenticated/anon so hmac_secret is privilege-hidden, not just RLS-hidden), webhook_ingestion_log (rate-limit window + audit), enrichment_log (doubles as queue: pending|done|error|throttled|skipped); leads.source_webhook_id FK attached; organizations += enrichment_monthly_quota (default 200). 12 RPCs: webhook CRUD (list/create/update/rotate/get_signing_info/delete; create+rotate return secret once; reads org-member, writes admin), ops_webhook_resolve + ops_log_webhook_ingestion + ops_ingest_captured_lead (rate-check + NOT-NULL fallbacks + auto-enrich enqueue + accepted-log, all service_role), ops_due_enrichments (quota-guarded drain) + ops_record_enrichment_result (writes enrichment_data back to lead) + ops_enqueue_enrichment (manual, authenticated). Edge fns: ops-webhook-lead-capture v1 (public, verify_jwt false; token-resolve, HMAC verify, honeypot→silent 200, throttle→429, 5 recipe normalizers generic/lovable_form/tally/microsoft_bookings/stripe/zapier + field_mapping override) and ops-enrichment-cron v1 (jobid 30, 2,17,32,47; consolidated Apollo org-enrich + Hunter email-verify, Apollo people path records skipped, plan-gated). Decisions: 6A Apollo ORG-ENRICH ONLY (people gated by plan) + Hunter email; 6B per-(webhook,IP) sliding-window default 60/min; 6C enrichment_log=queue cron-drained, auto-enrich default OFF; 6D secret service-role-only; 6E 5 recipes. DEVIATION: single drain cron instead of separate apollo/hunter functions. (Doc 2 §5.8–5.9.)
- **Phase 7: Lead scoring + assignment + workflow rules. COMPLETE (backend + frontend), Session 122.** Backend: `lead_scoring_rules` (demographic/behavioral/decay rule_type) + append-only `lead_score_events`; scoring engine `operations._recompute_lead_score` + public `ops_recompute_lead_score`; score-decay cron (jobid 31); round-robin owner assignment; `workflow_rules` + `workflow_execution_log` (dedup); scan-based engine `ops_workflow_engine_run` with 4 kinds (won_to_customer / deal_stale / lead_qualified_assign / deal_discovery_task) + additive `operations._won_bridge_create_customer` (Won bridge runs here, additive so it never raises under the service-role scan); 4 seeded workflow rules; engine cron jobid 32 (`3-59/5 * * * *`). DECISION: scan-based engine (no per-row table triggers) to avoid recursion; the Won bridge is an additive internal helper rather than an on-Won trigger. Frontend (verified by GitHub API SHA): ScoreCard on OperationsLeadDetail (recompute via ops_recompute_lead_score + score-event history from lead_score_events); LeadScoringRulesCard (full CRUD, conditional dialog by rule_type) + WorkflowRulesCard (edit-only, per-kind params, active toggle) in a new "CRM Automation" tab on OperationsSettings. (Doc 2 §5.10, §5.13.)
- **Phase 8: Campaigns, attribution, reports, dashboard, settings UI. COMPLETE (backend + frontend), Session 122.** Backend: `campaigns` + `campaign_attribution`; UTM capture into the lead row via the Phase-6 webhook path; 21 `report_crm_*` views (all `security_invoker=true`, GRANT SELECT authenticated+service_role); `ops_auto_attribute_lead_utm` public RPC (manual re-attribution). Frontend (verified by GitHub API SHA): OperationsCampaigns CRUD page + route + nav; 21 `crm_*` report defs appended to OperationsReports (date filter / column toggle / CSV / PDF already on the page); NEW OperationsDashboard at guarded `/operations/dashboard` (4 KPI cards, 2 recharts bar charts navy #021F36 Total / orange #F5741A Weighted, 4 tables). **TWO backend gaps caught + fixed this session.** (1) **org_id default gap:** the 4 new Phase 7/8 tables (campaigns, campaign_attribution, lead_scoring_rules, workflow_rules) had NO DEFAULT on org_id (unlike older ops tables), so UI create dialogs that omit org_id would fail NOT NULL; migration `s122_p8_orgid_default_backfill` set `DEFAULT operations.current_org_id()` on all four. (2) **Campaign attribution was fully unwired:** `ops_auto_attribute_lead_utm` existed but was orphaned (org-guarded so it raises on the service-role webhook path), so no `campaign_attribution` rows were ever created and `report_crm_campaign_roi` + the dashboard campaign view always read empty. The ROI view counts `attributed_leads` from target_type='lead' rows AND `attributed_deals`/`won_revenue` from target_type='deal' rows, so both capture-time and conversion-time wiring were needed. Fix (`s122_p8_campaign_attribution_wiring`, fully additive, rolled-back functional test PASSED `SENTINEL_OK lead_attr=1 deal_attr=1 roi_leads=1 roi_deals=1`): internal service-role `operations._attribute_lead_utm(uuid)` (no current_org_id guard, derives org from the lead, REVOKE from authenticated/anon/PUBLIC + GRANT service_role per §156); AFTER INSERT trigger `tg_lead_attribute_on_insert` on operations.leads (attributes a captured lead to its campaign by name match when utm_campaign present, covers webhook + manual + future import); AFTER UPDATE OF converted_deal_id trigger `tg_lead_attribution_to_deal` (copies the lead's attribution to the new deal as target_type='deal' on conversion, drives attributed_deals + won_revenue). Public `ops_auto_attribute_lead_utm` kept as the manual re-attribution entry. Dashboard frontend fix: Lovable guessed 3 wrong column keys, corrected to match the views (win rate `deals_reached`/`deals_won`, leads-by-source `source_name`, lost-reason `lost_amount`). DEVIATION from the original scope line: 21 report views shipped (not the planned 18); dashboard is a fixed layout (per-user configurable widgets deferred). (Doc 2 §5.11–5.12, §5.15.)
- **Phase 9: QA. COMPLETE (Session 123).** Zoho CRM import tooling DROPPED (Cole never used Zoho CRM, starting from scratch). Backend integrity sweep clean across both platforms (invoices + payments + CRM); live campaign-attribution chain seeded as first real data and verified end to end (Website Inbound campaign, attributed lead + deal, ROI report populated); Won bridge fired and verified (first real customer). (Doc 2 §7.8.)
- **Phase 10: Owner/team RLS hardening. COMPLETE (Session 123).** manager_id self-FK on operations.users (team = direct reports, flat one-level; territory/multi-team deferred to v3); shared operations.crm_can_write(owner) helper; INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies on leads/accounts/contact_persons/deals/activities gated to admin=any, sales_manager=own+team, sales_user=own, read_only=none; SELECT stays org-wide read-all for every role (so dashboard/reports unaffected). Verified by JWT-sim role matrix across all 5 tables (rolled back). Frontend follow-up carried: create dialogs must stamp owner_user_id before the first non-admin user. (Doc 2 §5.14.)
- **Decided, not raised as questions:** CRM compose templates extend the existing Doc-1-shaped `email_templates` (add nullable name/category/merge_tags_used + a domain discriminator) rather than a second templates table; accounts.type uses a CHECK (customer|prospect|partner|vendor|reseller) in Phase 1, an editable account_type picklist reconsidered in Phase 8 settings.

### LMS + course-sharing additions (added Session 108, Cole)
- **SCORM export + per-course external-LMS API link** (EPIC, needs design). Two linked capabilities: (a) export a lesson-block course (the Rise-style authored content) as a SCORM package; (b) generate a per-course API / launch link to hand to a company so they can embed the course in their own LMS, with completion/progress tracking flowing back to and stored on the BrainWise side. Open design questions before build: SCORM 1.2 vs 2004 vs xAPI/cmi5, or LTI for the launch-link path; how tracking is reported back (SCORM runtime → our endpoint, or xAPI LRS); auth/scoping of the per-course link per company; what "course" maps to in our model (cert path / curriculum / module). Own sequencing decision.
- **Resources page + LMS discoverability rework** (needs MORE LMS RECON FIRST — Cole got feedback that modules are hard to find). (a) Pull "my learning" OUT of the Resources page (separate the learner's enrolled learning surface from the resource library). (b) Fix learning discoverability so it is not only cert paths that are visible — any associated curricula AND modules should surface too (standalone/associated curricula and modules are currently buried). Do a focused recon of the current learning-visibility surfaces (Resources, My Learning, cert-path/curriculum/module tiles, the RPCs feeding them) before drafting the rework.
- **Lesson-block editor into its own tab.** Surface the lesson-block editor as its own dedicated tab rather than nested/hidden where it currently lives. (Frontend; confirm current mount location during recon.)
- **Per-coach custom cover page.** One specific coach needs a different cover page than the default. Scope which surface this is (report cover / course cover / branded landing) and how the per-coach override is keyed before building.
