*v139 - Sessions 131 + 132 CLOSE (one continuous edge-function remediation arc). **Platform-wide "Conflicting API keys" edge-function bug found and fully eradicated; no SaaS-feature or Operations work.** One new standing rule (§158); §1-§157 hold. **THE BUG.** Super-admin/org invite flows (set-org-admin, single invite, bulk invite) silently showed a "manual code" fallback instead of emailing the invitation. Root cause: a recent Supabase gateway change rejects any edge-to-edge fetch to another /functions/v1/ function whose apikey and Authorization headers carry two DIFFERENT Supabase keys (anon in one, service-role in the other) as "Conflicting API keys", a 401 returned BEFORE the target function runs. invitation_send/bulk_invitation_send sent apikey=ANON + Authorization=Bearer SERVICE_ROLE, so every call to send-email died at the gateway. Diagnosed via a throwaway diag function after edge logs showed each invitation_send 200 paired with a send-email 401 (null function_id, 0ms). The frontend (CompanyMembersSection, CompanyInvitationsSection) was CORRECT, it only shows the manual code when the function returns email_sent:false, so this was never a Lovable fix. **THE FIX (everywhere): send the SAME key in both headers (anon in both).** **§158 NEW: edge-to-edge fetch must use matched API keys.** Any edge function that fetches another /functions/v1/ function must send the same key in apikey and Authorization (anon in both is canonical). Three patterns are immune and need no change: supabase.functions.invoke() (sends matched keys automatically), Resend-direct sends (no Supabase key pair at all), and fetches carrying only x-internal-secret (no apikey/Authorization pair). A user-JWT in Authorization + apikey:anon is also safe (a user JWT is not a Supabase API key, so no anon-vs-service conflict), that is the normal authenticated-request shape (generate-report, ai-chat, and the AI generator family calling check-ai-usage all use it). service+service matched keys also pass. **15 FUNCTIONS FIXED (lowered the send-email/send-departure-emails fetch Authorization from service-role to anon to match apikey; all boot-verified).** Session 131 (14): invitation_send v23, bulk_invitation_send v21, assign_epn_send, airsa-supervisor-invite, invite-coach, coach_invitation_resend, airsa-supervisor-reminder, deactivate-and-notify, bulk-deactivate-and-notify, dispatch-grace-reminders, verify-conversion, generate-departure-export, admin_trigger_password_reset, bulk_coach_invite. Session 132 (1): stripe-webhook v41 (the idiom lived only in the sendInvitationEmail helper's fetch to send-email, firing on the coach_order/coach_bulk_order branches; it does not touch signature verification, DB writes, coupon recalc, or subscription tiering; Authorization lowered to anon, redeployed verify_jwt false, boot-verified HTTP 400 "Missing signature or webhook secret"; held in 131 pending Cole's greenlight per the never-touch-platform-stripe-webhook-by-reflex rule, greenlit + fixed in 132). NOTE: an interim deploy briefly pushed a placeholder stub as v40 for ~90s; Stripe auto-retries non-2xx so no events were lost, and v41 restored the full corrected source immediately. **FULL SWEEP (Session 132), all ~95 edge functions reconciled, every other function CLEAN.** Email senders are either fixed (the 15 above) or already safe: the ops sender/cron family (ops-invoice-send, ops-send-reminder-now, ops-payment-receipt, ops-payment-reminder-cron, ops-day-of-digest-cron, ops-meeting-reminder-cron, ops-estimate-send) and newsletter-dispatch send Resend-direct; send-departure-emails / crm-email-send / crm-email-inbound are Resend-direct; submit-contact-request and submit-briefing-request call send-email with matched service+service keys (not the bug). Every edge-to-edge orchestration call elsewhere uses functions.invoke, x-internal-secret-only fetches, or the safe user-JWT+anon shape. Both Stripe webhooks, the storage/mux/asset functions, impersonation, check-*/log-audit, newsletter read-only, and the diagnostics carry no edge-to-edge send-email call at all. **NO schema/RLS/cron/table changes, edge-function source only.** diag-internal-secret-check and diag-resend-harness neutered to inert 410 stubs (Cole to delete from the dashboard; MCP has no delete-function). A related same-day side-task cleared 4 stale tanya@symbiokinetics.com corporate_invitations to enable re-test of the fixed flow. **DEFERRED (intended Session-131 work, never started, displaced by the bug):** the content-authoring restructure / content-item viewer (scope docs scope-content-authoring-restructure.md + content-item-viewer-scope.md already in the repo). **Edge fn versions: the 15 fixed above; no others changed. No cron/Operations/RLS/schema changes; ops-stripe-webhook never touched.** Carryforward (still open): the content-authoring restructure above; active-backlog items 4, 5, 6, 7, 11 untouched; ai-chat v50/v51 repo commit (Cole, manual); BQ-PTP-PERFECT-VERIFY; newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder; Doc-1 invoice live refund test; BUG-NWS-1; §82 facet_interpretations {public}-role nit; Operations Docs 3/4/5 backlog. Close artifacts (markdown only, Session-74): this v139 entry, architecture-reference v133, session-131-to-132.md.*

*v138 - Session 130 CLOSE. **BrainWise SaaS platform session: backlog items 2 (+10/25 AI-chat credits), 12 (lesson-block editor surfacing), and 8 (super-admin learning report, incl. a cert-path data cleanup) all built end-to-end and verified; no Operations work.** No new numbered standing rule (§1-§157 hold). Backend-first; every apply_migration followed by a separate execute_sql verify; functional tests single rolled-back DO blocks (sentinel RAISE, zero residue, JWT-sim via set_config request.jwt.claims in the same call as the RPC); all frontend verified by GitHub API SHA. **ITEM 2 — +10/25 one-time AI-chat credits, COMPLETE + live-verified.** Phase A (the real fix): check-ai-usage v61 now honors users.one_time_chat_credits as a FALLBACK at every denial site (except contract-disabled corporate chat); checkOnly probes surface credit_balance/subscription_active/used_credit, real calls consume one credit + best-effort ai_usage increment. grant_one_time_chat_credits / consume_one_time_chat_credit REVOKED from anon + authenticated (service_role/postgres only) to close self-mint/grief. AiChat.tsx was ALREADY credit-aware (gates on usage.allowed, renders credit_balance + the credit-aware LimitReached card) and ai-chat v51 calls check-ai-usage as the consume call, so v61 lit up the pre-built UI with ZERO frontend change. Phase B: two DB trigger families — (1) trg_grant_chat_credits_on_coach_client_link (AFTER UPDATE OF client_user_id ON coach_clients, null->not-null) grants +10 to a coach-FUNDED client only (stripe_payment_intent_id NOT NULL; pi_->coach_paid_client, comp_->super_admin_comp_invite; NULL self-pay skipped to avoid double-grant), (2) trg_grant_chat_credits_coach_onboard_insert/_update grant +25 once to any user becoming account_type='coach' (super admins excluded). Source_refs namespaced (cc_link:<id>, coach_onboard:<id>) so the three grant types never collide on chat_credit_grants_source_ref_uniq; self-pay backfill keeps the live {pi}:{instrument} format. Backfills (idempotent, committed, re-run proven no-op): 38 ledger rows / 590 credits / 26 users (14 non-test coaches x25=350, 14 coach-funded client grants=140, 10 historical self-pay=100). Credits are FALLBACK-ONLY so they compose with a future coach subscription pool (the pool would be primary, the 25 one-time tops up on exhaustion). Uniform across chat_message + report_generation (Cole's call). BQ-CREDIT-ENFORCE-VERIFY RESOLVED (Cole smoke-tested cbastian@mybrainwisecoach.com, a backfilled coach). **ITEM 12 — "Lesson Builder", COMPLETE (Option C, child of Content Authoring).** New /super-admin/content-authoring/lessons picker page (src/pages/super-admin/LessonBuilderList.tsx) listing item_type='lesson_blocks' content items (client-side modules-name join), each opening the existing LessonBlocksEditor; sidebar child "Lesson Builder" under Content Authoring (static indented, mirrors the My Clients precedent); the editor returns to the picker via a ?from=lesson-builder param (useSearchParams -> backTarget, both nav sites). Frontend-only, no backend; 4 files (LessonBuilderList.tsx, App.tsx route, LessonBlocksEditor.tsx, AppSidebar.tsx) SHA-verified. **ITEM 8 — Super-admin learning report, COMPLETE (backend + cert cleanup + dashboard).** READ ENGINE (both super-admin-gated, SECDEF): get_learning_report_detail(p_tier,p_target_id,p_target_name,p_user_ids,p_status,p_limit,p_offset) returns granular per-user x per-target rows across all 4 tiers; both axes are just filters (content-first = tier+target, person-first = user_ids, none = everything); p_limit NULL = full export (CSV); the expected population is DERIVED (curriculum membership via curriculum_modules + content_items, UNION direct module assignments, UNION actual completion holders so no real completion is hidden) so not_started surfaces; archived modules/content excluded from expected UNLESS a completion exists. get_learning_report_summary wraps detail into a per-(tier,target) rollup (total/done/in_progress/not_started/revoked/completion_rate; revoked excluded from the rate denominator). NET-NEW cert-tier mutation: set_certification_completion(+_bulk) toggles coach_certifications.status certified<->in_progress, idempotent, audited, manual-override (NO post-cert benefit auto-apply, NO cascade); registered action_type certification_completion_set (FK to super_admin_action_types). Existing set_content_item/module/curriculum_completion_bulk reused (all 8 completion RPCs confirmed super-admin gated). CERT-PATH DATA CLEANUP (so the report keys cert by real path): coach_certifications had NO FK to certification_paths and cc.certification_type was ambiguous (BOTH active ptp_coach paths shared certification_type='ptp_coach', and 2 cert types matched no path); added coach_certifications.certification_path_id FK + index, backfilled from the uca.certification_id->curriculum->certification_path_curricula chain (14 -> published "BrainWise Personal Threat Profile Coach" fa22e4aa, 5 -> the distinct-curriculum draft "PTP-Coach" 57db528d, 1 left NULL for the legacy my_brainwise_coach), DELETED a zero-dependent test ai_transformation_ptp_coach cert, soft-archived the draft duplicate path (only test accounts + Cole's own in_progress were on it; the real active coach Caroline Perry was already on the published path), and patched enroll_user_in_certification_path + self_enroll_in_certification_path to stamp certification_path_id going forward. The report cert tier now keys on certification_path_id -> certification_paths.name, falling back to certification_type for null-path (combined/legacy) certs. NOTE: ai_transformation_ptp_coach is a real (currently dormant) auto-granted combined cert type with no path; the null-path fallback covers it. FRONTEND: src/pages/super-admin/LearningReport.tsx dashboard (per-tier KPI cards + stacked horizontal recharts breakdown + drill-down detail table + full-filtered CSV via src/lib/csvUtils.ts + bulk complete/incomplete across all 4 tiers, constrained to a single tier+target; cert bulk resolves certification_type from a path_id->type map); /super-admin/learning-report route + "Learning Report" (ClipboardList) sidebar entry after Content Authoring; 3 files verified. **Edge fn versions: check-ai-usage v61 (ai-chat observed at v51, unchanged). No cron/Operations/Stripe-webhook changes; ops-stripe-webhook / platform stripe-webhook never touched.** Carryforward (still open): backlog items 4, 5, 6, 7, 11 untouched; ai-chat v50/v51 repo commit (Cole, manual); BQ-PTP-PERFECT-VERIFY; newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder; Doc-1 invoice live refund test; CRM tail; §82 facet_interpretations {public}-role nit; Operations Docs 3/4/5 backlog. *v137 - Session 129 CLOSE. **BrainWise SaaS platform session: super-admin organization management (backlog item 1) built end-to-end and verified, backend + frontend; no Operations work.** No new numbered standing rule (§1-§157 hold; reinforces §156/§157 grant discipline, backend-first + rolled-back-DO-test discipline, and §117 GitHub-API-SHA frontend verification). Backend-first; every apply_migration followed by a separate execute_sql verification; functional tests single rolled-back DO blocks (sentinel RAISE, zero residue; JWT-sim via set_config request.jwt.claims in the same call as the RPC); all 7 shipped frontend files verified by GitHub API SHA. **ITEM 1 COMPLETE: super-admin org management consolidated into ONE CompanyDetail tabbed surface** (Overview / Members / Departments / Invitations / Contract & Features), managing any org cross-org, replacing the impersonation-only path. RECON FINDING: the backend was ~90% already super-admin-capable — most org-admin write RPCs (invitation_create, bulk_invitation_create, department_create/rename/delete, user_assign_supervisor, user_deactivate/reactivate, member_feature_override_set, reconcile_supervisors_for_org, admin_promote_to_org_admin) already carry a gate-read brainwise_super_admin cross-org branch, and users/corporate_invitations/departments/organizations all have a super-admin SELECT-all policy, so reads + most writes needed no change. **BACKEND CHANGES (4, all rolled-back-tested).** (1) NEW public.admin_assign_or_invite_org_admin(p_organization_id, p_email) RETURNS jsonb (SECDEF, search_path public, assert_super_admin, REVOKE PUBLIC/anon + GRANT authenticated/service_role): existing in-org corporate_employee|company_admin -> PERFORM admin_promote_to_org_admin (transfer, demotes prior org_admin) + super_admin_audit_log 'org_admin_assigned' -> {mode:promoted}; existing org_admin -> {already_org_admin}; user in a DIFFERENT org -> 42501; brand-new email (no user) -> {mode:invite_needed} WITHOUT creating the invite (frontend then sends it via invitation_send, single-sourcing the branded email). (2) supervisor_dashboard_set gained a brainwise_super_admin cross-org branch (was org-admin-own-org-only; writes company_admin_audit_log actor_role brainwise_super_admin). (3) invitation_send v22 (id e5d7f6b4-3554-4f4a-a202-7ac3109122b3, verify_jwt false preserved, boot-verified 401 + deployed-source re-verified no drift): removed the hard super_admin_session_id requirement; now a session-LESS super admin routes to invitation_create (its super-admin branch) while a session-bearing super admin still uses admin_invitation_create (impersonation path preserved) — this makes non-impersonation super-admin invites (org-admin AND general member, single AND bulk) send a real branded email. account_type passes through (org_admin supported). (4) NEW public.admin_invitation_revoke(p_invitation_id) RETURNS jsonb (SECDEF, super-admin-only): hard-deletes a pending (redeemed_at IS NULL) corporate invitation + super_admin_audit_log 'invitation_revoked'; guards not-found and already-redeemed (22023). Two super_admin_action_types registered (these are FK targets, NOT a CHECK — the first functional test caught the FK): 'org_admin_invited' (left registered, now unused/harmless) + 'invitation_revoked' (cloned settings from corporate_invitation_created). bulk_invitation_create super-admin branch verified end-to-end (rolled-back test created a row); bulk_invitation_send edge fn needs NO change (no session requirement, wraps bulk_invitation_create). corporate_invitations has SELECT policies only (super-admin SELECT-all) + a redeemed_consistency CHECK (redeemed_at and redeemed_by_user_id set together). **FRONTEND (5 Lovable cycles, all GitHub-API-SHA-verified).** Mirror source = the org-admin people page src/pages/admin/AdminUsers.tsx (which only creates departments + invites + roster actions; it does NOT reassign existing-member departments or change roles, and member_feature_override_set lives on the platform Members drawer, so department-reassign + per-member role/feature-override were intentionally OUT of parity scope). **C2 Members tab** — NEW src/components/super-admin/CompanyMembersSection.tsx (SHA b153331c): Card A org-admin assign/transfer/invite (admin_assign_or_invite_org_admin; invite_needed -> invitation_send with account_type:'org_admin', session bearer + apikey, manual-code Alert when !email_sent), Card B roster from admin_org_users_view + per-row assign-supervisor (user_assign_supervisor, __none__ sentinel -> null) / deactivate (user_deactivate) / reactivate (user_reactivate) + Reconcile (reconcile_supervisors_for_org, reads out_users_patched), Card C supervisor-dashboard Switch (supervisor_dashboard_set p_org/p_enabled). **C3 Invitations tab** — NEW CompanyInvitationsSection.tsx (SHA bda31965): single invite (invitation_send, role Member|company_admin, department Select value=NAME, supervisor, org_level, __none__ sentinels), bulk invite (BulkInviteCard mirrors AdminUsers exactly: XLSX parse, 75-row cap, bulk_invitation_send {organization_id,rows}, results + downloadFailedCsv) scoped to orgId, pending list (corporate_invitations unredeemed incl expired, Expired badge, Revoke confirm -> admin_invitation_revoke). **C4 Departments tab** — NEW CompanyDepartmentsSection.tsx (SHA ee4fb53a): create (department_create), member counts (admin_org_users_view department_id reduce), rename (department_rename, surfaces 23505 duplicate), delete (0 members -> p_action unassign null; N>=1 -> RadioGroup reassign[Select other dept, requires selection, disabled if none]/unassign; department_delete p_action is 'reassign'|'unassign' ONLY). All three rendered as new tabs in src/pages/super-admin/CompanyDetail.tsx (SHA 68aaeb9b; Overview reduced to the scoped banner, old Org-Admin card + Users table removed, assign state/handler/dialog removed, load()/users/audit logging intact, ContractFeaturesSection untouched). **C1 sidebar consolidation** — AppSidebar.tsx superAdminNav (SHA ef9d4892) "Company Accounts" -> "Organizations" + "Create Organization" entry removed (Plus import dropped); CompanyAccounts.tsx h1 -> "Organizations" + a right-aligned "Create Organization" header button -> navigate("/super-admin/create-organization") (route + CreateOrganization page untouched, just no longer a sidebar entry); CompanyDetail back button -> "Back to Organizations". NODE LESSON reinforced: a Radix Select item value must be non-empty (the supervisor/department "None" options use a __none__ sentinel mapped to null, never value=""). **Edge fn versions: invitation_send v22; no other fns changed. No cron/Operations/Stripe-webhook changes; ops-stripe-webhook / platform stripe-webhook never touched.** Carryforward (still open): backlog items 4, 6, 7, 8, 11 untouched; items 2 (+10 AI-chat enforcement, Phase A/B), 5 (white-label full track), 12 (lesson-block editor surfacing, Option B) carry Session-129 decisions and remain unbuilt; ai-chat v50 repo commit (Cole, manual, from Session 126); BQ-PTP-PERFECT-VERIFY; newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder; Doc-1 invoice live refund test; CRM tail; §82 facet_interpretations {public}-role nit; Operations Docs 3/4/5 backlog. *v136 - Session 128 CLOSE. **BrainWise SaaS platform session: three low-hanging-fruit backlog items shipped end-to-end and verified (build-queue items 9, 10, 3) plus a highlight-popover dismissal fix and coach-dashboard additions; then session-tail RECON/READOUTS on items 2, 5, 12 (none shipped); no Operations work.** No new numbered standing rule. **KEY OUTCOME: every shipped item this session was frontend-only, backend recon confirmed zero migrations were needed in all three cases.** All four shipped frontend artifacts verified by GitHub API SHA. NOTE on this closeout: the original Session-128 chat ("Session 128 kickoff: highlight comments feature") edited the local markdown copies but could not produce downloadable files, and GitHub was never updated, so this v136 was rebuilt from the committed v135 plus the recovered Session-128 transcript. **DONE THIS SESSION (shipped + verified):** (1) **Item 9, highlight comments.** Backend verified, NO change: `public.ptp_report_highlights.note` (text NULL) already exists and the existing per-viewer INSERT-own + UPDATE-own RLS already cover writing it (neither policy references `note`; authenticated holds table-level and column-level INSERT/UPDATE on `note`, confirmed live via has_table_privilege + has_column_privilege). Decision: note is optional at create AND editable after (one textarea serves both popovers; delete clears it to null). Frontend (2 files): `useReportHighlights.ts` gained `note` in the interface/select/insert plus a new `updateHighlightNote(id, note)` doing a direct RLS-scoped supabase update; `ReportHighlight.tsx` create popover gained an optional comment textarea (one-click no-comment highlight preserved by committing on color-pick with `note: createNote.trim() || null`), the former Remove popover became an edit popover (note textarea prefilled + Save→updateHighlightNote / Cancel / Remove), commented marks render `borderBottom 2px dotted currentColor` + the note as the mark `title` (hover tooltip); note survives heal-on-mismatch via the existing `{...h}` spread; the whole UI stays gated by `ctx.enabled`/`allowHighlighting` (OFF on the share / org-peer / super-admin read surfaces). (2) **Highlight-popover dismissal.** `ReportHighlight.tsx` gained a `useEffect` (placed BEFORE the early return, per rules-of-hooks) that, only while a popover is open, registers a capture-phase document `pointerdown` plus a `keydown` listener: a pointerdown whose target is outside both popover refs (popRef/editRef), or Escape, clears pop/editPop and the in-progress comment. (3) **Item 10, My Learning** pulled out of Resources into its own standalone page/tab. (4) **Item 3, Individual Dashboard** built as option (a): cards/widgets that launch the assessment-results pages and resources (not removed). (5) Coach dashboard additions. **RECON / READOUTS THIS SESSION (NOT shipped, the session toolset had no Edge Function read/deploy access):** **Item 2 (free +10 AI chats)**, found the +10 grant was scaffolded but never wired into enforcement (`check-ai-usage` ignores `one_time_chat_credits`), so the +10 is dead for everyone and all 17 coaches get NO AI chat; the DB grant fn is idempotent/correct, the gap is edge-function-bound. Cole's Session-129 decision: SPLIT, Phase A fixes enforcement for regular per-assessment purchasers FIRST, Phase B extends to coach-paid + super-admin-invited NEXT (see item 2 above). **Item 5 (white-label)**, full greenfield audit: no branding columns anywhere, uneven tenancy (ops/CRM org-partitioned, core public schema not), no domain routing, global theming; net-new multi-session capability. Cole's Session-129 decision: build ALL of it (full track, see item 5 above). **Item 12 (lesson-block editor)**, editor is at `/super-admin/content-authoring/lessons/:contentItemId`, reachable only via a saved lesson_blocks content item; "surfacing" = a direct entry point. Cole's Session-129 decision: Option B (the transcript ended before the A/B/C text was captured, restate the options in the opener; see item 12 above). **Carryforward (still open):** backlog items 1, 4, 6, 7, 8, 11 untouched; items 2, 5, 12 carry Session-129 decisions; super-admin org management (item 1) is the Session-129 START; ai-chat v50 repo commit (Cole, manual); BQ-PTP-PERFECT-VERIFY (live guardrail test via authenticated-app JWT); newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder; Doc-1 invoice live refund test; CRM Phase 9/10 tail; the §82 facet_interpretations {public}-role nit; Operations Docs 3/4/5 backlog. *v135 - Session 127 CLOSE. **BrainWise SaaS platform session: Item 2a (PTP report persistent highlighting) shipped end-to-end and verified (backend + frontend), assessment slider/navigation fixes shipped, a security-scan remediation + triage pass completed, and a coach-curriculum completion report pulled; no Operations work.** No new numbered standing rule. Backend-first; apply_migration + a separate execute_sql verify; the RLS functional test a single rolled-back DO block (zero residue); frontend verified by GitHub API SHA. Full architectural detail in architecture-reference v129; summary here. **DONE THIS SESSION:** (1) Item 2a highlighting: public.ptp_report_highlights table + 5 RLS policies (per-viewer-private; the INSERT policy's cross-table EXISTS rides assessment_results RLS so a highlight can only be created against a PTP result the caller can already see); teal/amber/purple palette (teal default, green excluded); 3 new frontend files (reportHighlightColors.ts, useReportHighlights.ts, ReportHighlight.tsx) wired into every PTP report block in PTPNarrativeSections.tsx; gated ON for owner + coach client view, OFF on the three read-only share/super-admin surfaces via a new allowHighlighting prop; the Assessment Responses question-text row converted from a native button to a role="button" div so text selection works. Commits: migration ptp_highlights_01_table_rls; 2db591c, 3b6b139, 27669e0, fec4702. (2) Assessment slider no-prefill + "Next Unanswered" cleanup mode in AssessmentFlow.tsx (commit 1bb595b). (3) Security pass: FIXED + live-verified email HTML escaping (escHtml.ts), retry-ptp-narratives raw-error leak (generic error + log), function search_path pins (3 fns), newsletter author_user_id anon exposure (REVOKE table SELECT FROM anon; the column-level revoke was a no-op); the rest of the scan triaged as false positives (trainee quiz/content writes blocked by RLS, the 3 XSS paths already mitigated by isSafeHttpUrl/DOMPurify/validate, email_logs/cohorts/highlights-insert) or accepted-by-design (Stripe price IDs public, chat_credit_grants service-role). LEARNING: the scanner is heuristic and two of Lovable's self-verifications were wrong, so trust live pg checks; mark resolved/false-positive items Ignored with a rationale and never click "Try to fix all." (4) Ad-hoc: Coach "Foundations of the BrainWise PTP" curriculum completion report (16 required modules, 10 coaches) delivered inline; not a code change.

**NEW BACKLOG, dictated by Cole this session (the active near-term queue; item 1 is the Session-128 START priority):**

1. [SESSION-128 START] **Super-admin organization management.** Orgs can already be created (the contract), but today only an org_admin can invite and manage people. Build the equivalent of the org-admin page/functionality available to a super admin, so BrainWise can support organizations through onboarding (invite members, manage members and roles, and the rest of the org-admin surface). Backend-first: confirm the live org-admin RPCs/RLS and whether a super-admin can already call them cross-org or needs a super-admin-scoped path.

2. **[DONE Session 130 — Phases A+B, backend, live-verified]** **Extend the free 10 AI chats beyond per-assessment purchasers.** The +10 free AI-chat grant currently given to per-assessment purchasers must ALSO be granted to (a) coach-paid clients and (b) anyone a super admin invites to take the PTP for free. Enforcement of the +10 grant lives in stripe-webhook and the display mirror lives in plan_tiers (the display-vs-enforcement split in architecture-reference), so verify BOTH the coach-order / $0-comp path and the super-admin-invite path actually receive the grant, and decide where the grant fires for the non-Stripe invite path (no checkout event). **[SESSION-128 RECON FINDING, not shipped]** The grant was scaffolded but NEVER wired into enforcement: `check-ai-usage` does not honor `one_time_chat_credits`, so the +10 is currently dead for everyone and all 17 coaches get NO AI chat at all. The DB grant function itself is idempotent and correct; the missing work is edge-function-bound (`check-ai-usage` + the purchase/comp/invite webhooks), which that session's toolset could not read or deploy. **[COLE DECISION, SESSION-129, split into two phases]** Phase A (do FIRST, this is the real fix): wire `check-ai-usage` to honor `one_time_chat_credits` and fire the grant on the per-assessment purchase path so REGULAR purchasing users actually receive their +10. Phase B (NEXT, after A is verified): extend the grant to coach-paid clients and super-admin-invited clients, resolving the non-Stripe invite-path trigger and the `source_ref` idempotency key per path. Open lock-first question for Phase B: does +10 stack on top of the monthly tier allowance, or is it the sole allowance for users who would otherwise be denied (comped clients, coaches)?

3. **[DONE Session 128, built option (a), frontend-only, SHA-verified]** **Individual user Dashboard page.** The dashboard route was live but rendered nothing. RESOLVED by populating it with cards/widgets that launch the assessment-results pages and resources (option a; not removed).

4. **In-system support chatbot.** A help chatbot that walks a user through diagnosing technical problems (accessing something, taking an assessment, finding something), assists to the degree it can, and reports to the admin side what it worked on and any bugs it identifies. Needs an admin-facing capture/report surface for those sessions and identified bugs. **[SESSION-133 DESIGN NOTE, deferred for later, not started]** Cost-conscious design agreed: separate widget from the coaching ai-chat; runs on Haiku 4.5 (not Sonnet/Opus); answers from a single static curated "how the system works" help doc held in the system prompt and prompt-CACHED (the doc is human-authored static reference text, identical every call; caching is a billing optimization, NOT generation, and the model never emits a help doc, only the user-facing answer); a deflection/canned-FAQ layer in front of the model; short output cap + trimmed history + per-user server-side rate limit; bypasses the credit ledger (support should not consume report/chat credits). Admin capture surface = transcript log + model-tagged issue category + recurring-issue flag, surfaced in a super-admin report view (reuses the Session-130 learning-report / Session-129 org-management read-RPC + dashboard pattern). Est. cost on Haiku+caching: well under 1c per single question, ~2-3c per multi-turn help conversation; the dominant cost lever is model choice (Opus would be ~7x). **BLOCKER / FIRST TASK is content, not code:** there is NO FAQ or help content anywhere in the system today, so the help doc must be created before the bot has anything authoritative to answer from. **COLE REQUIREMENT (Session 133):** wants an AUTONOMOUS way to build the help docs by driving the live system and capturing screenshots (e.g. an agent that walks the real UI flows and emits help content + screenshots), rather than hand-authoring them; design this generation pipeline as part of the item-4 scope. The generated corpus doubles as a static help-center / FAQ page independent of the bot.

5. **White-label readiness.** Assess whether the product can be white-labeled now from a multitenant perspective (per-org branding, domains, isolation), and what is missing if not. **[SESSION-128 READOUT, investigation complete]** There is NO white-label layer today. (a) No branding data: `organizations` holds only operational columns; a schema-wide scan found ZERO logo/color/theme/favicon/custom-CSS/subdomain/custom-domain columns anywhere. (b) Tenancy is uneven: the operations/CRM schema is thoroughly org-partitioned (~100 tables), but the core customer-facing public schema (users, assessments, learning) is largely NOT org_id-partitioned; org affiliation runs through `organizations.admin_user_id` + seat/membership relationships, not a uniform tenant key, so there is no "which org's brand applies to this session" resolver. (c) No custom-domain or subdomain routing (single domain today). (d) Theming is global (brand tokens baked in as CSS variables, not org-driven). Verdict: net-new capability, not a config toggle. **[COLE DECISION, SESSION-129, full scope, "all of it"]** Build the complete white-label track, NOT just the branding-layer first slice. Sequence within the track: (1) branding layer on `organizations` (logo asset, primary/accent colors, optional custom CSS) + session-time CSS-variable injection keyed to the resolved org; (2) a per-session org-brand resolver; (3) tenant-isolation hardening where the public schema is not org-partitioned; (4) custom-domain / subdomain support (domain columns + domain→org resolver + DNS/cert handling at the hosting layer). This is a multi-session track; scope it as its own arc, not a tail item. **[SESSION-133, sub-step (1)/5.1 BACKEND SHIPPED + VERIFIED]** Re-verified the Session-128 readout live (organizations still branding-free; the only schema "branding-ish" hits are false positives). CORRECTION to the readout: `users.organization_id` DOES exist and is populated for corporate members (59/116 users carry an org; 57 null = individuals/coaches), so the brand resolver is a one-column lookup, NOT a membership-graph traversal, and branding is genuinely independent of 5.3 isolation. Migration `whitelabel_5_1_org_branding_layer`: added `organizations.brand_primary_color` + `brand_accent_color` (each nullable, named hex CHECK `^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$`) + `brand_logo_path` text; created public `org-branding` storage bucket (2MB, png/jpeg/webp/svg+xml) with 3 policies (public SELECT, super-admin write via `current_user_account_type()='brainwise_super_admin'`, service_role ALL); registered `org_branding_set` super_admin_action_type (§99, category org_management, requires_justification); RPC `get_org_branding_for_current_user()` (STABLE SECDEF, returns resolved org brand or `{is_default:true}` for null-org/unset) and `admin_set_org_branding(p_organization_id,p_primary_color,p_accent_color,p_logo_path,p_reason)` (SECDEF, assert_super_admin + reason>=10 + hex guard, nulls clear to default, audit-logged via log_super_admin_action mode 'direct'). Both RPCs REVOKE PUBLIC/anon + GRANT authenticated/service_role (§156/§157). Rolled-back DO-block functional test passed all axes (set / resolve-as-member / clear-to-default / bad-hex raise / short-reason raise); zero residue. Custom CSS DEFERRED from v1 (Cole-approved Session 133, XSS/defacement vector). 5.2 frontend recon done: theme uses TWO token sets in `src/index.css` (raw hex `--bw-*` AND shadcn HSL `--primary`/`--accent`/`--ring`/`--sidebar-*`); components consume the HSL ones via Tailwind, so the injector must convert org hex to `H S% L%` and override the HSL vars on `:root`. Provider tree: QueryClientProvider > TooltipProvider > BrowserRouter > AuthProvider > ImpersonationProvider > Routes; auth via `@/hooks/useAuth` ({session,user,loading,signOut}). **5.2 EDITOR (Lovable prompt B) SHIPPED + SHA-VERIFIED:** new `src/components/super-admin/CompanyBrandingSection.tsx` (SHA 911409db; loads org brand, two color toggles+pickers+hex validation, logo upload to org-branding bucket at `org/<orgId>/logo-<ts>.<ext>`, reason>=10, calls admin_set_org_branding, Save + Reset-to-default; casts on the stale-types select + RPC) wired as a Branding tab on `CompanyDetail.tsx` (SHA 23dd60fa, 3 additive edits, ContractFeaturesSection untouched). NEXT: 5.2 INJECTOR (Lovable prompt A) = session-time CSS-var painting from get_org_branding_for_current_user. **SHIPPED + SHA-VERIFIED:** new `src/components/OrgBrandingInjector.tsx` (SHA 28b74cc8; headless, on auth-resolve calls the resolver, converts org hex -> shadcn `H S% L%`, sets `--primary`/`--primary-foreground` from primary and `--accent`/`--accent-foreground`/`--ring`/`--sidebar-primary`/`--sidebar-primary-foreground`/`--sidebar-ring` from accent, WCAG-luminance foreground pick, clears on no-user/error/is_default/unmount) mounted as first child of `<ImpersonationProvider>` in `src/App.tsx` (SHA 72ddd220, 2 additive edits). **5.1 + 5.2 = white-label branding (colors) COMPLETE end-to-end.** REMAINING in item 5: A2 = logo/header render **SHIPPED + SHA-VERIFIED** (`src/components/AppLayout.tsx` SHA 581f807a: header now reads `hsl(var(--primary))` bg + `hsl(var(--primary-foreground))` text/border instead of the fixed `--bw-navy` hex, and does its own resolver fetch to swap in the org logo, or the org name when no logo, falling back to brain-icon + "BrainWise Enterprises" wordmark for default/individual users; coupon banner/main/sidebar untouched). Minor open polish: NotificationBell color is its own (may render dark on a light org primary) - non-blocking. 5.3 = tenant-isolation hardening of the non-partitioned public schema (the big/risky sub-step, needs a design-doc-before-migration pass; foundation for items 6/7 seat/attempt attribution); 5.4 = custom-domain/subdomain (last). **[SESSION-133 RECON, 5.3 + 5.4, no build]** 5.3: RLS is ENABLED on every public base table (no unprotected surface; only chat_credit_grants has 0 policies = intentional default-deny/service-role-only). Core per-user customer tables (assessments, assessment_results, content_item_completions, module_completions, lesson_block_progress, quiz_attempts, written_submissions, user_*_assignments, development_plan_*) key on user_id (owner reads own); cross-user access is RELATIONSHIP-based. CRITICAL CORRECTION to the Session-128 "no uniform tenant key" claim: `users.organization_id` IS the tenant key and RLS already uses it. Verified on `assessments`: the corporate-admin SELECT policy is bounded `current_user_account_type() IN (company_admin,org_admin) AND user_id IN (SELECT id FROM users WHERE organization_id = current_user_org_id())`; `current_user_org_id()` = `SELECT organization_id FROM users WHERE id=auth.uid()` (STABLE SECDEF). So 5.3 is NOT a retrofit-org_id-everywhere project. It narrows to: (a) AUDIT every member-reading / org-dashboard / org-report policy + view + RPC to confirm uniform `current_user_org_id()` bounding (find any path that reads members NOT org-bounded = cross-tenant leak); (b) the one real structural gap: core rows carry no own org_id, so org is derived live from users.organization_id, which is a MOVING pointer (a user changing orgs re-attributes their history) - fine for access control, but matters for items 6/7 seat/attempt ATTRIBUTION, so decide derive-live vs stamp-org_id-at-attempt-time there. 5.4: confirmed zero domain/subdomain columns anywhere; DB side is trivial (add org domain/subdomain cols or domains table + domain->org resolver, mirrors branding resolver). Real work is the HOSTING layer (Lovable: can it route per-org subdomains and/or customer custom domains to one app with auto-TLS? - platform-capability question, not answerable from DB). Design difference vs 5.1/5.2: branding resolves from the AUTH'd user; a custom domain implies the brand PRE-LOGIN (login page on acme.brainwise.com must already be Acme-branded), so 5.4 needs a HOSTNAME-based resolver readable by anonymous visitors (a public domain->brand read path), a new resolver shape, not a reuse of get_org_branding_for_current_user. NEXT: 5.3 = write the design doc (targeted RLS/view/RPC member-read audit + attribution decision) before any migration; 5.4 gated on a Lovable custom-domain capability check. **[SESSION-133 5.3 DESIGN DOC WRITTEN: `whitelabel-5_3-tenant-isolation-design.md`]** Swept every org-admin RLS policy schema-wide: the PII surface (assessments, assessment_results [further gated via permissions.viewer_organization_id], users, ai_usage_counters, member_feature_overrides, company_admin_audit_log, corporate_*, org_* narratives/interventions/history, executive_perspective_assignments) is uniformly bounded by current_user_org_id() = CLEAN. ONE real gap found: `teams` + `team_members` WRITE policies gate on admin role WITHOUT org bounding (any corporate/org admin could insert/update/delete another org's teams; low blast radius, no PII, but a genuine cross-tenant write hole) - fix = add org bound preserving the manager_user_id/super-admin branches. 5.3 work plan: (1) DONE Session-133: migration `whitelabel_5_3_bound_teams_team_members_to_org` ALTERed the teams ALL policy + the 3 team_members write policies (insert/update/delete) to bound the company_admin/org_admin branch by current_user_org_id() (super-admin cross-org + team-manager-own-teams branches preserved); rolled-back RLS functional test as a real Test Corp company_admin passed every axis (cross-org teams update=0 rows, cross-org delete=0, cross-org insert DENIED by WITH CHECK, own-org update=1, own-org insert OK, team_members own-team visible / cross-team not). (2) DONE Session-133 (read-only audit, no schema changes): VIEWS - all 4 member-exposing views (admin_org_users_view, coach_clients_client_view, org_users_public, organization_features_view) are security_invoker=true so they inherit the querier's RLS (no definer-bypass leak). ORG-PARAM SECDEF FUNCTIONS - every member-data reader/mutation that takes an org param guards inline with admin-role + (super-admin OR v_caller_org = p_organization_id); verified bodies of get_nai_epn_delta, get_ptp_leader_workforce_delta (both also enforce a 5-participant suppression floor), assign_executive_perspective_assessment (also rejects assignees not in the target org), org_set_mfa_required, reconcile_supervisors_for_org. NOTE: these did NOT call current_user_org_id() (they inline the v_caller_org=p_organization_id compare), which is why a helper-name grep alone would miss them - read the bodies. INSERT WITH CHECK - core member-writable tables tie inserts to the owner (assessments user_id=auth.uid(); assessment_responses joins through an owned in_progress assessment; chat_sessions; resource_access_log), and the other member tables (assessment_results, content_item_completions, module_completions, lesson_block_progress, quiz_attempts, written_submissions, development_plan_*, user_*_assignments) have NO authenticated INSERT policy (writes via SECDEF RPC/service-role) = tighter. VERDICT: CLEAN. Low-priority tidy-ups (NOT leaks, optional): confirm EXECUTE is revoked from authenticated/anon on internal helpers log_super_admin_action (audit writer), seat_count_used/seat_count_available + org_has_feature (would expose only an org seat-count/feature-boolean if callable with arbitrary org_id - minor); the ops_* webhook/service helpers belong to the separate operations.* externalization arc, not the public-schema 5.3 surface. **5.3 NOW CLOSEABLE: step-1 fix applied+verified, step-2 audit clean, step-3 (org_id attribution-stamping) deferred to items 6/7 by design.** (3) DEFER org_id attribution-stamping to items 6/7 (stamp org_id on launch/attempt records at write time for stable attribution; do NOT mass-retrofit/RLS-partition learning tables - access control already works via current_user_org_id). NET: 5.3 is small (one policy fix + one mechanical audit + one decision that lands in 6/7), NOT a multi-session migration arc - the Session-128 "retrofit everything" framing is wrong. **[SESSION-133 5.4 LOVABLE HOSTING ANSWER]** Multiple custom domains per project YES (paid plan, manual per-domain dashboard entry + DNS A `185.158.133.1`/CNAME + `_lovable` TXT, auto-TLS, NO bulk/API); wildcard subdomains NO (each `tenant.host` = separate manual entry+cert); customer vanity domains via CNAME YES (manual per-tenant); request hostname preserved so client-side `window.location.hostname` tenant resolution is viable (no server-side per-host routing/env, same SPA on all hosts); SPA deep-link history-rewrite built-in (BrowserRouter, already used). IMPLICATION: per-org AUTO-subdomains NOT feasible on Lovable hosting alone (no wildcard); vanity domains feasible but manual (tractable at tens, painful at hundreds); 5.4 resolver must be HOSTNAME-based + PRE-AUTH (anonymous public RPC keyed on hostname - new shape vs get_org_branding_for_current_user). For wildcard/programmatic at scale: front with Cloudflare-for-SaaS / CloudFront+ACM terminating TLS. 5.4 buildable for vanity-domain tenants now; scale-automation deferred. **[SESSION-133 5.4 BACKEND SHIPPED + VERIFIED]** Migration `whitelabel_5_4_org_custom_domains`: new table `public.org_custom_domains` (organization_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE, hostname, kind subdomain/vanity, is_verified, is_primary, created_by, timestamps; unique index on lower(hostname); index on organization_id). RLS: service_role ALL; super-admin manage (ALL); org-admins read own (SELECT, current_user_org_id bound). REVOKE ALL FROM anon on the table; GRANT select/insert/update/delete to authenticated (RLS-gated). Public PRE-AUTH resolver RPC `get_org_branding_for_hostname(p_hostname)` (SQL, STABLE, SECDEF, search_path public): normalizes hostname (lower + strip port via split_part ':'), matches a VERIFIED domain, returns the same brand shape as get_org_branding_for_current_user (is_default/organization_id/organization_name/brand_primary_color/brand_accent_color/brand_logo_path); REVOKE FROM PUBLIC then GRANT EXECUTE to anon+authenticated+service_role (anon REQUIRED so a vanity domain shows brand pre-login). Super-admin RPCs `admin_set_org_custom_domain(p_organization_id,p_hostname,p_kind,p_is_verified,p_is_primary,p_reason)` (upsert ON CONFLICT lower(hostname); single-primary-per-org enforced in-RPC; assert_super_admin + reason>=10; audit via log_super_admin_action mode 'direct') and `admin_remove_org_custom_domain(p_hostname,p_reason)` (assert_super_admin + reason>=10 + audit); both REVOKE PUBLIC,anon + GRANT authenticated,service_role. Two new super_admin_action_types registered: org_custom_domain_set, org_custom_domain_removed (category org_management, requires_justification). Rolled-back functional test passed all axes: super-admin set (verified+primary, org A); anon resolve of 'Acme.Example.Test:443' -> is_default=false org A (proves case+port normalization); unknown host + unverified host -> is_default=true; org-A admin sees own 2 domains / 0 of org B (RLS isolation); remove returns row; re-resolve removed host -> is_default=true. FRONTEND (Lovable, handoff prompts written, Cole runs): (P1) OrgBrandingInjector reads window.location.hostname; if it is not the primary app host, call get_org_branding_for_hostname pre-auth and apply brand (anonymous, before login); (P2) super-admin Domains tab in CompanyDetail to add/verify/remove org_custom_domains via the admin RPCs. NOT YET RUN. **[SESSION-133 NEW WORKSTREAM: MODULARIZE + SELL - scope doc `module-entitlement-scope.md`]** Cole wants to sell platform MODULES (Assessments, LMS, CRM, Operations) bundled OR a la carte (LMS without assessments and vice versa, etc.). Entitlement is ORTHOGONAL to 5.3 isolation - does NOT change 5.3. Existing entitlement machinery (grounded): subscription_tiers defaults + corporate_contracts per-org overrides resolved via organization_features_view (COALESCE override/tier) covering instruments/AI/dashboard/supervisor-dashboard/seats; platform_features = GLOBAL kill-switch (only assessment instruments registered); member_feature_overrides per-member; frontend SubscriptionGate + RoleGuard. GAP: no module concept for LMS/CRM/Operations. Assessments already entitlement-ready (instrument granularity). LMS = needs a flag + gating + a catalog-ownership decision (resell BrainWise catalog [easy] vs per-org authored/imported catalog [big, ties to SCORM import item 6]). CRM+Operations = super-admin-only internal back-office in operations.* (org-partitioned but single-tenant-built, no operations.users membership, UI super_admin-gated) -> selling externally = LARGE arc (operations.* multi-tenant membership/roles + UI de-gate + full operations.* isolation audit + entitlement), and it ADDS operations.* to the tenant-isolation surface 5.3 never covered. Enforcement must be 2-layer (frontend gate + backend RLS/RPC deny). See scope doc for the phased plan.Known v1 behaviors to confirm in testing: org colors also tint marketing pages while an org member is logged in; impersonation paints whatever identity the resolver resolves to.

The ptp_report_highlights table already has a note text NULL column reserved for this; reuse it. *v134 - Session 126 CLOSE. **BrainWise SaaS platform session: Item 2c (share PTP results by email) built end-to-end and verified, backend + frontend; no Operations work. Item 2a (PTP report persistent highlighting) deferred to Session 127.** Backend-first; every apply_migration followed by a separate execute_sql verification; functional tests single rolled-back DO blocks (sentinel RAISE, zero residue); all frontend verified by GitHub API SHA. No new standing rule (existing set holds; this session reinforces §156/§157 grant discipline and §92 repo↔runtime sync). **DESIGN CORRECTION (supersedes v133/v132):** those entries described 2c as a "backend tokenized link." That was wrong. 2c shipped as **account-to-account directed sharing**, NOT a tokenized public link. **DESIGN (Cole-approved):** a logged-in owner types another account-holder's email; the system reports whether an account exists (yes → share granted; no → "no account exists with that email"); a durable grant is created; the recipient gets an in-app NOTIFICATION (not a link email); the recipient sees a new "Shared With Me" sidebar surface (distinct from the existing org-peer /shared-results), opens the sharer's FULL PTP report, and can pull both the shared results and their own into the AI chat for a combined dialogue. Cross-organization (any account-holder can share with any other), unlike org-bounded peer sharing. Locked decisions: NEW dedicated grant table (Option B, not a reuse of peer_access_requests); shares ALL of the owner's PTP across all four report tables; one-directional owner-initiated; durable until revoked (soft-revoke, row retained as audit trail); AI-chat combine IN v1. Email existence check returns yes/no, judged defensible for SOC 2: the enumeration vector is weak because it is authenticated, attributable, and membership-only, and is backed by a server-side rate-limit + a share/revoke audit log. **BACKEND COMPLETE + VERIFIED (2 migrations on svprhtzawnbzmumxnhsq, every apply_migration + a separate execute_sql verify).** ptp_share_01_table_helper_rls_disjuncts: public.ptp_result_shares (id, owner_user_id FK users CASCADE, viewer_user_id FK users CASCADE, created_at, revoked_at NULL; CHECK owner<>viewer; partial UNIQUE uq_prs_active on (owner_user_id, viewer_user_id) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL; idx_prs_viewer_active; idx_prs_owner). RLS: owner FOR ALL TO authenticated (owner_user_id=auth.uid()), viewer FOR SELECT TO authenticated (viewer_user_id=auth.uid()), service_role FOR ALL. Helper public.direct_ptp_share_visible(p_owner_user_id, p_viewer_user_id) RETURNS boolean SQL STABLE SECDEF search_path='' (EXISTS an active share AND both users not deactivated; RLS-facing → REVOKE PUBLIC/anon, GRANT authenticated+service_role per §156). Four additive SELECT disjuncts ("...: PTP directly shared") on assessment_results (owner_user_id=user_id), assessments (user_id), assessment_responses (via assessment_id→assessments.user_id), facet_interpretations (via assessment_result_id→assessment_results.user_id) — all INST-001-scoped, user_id<>auth.uid(), routed through the helper so access is enforced by RLS not by trust. Functional test (rolled-back DO, JWT-sim via set_config request.jwt.claims + SET LOCAL ROLE authenticated to force RLS): before ar=0 / after ar=2 asmt=2 resp=158 fi=32 / after revoke ar=0; zero residue. ptp_share_02_rpcs_attempts_notification: public.ptp_share_attempts (internal rate-limit/enumeration log; idx on (actor, time); RLS service_role-only + REVOKE ALL FROM authenticated,anon per §157). notification_types_catalog row 'ptp_results_shared' (importance important, in_app, user_configurable true, category 'sharing', is_v1_active true; ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). notification_display CREATE OR REPLACE adding the 'ptp_results_shared' case (preserves all prior cases verbatim; title "Someone shared their PTP results with you", url COALESCE(action_url, /shared-with-me)). Four SECDEF plpgsql RPCs (search_path '', REVOKE PUBLIC/anon, GRANT authenticated+service_role): share_ptp_results(p_target_email text)→jsonb (auth check; rate-limit ≥10/min raises 'rate_limited' SQLSTATE 53400; logs an attempt; resolves the lower/trim active email; returns {found:false} | {found:true,status:self} | {found:true,status:already_shared,viewer_name} | else inserts the share + notify_user with dedup_key 'ptp_results_shared:'||share_id and returns {found:true,status:shared,viewer_name}); revoke_ptp_share(p_viewer_user_id uuid)→{revoked:bool} (soft-set revoked_at, GET DIAGNOSTICS rowcount); list_my_ptp_shares()→{shares:[{share_id,viewer_user_id,viewer_name,viewer_email,created_at}]}; list_ptp_shared_with_me()→{shared_with_me:[{share_id,owner_user_id,owner_name,shared_at}]}. Functional test (rolled-back DO, JWT-sim sub=owner): share→shared + share rows=1 + notif=1 + attempts=1 / re-share→already_shared / bogus email→found:false / self→status:self / revoke→revoked:true + rows=0 / lists empty after revoke / rate_limited=true at the 11th call; zero residue. NOTIFY pgrst run. **AI-CHAT EDGE FUNCTION (combine) — DEPLOYED + VERIFIED.** Reconciled the deployed v49 against the repo (byte-equal, no drift, verify_jwt=false). Found the deployed function read only assessment_result_ids hard-filtered to user_id=caller and IGNORED peer_result_ids entirely (so no pre-existing security hole, but the combine required a backend change). Deployed v50 (ACTIVE, verify_jwt preserved FALSE): the own-results path is unchanged (service role + user_id filter); a NEW shared-results path fetches peer_result_ids via the caller's RLS-scoped userClient (access enforced by the new disjuncts — arbitrary ids return nothing), filters out the caller's own ids, labels each block "Shared by {name}", adds a framing note, and caps total context at MAX_CONTEXT_RESULTS=12. Verified the deployed source contains the new logic. **MANUAL FOLLOW-UP for Cole: commit the deployed ai-chat v50 source (functions/ai-chat/index.ts) into cbastianBWE/brainwise-blueprint — GitHub MCP is read-only, so the repo is one commit behind the runtime until Cole pushes it (§92).** **FRONTEND COMPLETE (3 Lovable cycles, all SHA-verified).** C1 (commit 4314726): src/pages/PrivacySettings.tsx gained a "Share My PTP Results" card (gated on a hasPtp check against assessment_results INST-001) with an email input + Share button + a revoke list, calling share_ptp_results / list_my_ptp_shares / revoke_ptp_share; types.ts auto-regenerated; placed between the coach Data Sharing card and the Corporate Peer Sharing card. C2 (commit c17099e): new src/pages/SharedWithMe.tsx (two-pane — left list from list_ptp_shared_with_me, right renders MyResults for the selected sharer); App.tsx route /shared-with-me (no SubscriptionGate) + import; AppSidebar "Shared With Me" (Inbox icon) added after "My Development Plan" in all FIVE nav arrays. C3 (commit 3dd14f0): SharedWithMe.tsx gained a "Discuss with AI" button (navigates to /ai-chat?peers=<ownerId>&self=true) in a header bar above the report; AiChat.tsx gained a sharedWithMeMap (loads list_ptp_shared_with_me) so a directly-shared owner renders by name in the selector (owner_label = peerMap || sharedWithMeMap || "Peer"), with sharedWithMeMap added to that effect's deps. **KEY FINDING (answers Cole's mid-session concern about isCoachView on a non-coach relationship):** verified by reading MyResults.tsx + PTPNarrativeSections.tsx in full — isCoachView functions as a "third-party read-only viewer" flag, NOT a coach-relationship gate. Its only two effects on the PTP report are (a) the isCoachLimited gate, which requires permissionLevel='score_summary' to fire; the shared view passes full_results (or undefined), so it never fires and the full report renders, and (b) it hides the "Add to my Development Plan" button, which is correct because a viewer should not add the owner's actions to their own plan. The coach_clients filter never runs (no coachUserId is passed) and the NAI coach toggle is unreachable (a share viewer can only RLS-read the owner's PTP). No coach-specific content is injected. This is consistent with the existing org-peer SharedResults page, which already passes isCoachView for non-coach peers. permissionLevel='full_results' and defaultInstrumentId='INST-001' were not restored by Lovable on the shared MyResults call but are confirmed INERT (only PTP is RLS-readable so the instrument selector lands on PTP regardless; permissionLevel only matters through the score_summary gate). Optional future cleanup logged: rename isCoachView to a sharedView/third-party-viewer prop (no functional gain; declined for 2c). **STATUS:** Item 2c DONE. Item 2a (PTP report persistent highlighting) DEFERRED to Session 127 — net-new, no existing highlight infrastructure; central risk is durable anchoring of AI-narrative text across React-rendered sections; open with an anchoring spike. A live end-to-end run of the 2c AI-chat combine is deferred to the authenticated app (needs a super-admin JWT that cannot be minted from MCP — same constraint as BQ-PTP-PERFECT-VERIFY). **Edge fn versions: ai-chat v50; no other fns changed. No cron/Operations changes; ptp_result_shares is public-schema; ops-stripe-webhook / platform stripe-webhook never touched.** Carryforward (still open): Item 2a above; the ai-chat v50 repo commit (Cole, manual); BQ-PTP-PERFECT-VERIFY (live guardrail test via authenticated-app JWT); newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder (no sitemap impact this session — every 2c surface is auth-gated); Doc-1 invoice live refund test; Phase-10 owner/team RLS hardening; CRM Phase 9 (QA + Zoho import) / Phase 10; a logged §82 nit (facet_interpretations "super admin can read all" SELECT policy has roles {public} instead of {authenticated}; qual is account-type-scoped so not a blanket leak — NOT fixed, for a future §82 pass); the longer Operations Docs 3/4/5 backlog. Close artifacts (markdown only, Session-74): this v134 entry, architecture-reference v128, session-126-to-127.md.*

*v133 - Session 125 CLOSE. **BrainWise SaaS platform session: the "My Development Plan" feature (build-queue Item 2b) built end-to-end and verified, backend + frontend; no Operations work.** Backend-first; every apply_migration followed by a separate execute_sql verification; the functional test a single rolled-back DO block (sentinel RAISE, zero residue, in-app-only so no external side effects); all frontend verified by GitHub API SHA. No new standing rule (the existing set holds; this session reinforces §156 grant discipline and the "full-file replacement is the most reliable Lovable shape" practice). **DOC CORRECTION (supersedes v132):** v132 described Item 2b as an "Action-Plan to Development Plan rename." That was wrong. 2b is a NET-NEW persistent "My Development Plan" feature. The PTP report's Action Plan section (the 3 AI cards in src/components/results/PTPNarrativeSections.tsx) is UNCHANGED except for an added "Add to my Development Plan" button; no section was renamed. **DESIGN (Cole-approved):** flexible single-item model (PTP-sourced or custom), NO subscription gate (all users), soft-archive (recoverable), coach auto-loses access when the coach_clients relationship ends, two-way comments, in-app notifications only for v1 (email deferred by default but works generically if a user opts a type up to email; see 6b). **BACKEND COMPLETE + VERIFIED (5 migrations on svprhtzawnbzmumxnhsq, every apply_migration + a separate execute_sql verify).** dev_plan_01_tables_rls_helpers: 4 public-schema tables. development_plan_items (user_id FK users CASCADE, source 'ptp'|'custom', source_result_id FK assessment_results SET NULL, source_context 'professional|personal|combined', card_title, dimension_tags text[], action_text, status not_started|in_progress|done|paused, target_date, progress_pct 0-100, sort_order, archived_at; partial UNIQUE uq_dpi_ptp_dedup on (user_id, source_result_id, md5(action_text)) WHERE source='ptp' AND archived_at IS NULL). development_plan_entries (dated progress log; item_id CASCADE, note, progress_pct, metric_value/label). development_plan_comments (item_id CASCADE, plan_owner_user_id, author_user_id, author_role client|coach, body, edited_at, deleted_at). development_plan_coach_shares (client_user_id, coach_user_id, granted_at, revoked_at; partial UNIQUE uq_dpcs_active WHERE revoked_at IS NULL). Helpers (SECDEF search_path ''): dp_is_active_coach_of(coach,client) INTERNAL (§156-locked, service_role only) and dp_coach_can_view(coach,client) RLS-facing (active share AND active coach_clients relationship; granted authenticated+service_role). RLS: items/entries owner FOR ALL + coach FOR SELECT via dp_coach_can_view; comments select both parties / insert author-with-context / update+delete own; shares client FOR ALL + coach FOR SELECT. dev_plan_02_notifications_catalog_display: 5 catalog rows category='development_plan', channel in_app, is_v1_active=true, user_configurable=true (dp_item_due_soon, dp_item_overdue, dp_comment_added, dp_plan_updated, dp_share_granted); notification_display CREATE OR REPLACE preserving all prior cases verbatim + 5 new (COALESCE(payload->>'action_url', default); owner url /development-plan, coach url /coach); internal dp_notify_shared_coaches(client,type,payload,dedup_key) service_role-only. dev_plan_03_owner_rpcs (8 SECDEF authenticated+service_role): dp_list_my_plan(p_include_archived default false) returns {items:[...+entries+comments]}; dp_add_items_from_ptp(p_items jsonb) bulk ON CONFLICT dedup returns {added,skipped,submitted} + notifies shared coaches; dp_create_custom_item; dp_update_item (partial via payload?key, notifies on status change); dp_archive_item; dp_add_entry (reflects progress to the item); dp_update_entry; dp_delete_entry. dev_plan_04_comments_sharing_coach_rpcs (6 SECDEF authenticated+service_role): dp_add_comment (derives author_role, notifies the other party), dp_edit_comment, dp_delete_comment (soft), dp_list_my_coaches returns {coaches:[{coach_user_id,coach_name,coach_email,is_shared}]}, dp_set_coach_share (validates an active relationship on grant, notifies the coach), dp_get_client_plan (coach-read, gated on dp_coach_can_view, raises not_authorized when not shared). dev_plan_05_due_date_scan_cron: dp_run_due_date_scan() service_role-only (target_date <= current_date+2, status<>done, not archived; notifies owner + shared coaches, due_soon/overdue split, deduped per target_date); cron jobid 33 'dp_due_date_scan_daily' '0 7 * * *' active. Functional test PASSED (rolled-back DO block, JWT-sim via set_config request.jwt.claims in the same call as the RPC): add 3 / dedup re-add 0 / 2 custom / plan=5 / entry progress 40 reflected to item / share grant -> coach sees 5 items + comments / stranger denied / after revoke coach denied / scan overdue 1 + due_soon 1; zero residue. NOTIFY pgrst run after the new tables. **FRONTEND COMPLETE (6 Lovable cycles, all SHA-verified).** C1: new src/pages/DevelopmentPlan.tsx (calls dp_list_my_plan, no SubscriptionGate) + route /development-plan (no guard, in the AppLayout block after /shared-results) + "My Development Plan" (Target icon) added to ALL FIVE AppSidebar nav arrays after "My Results"; caught + fixed a super-admin practitioner-coach nav DEDUPE (getNavItems now drops coachNav urls already present in superAdminNav so the Coach Tools section does not duplicate My Results/Development Plan/etc). C2: new src/components/results/AddToDevelopmentPlanModal.tsx (loads the existing plan on open to mark already-added steps, dedup-aware; calls dp_add_items_from_ptp) + an "Add to my Development Plan" button in PTPNarrativeSections.tsx gated !isCoachView && actionPlan.length>0; caught + fixed a rules-of-hooks violation (Lovable placed a useState after the isCoachLimited early return). C3: full rewrite of DevelopmentPlan.tsx into the interactive tracker (status Select, target-date input, progress Slider, dated entry log add/delete, add-custom dialog, soft-archive + show/hide-archived + restore); caught + fixed Lovable shipping a controlled Slider with no onValueChange (final = key={item.id-pct} + defaultValue + onValueCommit; the fix needed a re-run after a no-op same-SHA first attempt). C4: two-way comments thread per item (restored the PlanComment type + comments field that the C3 rewrite had dropped; useAuth gates edit/delete to the author; dp_add_comment/dp_edit_comment/dp_delete_comment). C5: new src/components/development-plan/ShareWithCoachDialog.tsx (dp_list_my_coaches + per-coach Switch toggling dp_set_coach_share) wired into the plan header (DevelopmentPlan.tsx SHA 7510e470, dialog SHA 8242c2b0). C6a: new src/components/development-plan/CoachClientPlan.tsx (read-only items + interactive comments via dp_get_client_plan with retry:false + a graceful "hasn't shared" state) wired into src/pages/coach/ClientResults.tsx in the per-client AssessmentList view below the assessments list (CoachClientPlan SHA eb82a8de, ClientResults SHA 8ba16cdd). C6b: NO-OP by design. The five development_plan notification types auto-surface in src/pages/NotificationSettings.tsx because that page is fully catalog-driven (get_notification_preferences returns every is_v1_active catalog row, grouped by category); they render under a "DEVELOPMENT_PLAN" heading consistent with the existing snake_case categories (actor_flow, certification_lifecycle, mentor_review, skills_signoff), and compose_notification_email is generic (it builds subject/body from notification_display), so a user who opts a dp type up to email/both gets a proper branded email with no extra work. **STATUS:** Item 2b DONE. Item 2a (PTP report persistent highlighting, backend-first) and Item 2c (share PTP results by email, backend tokenized link) remain OPEN, each its own sub-project. **Edge fn versions: none changed. Cron: jobid 33 NEW (dp_due_date_scan_daily). ops-stripe-webhook / platform stripe-webhook never touched.** Carryforward (still open) unchanged from v131/v132: 2a + 2c above; BQ-PTP-PERFECT-VERIFY (live guardrail test via authenticated-app JWT); newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder; Doc-1 invoice live refund test; the Phase-10 owner-stamp frontend follow-up; plus the longer Operations Docs 3/4/5 backlog. *v132 - Session 124 CLOSE. **BrainWise SaaS platform session: PTP results-report polish (Item 3) COMPLETE + newsletter AI co-pilot bug fixed (Item 1); no Operations work.** Backend-first; deployed source verified by re-fetch + esbuild parse + byte diff; frontend verified by GitHub API SHA. **ITEM 3 (PTP report) DONE.** This session shipped 3c (new PtpDimensionLegend.tsx per-section color legend, dropped into the facet charts and narrative sections), 3g (PTPDomainCards "Dimension Scores" boxes gained a per-dimension facet "Lowest {low}, Highest {high}" range; the separate PTPDimensionHighlightsSection cards were left untouched), the full-color Dimension Scores cards (filled with the dimension color + a new textColorFor luminance-threshold contrast helper so light fills get navy text and dark fills get white + inverted band pill; NAIDomainCards untouched), and 3a (the "perfect score" wording guardrail). 3b/3d/3e/3f had landed earlier in the session. **3a guardrail:** the phrase is model-generated (0 in assessment_results.ai_narrative; ~33 "perfect score" plus a few "near-perfect"/"perfect 100s" rows in facet_interpretations across several section types, all produced by generate-facet-interpretations). Fix = a SCORING_LANGUAGE_RULE injected into the 6 PTP prompt builders only (NAI builders + all logic untouched); deployed generate-facet-interpretations v53 (verified ACTIVE, byte-identical to the provided file, 7 occurrences, parses). Existing rows LEFT AS-IS (Part 2 = C, no bulk text-replace). **ITEM 1 newsletter co-pilot:** the "Reformat as importable HTML" button produced nothing because newsletter_ai_generate was truncating at MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS=8000 mid-html-fence (no block emitted); fixed in v12 (ceiling raised to 16000 + resilient extractFirstHtmlBlock that recovers a truncated block + stop_reason captured into the audit log and response). Cole confirmed working. **PTP DIMENSION RECOLOR (decision; Lovable prompt delivered, Cole runs + commits next):** Prediction #C2622E to #6D6875 (Gray, reverting to its long-standing canonical color) and Pleasure #7a5800 to #FFB703 (Amber) in ptpDimensionColors.ts, plus matching PDF pastels in assemblePdfDataForUser.ts (DIM-PTP-03 #F4EAE2 to #ECEBEE, DIM-PTP-05 #F1ECDC to #FFF4D9). #FFB703 is the reserved --bw-amber/--warning token, so Pleasure now shares a color with warning indicators (Cole's deliberate choice; recorded as an override of the "amber = warning only" rule in architecture-reference 5.2). Contrast is fine on the full-color cards (amber is light so textColorFor renders navy; gray is dark so white). **WHAT IS LEFT from the session's starting list = Item 2 only:** 2a persistent highlighting, 2b Action-Plan to "Development Plan" rename, 2c share PTP results by email; each is its own backend-first sub-project for the next session. **NEW BUILD-QUEUE ITEMS (this session):** (i) BQ-PTP-PERFECT-VERIFY: verify the 3a "perfect score" guardrail on a freshly generated PTP report (the deferred live model-behavior test; must run through the authenticated app since the generator gates on a super-admin user JWT that cannot be minted from MCP and the model key is not reachable); (ii) generator score-in-name fix (LOW); (iii) interpretation match-hardening keyed on item_number not facet name (MEDIUM, optional); (iv) optional driving-anchor render-time hardening (the 3e closure-race where anchors are baked at fetch time and could theoretically bake empty strings on a race; in practice assessmentResponses is reliably populated). **Edge fn versions: newsletter_ai_generate v12, generate-facet-interpretations v53; no other fns changed; no cron/Operations changes.** Close artifacts (markdown only, Session-74): this v132 entry, architecture-reference v126, session-124-to-125.md.*

*v131 - Session 123 CLOSE. **Operations Doc-2 CRM CLOSED OUT: Phase 9 (QA) and Phase 10 (owner/team RLS hardening) COMPLETE and verified; Doc-1 invoice Phase 9 largely closed; first real CRM data seeded.** Option A ordering (RLS first, then QA). Backend-first; every apply_migration followed by a separate execute_sql verification; every functional test a single rolled-back DO block (sentinel RAISE, zero residue); JWT-sim via set_config request.jwt.claims in the same call; frontend verified by GitHub API SHA. **PHASE 10 (3 migrations).** s123_p10_users_manager_id: manager_id self-FK on operations.users (ON DELETE SET NULL, partial index; team = direct reports; flat one-level, territory/multi-team deferred to v3 per §5.14). s123_p10_crm_can_write_helper: operations.crm_can_write(p_owner uuid) STABLE SECDEF search_path '' returns COALESCE(CASE current_user_role() WHEN admin THEN true, sales_user THEN owner=auth.uid(), sales_manager THEN owner=auth.uid() OR owner IN (the manager's direct reports), ELSE false END, false); REVOKE FROM PUBLIC, GRANT authenticated+service_role (it must be EXECUTE-able by the policy-evaluating role, so it is deliberately NOT revoked from authenticated; it takes only an owner uuid and computes from the caller's own identity, so no forge surface). s123_p10_owner_team_write_rls: rewrote INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies on leads, accounts, contact_persons, deals, activities to org_id=current_org_id() AND crm_can_write(owner_user_id) (UPDATE in both USING and WITH CHECK); SELECT left untouched (org-wide read-all for every role, which is why the dashboard and the report_crm_* views are unaffected); per-table role targeting preserved (public x4, authenticated for contact_persons); child/junction/config tables left org-scoped (no owner column). KEY FACT: operations.users.id = auth.uid(), so write-own is owner_user_id=auth.uid() with no lookup. DELETE moved from admin-only to owner-based (write-own includes delete-own per §5.14). SECDEF RPCs (ops_convert_lead, Won bridge, scoring engine) and the attribution triggers run as owner and bypass RLS, so conversion/webhook/scoring paths are unaffected. Verified by a rolled-back JWT-sim role matrix on leads (all 5 roles) and again across accounts/contacts/deals/activities: sales_user own=write / other=0-rows / insert-as-other=blocked, sales_manager report=write / non-report=0, read_only=all-blocked, admin=any; synthetic auth.users seeded under session_replication_role=replica to skip the two on_auth_user sync triggers, all rolled back, zero residue. FRONTEND FOLLOW-UP (carryforward, non-blocking): crm_can_write treats a null owner as not-writable for non-admins, so when the first real sales_user/sales_manager is invited the CRM create dialogs must stamp owner_user_id=auth.uid(); no effect today (only Cole, admin). **PHASE 9 (QA; import tooling DROPPED).** Zoho CRM import tooling removed from scope (no Zoho CRM history to import), so Phase 9 collapsed to QA. Backend integrity sweep clean across both platforms: 8 invoices with no duplicate numbers, balance_due consistent with total minus paid, total composition correct, amount_paid reconciles to allocations + applied credits + retainers, no orphan lines, no over-allocated payments, no refund exceeding its payment; migrated customer still reconciles to 3274.67 outstanding / 1 open; CRM has no orphan attribution, every deal points at a valid stage, no CRM table has RLS without a policy. **CLASSIC-ROI FIX (s123_p9_campaign_roi_classic_roi).** report_crm_campaign_roi.roi_pct was won_revenue/budget*100 (ROAS) under an ROI label; redefined to net/budget*100 (classic ROI) so the label matches; net and won_revenue unchanged; view kept WITH (security_invoker=true), anon denied. **LIVE CAMPAIGN-ATTRIBUTION QA (seeded as first real CRM data, Cole's call to keep it).** Created campaign Website Inbound (type other, budget 1000), captured a UTM-tagged lead (capture-time attribution trigger fired), converted it via ops_convert_lead (account + contact + deal created, conversion-time trigger copied attribution to the deal), moved the deal to Closed Won (amount 5000). Verified report_crm_campaign_roi: attributed_leads=1, attributed_deals=1, won_revenue=5000, net=4000, roi_pct=400. **WON BRIDGE fired manually** (operations._won_bridge_create_customer; the won_to_customer workflow rule has delay_minutes=5 so the scheduled scan skips a fresh win): created the first real customer (Acme Demo Co, type business), set account.customer_id + type='customer', linked the primary contact; idempotent (second call returned the same id, no duplicate). **DOC-1 INVOICE PHASE 9 (largely closed).** statusVariant 'done'->success + 'throttled'->outline cosmetic fix shipped in OperationsLeadDetail.tsx and SHA-verified (new blob 18ba1486); the other two carryforwards (Deals LIST row-click navigation, OperationsLeadCapture total_received column) were found ALREADY FIXED in the live repo. Stripe charge.refunded dashboard subscription DONE (Cole). The ops-stripe-webhook charge.refunded handler (v11) + ops_handle_stripe_refund RPC are already built and verified; LIVE REFUND TEST DEFERRED until a real Stripe transaction exists to refund safely (the migrated customer's payments are bank/cash, no payment_intent). **Edge fn versions: NONE changed this session.** Cron slots unchanged (jobids 1-32). ops-stripe-webhook (be435d9f) / platform stripe-webhook never cross-modified. **NEXT: Operations Doc-4 (e-sign / BoldSign), scoped, sequenced after Docs 1+2; Doc-3 (QuickBooks) and Doc-5 (cross-cutting) remain.** **Carryforward (still open):** the Doc-1 live refund test (deferred, needs a real Stripe txn); the Phase-10 owner-stamp frontend follow-up (before the first non-admin CRM user); PTP verification tail; BQ-PTP-ANCHOR-REWRITE; BQ-NARRATIVE-FANOUT-STATUS; BQ-FANOUT-COLDFAIL; BQ-PDF-EXPORT-COLDCACHE; BUG-NWS-1; newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder; Mux soak/reclaim; the migrated customer's timesheet gap; Docs 3 (QuickBooks) / 4 (e-sign BoldSign) / 5 (cross-cutting).
