*v130 - Session 122 CLOSE. **Operations Doc-2 CRM Phases 7 (lead scoring + assignment + workflow engine) and 8 (campaigns, attribution, reports, dashboard, settings UI) COMPLETE end-to-end (backend + frontend), both verified.** Backend-first throughout; every apply_migration followed by a separate execute_sql verification; every functional test a single rolled-back DO block (sentinel RAISE, zero residue); frontend verified by GitHub API SHA (not raw CDN). **PHASE 7:** lead_scoring_rules (demographic/behavioral/decay) + append-only lead_score_events; scoring engine operations._recompute_lead_score + public ops_recompute_lead_score; score-decay cron jobid 31; round-robin owner assignment; workflow_rules + workflow_execution_log (dedup); scan-based engine ops_workflow_engine_run with 4 kinds (won_to_customer / deal_stale / lead_qualified_assign / deal_discovery_task) + additive operations._won_bridge_create_customer (Won bridge is an additive internal helper invoked by the scan, NOT an on-Won trigger, so it never raises under the service-role path); 4 seeded workflow rules; engine cron jobid 32 ('3-59/5 * * * *'). Decision: scan-based engine (no per-row table triggers) to avoid recursion. Frontend: ScoreCard on OperationsLeadDetail (recompute + lead_score_events history); LeadScoringRulesCard (full CRUD, dialog conditional by rule_type) + WorkflowRulesCard (edit-only, per-kind params, active toggle) in a new "CRM Automation" tab on OperationsSettings (existing tabs untouched). **PHASE 8:** campaigns + campaign_attribution; 21 report_crm_* views (all security_invoker=true, GRANT SELECT authenticated+service_role); ops_auto_attribute_lead_utm public RPC. Frontend: OperationsCampaigns CRUD page + route + nav; 21 crm_* report defs appended to OperationsReports (page already had date filter / column toggle / CSV / PDF); NEW OperationsDashboard at GUARDED /operations/dashboard (RoleGuard brainwise_super_admin + SuperAdminSessionProvider, same wrapper as siblings): 4 KPI cards (open weighted pipeline, open deals, avg deal cycle, avg time to qualify), 2 recharts bar charts (pipeline by stage, forecast by month; navy #021F36 Total, orange #F5741A Weighted), 4 tables (win rate, leads by source, top accounts, lost reasons). **TWO BACKEND GAPS CAUGHT + FIXED THIS SESSION.** (1) org_id default gap: the 4 new Phase 7/8 tables (campaigns, campaign_attribution, lead_scoring_rules, workflow_rules) had NO DEFAULT on org_id unlike older ops tables, so UI create dialogs omitting org_id would fail NOT NULL; migration s122_p8_orgid_default_backfill set DEFAULT operations.current_org_id() on all four (verified). (2) Campaign attribution was fully unwired: ops_auto_attribute_lead_utm existed but was orphaned, org-guarded so it raises on the service-role webhook path, so no campaign_attribution rows were ever created and report_crm_campaign_roi + the dashboard campaign view always read empty. report_crm_campaign_roi counts attributed_leads from target_type='lead' rows AND attributed_deals/won_revenue from target_type='deal' rows, so BOTH capture-time and conversion-time wiring were required. Fix (s122_p8_campaign_attribution_wiring, fully additive, no existing RPC modified, rolled-back functional test PASSED 'SENTINEL_OK lead_attr=1 deal_attr=1 roi_leads=1 roi_deals=1 won=0'): internal service-role operations._attribute_lead_utm(uuid) (no current_org_id guard, derives org from the lead, mirrors the public RPC match+insert, first_touch, idempotent; REVOKE EXECUTE FROM authenticated/anon/PUBLIC + GRANT service_role per §156); AFTER INSERT trigger tg_lead_attribute_on_insert on operations.leads (when utm_campaign present, attributes the captured lead to its campaign by lower(name)=lower(utm) or custom_fields->>'utm_campaign' match, covers webhook + manual + future import in one place); AFTER UPDATE OF converted_deal_id trigger tg_lead_attribution_to_deal (WHEN old null -> new not null, copies the lead's campaign_attribution rows to the new deal as target_type='deal', drives attributed_deals + won_revenue, fires regardless of which RPC performs the conversion). Public ops_auto_attribute_lead_utm kept as the manual re-attribution entry. Dashboard frontend fix: Lovable guessed 3 wrong column keys, corrected to match the live views (win rate deals_reached/deals_won, leads-by-source source_name, lost-reason lost_amount); KPIs/charts/top-accounts were already correct. DEVIATIONS from the original Phase-8 scope line: 21 report views shipped (planned 18); dashboard is a fixed layout (per-user configurable widgets deferred to a future pass). **Edge fn versions: NONE changed this session** (ops-webhook-lead-capture v1 was inspected only, to diagnose the attribution gap; confirmed it normalizes UTMs into the lead but never called the attribution RPC). Cron slots in use: jobids 1-32. ops-stripe-webhook (be435d9f) / platform stripe-webhook (fb9725d2) never cross-modified. **NEXT SESSION = Phase 9 (QA + Zoho CRM import tooling: per-entity import RPCs, hand-curated stage/reason mapping, end-to-end QA), then Phase 10 (owner/team RLS hardening, gated on the second CRM user).** Doc-1 Phase 9 QA/polish/deploy still deferred. **Carryforward (unchanged, still open):** the two Phase-6 cosmetic frontend fixes (OperationsLeadCapture received_count vs total_received; OperationsLeadDetail 'done' status color); Deals LIST row-click opens edit dialog instead of detail; Doc-1 Phase 3 estimate->project/retainer conversions never built; Doc-1 Phase 9; P1 live refund test + Stripe charge.refunded subscription; PTP verification tail; BQ-PTP-ANCHOR-REWRITE; BQ-NARRATIVE-FANOUT-STATUS; BQ-FANOUT-COLDFAIL; BQ-PDF-EXPORT-COLDCACHE; BUG-NWS-1; newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder; Mux soak/reclaim; the migrated customer's timesheet gap; Docs 3 (QuickBooks) / 4 (e-sign BoldSign) / 5 (cross-cutting). *v129 - Session 121 CLOSE. **Operations Doc-2 CRM Phases 5 (Calendar) and 6 (Lead capture + enrichment) COMPLETE end-to-end (backend + frontend), both verified.** Backend-first; every apply_migration followed by a separate execute_sql verification; every functional test a single rolled-back DO block (sentinel RAISE, zero residue); all four new Edge Functions boot-verified; all frontend verified by GitHub API SHA. **One new standing rule §157:** this Supabase instance auto-GRANTs privileges to `authenticated` on newly created TABLES too (the table-level analog of §156's function behavior), so a secret-bearing table must be locked with an explicit `REVOKE ALL ON ... FROM authenticated, anon` (RLS-deny alone is not sufficient defense-in-depth. **PHASE 5 (Calendar):** organizations += timezone / day_of_digest_hour / reminders_enabled; meeting_calendar_links (Add-to-Calendar audit) + meeting_reminders (send-log + dedup, unique index on activity+type+user), both SELECT-only RLS. RPCs (search_path '', SECDEF): ops_record_calendar_link (authenticated), ops_record_meeting_reminder_sent / ops_record_day_of_digest_sent / ops_due_meeting_reminders / ops_due_day_of_digest / ops_update_reminder_settings, service_role-only except the calendar-link recorder and the settings updater; selectors render their own branded email so the edge fns are dumb senders. Edge fns ops-meeting-reminder-cron v1 (jobid 28, 5,20,35,50) and ops-day-of-digest-cron v1 (jobid 29, hourly :10, org-local-hour gate), both reuse reminders@mail.brainwiseenterprises.com. Decisions: email-only (1hr-before + day-of digest; 15-min in-app deferred), owner-only recipients, compute-in-cron dedup, digest gated on org-local hour and deduped via an audit_log event. A ROW_COUNT-into-boolean bug in the reminder recorder was caught by the functional test and fixed. Frontend: AddToCalendarButton.tsx on EntityTimeline meeting rows + a Calendar-reminders card in OperationsSettings (Templates & Reminders tab). **PHASE 6 (Lead capture + enrichment):** lead_capture_webhooks (RPC-mediated, REVOKE ALL FROM authenticated/anon so hmac_secret is privilege-hidden per §157), webhook_ingestion_log (rate-limit window + audit), enrichment_log (doubles as the queue: pending/done/error/throttled/skipped); leads.source_webhook_id FK attached; organizations += enrichment_monthly_quota (default 200). 12 RPCs: webhook CRUD (list/create/update/rotate/get_signing_info/delete; create+rotate return the secret once; reads org-member, writes admin), ops_webhook_resolve + ops_log_webhook_ingestion + ops_ingest_captured_lead (rate-check + NOT-NULL fallbacks for last_name/company_name_text + auto-enrich enqueue + accepted-log, atomic) all service_role, ops_due_enrichments (monthly-quota-guarded drain) + ops_record_enrichment_result (writes enrichment_data back to the lead) + ops_enqueue_enrichment (manual, authenticated). Edge fns: ops-webhook-lead-capture v1 (public, verify_jwt false; token-resolve, HMAC-SHA256 verify, honeypot→silent 200, throttle→429, 5 recipe normalizers generic/lovable_form/tally/microsoft_bookings/stripe/zapier + field_mapping override) and ops-enrichment-cron v1 (jobid 30, 2,17,32,47; consolidated Apollo org-enrich + Hunter email-verify, Apollo people path records skipped, plan-gated). Decisions: Apollo ORG-ENRICH ONLY (people gated by plan) + Hunter email; per-(webhook,IP) sliding window default 60/min; enrichment_log=queue cron-drained, auto-enrich default OFF; secret service-role-only; 5 recipes. DEVIATION from the 2-function plan: a single drain cron handles both providers. LIVE PROVIDER SMOKE TEST PASSED (Apollo org-enrich stripe.com → full firmographics; Hunter verify → status=valid; test rows cleaned up); Apollo key confirmed working for organizations/enrich on the current plan. Frontend: OperationsLeadCapture.tsx (new page + route + nav, one-time secret reveal) and the Enrichment card + "Captured via web form" badge on OperationsLeadDetail.tsx. **TWO trivial cosmetic frontend follow-ups (non-blocking):** OperationsLeadCapture reads received_count but the list RPC returns total_received (Received column always shows 0, one-word fix); OperationsLeadDetail statusVariant does not map 'done' to the success colour (completed rows show grey). **Edge fn versions at close:** ops-meeting-reminder-cron v1 NEW, ops-day-of-digest-cron v1 NEW, ops-webhook-lead-capture v1 NEW, ops-enrichment-cron v1 NEW; all other ops and platform fns unchanged; ops and platform Stripe webhooks never cross-modified. Cron slots in use: jobids 1-30. **NEXT SESSION = Phase 7 (lead scoring / assignment, per the Doc-2 roadmap)** unless Cole redirects; Doc-1 Phase 9 QA/polish/deploy still deferred until after the CRM. **Carryforward (unchanged, still open):** the two Phase-6 cosmetic frontend fixes; P1 live refund test + Stripe charge.refunded dashboard subscription; PTP verification tail; BQ-PTP-ANCHOR-REWRITE; BQ-NARRATIVE-FANOUT-STATUS; BQ-FANOUT-COLDFAIL; BQ-PDF-EXPORT-COLDCACHE; BUG-NWS-1; newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder; Mux soak/reclaim; the migrated customer's timesheet gap; Phase 3 polish (Deals LIST row-click); Doc-1 Phase 3 estimate→project/retainer conversions never built; Docs 3 (QuickBooks)/4 (e-sign BoldSign)/5 (cross-cutting). users.is_salesperson; idempotent §156-locked seeder operations.seed_crm_defaults(org) seeded BrainWise (6-stage default pipeline +Won/Lost, 5 lead statuses, 10 lead_source/4 won/9 lost/10 industry picklists). **Phase 1:** accounts, contact_persons extension, leads, deals + deal_stage_history/team_members/stakeholders/line_items; deals triggers (auto-probability follows stage unless overridden; stage history on every transition with time-in-prior); ops_convert_lead + ops_bulk_convert_leads + ops_create_customer_from_deal (Won bridge, plain RPC; auto-on-Won 5-min-delay is Phase 7). expected_revenue generated. **Phase 2:** activities (task/call/meeting/email/note) + attendees + attachments; polymorphic related-to integrity trigger; attachment_count trigger; ops_entity_timeline RPC (activities ∪ audit_log); deal_pipeline_health view (rot flag + no-next-activity warn flag). **Phase 3 (Lovable, all verified by API SHA):** P3.1 nav + 4 routes + 4 list pages + 4 create/edit dialogs (commit 5a8e29b); P3.2 Pipeline Kanban (native HTML5 DnD, deal_pipeline_health glow); P3.3 Deal detail (header + stage Select + Won→customer; tabs Overview/Activities/Team/Stakeholders/Line items); P3.4 Lead detail + ConvertLeadDialog (single + bulk) + reusable EntityTimeline + leads-list multi-select; P3.5 Account detail (parent self-embed) + Contact detail (opt-out toggles write _at); P3.6a activity dashboard (/operations/activities Overdue/Today/This-week); P3.6b SavedViewsBar wired into all 4 lists with minimal filters. RPC client split confirmed (public RPCs via default supabase client, tables via opsSupabase). saved_lists backend = operations.saved_lists, 4-policy org-scoped RLS + set_updated_at + grants, verified via rolled-back JWT-sim insert (org defaulted correctly), cache reloaded. Rich query-builder deferred to v2 (Doc 2 §8). **CRM access model:** basic entity CRUD via direct opsSupabase table writes under RLS (no CRUD RPCs); only convert/bulk-convert/Won-bridge/timeline are RPCs. **Phase 3 polish carryforward:** Deals LIST row-click still opens edit dialog instead of navigating to /operations/deals/:id (one-line fix). Config editors for Phase-0 picklists/stages are Phase 8, not Phase 3. **Carryforward (unchanged, still open):** Doc-1 Phase 9 QA/polish/deploy (now after CRM), P1 live refund test + Stripe charge.refunded dashboard subscription, PTP verification tail (2 Deidentified reports + 1 org stale caches), BQ-PTP-ANCHOR-REWRITE, BQ-NARRATIVE-FANOUT-STATUS, BQ-FANOUT-COLDFAIL, BQ-PDF-EXPORT-COLDCACHE, BUG-NWS-1, newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder, Mux soak/reclaim + storage-source-removal decision, the migrated customer's timesheet gap (option A). Build order A (cross-cutting primitives first). **One new standing rule §156** (this Supabase instance's default privileges auto-GRANT EXECUTE to `authenticated` on new functions → internal SECDEF helpers need an explicit REVOKE EXECUTE FROM authenticated, not just FROM anon, PUBLIC). **Sub-area 1 — activity-log substrate + comments:** operations.audit_log (append-only, RLS select-own-org) + operations.document_comments + internal writer operations.log_activity + public ops_list_activity / ops_list_comments / ops_add_comment. **Sub-area 2 — merge renderer + email templates:** SQL-side operations.render_merge (blanks unresolved tokens, escapes backslashes) + ops_get_merge_tag_catalog + email-template CRUD/preview (ops_list/upsert/delete_email_template, ops_render_email_preview) + 5 seeded defaults; ops_due_payment_reminders() retrofitted in place to return server-rendered subject/body_html; ops-payment-reminder-cron v7 became a dumb sender. **Sub-area 3 — reminder dedup + send-log + manual send:** dedup guard on ops_due_payment_reminders() via a 'reminder_sent' audit event (no new table); ops_record_reminder_sent (dual-context cron/manual with cross-org forge guard); ops_render_invoice_reminder; reminder_schedules CRUD; ops-payment-reminder-cron v8 (records sends) + ops-send-reminder-now v1 NEW (verify_jwt true, acts as caller). **Sub-area 4 — late-fee engine:** operations.late_fee_applications (UNIQUE(invoice,rule) idempotency) + ops_apply_late_fees() runner (adds a visible free_form line + bumps subtotal/total, balance_due moves via the generated column) + ops_late_fee_daily cron jobid 27 + late_fee_rules CRUD; v1 = one-time-per-rule, apply_to='all' only. **Sub-area 5 — salesperson/commission:** operations.users.commission_rate + ops_list_salespeople / ops_set_user_commission_rate / ops_set_document_salesperson + operations.report_commission view (both invoiced and paid bases). **Sub-area 6 — custom-fields engine:** custom_fields jsonb now on all 8 entity tables (contact_person is NOT a valid entity_type per the live CHECK, so 4 tables not 5 got the column) + operations.validate_custom_fields helper + definition CRUD + ops_set_custom_field_values whitelisted-table setter. **Sub-area 7a — remaining settings CRUD:** numbering (list + safe-update, next_number deliberately NOT exposed), tax authorities + tax rates CRUD, currencies list+upsert. **Sub-area 7b — consolidated settings UI (Lovable, 3 prompts, all verified against the repo):** OperationsSettings.tsx became a 6-tab shadcn shell — P1 shell + Branding (moved verbatim) + Numbering & Tax CRUD; P2 Templates & Reminders (type-aware editor, merge-tag chips inserting at the focused-field caret, live iframe srcDoc preview + server-render verify, reminder schedules with __auto__→null sentinel); P3 Late Fees (apply_to pinned 'all' disabled) + Sales & Commission (per-user rate dialog) + Custom Fields (entity-type selector, dropdown_options round-trip, entity_type+field_name locked on edit). *v124 - Session 116 CLOSE — **Two streams: (A) Mux video migration for the learning platform — COMPLETE and verified end-to-end; (B) Operations Doc-1 invoice bug fixes — logo-upload RLS FIXED + expense-receipt-on-invoice-email opt-in (backend shipped + verified, frontend prompt delivered/pending).** Backend-first throughout; every apply_migration followed by a separate execute_sql verification; every Edge Function boot-verified (unauth→401); every shipped Lovable file confirmed against the live repo via GitHub MCP. **(0) S116 OPENER DONE.** `session116_opener_revoke_anon_public_s115_rpcs` — the 5 S115 RPCs (ops_convert_estimate_to_retainer / ops_convert_estimate_to_project / ops_get_refundable_payment / ops_list_invoice_payments / ops_update_org_branding) actually had EXPLICIT anon grants (not just inherited PUBLIC), so the carried wording was off; revoked with the comma form `REVOKE EXECUTE … FROM anon, PUBLIC` and verified all 5 anon=false / authenticated=true / service_role=true. **(A) MUX VIDEO MIGRATION — COMPLETE (closes the S77/v85 "react-player + mux support" gap).** Product decisions: signed playback, 1080p, skip captions v1; single production Mux environment (no test env / no secret swap); delete originals after a ~1-week soak (deferred). Migrations (all verified): `session116_mux_video_columns` (content_items += mux_asset_id, mux_status, duration_seconds, all nullable, no enum/CHECK change); `session116_mux_get_content_item_video_playback` (RPC get_content_item_video_playback(uuid) SECDEF search_path '', mirrors the get_content_item_video_asset access gate — auth/self/admin/mentor + module-published + curriculum-assignment — returns playback_id/mux_status/video_source_type, null playback while preparing; authenticated+service_role, anon/PUBLIC revoked); `session116_upsert_content_item_mux_source_id_rules` (body-only CREATE OR REPLACE, single signature, no overload — exempts mux from required-source-id at save AND preserves the webhook-set playback id on a re-save that submits empty); `session116_content_items_video_required_exempt_mux` (dropped+recreated the CHECK constraint content_items_video_required to exempt video_source_type='mux'; the constraint had only been exempting supabase_storage — scope had missed it). Edge Functions: `mux-create-upload` v1 (verify_jwt true, super-admin only; brokers a Mux direct-upload URL, signed playback policy, passthrough=content_item_id, 1080p, mp4_support none; sets mux_status='preparing'); `mux-webhook` v1 (verify_jwt FALSE, Mux-Signature HMAC-SHA256 over `${t}.${rawBody}`; video.asset.ready → writes mux_asset_id + video_source_id=signed playback id + duration_seconds + mux_status='ready', flips video_source_type='mux'; asset.errored→errored; upload.asset_created captures asset id; idempotent; unsigned→401); `get-content-item-video-url` → v4 (verify_jwt true; branches on video_source_type — mux: playback RPC + mints an RS256 signed Mux JWT via **npm:jsonwebtoken@9** (DEVIATION from the scope's Web Crypto — jsonwebtoken handles PKCS1/PKCS8 PEM like Mux's SDK; key is base64 PEM via atob; claims sub=playback_id, aud='v', exp now+7200, kid), returns {kind:'mux', playback_id, token, expires_in_seconds} or {kind:'mux', processing:true}; supabase_storage: unchanged legacy signed-URL path); `mux-ingest-existing` → v3 (verify_jwt true, super-admin; one-time URL-pull ingest with CORS preflight + optional exclude_ids[]). CUTOVER: record_video_progress(uuid,int,int) confirmed UNCHANGED. Pilot ingest of "Purpose" → preparing → webhook flip to mux/ready → Cole confirmed it plays via the signed stream; then full ingest of the other 18 real videos (5 test items excluded, left on storage). Final state verified: 19 mux/ready, 0 errored. Deleted one orphan "testing video fix again" (zero FK dependents). Frontend SHIPPED + verified end-to-end: viewer swap — VideoViewer.tsx renders @mux/mux-player-react MuxPlayer for kind=mux (signed token, onTimeUpdate+onEnded → record_video_progress), processing placeholder, native <video> for supabase_storage, iframe for embeds (dep @mux/mux-player-react); authoring upload — NEW MuxVideoUploadField.tsx (state machine idle/uploading/processing/ready/error, mux-create-upload + @mux/upchunk, polls mux_status every 5s) wired into ContentItemEditor.tsx (import + default ?? "mux" + sourceIdOk includes mux + dirty baseline + mux render branch — all 4 edits confirmed in repo lines 26/59/187/242/662) (dep @mux/upchunk). Cole tested both ends; works. **MUX REMAINING (PENDING):** (1) soak ~1 week then delete the 19 originals from lesson-assets (~2.5GB) — NOTE the flip overwrote video_source_id with the playback id, erasing the original storage-asset pointer, so snapshot content_item→content_asset mappings before any future bulk flip if rollback is wanted (Purpose original = 105cf0ed); (2) decide whether to remove Supabase Storage as a selectable video source in the authoring dropdown (non-blocking one-liner). **(B1) LOGO-UPLOAD RLS — FIXED (root cause = missing SELECT policy, not the client).** Branding logo upload to operations-branding threw "new row violates row-level security policy." First diagnosis (opsSupabase stale token → swap to the main supabase client) was WRONG; the swap shipped (harmless, left in place) and the error persisted. Real cause found by comparing the three operations buckets' full policy sets: operations-documents and operations-receipts each have SELECT+INSERT+UPDATE+DELETE and upload fine; operations-branding (created S115 P6) had INSERT+UPDATE+DELETE but **NO SELECT policy** — the Supabase storage upload path reads the object back after writing, so without a SELECT policy the upload is rejected as an RLS violation even though the INSERT with_check passes (a direct SQL INSERT as authenticated passed, which had misled the token theory). Fix: `session116_ops_branding_add_missing_select_policy` (CREATE POLICY ops_branding_select FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING bucket_id + (storage.foldername(name))[1]=current_org_id()::text — mirrors the siblings); verified branding now has all 4 policies; Cole confirmed the logo uploads. **(B2) EXPENSE RECEIPT ON INVOICE EMAIL — backend DONE + verified, frontend pending.** Cole wants billable expense receipts attached to the invoice email, gated by an OPT-IN checkbox (chose B over auto-attach). Backend: `session116_ops_get_invoice_expense_receipts` (RPC public.ops_get_invoice_expense_receipts(p_invoice) SECDEF search_path '', org-gated — invoice.org_id must = current_org_id() else raise — returns receipt_storage_path + a clean suggested_filename 'Receipt-<basename>' for billable invoiced expenses with a receipt; linkage expenses.invoice_line_id → document_lines(document_type='invoice', document_id=invoice); authenticated+service_role, anon/PUBLIC revoked; verified returns Receipt-episode_thumbnail.png for the test invoice); `ops-invoice-send` → v9 (accepts opt-in body flag include_expense_receipts, default OFF so behavior is unchanged; when true, calls the RPC via the caller's client for authorization, downloads each receipt from operations-receipts via service role, appends to the email attachments alongside the invoice PDF with a 38MB total-size guard, updates copy to note N receipts, returns {success, attached, pdf_attached, receipts_attached, receipts_skipped}; boot-verified unauth→401). FRONTEND (Lovable prompt APPROVED + delivered, PENDING Cole run + verify): edits OperationsInvoiceDetail.tsx only — adds a Send-invoice dialog with an opt-in "Attach N expense receipt(s)" checkbox shown only when the invoice has receipts (count from the RPC on dialog open, default unchecked), passes include_expense_receipts when ticked, AND swaps the operations-documents PDF upload from opsSupabase.storage to the main supabase.storage (the PDF upload had the same swallowed-error pattern as the logo, so invoice PDFs may have been silently not attaching). **Edge fn versions at close:** `mux-create-upload` v1 NEW, `mux-webhook` v1 NEW, `mux-ingest-existing` v3 NEW, `get-content-item-video-url` v4, `ops-invoice-send` v9; all other ops + platform fns unchanged. **Next session = Operations Doc-1 Phase 7 (settings/automation: settings UI + merge tags, reminders, late fees, salesperson/commission, activity log + comments, custom-fields engine) then Phase 8 (customer depth + contact persons + statements + credit balance + CSV import + Zoho-CSV import/reconcile).** First open item next session: run + verify the B2 expense-receipt checkbox frontend prompt (backend already live). **Carryforward (unchanged, still open):** PTP verification tail (5 "both" reports + 2 Deidentified + 1 org's stale caches), BQ-PTP-ANCHOR-REWRITE decision, BQ-NARRATIVE-FANOUT-STATUS, BQ-FANOUT-COLDFAIL, BQ-PDF-EXPORT-COLDCACHE, BUG-NWS-1 (Group H closure gate), newsletter STATIC_ROUTES manual-edit reminder, Mux soak/reclaim + storage-source-removal decision. *v123 - Session 115 CLOSE — **Operations Doc-1: P3 estimate conversions completed + P1 in-app refund + P5 Reports + P6 PDF engine & branding — all shipped and verified.** Backend-first throughout (every apply_migration followed by execute_sql verification; functional tests as one rolled-back DO block consuming zero document numbers); every Lovable prompt run by Cole and every shipped file verified against the live repo via the GitHub MCP API. **(1) P3 conversions COMPLETE.** Added `estimates.converted_retainer_id` (+FK→retainer_invoices) and `ops_convert_estimate_to_retainer` (draft RET- retainer, amount=total) + `ops_convert_estimate_to_project` (project budget=total; `p_billing_method` in none|project_hours|task_hours|staff_hours; under 'none' emits one billable project_charges row per estimate line — fallback single charge — hourly methods emit 0 charges, avoiding double-bill); patched `ops_convert_estimate_to_invoice` (body-only CREATE OR REPLACE, no overload) to guard all three `converted_*_id` for mutual exclusion. All three conversions mutually exclusive; estimate status unchanged on project/retainer convert (UI gates on `converted_*_id`). DO-block test 5/5; zero residue. Frontend: OperationsEstimateDetail Convert dropdown (To invoice/project/retainer) gated `canConvert && !alreadyConverted`, project dialog (name + billing_method), "View {target}". **(2) P1 refund = Option B (in-app initiate).** `ops_get_refundable_payment` (org-gated; refundable = amount − excess − refunded) + `ops_list_invoice_payments` (per-payment jsonb allocated to the invoice; `is_stripe` = pi_ prefix AND mode in stripe_card|stripe_ach) + edge fn `ops-issue-refund` v1 (verify_jwt true, mirrors ops-invoice-checkout: stripe@18.5.0/2025-08-27.basil; authorizes via ops_get_refundable_payment, validates pi_/stripe-mode/0<amt<=refundable, calls stripe.refunds.create). **The edge fn initiates ONLY — it writes NO ledger;** the existing `ops-stripe-webhook` v3 `charge.refunded` branch → `ops_handle_stripe_refund` remains the sole, idempotent, delta-based ledger writer. Frontend: OperationsInvoiceDetail Payments card (via ops_list_invoice_payments) with a Refund button only when `is_stripe && refundable>0` → invoke ops-issue-refund; invalidates the invoice + payments queries. **LIVE refund test DEFERRED** (Cole's ops Stripe `charge.refunded` subscription is live; fire against the existing $1 paid invoice next session). **(3) P5 Reports.** Migration `session115_p5_report_fact_views`: 5 security_invoker views — report_invoices, report_invoice_lines (with item name), report_payments (string_agg invoice_numbers + is_stripe), report_time (excludes running timers; cost via project_users.cost_rate), report_expenses (billable_amount = amount×(1+markup/100); customer = coalesce(expense, project)). GRANT SELECT authenticated+service_role only (NO anon → NOT in the S116 revoke set). Reuse project_financials_rollup + customer_balance_summary. Frontend: NEW OperationsReports page (super-admin route + sidebar) — 4 detail + 6 grouped reports (incl. commission by salesperson), date filter, column toggle, CSV, jsPDF landscape PDF export. **LESSON: raw.githubusercontent.com can serve STALE byte-identical content even with a ?cb cache-bust** (gave a false "ship failed" on one prompt) — ALWAYS confirm a ship via GitHub MCP get_file_contents (API), never the raw CDN. **(4) P6 PDF engine + branding.** Decisions: client-side jsPDF; brand as BrainWise (org identity/colors) with the recipient block from the customer record; all three templates (Standard/Corporate/Detailed); attach the PDF to the invoice email. **D1↔D4 reconciliation (now §154): the client generates the PDF → uploads it to the private operations-documents bucket → the server edge fn downloads + attaches it — ONE generator, no duplicated layout logic.** Migrations: `session115_p6_org_branding_fields_and_seed` (operations.organizations += legal_name, address jsonb, email, phone, tax_id, website, logo_url, brand_color DEFAULT #021F36, accent_color DEFAULT #F5741A; seeded BrainWise — Navy/Orange, invoices@ email, website; logo_url + tax_id null), `session115_p6_storage_buckets` (operations-branding public-read for the logo + operations-documents private; 4 org-path-scoped policies each, mirroring operations-receipts: path = {orgId}/…, (storage.foldername(name))[1]=current_org_id()::text), `session115_p6_ops_update_org_branding` (SECDEF admin-only — current_user_role()='admin' — partial patch via COALESCE on p_patch; verified + rollback). Edge fn `ops-invoice-send` v2→v3 (verify_jwt true): optional attachment_path, validates startsWith(`${inv.org_id}/`), downloads from operations-documents via service role, attaches base64 via Resend (deno std 0.224 encodeBase64), returns {success, attached}; falls back to send-without-attachment if the file is missing. organizations RLS: SELECT authenticated scoped to current_org_id() (client reads branding); NO update policy (use the RPC). Frontend (3 prompts, all verified against the repo): NEW lib `src/lib/operations/documentPdf.ts` — one engine, 3 template variants (standard = white header + accent rule + light-gray table head; corporate = full-width brand band + brand-fill head; detailed = accent-fill head + itemized totals), lazy `await import("jspdf")`, logo fetched from the public bucket → dataURL → addImage (try/catch), exports generateDocumentPdf(→Blob) + downloadDocumentPdf; Download-PDF dropdowns on invoice (3 templates + Receipt when amount_paid>0) and estimate detail; the invoice "Send" flow now generates the Standard PDF → uploads to operations-documents/{org}/Invoice-{id}.pdf → invokes ops-invoice-send with attachment_path (graceful fallback, toast reflects `attached`); NEW OperationsSettings page (read org via opsSupabase, logo upload to operations-branding, save via ops_update_org_branding) + super-admin route + sidebar "Settings" entry. **(5) S116 OPENER (carried): `REVOKE EXECUTE … FROM PUBLIC` (per §140 — not FROM anon) on the 5 new authenticated-only public RPCs** — ops_convert_estimate_to_retainer, ops_convert_estimate_to_project, ops_get_refundable_payment, ops_list_invoice_payments, ops_update_org_branding — then verify `has_function_privilege('anon',oid,'EXECUTE')`=false / authenticated=true. (Each self-gates on current_org_id()/role in the interim; the report views are authenticated+service_role only and need no revoke.) **(6) PTP verification tail (carried from S114, STILL OPEN):** the 5 "both" PTP reports are backfilled; STILL NEED to confirm the 2 Deidentified reports regenerated BEFORE invalidating the 1 affected org's stale caches (5 org_dashboard_narratives INST-001 + 3 org_ptp_delta_narratives). BQ-PTP-ANCHOR-REWRITE decision still pending. *v122 - Session 114 CLOSE — **Two streams: Operations P2-extras frontend (time/expense depth, slices 1–5) + a PTP reverse-scoring audit/correction/historical-rescore + assessment-integrity frontend.** Backend-first; every `apply_migration` followed by `execute_sql` verification; every Lovable claim verified against the live repo before approval and every shipped file after. **(1) Operations Doc-1 re-cut into a standing 9-phase roadmap** (see the Open Queue Operations section above; supersedes the backend Phase 0–4 sequencing for product tracking). P1–P4 shipped across S109–S114 with two exceptions: P3 has only convert→invoice (convert→project + convert→retainer NOT built), and P1 has open residuals (Stripe `charge.refunded` dashboard subscription, refund-flow UI, first invoice/reminder email live-verification). P5 Reports is the next phase and is ~all 11 views + the whole reports UI (existing ops views are AR-aging/unbilled/balance only). **S115 order locked: P3 conversions → P1 residuals → P5 Reports.** **(2) Operations P2-extras: 4 migrations + 5 frontend slices, all verified.** Migrations: `session114_p4_revoke_public_eight_rpcs` (the carried S114 opener — REVOKE EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC on the 8 P4 RPCs; verified anon=false/auth=true; this CORRECTED §152 — the real mechanism was anon inheriting via PUBLIC, `REVOKE FROM anon` was a no-op, `REVOKE FROM PUBLIC`=§140 is the fix), `session114_p2x_project_users_org_created_defaults`, `session114_p2x_timer_rpcs` (`ops_start_timer`/`ops_stop_timer`, server-side single-running-timer invariant, functional test passed), `session114_p2x_project_financials_rollup_view` (`operations.project_financials_rollup` security_invoker: cost=time×cost_rate+expenses, revenue=invoiced-to-date excl draft/void/written_off, margin/margin_pct/cost_untracked_hours flag). Slices: (1) project members Team card + TeamMemberDialog; (2) live timer on the project Time card chaining into LogTime edit; (3) DurationPicker 15-min retrofit + per-project weekly card + standalone `/operations/my-time` (super-admin guarded, current-user-only v1, append write model); (4) project financials card (Number()-coerce numeric-as-string cols; invalidation wired on LogTime save + time-entry delete); (5) My Time Entry/Overview tabs + entry-enabled month calendar (month query under the existing `["ops","my-time"]` prefix). DEFERRED: all-staff admin weekly timesheet (multi-user grid; needs user-select + read scope) — build after current-user My Time. **(3) PTP reverse-scoring correction.** Audit found 7 items (37,40,42,45,66,68,72) with descending-anchor structure that should reverse, beyond the original 3 (8,17,43); all 10 live in DIM-PTP-03. Applied: anchor-text fixes on items 17 + 76, flipped `reverse_scored=true` on the 7 (all 10 now true, verified — ongoing assessments correct). PROPOSED-only: BQ-PTP-ANCHOR-REWRITE (MEDIUM, decision pending) — optional non-reverse anchor rewrites in `PTP_Reverse_Scoring_Review.docx`, mutually exclusive with the flag flip. **Historical rescore (BQ-PTP-REVERSE-BACKFILL — DONE):** SQL in-place method (parity gate → backfill the 7 snapshots → set-based UPDATE of dimension_scores/overall_profile → DELETE facet_interpretations → reset narrative_status=null → on-view self-heal regen) applied to 15 real result rows / 14 people (parity 73/73 dim rows, 0 mismatch). 4 results dropped a DIM-PTP-03 band; 11 shifted within band. **Edge deploy: `generate-all-facets` v6** — added a super-admin + linked-coach branch to the JWT auth path (was owner-only, 403'd super-admin debrief views so the 89-row facet_insights_all master never built); internal-secret + owner paths unchanged, verify_jwt=false. (Self-chain across the 110s budget is unreliable — stalled at 70/89 on the pilot, needs an accordion re-open to resume; prefer server-side fire for batches when the internal secret is reachable.) **One real assessment found at 69/89** (a one-off incomplete submission, the only one in this state; internally consistent on its 69 items) — user to retake. **(4) Assessment-integrity frontend (Cole ran via Lovable; all verified in repo):** submit completeness gate in `AssessmentFlow.tsx` (`allAnswered` with `!= null`, dialog copy, `disabled={submitting || !allAnswered}`) — the durable fix for partial submission (trusts in-memory responses, so does not catch a silent dropped save); reverse-item "read carefully" callout (NO scoring-flip wording, by psychometric rule); PTP acknowledgement normalization note in `assessmentAcknowledgments.ts` (`INST-001:self`, conceptual + anti-compensation instruction, data-driven by instrument+rater). **Server-side completeness guard for calculate-scores: CONSIDERED + DECLINED** (Cole: front-end gate resolves it going forward; do not build unless asked; residual = rare silent dropped-save). **REMAINING (verification tail):** 5 straggler `both` reports one context short of 16 sections + 2 Deidentified reports to reopen; then invalidate/regenerate the 1 affected org's stale caches (5 `org_dashboard_narratives` INST-001 + 3 `org_ptp_delta_narratives`). *v121 - Session 113 CLOSE — **Operations Platform Phase 4 backend COMPLETE: recurring expenses + retainers + credit notes + overpayment-to-credit.** All built via Supabase MCP, every apply_migration followed by execute_sql verification, every functional test run as ONE rolled-back DO block (set_config request.jwt.claims with is_local=true at top, RAISE at end encoding assertion values into the error text → zero document numbers consumed, zero fixture residue). No Lovable spend this session; frontend prompts queued for Cole. **Recurring INVOICES note: the recurring-invoice runner (ops_run_recurring_invoices) + ops_recurring_invoice_daily cron already shipped S108, so that P4 slice is FRONTEND-ONLY (template CRUD + generated-invoices view); the S108 runner ignores its auto_email + auto_charge_stored_card columns by design — auto-email/auto-charge deferred, v1 generates drafts only.** **SHARED FOUNDATION (M2 session113_p4_payment_mode_credit_and_apply_helper):** extended operations.payments_payment_mode_check to add 'credit'; created internal operations._ops_apply_credit_to_invoice(p_org,p_invoice,p_amount,p_reference) SECDEF search_path '' — the single primitive every credit-application path routes through: validates invoice not void/written_off/paid + amount<=outstanding, inserts payment(mode='credit',ref) + payment_allocations row, bumps invoices.amount_paid + flips status (partially_paid/paid) + stamps paid_at, returns new status. **DESIGN LOCKED (do not re-litigate): all credit application — retainer apply, credit-note apply, overpayment, customer-credit apply — reduces the target invoice through a payment + allocation so the existing amount_paid-drives-status / balance_due-generated math stays uniform; the helper is the only writer of those credit payments.** EXECUTE revoked from PUBLIC+anon+authenticated (verified anon=false/auth=false); the SECDEF public RPCs call it as definer. **SLICE 1 recurring expenses (M1 session113_p4_recurring_expense_runner_and_cron):** public.ops_run_recurring_expenses() SECDEF search_path '' mirrors the invoice runner (one generation per run; advances next_run_date by frequency×interval_count; deactivates when advanced past end_date; inserts a non-billable overhead expense — recurring_expense_templates has no customer/project/billable columns). REVOKE PUBLIC+anon+authenticated, GRANT service_role (cron runs as postgres superuser, bypasses grants). Cron ops_recurring_expense_daily jobid 26, '15 0 * * *', active. Test PASSED (rolled back): runner returned 1, 1 expense created, next_run advanced one month. **SLICE 2 retainers (M3 session113_p4_retainer_rpcs):** ops_create_retainer(jsonb) (numbered RET-, draft), ops_set_retainer_status(uuid,text) (mark_sent draft→sent; mark_paid draft|sent→paid; mark_refunded paid→refunded only when applied_amount=0 — partial-after-application refund deferred), ops_apply_retainer_to_invoice(uuid,uuid,numeric) (requires status=paid + same-customer invoice + amount<=available_balance; helper; bumps applied_amount; flips to 'applied' when drained). Test PASSED: create→mark_sent→mark_paid→apply $400 (invoice partially_paid, retainer paid)→apply $600 (invoice paid, retainer applied). **SLICE 3 credit notes (M4 session113_p4_credit_note_rpcs):** ops_create_credit_note(jsonb,jsonb) (numbered CN-, polymorphic document_lines on 'credit_note', total=subtotal+tax, validates associated_invoice_id same customer), ops_set_credit_note_status(uuid,text) (issue draft→open; void draft|open→void only when amount_applied=0 AND amount_refunded=0), ops_apply_credit_note_to_invoice(uuid,uuid,numeric) (requires open + same customer + amount<=balance; helper; closes when balance hits 0), ops_refund_credit_note(uuid,numeric) (offline refund vs remaining balance; Stripe refund stays Cole-side; closes when drained). Credit notes apply DIRECTLY from their own generated balance — customer_credits is NOT used for credit notes (no double-ledger). Test PASSED: issue→apply $100→refund $50→apply $150 (CN closed at balance 0); void-on-drained correctly blocked. **SLICE 4 overpayment-to-credit + customer-credit apply (M5 session113_p4_overpayment_to_credit_and_apply):** ops_record_payment(uuid,jsonb) body-only CREATE OR REPLACE (same signature, no overload) — now allocates least(amount,balance) to the invoice, stamps payments.excess_amount, and INSERTs customer_credits(source_type='overpayment', source_id=payment_id, amount=excess) when amount>balance; ops_apply_customer_credit_to_invoice(uuid,uuid,numeric) draws the wallet down via the helper, sets applied_at when drained. (payments.excess_amount + customer_credits.source_type were pre-wired S108.) Test PASSED: $300 on $200 invoice → invoice paid + $100 customer_credit minted; apply $80 of credit to $80 invoice → paid, credit balance $20; over-apply $50 of remaining $20 blocked. **GRANT/RECONCILER FINDING (extends §152):** all 8 new authenticated-only public RPCs (retainer create/status/apply + credit-note create/status/apply/refund + customer-credit apply) still read anon=true after explicit REVOKE FROM anon — tried same-migration, dedicated follow-up (session113_p4_revoke_anon_explicit_followup), AND single-function isolated (session113_p4_revoke_anon_retry_isolated); none held. ops_record_payment (OLD function, standalone revoke session113_p4_revoke_anon_record_payment) → anon=false HELD; ops_run_recurring_expenses (NEW, revoked from anon+authenticated+PUBLIC) → anon=false HELD; ops_mint_public_token (prior session) → anon=false HELD. **Conclusion: the Supabase grant reconciler re-applies the default anon EXECUTE grant to any newly-created, authenticated-EXPOSED public-schema function within the SAME session it is created; the explicit anon revoke only persists once the function has baselined into a prior session's migration (matches the §152 mint precedent, which worked a session later). Cannot revoke authenticated on the 8 (the frontend calls them as the authenticated super-admin). NOT a security gap — every one self-gates on current_org_id() returning NULL for anon and raises 'no operations org for current user' before any data access, identical to the posture ops_create_estimate has shipped with since S112.** S114 OPENER: re-run the 8-function anon revoke (it will hold once baselined) + verify. **FRONTEND SHIPPED + VERIFIED (5 Lovable prompts, Cole ran; every file verified against the live repo; tsc clean):** P1 retainers (list/create/detail + mark_sent/mark_paid/mark_refunded + apply-to-invoice) + NEW shared ApplyToInvoiceDialog.tsx + 4 _shared StatusBadge keys (applied/refunded/open/closed); P2 credit notes (list/create-with-lines/detail + issue/void/apply/refund); P3 recurring expenses (list + create/edit dialog, direct opsSupabase CRUD) + operations-types.ts Insert org_id made optional on BOTH recurring template tables; P4 recurring invoices (list + template form serializing template_lines jsonb + detail with generated-invoices view on invoices.parent_recurring_id); P5 overpayment-to-credit (RecordPaymentDialog over-balance block removed, inline excess→credit note + customer-credits invalidation) + Account Credits card on OperationsCustomerDetail (apply via ApplyToInvoiceDialog → ops_apply_customer_credit_to_invoice). All reads via opsSupabase; all public RPCs via the default supabase client as supabase.rpc('name' as any,…), so public types.ts needs NO hand-edit (Lovable regenerates it on sync). Routes + AppSidebar entries (Wallet / FileMinus2 / Repeat×2) all additive; existing invoice/estimate files untouched. **TWO BACKEND ADDS beyond M1–M5 this session:** (M7) session113_p4_recurring_template_org_created_defaults — org_id DEFAULT operations.current_org_id() + created_by DEFAULT auth.uid() on both recurring template tables so the dialogs omit them (mirrors customers/expenses); operations-types.ts Insert org_id flipped to optional to match. (M8) session113_p4_recurring_invoice_runner_derive_line_total — **bug fix caught by backend-first verification, NOT by tsc (latent until the cron generates):** the S108 ops_run_recurring_invoices runner reads line_total off each template_lines element, but the form serializes {item_id,description,quantity,unit_price,discount_amount} with NO line_total, so generated invoices would have come out $0; the runner now derives line_total = COALESCE(line_total, quantity*unit_price - discount_amount) for both the per-line value and the subtotal. Verified via rolled-back DO block: template lines without line_total → generated invoice subtotal 120.00, lines 90.00 + 30.00, then rolled back (no invoice, no number consumed). **COLE-SIDE STILL PENDING (carried):** subscribe the ops Stripe endpoint to charge.refunded (operations.stripe_webhook_events still has only the checkout event); GA sales-tax CPA confirmation. **A2 ROADMAP:** P1 money DONE(109), P2 time+expenses+charges DONE(110), S111 invoicing depth, P3 estimates+public-pay DONE(112), **P4 recurring/retainers/credit-notes/overpayment COMPLETE(113) — backend + frontend shipped + verified.** P2-extras still open (weekly time grid, live timer UI, calendar, project members for staff-hours, actual-vs-budget dashboard). Edge fn versions UNCHANGED from 112 close (no Edge Function work this session): ops-invoice-send v2, ops-public-invoice-checkout v1, ops-estimate-send v1, ops-invoice-checkout v1, ops-stripe-webhook v3, ops-payment-receipt v1, ops-payment-reminder-cron v1. Close artifacts (markdown only, Session-74): this v121 entry, architecture-reference v115, session-113-to-114.md.*

*v120 - Session 112 CLOSE — **Operations Platform Phase 3 COMPLETE: estimates + accept-by-link + convert + Option A public customer pay page + estimate-send email.** All backend built + verified via Supabase MCP (apply_migration always followed by execute_sql verification; functional tests run as ONE execute_sql DO block with set_config request.jwt.claims then RAISE to roll back — zero document numbers consumed, zero fixture residue); Cole ran all six Lovable prompts; every shipped file verified against the live repo after (incl. blob-SHA regression check on the invoice files). **BACKEND SHIPPED + VERIFIED.** (1) **Shared public tokenized-access model.** operations.public_access_tokens (HASH-ONLY sha256, RLS on, 4 policies, anon has NO table grant) + ops_mint_public_token (AUTHENTICATED-ONLY) + ops_get_public_document_by_token + ops_accept_estimate_by_token + ops_decline_estimate_by_token (the latter three anon-callable SECDEF, search_path ''). Decisions locked (do not re-litigate): HASH-ONLY storage (raw 32-byte/64-hex token returned once by mint, never recoverable; re-send mints a fresh link and revokes the prior one, so the previously-emailed link goes dead); NO hard expiry (gated on document status — invoice read hidden when void/written_off, estimate read hidden when draft, accept only from sent/viewed); convert allowed from accepted OR sent/viewed. Verified in one rolled-back DO block: mint→64-char token; public read returns the SANITIZED bundle (no email/address/internals) and flips sent→viewed; accept idempotent (already_accepted); re-mint revokes prior (old→null / new→ok); garbage token→null; voided invoice→null. (2) **Estimate CRUD + convert.** ops_create_estimate / ops_update_estimate / ops_set_estimate_status (mark_sent|mark_accepted|mark_declined|mark_expired) / ops_convert_estimate_to_invoice — mirror the invoice RPCs minus shipping (total = subtotal − discount + adjustment; expiration_date instead of due_date/payment_terms; document_lines polymorphic on document_type='estimate'; numbering type 'estimate' EST-YYYY-NNNN; all authenticated-only). Verified end to end (rolled back): EST-2026-0001 created (total 250 after discount), updated (300, lines replaced), mark_sent, convert→INV-2026-0004 with copied line + estimate flipped invoiced + converted_invoice_id match, double-convert blocked; numbering then restored (estimate next 1, invoice next 4). (3) **Edge layer.** ops_get_invoice_checkout_bundle_by_token (SECDEF, anon, FULL checkout shape incl. email/address/stripe id/tax codes, token-gated — the public pay page checkout uses THIS, not the sanitized read) + ops_get_estimate_send_bundle (SECDEF, org-gated, authenticated) + NEW ops-public-invoice-checkout v1 (verify_jwt FALSE, token-authorized, success/cancel → /pay/:token) + NEW ops-estimate-send v1 (verify_jwt true, mints accept token via user client, emails respond link → /estimate/:token, marks draft sent, from estimates@) + ops-invoice-send v1→v2 (mints pay token, button now /pay/{token}). HTTP-probed with the anon key: public-checkout reaches the handler unauthenticated and returns 400 on missing token / 404 on unknown token (proves verify_jwt:false routing + the token gate); ops-estimate-send + ops-invoice-send 401 without auth. **THE SUPABASE-DEFAULT-PRIVILEGES GOTCHA (now §152):** REVOKE EXECUTE ... FROM PUBLIC did NOT drop anon from ops_mint_public_token — Supabase ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES grants EXECUTE directly to the named anon role, so a second migration REVOKE ... FROM anon was required. Verify with has_function_privilege('anon', oid, 'EXECUTE'). Migrations: session112_p3_public_access_token_model, session112_p3_mint_revoke_anon_explicit, session112_p3_estimate_crud_and_convert, session112_p3_checkout_bundle_by_token, session112_p3_estimate_send_bundle. **FRONTEND SHIPPED (6 Lovable prompts) + VERIFIED against the repo:** (1) _shared.tsx StatusBadge +4 estimate keys (accepted/declined/invoiced/expired, additive). (2) EstimateForm.tsx + /operations/estimates/new + :id/edit (InvoiceForm minus shipping, with expiration_date; ops_create_estimate/ops_update_estimate on the default client; reads via opsSupabase). (3) OperationsEstimates.tsx list + /operations/estimates + AppSidebar "Estimates" entry (FileText icon confirmed imported). (4) OperationsEstimateDetail.tsx + /operations/estimates/:id — Convert-to-invoice (accepted/sent/viewed), Send-to-customer (ops-estimate-send), Mark sent/accepted/declined/expired via ops_set_estimate_status; no clone/payment/pay-now/delete. (5) PublicInvoicePay.tsx at /pay/:token (anon, noindex, ops_get_public_document_by_token + ops-public-invoice-checkout → window.location.href, ?paid=1/?canceled=1). (6) PublicEstimateRespond.tsx at /estimate/:token (anon, noindex, accept/decline with optional reason). Verification: App.tsx routes ordered list→new→:id/edit→:id with BOTH public routes OUTSIDE ProtectedRoute; client split respected (opsSupabase reads / default supabase for public RPCs + functions.invoke); InvoiceForm.tsx + OperationsInvoiceDetail.tsx blob SHAs byte-identical to pre-build (no invoice file touched except _shared.tsx). **PRODUCTION-PUBLISH LESSON:** the /pay and /estimate links 404'd at first because Lovable "ship" updates repo + preview but the custom domain serves the last PUBLISHED build; confirmed by grepping the live bundle (lacked the new route strings, had the old ones). After Cole published, both links resolved. Diagnostic kept: fetch the live index.html → its hashed /assets/index-*.js → grep new route path strings vs known-old to distinguish stale-deploy from code-bug. **SMOKE TESTS (Cole):** estimate accept and invoice pay both worked end to end after publish. Re-send-revokes-prior is backend-verified (re-mint test + ops-invoice-send mints through the same RPC); "re-send" in the UI is just Send again (no separate control). One caveat surfaced for Cole: testing revoke cleanly needs an UNPAID invoice (a paid invoice's pay page shows "paid in full" regardless of token freshness). **DEFERRED (by design, not built):** delete-draft-estimate (no delete RPC; drafts editable), estimate PDF, partial accept, sitemap entry for the public pages (intentionally noindex, out of sitemap). **COLE-SIDE STILL PENDING (carried):** subscribe the ops Stripe endpoint to charge.refunded (operations.stripe_webhook_events still has only the checkout event); GA sales-tax CPA confirmation. **A2 ROADMAP:** P1 money DONE (109), P2 time+expenses+charges DONE (110), S111 invoicing depth, **P3 estimates + accept-by-link + convert + public pay page DONE (112). P4 NEXT: recurring invoices + retainers + credit notes + overpayment-to-credit + recurring expenses** — note the recurring BACKEND foundation already exists from S108 (recurring_invoice_templates + ops_recurring_invoice_daily cron + overdue/reminder logic), so P4 is more frontend-weight than P1–P3. P2 EXTRAS still open as separate slices (weekly time grid, live timer UI, calendar, project members for staff-hours, actual-vs-budget dashboard) and could be pulled forward instead — Cole's call. Edge fn versions at close: ops-invoice-send v2, ops-public-invoice-checkout v1, ops-estimate-send v1, ops-invoice-checkout v1, ops-stripe-webhook v3, ops-payment-receipt v1, ops-payment-reminder-cron v1; platform stripe-webhook v31 untouched. Close artifacts (markdown only, Session-74): this v120 entry, architecture-reference v114, session-112-to-113.md.*

*v119 - Session 111 CLOSE — Operations Platform invoicing depth: edit/delete for billable inputs + customer-level "invoice from work" (Way B) + invoice detail levels with project-name line labels. All backend via Supabase MCP (apply_migration always followed by execute_sql verification; functional tests run as one execute_sql DO block with set_config request.jwt.claims then RAISE to roll back, so no document number is consumed); Cole ran all Lovable prompts; every Lovable plan verified against the live repo before approval and every shipped file verified against the repo after. **(1) Edit + delete for time entries, expenses, and project tasks (frontend-only — backend already permitted via existing UPDATE/DELETE RLS).** The gap was insert-only dialogs. LogExpenseDialog + LogTimeDialog gained an edit branch (record prop → update); OperationsProjectDetail gained explicit Edit/Delete actions gated to uninvoiced rows (is_invoiced=false; invoiced rows stay read-only until void/delete releases them), FK-aware task delete (a task with logged time can't delete; Postgres 23503 surfaced as a friendly message), and receipt cleanup from operations-receipts storage on expense delete. Plan-review catch: Lovable's plan extended the time query select but NOT the expenses query select, which would have silently dropped category/markup/mileage/receipt/notes on edit and broken receipt cleanup on delete — required extending expensesQ select (expense_category_id, markup_percentage, miles_driven, per_mile_rate, receipt_storage_path, notes) before approving. **(2) Way B — customer-level "invoice from work."** New operations.invoiceable_candidates (security_invoker VIEW, 14 cols: candidate_type/org_id/customer_id/project_id/project_name/ref_id/ref_user_id/label/quantity/unit/unit_price/amount/currency_code/candidate_key; GRANT SELECT authenticated) returns one selectable row per charge, per expense, per rated task, per project (project-rate), and per person (staff-rate; null amount when no rate). New public.ops_create_invoice_from_selection(p_customer, p_selection jsonb) — SECDEF search_path '', recomputes amounts server-side, validates each id against customer+org+billable+uninvoiced, blocks mixed currencies, sets invoice.project_id to the lone project or NULL when the selection spans projects, raises on stale items. p_selection {charge_ids[], expense_ids[], task_ids[], project_time_ids[], staff_time:[{project_id,user_id}]}. New page InvoiceFromWork.tsx (opsSupabase for candidates, default supabase for the RPC; ?customer= lock or picker; grouped by project; single-currency guard; no-rate staff disabled) at /operations/invoices/from-work; entry buttons on the customer detail page and the invoices list. Existing delete/void source-release covers selection-built invoices unchanged. **(3) Invoice detail levels + project-name line labels.** Decision: line descriptions carry the project name prefixed (separator " - ", a hyphen, not an em dash); two detail levels chosen at generation time — Itemized (default; one line per task/charge/expense, "Project - Item") and Summary by project (one line per project = rolled-up total). Selector on BOTH invoice surfaces (the Way B page and the per-project Generate dialog). New shared helper public._ops_emit_from_sel(p_org, p_invoice, p_detail, p_uid) RETURNS numeric (SECDEF search_path '') reads a pg_temp._sel component temp table the composer populates, emits itemized or summary lines, and flips all source rows; summary total equals itemized total to the penny by construction (sums the same rounded components). Both composers DROP+CREATE with an added p_detail text DEFAULT 'itemized' (overload trap, §59/§125). Existing callers omitting p_detail still work; per-project Generate descriptions now carry the project-name prefix (intended). Helper EXECUTE revoked from PUBLIC, authenticated, AND anon — only the SECDEF composers (running as definer) call it; closes a multi-tenant hole where an authenticated user could craft their own temp table and manipulate invoices. Functional test (rolled back) covered both composers × both modes: itemized produced project-prefixed lines, summary produced one line per project, totals penny-consistent (1205.00 and 755.00). Frontend: detail selector on InvoiceFromWork + the OperationsProjectDetail Generate dialog; public types.ts Args gained p_detail?: string on both RPCs (the operations Functions block stays empty — these are public-schema RPCs called via the default client). **(4) P6 scope note (recorded, not built).** Invoice email currently sends a branded view-and-pay link only (no PDF; the PDF engine is Phase 6). Folded into P6: when the email sends, attach receipts for the expenses on that invoice (resolve via document_lines.source_expense_ids → expenses.receipt_storage_path) alongside the PDF. **Numbering:** invoice next_number = 4 = max+1 (real invoices TEST-LIVE-1USD-001 paid, INV-2026-0001 draft, INV-2026-0002 sent, INV-2026-0003 draft — advanced by Cole's real UI testing; all Claude functional tests rolled back, zero fixture residue). Standing rule held: never reset to 1; restore to max+1 after number-consuming tests. **COLE-SIDE STILL PENDING (carried):** subscribe the ops Stripe endpoint to charge.refunded (operations.stripe_webhook_events still has only the one checkout event); GA sales-tax CPA confirmation. **MCP patterns reinforced:** temp-table emission under search_path '' via pg_temp.-qualified refs; schema default privileges auto-grant EXECUTE to authenticated/anon on new public functions, so internal SECDEF helpers must be explicitly REVOKEd; functional-test rollback-via-RAISE consumes no document number. **A2 ROADMAP:** P1 money path DONE (109); P2 time+expenses+charges into billing DONE (110); Session 111 added edit/delete + Way B + invoice detail levels (P2-adjacent invoicing depth). **P3 NEXT: estimates + accept-by-link + convert + Option A public customer pay page (public /pay/:token tokenized read + on-demand Stripe checkout, repoint the invoice-email button; when /pay ships, add it to newsletter-sitemap STATIC_ROUTES).** Close artifacts (markdown only, Session-74): this v119 entry, architecture-reference v113, session-111-to-112.md.*

*v118b - Session 110 PARALLEL PLATFORM FIX (run alongside the Operations Phase 2 session; main-platform, NOT operations) — two unrelated production bugs root-caused + fixed, both verified live. No build-queue feature work. Touched: platform `stripe-webhook` (live v31), `generate-facet-interpretations` (live v44, redeployed), `CoachInvoices.tsx`, one production data backfill. **VERSION RECONCILIATION:** v117/v111 (Session 109 close) recorded platform `stripe-webhook` as "v31, untouched"; live is v31 AND now carries the comp fix below, so that earlier label was off by one (the pre-fix webhook was v30; this fix is the deploy that produced v31). Treat live `get_edge_function` as truth; the "untouched" note in v117/v111 is superseded for this function only. **ISSUE 1 — PTP combined-context narrative not rendering (RESOLVED).** One client's PTP report showed no Combined sections; the Combined-tab self-heal kept firing and the combined invoke 500'd (~15-19s, post-AI). Root cause: `parseAiJson` in `generate-facet-interpretations` threw on malformed model JSON (an unescaped character mid-string in a long `profile_overview` value) — NOT truncation; `max_tokens` (4000 on combined paths) was irrelevant. Deterministic for that client's combined input; her personal + professional parsed fine. Fix (deployed, confirmed): added a `jsonrepair@3.14.0` fallback inside `parseAiJson` — on a parse throw it attempts `JSON.parse(jsonrepair(candidate))` before re-throwing the original error. All four combined `section_type` rows then wrote well-formed (`profile_overview_combined`, `personal_summary_combined`, `dimension_highlights_combined`, `cross_and_action_combined`). DURABLE NOTE: jsonrepair is a safety net, not the real fix; the combined paths still emit free-form JSON. **ISSUE 2 — comped client charged on a super-admin coach invite (RESOLVED).** A super admin sent a free (comp-coupon) coach invite for PTP, but the invited client saw the self-pay $29.99 screen instead of the free path. Root cause: a $0 Stripe Checkout produces NO PaymentIntent, so `stripe-webhook` wrote NULL into `coach_clients.stripe_payment_intent_id`; the client "coach paid" gate in `InstrumentSelection.tsx` (~109-122) keys on `stripe_payment_intent_id IS NOT NULL` (+ `invitation_status IN ('sent','opened','partially_completed')`) read through `coach_clients_client_view`, so NULL dropped the client into self-pay. Fix A (webhook, live v31, verify_jwt false): in `checkout.session.completed`, added `const coachPaymentRef = (session.payment_intent as string | null) ?? (isSuperAdminComp ? \`comp_${session.id}\` : null);` and replaced `session.payment_intent as string` with `coachPaymentRef` in the four inserts inside the `coach_order` + `coach_bulk_order` branches ONLY (two `coach_clients`, two `assessment_purchases`); `payment` (self-pay) + `subscription` branches untouched. Real PIs start `pi_`; the comp sentinel starts `comp_`. Fix B (frontend, deployed): `CoachInvoices.tsx` listed every coach_clients row with a non-null PI x per-assessment price, so a comp would show as a fake N x $29.99 invoice on the coach's own page; added `.not("stripe_payment_intent_id","ilike","comp_%")` to the coach-scoped query (real `pi_` rows kept, `comp_` excluded from list + total). Backfill (production data, forward-only fix did not touch the existing row): set `coach_clients.stripe_payment_intent_id` to a `comp_`-prefixed sentinel (referred to generically as `comp_manual_<client>`) for the one affected client, row guarded on `IS NULL` so it could not clobber; verified the row now passes the coach-paid gate and is hidden from CoachInvoices; client confirmed the free path works. **FOLLOW-UPS THAT EMERGED (none started):** (1) move the three combined narrative paths in `generate-facet-interpretations` to Anthropic tool-use (typed JSON) to eliminate the malformed-JSON class — jsonrepair is only a safety net. (2) `calculate-scores` stamps `narrative_status='failed'` on any facet-invoke rejection even when content lands fine; make the status write content-aware, and consider switching the fan-out to raw fetch + `x-internal-secret` rather than `admin.functions.invoke` (suspected auth/transport failure under signing-keys mode) — THIS OVERLAPS the already-tracked BQ-NARRATIVE-FANOUT-STATUS (v109 line-388 finding) + BQ-FANOUT-COLDFAIL (v105); treat as the same item, now with a proposed transport fix. (3) if a refund worker is ever added, gate it on `amount_paid > 0` or `stripe_payment_intent_id LIKE 'pi_%'` before calling `stripe.refunds.create`, so `comp_` sentinels never reach Stripe. (4) optional cleaner design (tradeoff, not required): replace the overloaded `stripe_payment_intent_id` sentinel with a dedicated `is_comp` flag (or amount column) on `coach_clients` + update the gate and CoachInvoices to read it — avoids overloading a Stripe-id field, costs a migration + frontend change.*

*v118 - Session 110 CLOSE — **Operations Platform Phase 2 COMPLETE: time + expenses + charges into billing.** All backend built + verified via Supabase MCP (apply_migration always followed by execute_sql verification; JWT simulated with set_config request.jwt.claims in the same call); Cole ran all four Lovable prompts (additive, type-check clean, batched to one untested test pass). **MODEL REVISION — single-mode billing replaced by COMPOSABLE billing (do not re-litigate).** The v117 locked model had projects.billing_method as ONE of fixed/project_hours/task_hours/staff_hours. Cole needs projects that simultaneously carry a fixed fee + hourly tasks + expenses, so: billing_method now names the HOURLY scheme ONLY (project_hours|task_hours|staff_hours|none); fixed fees become project_charges rows; expenses are always independent. Chose Option B (separate project_charges table) over Option A (flag-on-project). ALTERed projects_billing_method_check to add 'none' ('fixed' kept valid for safety; 0 projects existed so no data migration). **BACKEND SHIPPED + VERIFIED (7 migrations):** (1) operations.project_time_rollup VIEW (security_invoker, project grain: org_id/customer_id/customer_name/project_id/project_name/entry_count/total_hours/billable_hours/nonbillable_hours/invoiced_hours/uninvoiced_hours/unbilled_billable_hours; GRANT SELECT authenticated) — step-2 rollup chosen as a SQL view (Cole approved over an RPC); FILTER math verified. (2) expenses + expense_categories gained org_id DEFAULT operations.current_org_id() + created_by DEFAULT auth.uid(). (3) private storage bucket operations-receipts + 4 org-path-scoped RLS policies on storage.objects (bucket_id + (storage.foldername(name))[1] = current_org_id()::text); seeded 6 expense_categories (Software, Travel, Meals & Entertainment, Office Supplies, Contractor, Other; idempotent, only if org has none). (4) operations.project_charges table (org_id default current_org_id(), project_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE, customer_id nullable, date default today, description NOT NULL, amount default 0, currency_code default USD, is_billable default true, is_invoiced default false, invoice_line_id, notes, timestamps, created_by default auth.uid(), updated_by) + 4 RLS policies (select/insert/update same-org, delete admin-only) + GRANT authenticated. (5) document_lines.source_charge_ids uuid[] added; public.ops_create_invoice_from_project(p_project, p_date_from, p_date_to) RETURNS uuid (new draft id), SECURITY DEFINER search_path '', GRANT authenticated — composes, for only is_billable AND NOT is_invoiced rows in range: charges -> one free_form line each; time per billing_method [task_hours: one line per task that HAS a rate, untasked/rate-less time EXCLUDED and left uninvoiced; project_hours: one line at project_hourly_rate, excluded if null; staff_hours: one line per user at coalesce(project_users.billing_rate, users.default_billing_rate), RAISE EXCEPTION if any billable user lacks a rate; fixed/none: no time billed]; expenses -> one line each at amount x (1 + markup%/100); sets invoice.project_id + per-line source_* arrays, flips consumed rows is_invoiced=true + invoice_line_id; RAISES "nothing billable" (rolls back, releasing the consumed number) if 0 lines. Verified end-to-end with a fixture (INV-2026-0002, subtotal/total 755.00, 3 lines Deposit 500 / Build-time 200 / Vendor 55, correct exclusions + source links). (6) Patched ops_delete_draft_invoice AND ops_set_invoice_status (void branch) to RELEASE sources (is_invoiced=false, invoice_line_id=NULL on time_entries/expenses/project_charges whose invoice_line_id belongs to that invoice's document_lines) BEFORE delete/void; write_off intentionally does NOT release (invoice stands); mark_sent unchanged. Verified: delete-draft AND void both revert all three source types; voided invoice remains as a record. All fixtures cleaned; numbering restored to next_number=2 (real invoices present: TEST-LIVE-1USD-001 paid + INV-2026-0001 $150 draft). **LOVABLE PROMPTS (all four built, untested):** Step1+2 time logging + rollups (LogTimeDialog + Time card on project detail via users!time_entries_user_id_fkey disambiguated embed + customer-time rollup card); Step3 expenses (LogExpenseDialog with mileage mode + receipt upload to operations-receipts at `${orgId}/${uuid}-${name}` + Expenses card); Step4 charges + composable + generate (ProjectFormDialog billing_method union -> project_hours|task_hours|staff_hours|none default none, Fixed-cost field removed, legacy 'fixed' coerced to none; AddChargeDialog; Charges card + Generate-invoice date-range dialog calling supabase.rpc('ops_create_invoice_from_project') then navigate to the draft). operations-types.ts edits: org_id optional on expenses.Insert + expense_categories.Insert, project_charges table type, document_lines.source_charge_ids, project_time_rollup view type, and the public ops_create_invoice_from_project Functions entry. **CUSTOMER-PAY GAP DISCOVERED + slotted to P3.** ops-invoice-send sends a branded Resend email whose "View & Pay Invoice" button links to /operations/invoices/{id} — but that route is RoleGuard brainwise_super_admin gated, so external customers cannot pay from the email today. The Stripe machinery (ops_get_invoice_checkout_bundle, create-checkout, webhook) is all built; only a public landing page is missing. DECISION: Option A (public /pay/:token page + tokenized read + on-demand Stripe checkout, repoint the email button) — approved, slotted into Phase 3 (not pulled forward). Option B (direct Stripe-hosted URL in email) rejected. **A2 ROADMAP UPDATED:** P1 money path DONE (109). **P2 time+expenses+charges into billing DONE (110).** P2 EXTRAS still open as separate later slices (NOT done): weekly time grid, live timer UI, calendar view, project members for staff-hours, project actual-vs-budget/margin dashboard. P3 estimates + accept-by-link + convert + (NEW) Option A public customer pay page. P4 recurring invoices + retainers + credit notes + overpayment-to-credit + recurring expenses (recurring expenses deferred here from P2 per Cole). P5 reports (~11 views + UI + CSV/PDF). P6 PDF engine 3 templates + branding. P7 settings/automation + activity log/comments. P8 customer depth + Zoho migration. P9 QA/polish/deploy. **COLE-SIDE PENDING:** (1) STILL OPEN from 109 — subscribe the ops Stripe endpoint to charge.refunded (operations.stripe_webhook_events still has only the one checkout.session.completed event; definitive test = issue the parked $1 refund and watch for a charge.refunded row + invoice reopening). (2) Run the one-pass Phase 2 test: create project (form shows "No hourly billing", no Fixed-cost field) -> add rated task -> log time -> add expense (mileage + markup + receipt) -> add charge -> Generate invoice (draft, one line per piece, sources flip to Invoiced) -> delete/void (sources revert to Unbilled). **STATIC_ROUTES reminder still standing** (newsletter-sitemap hardcoded marketing-route list — when a new public marketing page or the P3 public /pay page ships, STATIC_ROUTES needs the ~10-second manual edit). **SESSION 111 OPENS:** Cole runs the Phase 2 test pass; on green, begin Phase 3 (estimates + accept-by-link + convert + Option A public pay page). Close artifacts (markdown only, Session-74): this v118 entry, architecture-reference v112, session-110-to-111.md.*

*v117 - Session 109 CLOSE — **Operations platform went frontend + Phase 1 money path COMPLETE.** Both pre-frontend gates passed at open (operations schema exposed; $1 live-Stripe end-to-end test — checkout to ops-stripe-webhook to payment recorded to invoice paid, Stripe Tax $0 correct pending GA registration). Target locked = A2 (all 14 Doc 1 v1 modules), ~5.5-8mo, backend-first by phase. **LOCKED PRODUCT MODEL (do not re-litigate):** customer to invoice via nested in-context creation — a customer has many named projects; project.billing_method one of fixed (fixed_cost_amount) / project_hours (project_hourly_rate) / task_hours (rate on each project_tasks.task_hourly_rate) / staff_hours (rate on project_users.billing_rate); tasks (project_tasks) are the rate-bearing unit for task-hours and are NOT items (items = separate fixed-price catalog for invoice/estimate lines, no FK, time logs against a task never an item); time logs against project (+optional task), rolls up to project then customer, invoiced on any cadence. UX = in-context modals, multiple intuitive entry points OK, customer-detail is the hub + project-detail a sub-hub, avoid orphan pages. **A2 PHASED ROADMAP:** done = foundation + Customers(basic) + Items + Invoices(create/edit free-form+item lines + Stripe Pay Now) + Projects-to-Tasks spine + Phase 1. P1 (DONE) money path; P2 (NEXT) time+expenses into billing (time logging modal/grid/timer/calendar, expenses+mileage+recurring, project members staff-hours+cost, project dashboard, ops_create_invoice_from_project RPC); P3 estimates+accept-by-link+convert; P4 recurring+retainers+credit-notes(+overpayment-to-credit); P5 reports (~11 more views + UI + CSV/PDF); P6 PDF engine 3 templates + branding (minimal invoice PDF may pull into P1); P7 settings/automation + activity log/comments; P8 customer depth + Zoho migration; P9 QA/polish/deploy. **PHASE 1 BACKEND (verified/deployed via MCP):** ops_record_payment(p_invoice,p_payment jsonb) — offline payment record+allocate+update amount_paid/status, partial supported, guards void/written-off + over-balance, overpayment-to-credit deferred to P4; verified partial ach + remainder check to paid. ops_set_invoice_status(p_id,p_action) mark_sent/void/write_off with guards, org via current_org_id(). ops_clone_invoice(p_id) copies header+lines to fresh-numbered draft (verified). ops_delete_draft_invoice(p_id) draft-only (verified). ops_handle_stripe_refund(p_event_id,p_event_type,p_payload,p_payment_intent,p_amount_refunded) — idempotent, finds payment by stripe_payment_intent_id, reduces invoice amount_paid by refund delta, reopens status + clears paid_at, records refunded_amount, granted service_role; verified full-refund reopen. payments.payment_mode CHECK += ach (now stripe_card|stripe_ach|ach|check|cash|wire|other). org_id/created_by defaults added to projects, project_tasks, time_entries (time_entries also user_id default auth.uid()); each direct-CRUD table's Insert-type org_id made optional in operations-types.ts. **EDGE FNS:** ops-stripe-webhook v2 to v3 — added charge.refunded branch AHEAD of the source guard (refunds identified by payment-intent lookup since the charge object lacks session metadata); checkout.session.completed handler + metadata.source==='operations' guard unchanged; verify_jwt stays false. ops-payment-receipt v1 NEW (Resend, emails total/paid-to-date/balance receipt, verify_jwt true). ops-invoice-send (pre-existing v1) confirmed complete. Platform stripe-webhook (v31) NEVER touched. **CRON:** ops_overdue_flag_daily (00:30, ops_flag_overdue_invoices) already active; ops_recurring_invoice_daily + ops_payment_reminder_daily also already scheduled for later phases. **FRONTEND SLICES (Cole ran prompts, type-check clean):** customer create/edit dialog, items module, invoice create/edit form (RPC-backed + item picker), projects+tasks spine (project create from customer hub + project detail with tasks). Three more invoice-detail prompts delivered to run in order: Record payment dialog, lifecycle Actions dropdown, Send invoice/Send receipt items. **NUMBERING-DRIFT FIX:** the reset-to-1-after-test habit had desynced the invoice scheme vs Cole's real INV-2026-0001 ($150 draft, UI-created); fixed next_number=2 + last_reset_at=now (no yearly snap-back until 2027). RULE GOING FORWARD: never reset numbering to 1; restore to max+1 after any test. All test invoices cleaned up. **COLE-SIDE:** (1) subscribe the ops Stripe endpoint to charge.refunded or the handler never fires; (2) run the 3 invoice-detail UI prompts in order; (3) GA sales-tax CPA confirmation + Sales-and-Use registration if taxable. **PARKED:** $1 live refund (optional); TEST-LIVE-1USD-001 + real INV-2026-0001 $150 draft live in operations.invoices (Cole's data). **SESSION 110 OPENS ON Phase 2:** time logging against a task (Log-time modal writing time_entries; defaults auto-fill org/user/created_by + land approved-uninvoiced), then project unbilled rollup, then expenses, then ops_create_invoice_from_project.
