*v116 - Session 108 CLOSE — **Zoho Operations Platform Wave 1 / Doc 1 build START. Backend-first; Phase 0 foundation shipped + verified via Supabase MCP; zero Lovable spend.** Session-open read Doc 1 (invoice) + Doc 5 (cross-cutting) from the repo, verified scope against live state, closed 5 gaps, got A/B/C approval, then built Phase 0. **Live-state probe findings (corrections to Doc 5's reuse assumptions):** no `operations` schema (greenfield); pg_cron/pg_net/pgcrypto present; notification system is `notification_types_catalog` + `user_notifications` + `user_notification_preferences` with fns `notify_user`/`compose_notification_email`/`notification_display` (Doc 5 §3.3 called it `notification_catalog` — wrong name); `public.custom_field_definitions` does NOT exist (Doc 5 §3.4 wrongly said "already partially scoped"); no `public.audit_log` (platform has super_admin_audit_log + company_admin_audit_log); NO Stripe mirror tables in public at all (confirms Stripe-as-source-of-truth; ops Stripe tables net-new). **Gaps closed:** (1) single `operations` schema; (2) `operations.organizations` tenant root, org_id never cross-schema FKs to public per Doc 5 §2.3 Rule 1; (3) `operations.users.id` = PK=FK to auth.users, Supabase auth only, Doc 5 four-role enum `admin|sales_user|sales_manager|read_only`; (4=B) custom-fields engine operations-local not shared-public (Phase 4); (5=A) Stripe routing via a SECOND webhook endpoint → `ops-stripe-webhook` + `metadata.source=operations` filter, existing platform stripe-webhook v29/create-checkout v60 untouched, corporate zero-Stripe boundary intact (deferred to Phase 3). **Phase 0 shipped (4 migrations, all verified by execute_sql after apply):** `operations` schema; `operations.user_role` enum; `operations.set_updated_at()`; `operations.organizations` (1 seed row: BrainWise Enterprises); `operations.users` (Cole seeded admin, email sourced from auth.users not hardcoded); `operations.current_org_id()` + `operations.current_user_role()` (SQL STABLE SECURITY DEFINER `search_path=''`, non-recursive, PUBLIC execute revoked then granted authenticated/service_role); core entities `customers` (incl. `platform_organization_id` soft-bridge as plain uuid, no FK), `contact_persons`, `items` — all with org_id NOT NULL FK to operations.organizations, canonical 4-policy RLS (select/insert/update = same org; delete = same org + admin role), org_id+secondary indexes, updated_at triggers. Schema/table privileges to authenticated + service_role only (anon has zero grant, verified). **Verification:** 5 tables all rls_enabled=true; policy counts contact_persons/customers/items=4, users=4, organizations=1; zero cross-schema FK violations; users.id FK→auth.users confirmed; functional RLS proven by simulated JWT — Cole's sub resolves org + role=admin and sees 1 org/1 user/0 customers, a stranger sub resolves NULL org and sees 0/0. `default_tax_rate_id` + computed balance columns intentionally omitted from Phase 0 (added Phase 3/4 when tax_rates/invoices exist). **NOT done (sequenced Phases 1–4 in the Open Queue):** projects/time, documents/invoices, Stripe/payments/tax, reports/settings/custom-fields/migration. **Cole-side deps flagged:** Stripe Tax config, ops Resend sender domains, second Stripe webhook endpoint, and exposing the `operations` schema in Supabase API settings before any Lovable access. Edge fn versions unchanged (check-ai-usage v50, stripe-webhook v29, ai-chat v40, create-checkout v60). *v116 - Session 108 CLOSE — **Operations platform (Zoho replacement, Doc 1 invoicing) backend built end-to-end across Phases 0–3 + Phase 4 partial. All via Supabase MCP, every functional claim verified; no Lovable spend; next session is frontend-first (after Cole exposes the `operations` schema).** Schema decisions locked at open (do not re-litigate): single `operations` schema (dot-notation), `operations.organizations` is the tenant root (Gap 2; no cross-schema FK ops→public per Doc 5 §2.3 Rule 1; one row = BrainWise Enterprises), `operations.users.id` = FK to `auth.users.id` shared auth (Gap 3), custom fields ops-local (Gap 4=B), Stripe isolation under one shared account via a SECOND webhook endpoint + `metadata.source=operations` tagging (Gap 5=A) — platform `stripe-webhook` v29 / `create-checkout` v60 NEVER touched. **~31 tables + 5 report views built** with a canonical RLS pattern: SECURITY DEFINER helpers `operations.current_org_id()` / `operations.current_user_role()` (`search_path=''`, non-recursive); per-table policies filter `org_id = current_org_id()`, DELETE adds `AND current_user_role()='admin'` except line/child tables. RLS proven both directions via simulated JWT. **Migrations M1–M19** (snake_case): foundation+enum+trigger+grants (anon NEVER granted), tenant+users (seeded Cole admin), RLS helpers, core (`customers` w/ `platform_organization_id` plain-uuid bridge + `custom_fields` jsonb, `contact_persons`, `items`), projects family, time_entries (partial-unique running-timer index, `numeric(8,4)` minute precision), numbering engine, document headers (generated `balance_due`/`available_balance`/`balance`; circular estimate↔invoice + credit_note→invoice FKs), polymorphic `document_lines`, line grouping cols, expenses family, money+stripe mapping (`payments`/`payment_allocations`/`customer_credits`/`stripe_customers`/`stripe_webhook_events`), config tables + deferred tax FKs (USD seeded), 5 `security_invoker` report views (AR bucketing verified), `stripe_tax_code` on items+document_lines (MIXED services/digital taxability — each line carries its own code), `ops_handle_stripe_checkout_payment` RPC (idempotent, verified 100/300→partially_paid + duplicate rejected), checkout-support RPCs (`ops_get_invoice_checkout_bundle` SECURITY INVOKER RLS-read, `ops_upsert_stripe_customer` SECDEF), recurring+cron logic (`recurring_invoice_templates`, org-explicit `next_document_number_for_org` so cron works without a JWT, `ops_flag_overdue_invoices` + `ops_run_recurring_invoices` both verified in SQL, `ops_due_payment_reminders` selector), `ops_mark_invoice_sent`. **Edge Functions deployed** (match platform conventions: Stripe `esm.sh/stripe@18.5.0` apiVersion `2025-08-27.basil`, supabase-js `2.57.2`): `ops-stripe-webhook` (verify_jwt false, verifies `OPS_STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, filters `metadata.source==='operations'`, calls payment RPC — live-verified unsigned→400), `ops-invoice-checkout` (verify_jwt true, RLS bundle read, get-or-create tagged Stripe customer, pre-tax line_items w/ per-line tax_code, `automatic_tax.enabled=true` — live-verified no-JWT→401), `ops-invoice-send` (verify_jwt true, Resend-direct from `invoices@`, marks sent — 401 verified), `ops-payment-reminder-cron` (verify_jwt false, `X-Dispatcher-Secret`/`DISPATCHER_SHARED_SECRET` constant-time gate, Resend-direct from `reminders@` — 401 verified). **3 crons scheduled + active:** `ops_overdue_flag_daily` (30 0), `ops_recurring_invoice_daily` (0 0) pure-DB `SELECT public.fn();`; `ops_payment_reminder_daily` (0 8) net.http_post w/ X-Dispatcher-Secret from vault `departure_dispatcher_shared_secret`. **DECISION: ops email sends Resend-direct from `invoices@`/`reminders@` on the already-verified `mail.brainwiseenterprises.com` domain (zero new DNS) rather than modifying the shared platform `send-email` fn — preserves platform boundary.** Cole-side done in LIVE Stripe (not test): Tax category + behavior set, registrations being added, second webhook endpoint registered, `OPS_STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` set. **Learnings:** simulated-JWT functional tests AUTO-COMMIT under MCP → every test cleaned back to seed-only + invoice counter reset; multi-statement execute_sql returns only the last result; `.schema('operations')` via supabase-js needs the schema EXPOSED (avoided in Edge Functions by routing all ops writes through public-schema RPCs). **PENDING (honest, next-session): the $1 end-to-end live-Stripe test + Stripe Tax behavioral verification (needs Cole's registrations live); first real reminder/invoice email; ~11 remaining report views; salesperson/commission; Zoho-CSV migration tooling; PTP-own-PDF engine (Doc 1 D5, 6–9 days, not started); then ALL frontend (Lovable), which REQUIRES Cole to expose the `operations` schema in Supabase API settings first.** Close artifacts (markdown only, Session-74): this v116 entry, architecture-reference v110, session-108-to-109.md. Keep the platform-side corporate-invoice/zero-Stripe boundary intact. Edge fn versions unchanged (check-ai-usage v50, stripe-webhook v29, ai-chat v40, create-checkout v60). Session 106 repo-commit carryover (check-ai-usage v50 + stripe-webhook v29) resolved per Cole (no longer needed). Close artifacts (markdown only, Session-74 decision): this v115 entry, architecture-reference v109, session-107-to-108.md.*

*v114 - Session 106 CLOSE — **Session 105 verification + DB-backed plan-copy single-source + out-of-credits fix.** Opened as a test-everything-from-105 session; surfaced and fixed real bugs. **(A) Session 105 backend re-verified live:** platform_features seed (PTP on, NAI/EPN/AIRSA/HSS off, keyed instrument:<uuid>), user_has_feature branch logic, individual_feature_override_set (rejects corp, 10-char reason, impersonation-gated), credit grant/consume (idempotent on source_ref partial unique index; consume atomic, -1 sentinel), check-ai-usage v49 (200/400 + credit path), stripe-webhook v29 (+10 per per-assessment, idempotent), subscription_plans anon read, test-corp organization_features_view = PTP-only. All passed. **(B) Pricing-display false alarm RESOLVED:** the $100/$18/$180 I flagged were STALE COMMENTS in create-checkout's PRICE_ID_ALLOWLIST, not charges. Cole confirmed he already updated the actual Stripe price objects to $90/$15/$130. lib/stripe.ts PLANS values were already correct. Marketing AND signed-in pricing pages read correct prices. **(C) DB-backed plan-copy single source of truth (NEW — replaces hardcoded feature bullets).** Decision (Cole): one dynamic source, no redeploy to change plan copy, mirroring the price model. Migration `create_plan_tiers_table`: NEW `plan_tiers` table (tier PK 'free'|'base'|'premium', display_name, features jsonb array, ai_coaching_limit int, one_time_credit_grant int, sort_order, is_active, timestamps). **ai_coaching_limit + one_time_credit_grant are DISPLAY-ONLY mirrors — enforcement stays in check-ai-usage (200/400) and the granted +10 stays in stripe-webhook; table does NOT drive enforcement (comment in migration).** RLS: public read {anon,authenticated} qual=true (logged-out marketing must read), service_role write. Seeded 3 rows. Per-tier table chosen over a column on subscription_plans because that table is keyed per price-point (base/premium each have monthly+annual rows + a one_time row) so features would duplicate and drift. Free is a row here (it has no price/billing-period, never belonged in subscription_plans). useSubscriptionPlans hook extended: second fetch of plan_tiers, exposes featuresFor(tier) + limitsFor(tier) alongside priceFor/oneTimePrice (purely additive, existing return keys unchanged). FRONTEND prompts shipped+verified: (1) hook extension; (2) lib/stripe.ts PLANS.base/premium.features updated to the NEW Story-2 copy AS FALLBACK (option A — keep fallback honest so a DB-read failure still shows correct text) + ASSESSMENT_PURCHASE.instruments trimmed ["NAI","AIRSA","HSS","PTP"]→["PTP"]; four render sites rewired to `featuresFor(tier) ?? plan.features` (signed-in Pricing.tsx, marketing Pricing.tsx PricingIndividual subcomponent, BillingSettings active-plan, BillingSettings upgrade card) + BillingSettings free-state list now maps featuresFor("free") + "AI chat limit"→"AI coaching limit" label; (3) per-assessment cards: signed-in grid auto-renders one PTP card, added a consolidated dashed "NAI, AIRSA & HSS — Available through a consultation / Contact us" card routing to /contact (signed-in has no BriefingModal; /contact is a real marketing route), marketing page gained onContact prop → existing BriefingModal, line copy changed to PTP-only + consultation. **Story 2 (Cole's decision): PTP self-serve; NAI/AIRSA/HSS available via consultation ("Contact us"), NOT "coming soon."** Approved plan copy — Base: ["PTP instrument unlimited","200 AI coaching messages/month","Core resource library"]; Premium: ["Unlimited PTP, with 400 AI coaching messages/month. Your always-on guide for putting results into practice","Full premium resource library, including interactive coaching tools to design the life you want","Priority support"]; Free: ["Per-assessment purchases available ($29.99 each)","10 AI coaching messages per assessment purchased","Limited resource library access"]. Premium framing = AI-coaching-led per Cole (the live differentiator is 400 vs 200; interactive resources + coach-on-your-shoulder are in development but Cole approved advertising them since there are no active subs yet and resources will be built before anyone joins). "10 AI coaching messages per assessment" verified accurate vs stripe-webhook p_amount:10. "Limited resource library access" (not "none") verified vs user_resource_audiences: a free individual gets ['free'] audience — currently only 1 published resource platform-wide, 0 audience-tagged, but the ENTITLEMENT is free-tier content. **Single-source proof:** changed a base bullet via SQL, confirmed reversible, reverted (no redeploy). **(D) Coach-picker gap (DECISION: superseded, will not fix in isolation).** The per-individual override card calls search_impersonation_targets with p_account_types:["individual"], so coaches (account_type='coach', no org) are filtered out. Root cause confirmed one-line, and the RPC + individual_feature_override_set + user_has_feature non-corp branch already support coaches. NOT fixing standalone because the card is moving into Members (Phase A). **(E) Super-admin page consolidation (DECISION, Cole):** keep the Features page but RENAME to "Global Features & Settings" (holds platform-wide toggles + room for future global settings); MOVE the per-individual override into the Members detail (MemberDrawer) — this auto-fixes the coach-picker gap since the drawer opens for coaches too; FOLD Coach Management into Members. 14 super-admin destinations inventoried (members, health, features, coaches, coach-report, companies, create-organization, company/:orgId, versions, content-authoring(+lessons/quizzes), resources, newsletter, coupons, asset-library). MemberDrawer already has tabs Learning/Assignments/Coach(show_coach_tab = is_practitioner_coach OR account_type='coach')/Audit via MemberDrawerCoach; CoachManagement = 3 sections (Invite single/bulk/Excel, Pending Invitations, Active Coaches→certify+report). Phase A = rename + override-into-drawer; Phase B = active-coach actions into drawer Coach tab + invite/pending into a Members-level "Invite Coaches" surface + redirect /super-admin/coaches→/members. **PHASE A SHIPPED+VERIFIED:** A1 renamed page h1 + description + sidebar label to "Global Features & Settings" (route /super-admin/features unchanged). A2 — NEW src/components/members/MemberDrawerAccess.tsx (props userId/accountType/organizationId/setHasUnsavedChanges; fetches platform_features instrument list w/ 5-entry FALLBACK_INSTRUMENTS, reads member_feature_overrides into Map, Default/Allow/Block ToggleGroup per instrument, reason dialog 10-char min, calls individual_feature_override_set allow→true/block→false/default→null); MemberDrawer.tsx TabId += "access", showAccess = member.organization_id===null (non-corp only = individuals + coaches, hides for corp), trigger after Coach + body render; PlatformFeatures.tsx trimmed to Card 1 only (deleted Card 2 JSX + override dialog + all Card-2 state/helpers + now-unused imports). **A2-FIX (caught in test):** the per-page TabId/VALID_TABS in super-admin/Members.tsx (lines 31-32, a SEPARATE union from the drawer's) still lacked "access" — line 205 validates rawTab against VALID_TABS and coerced the click back to "learning", so the tab rendered but wouldn't activate; the `as TabId` cast on 205 suppressed the type error so build passed. Added "access" to both. LESSON: duplicated union types across parent page + child drawer are where this hides — when adding a tab, grep ALL TabId/VALID_TABS definitions, not just the component's. Coach-picker gap (item D) closed as a side effect — coaches are now reachable via the drawer Access tab. NOTE: one pre-existing stray override row (deidentified corporate_employee, PTP, dated 2026-04-19) predates the corp-reject guard; harmless (corp visibility is contract-driven), left as-is. **PHASE B SHIPPED+VERIFIED (Option B chosen — NOT full fold).** Decision (Cole): move per-coach actions into the drawer, but KEEP a dedicated invites page rather than forcing the bulk/Excel invite tooling into Members (invites onboard not-yet-users = population-level, not per-member). B1 — MemberDrawerCoach.tsx gained a "Certification actions" section reusing the existing certsQuery: View PTP Report button (shown when a ptp_coach cert is in_progress|certified → navigate /super-admin/coach-report/<userId>) + Mark Certified button (shown when any cert in_progress → JustifiedActionDialog 10-char reason → direct coach_certifications UPDATE status='certified', certified_at, certified_by=auth user; reason enforced for friction but not persisted, matching prior page behavior + actually HARDENS vs the old no-reason certify). B2 — CoachManagement.tsx slimmed to invites-only, retitled "Coach Invitations": deleted Active Coaches card + Certify dialog + coaches/certifyCoach/certifyType/certifying/handleCertify/Coach interface, fetchData simplified to coach_invitations only. Import drops (verified against source, NOT guessed): Dialog* + useNavigate + useAuth deleted; Badge/Select*/Table* KEPT (Pending status badges use Badge, invite tabs use Select, Excel-preview uses Table — deleting any would have broken the surviving page). B3 — AppSidebar label "Coach Management"→"Coach Invitations", route /super-admin/coaches unchanged (NO redirect — page survives under Option B). RLS confirmed: super_admin has ALL on coach_certifications so the drawer certify write works identically. LIVE WRITE TESTED: marked a Cole-owned coach test account (<coach-test-uuid>) certified via the drawer → row flipped status=certified + certified_at + certified_by=super-admin UUID confirmed → REVERTED to in_progress (certified_at/certified_by nulled). Proves the move preserved the full write path, not just rendering (the silent-no-op failure mode was the real risk). LESSON: when relocating an action between components, "renders" ≠ "writes" — the wiring around the query (selectedCertType population, auth user id resolution, dialog onSubmit) is what moves and must be tested with a real (reversible) write. **(F) Chat-credit path TESTED end-to-end** on a non-subscriber individual test user (account_type individual, status inactive, stale tier 'base' — harmless, check-ai-usage gates on STATUS not tier). Granted 3 via grant_one_time_chat_credits RPC, sent 3 messages, balance 3→0 confirmed in DB, ai_usage tracked all 3. Test ledger row (a session-106 test source_ref) deleted at cleanup; balance left 0. **(G) BUG FOUND+FIXED — results-page Ask-AI was subscription-gated, ignored credits.** MyResults.tsx gated the Ask-AI bubble/panel on hasActiveAccess (subscription only), so a credit-holding non-subscriber got the upgrade dialog. ai-chat (v40) → check-ai-usage already honors credits, so frontend-only fix. Prompt 4 shipped: added useAiUsage to MyResults, non-consuming check_only preflight on mount, canUseChat = hasActiveAccess || credit_balance>0, swapped the two Ask-AI gate sites; active-subscriber + bypass-admin paths unchanged. **(H) BUG FOUND+FIXED — out-of-credits showed false "monthly limit/upgrade" to non-subscribers.** Root cause: FREE_TIER_DENIAL omitted subscription_active, and AiChat passes subscriptionActive={usage.subscription_active !== false} → undefined coerced to true → LimitReached rendered the active-subscriber monthly branch ("used all 200 monthly, resets [date]" + Upgrade) for a non-subscriber. **check-ai-usage v49→v50 DEPLOYED** (verify_jwt false, _shared/errors.ts included): inactive-individual denial now returns subscription_active:false; also made subscription_active explicit on the base/premium monthly-exhausted and race-guard responses (was correct only by undefined→true coincidence). Deployed source re-fetched + confirmed. DECISION (Cole): out-of-chat ALWAYS steers to Upgrade to Premium (not "buy another assessment"). Prompt 5 shipped: LimitReached non-subscriber branch rewritten to "You're out of AI chat messages / Subscribe to Premium for {premiumLimit} AI coaching messages every month" + Upgrade to Premium button (no false monthly-reset copy). Verified live: card renders correctly for the 0-credit inactive test user. Results-page Ask-AI bubble already shows the upgrade dialog when out of credits → already matches always-Premium intent (minor: its "Base plan also includes AI chat" secondary line slightly undercuts, left as-is). **Edge fn versions at this point:** check-ai-usage **v50** (was v49), stripe-webhook v29, ai-chat v40, create-checkout v60, generate-report unchanged. **STILL OPEN this session:** Phase A + Phase B (super-admin consolidation); repo-commit of check-ai-usage v50 + stripe-webhook v29 sources (close §92 stripe-webhook drift; both on disk); session close (handoff session-106-to-107, bump build-queue→v114 close + architecture-reference→v108). Carryforward from 105 still pending: NAI/AIRSA PDF rebuilds, narrative regen on score changes, interpretation row consolidation, pre-export readiness check, BUG-NWS-1 + Group H closure, narrative fan-out carryforwards, STATIC_ROUTES reminder.*

*v113 - Session 105 CLOSE — **Go-to-market PTP-only gating + subscription/pricing reconfig + one-time chat credits SHIPPED COMPLETE (backend + frontend, all verified).** Five work streams this session, all backend-first then Lovable, every Lovable claim verified against live source (raw.githubusercontent + grep) and every migration verified with execute_sql. **(1) Assessment auto-advance fix.** `src/components/assessment/AssessmentFlow.tsx` (shared runner): removed the 0.5s setTimeout auto-advance + the unused advanceTimer ref/cleanup; Next button left ungated. Responses still upsert per-question to assessment_responses (UNIQUE(assessment_id,item_id); scoring at submit). Cole-confirmed. **(2) Feature-flag system (backend, 3 migrations + test-corp contract — applied earlier in session).** `session105_platform_features_table_and_seed`: NEW `platform_features` table (cols feature PK = \"instrument:<uuid>\", enabled bool, label, category, updated_at, updated_by; RLS super_admin + service_role; super_admin SELECT). Seeded 5 rows category='assessment': PTP enabled=true, NAI/EPN/AIRSA/HSS enabled=false. `session105_user_has_feature_noncorp_and_superadmin`: extended `user_has_feature(p_user,p_feature)` — super_admin returns true before any gate; CORP branch byte-identical to prior (org_has_feature is the ceiling, a member_feature_overrides row may only SUBTRACT); NEW NON-CORP branch resolves member_feature_overrides first (wins either direction), else platform_features default, else true for an unflagged feature (do not hide un-flagged features). `session105_individual_feature_override_setter`: `individual_feature_override_set(p_user,p_feature,p_enabled,p_reason)` SECDEF — assert_super_admin + assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change'), reason>=10, REJECTS corporate targets (organization_id NOT NULL), p_enabled NULL clears the override, logs via log_super_admin_action (action_type 'individual_feature_override', category user_management, all four booleans true). Test-corp contract (`corporate_contracts.instruments_included_override`, org `<test-corp-org-id>`) set PTP-only. **(3) Gating-support RPCs (backend, this session — `session105_feature_gating_rpcs` + `session105_platform_feature_set_stamp_updated`).** `user_has_features_bulk(p_user uuid, p_features text[]) RETURNS TABLE(feature,enabled)` plpgsql STABLE SECDEF — a caller may only query their OWN features unless they are super_admin (p_user=auth.uid() guard with super_admin escape); RETURN QUERY wraps user_has_feature per unnest(p_features). GRANT authenticated+service_role, REVOKE PUBLIC. `platform_feature_set(p_feature,p_enabled,p_reason) RETURNS jsonb` SECDEF — assert_super_admin + assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change'), reason>=10, p_enabled required, errors 'platform_feature_not_found' for unknown key, no-op returns changed:false when unchanged, UPDATE platform_features SET enabled + updated_at=now() + updated_by=caller, logs via log_super_admin_action(NULL,NULL,'platform_feature_set',...). NEW action_type 'platform_feature_set' INSERTed into super_admin_action_types lookup (category usage_management — chosen from the fixed CHECK whitelist since there is no \"configuration\" category; is_mutation/requires_mfa/requires_justification/denylist_during_impersonation all true). Both RPCs NOT in types.ts → frontend uses (supabase.rpc as any). Verified: both funcs exist, action_type seeded, and user_has_feature for a test individual returns PTP=true, NAI/EPN/AIRSA/HSS=false. **(4) One-time chat credits (backend — migration + check-ai-usage v49 + stripe-webhook v29, all verified+deployed).** DECISIONS (Cole): a $29.99 self-pay per-assessment purchase grants the BUYER +10 one-time chat messages; credits never reset; only another purchase adds more; coach-PAID purchases grant the buyer NOTHING (coach pays, trainee gets the assessment not credits); subscribers ALSO accrue +10 per purchase, consumed only AFTER the monthly allowance; +10 per assessment/instrument, idempotent per purchase. NO BACKFILL of past buyers (all non-PTP results today are test users; a purchase, not \"taking PTP,\" is the trigger; PTP is included not a purchase). Artifacts: `session105_one_time_chat_credits` — users.one_time_chat_credits int NOT NULL DEFAULT 0; `chat_credit_grants` ledger (id, user_id FK→users ON DELETE CASCADE, amount, source, source_ref, created_at) + PARTIAL UNIQUE INDEX on source_ref WHERE NOT NULL (idempotency) + RLS enabled with NO policies (definer/service-role only); `grant_one_time_chat_credits(p_user,p_amount,p_source,p_source_ref)` SECDEF (INSERT ON CONFLICT(source_ref) DO NOTHING, bump balance ONLY when a row was actually inserted); `consume_one_time_chat_credit(p_user)` SECDEF (atomic decrement WHERE balance>0 RETURNING; returns -1 if none). REVOKE PUBLIC, GRANT service_role. `check-ai-usage` v48→**v49 DEPLOYED**: individual branch limit 200 base / 400 premium; userRow select adds one_time_chat_credits; a non-subscriber WITH credits is now allowed (was flat free-tier denial); a subscriber draws the monthly allowance first then credits via consume RPC; always increments ai_usage for analytics (credit path proceeds even if the increment fails, since the credit is already spent); ADDITIVE response fields credit_balance, subscription_active, used_credit. Corp + super-admin branches unchanged; verify_jwt false; _shared/errors.ts shipped. `stripe-webhook` v28→**v29 DEPLOYED**: in the payment branch ONLY, after each successful assessment_purchases insert, calls grant_one_time_chat_credits(amount 10, source 'per_assessment_purchase', source_ref `${payment_intent}:${instrumentId}`). Coach branches untouched; deployed source diffed against disk = exact match. NOTE: ai-chat (v40) delegates consumption to check-ai-usage via usage_type 'chat_message' (enforcement+increment lives in check-ai-usage, NOT inlined in ai-chat — Lovable recon was imprecise, corrected against source). stripe-webhook is NOT in the repo (§92 drift); both v49 + v29 sources are on disk to hand to Cole for a repo commit. **(5) Subscription/pricing reconfig (backend + frontend).** Chat limits raised base 30→200, premium 150→400. Prices: Base $10/mo + $90/yr (was $100), Premium $15/mo (was $18) + $130/yr (was $180), per-assessment $29.99 unchanged. **STRIPE IMMUTABILITY FINDING [RESOLVED]:** Cole's belief that subscription_plans updates from Stripe is FALSE — nothing syncs it; price_usd was STALE (held the old $100/$18/$180). DECISION = DB-sourced dynamic display: we maintain subscription_plans.price_usd ourselves (NOT Stripe-synced). `session105_subscription_plans_price_usd_update` set the new prices. `session105_subscription_plans_anon_read` broadened the \"public read\" RLS policy from authenticated-only to {anon,authenticated} so the logged-out /pricing page is actually dynamic (table is public pricing; the price_ids were already in the client bundle). DB stripe_price_ids UNCHANGED (Cole edited the prices in-place inside Stripe under the same price IDs). subscription_plans cols: id, plan_name, tier, billing_period, price_usd, stripe_price_id, is_active, created_at, updated_at. **IMPORTANT decoupling caveat: the DISPLAYED price (subscription_plans.price_usd, maintained by us) and the CHARGED price (Stripe under the stored price_id) are now independent — any future price change must update BOTH or the page will advertise a price Stripe doesn't charge.** **FRONTEND (all four prompts shipped + verified clean this session):** *Pricing + AI-chat prompt* — useUserProfile (one_time_chat_credits added to interface + select); SubscriptionGate (individual ai_chat passes if active OR credits>0; other features active-only; corp/bypass untouched); useAiUsage (+credit_balance/subscription_active/used_credit); NEW useSubscriptionPlans (priceFor(tier,interval)+oneTimePrice() from subscription_plans); stripe.ts PLANS static fixed (base 10/90, premium 15/130, ai_limit 200/400, \"200/400 AI chat messages/month\" copy, price_ids unchanged); Pricing.tsx + BillingSettings.tsx dynamic prices with static fallback + auto 200/400; LimitReached.tsx credit-aware copy (non-subscriber out-of-credits → \"purchase another\" no upgrade btn; else reset copy + premium-limit upgrade); AiChat.tsx + PrivacySettings.tsx credit-aware counters. *Gating Prompt 1 (individual)* — NEW useInstrumentFeatureAccess.ts (calls user_has_features_bulk via (supabase.rpc as any); exports INSTRUMENT_UUIDS + isPTP; featureAllowed false while loading/error, PTP via isPTP); InstrumentSelection.tsx computeIndividualVisible = isPTP || featureAllowed || existingEntitlementSignal (signals: subscription-by-tier, coach-paid, purchase by uuid/instrument_id/short_name, self-pay coach invite, actor debrief, free cert pool, completed, in-progress, AIRSA-awaiting); gateIndividual = !isCorp && !canBypassAssessmentPaywall; hidden cards collapse into ONE BriefingModal card source=\"individual_instrument_access_request\"; corp user_has_feature branch + super-admin bypass untouched; Assessment.tsx autostart useEffect runs the same predicate (PTP short-circuit → user_has_features_bulk → entitlement signals), clears autostart params + falls through to the picker if hidden; EPN/manager-AIRSA go via their own handlers not this effect; AiChat NO CHANGE (peer-instrument toggle is corp-only inside the isCorp block; own-results selector intentionally left untouched so users keep full use of their existing data). *Gating Prompt 2 (super-admin Features page)* — NEW src/pages/super-admin/PlatformFeatures.tsx (named PlatformFeatures to avoid the existing company/Features.tsx import collision): Card 1 platform-wide flags read platform_features via from(\"platform_features\" as any), Switch + reason dialog (>=10) with \"changes visibility for ALL individual users\" warning, write via (supabase.rpc as any)(\"platform_feature_set\") revert-on-error; Card 2 per-individual overrides search via supabase.rpc(\"search_impersonation_targets\",{p_account_types:[\"individual\"]}), read selected user's overrides from member_feature_overrides (super_admin RLS permits), 3-way ToggleGroup Default|Allow|Block mapped allow→true/block→false/default→null, write via (supabase.rpc as any)(\"individual_feature_override_set\"); App.tsx import + route /super-admin/features wrapped RoleGuard[brainwise_super_admin]>SuperAdminSessionProvider; AppSidebar.tsx SlidersHorizontal import + nav entry placed directly under \"Members\". *Gating Prompt 3 (corp dashboard contract gating)* — NEW useOrgInstrumentAccess.ts (exports DASHBOARD_INSTRUMENT_UUIDS {NAI,PTP,AIRSA}; reads organization_features_view.instruments_included once, RLS-scoped; super_admin short-circuits the query and is always-true; orgInstrumentIncluded(uuid)=isSuperAdmin||set.has(uuid); array narrowed via Array.isArray + string filter, no row-level any); AppSidebar.tsx gates each of the 3 dashboard children by its show* flag and the parent by showDashboardsMenu && hasAnyDashboard; CompanyDashboard(NAI)/PTPDashboard(PTP)/AirsaDashboard(AIRSA) each get a redirect guard (compute allowed = !orgAccessLoading && orgInstrumentIncluded(<page uuid>); effect navigates to /dashboard replace when !loading && !allowed; renders loader while loading then null on deny; route-level RoleGuard untouched so super-admins still allowed). **TYPING NOTE for the whole gating rollout: platform_features (table), user_has_features_bulk, platform_feature_set, and individual_feature_override_set are all NEW and NOT in src/integrations/supabase/types.ts yet → frontend uses (supabase.rpc as any) / from(\"x\" as any). search_impersonation_targets, member_feature_overrides, and organization_features_view ARE typed.** **Instrument UUIDs (canonical):** PTP 02618e9a-d411-44cf-b316-fe368edeac03 (INST-001), NAI 77d1290f-1daf-44e0-931f-b9b8ad185520 (INST-002), EPN e5b3e839-d861-45ff-9f79-42887f5ae2de (INST-002L, requires_assignment), AIRSA abb62120-8cc8-435f-babc-dd6a27fbc235 (INST-003), HSS 90216d9d-153c-4b7b-abe0-1d7845c9e6e0 (INST-004). **Session 105 close artifacts:** this v113 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v107, session-105-to-106.md handoff. Three markdown files for Cole to upload manually to cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs (flat root). **Carryforward / on the horizon:** confirm Cole's $130 checkout test was the in-app Subscribe button (not a Stripe Payment Link); in-app smoke test of the credit path (only meaningful for a non-subscriber WITH credits now that SubscriptionGate is relaxed — set users.one_time_chat_credits via SQL on a test user); commit check-ai-usage v49 + stripe-webhook v29 sources to the repo (closes §92 stripe-webhook drift); marketing-copy pass on Pricing.tsx + marketing/Pricing.tsx which may still advertise all 4 instruments in the Premium tier (copy, not code); NAI/AIRSA PDF rebuilds; narrative regeneration on score changes; interpretation row consolidation; pre-export readiness check for PTP; BUG-NWS-1 (newsletter attachment-send hang) + Group H formal closure; the PTP narrative fan-out carryforwards (BQ-NARRATIVE-FANOUT-STATUS calculate-scores terminal-status server-side fix, BQ-FANOUT-COLDFAIL, BQ-PDF-EXPORT-COLDCACHE); STATIC_ROUTES reminder (newsletter-sitemap hardcoded marketing-route list — surface on any newsletter/sitemap/SEO/new-marketing-page work).*

*v112 - Session 104 CLOSE — **Newsletter SEO/AEO/RSS (G3 + G9) SHIPPED + Newsletter email dispatch (G8 a/b/c) SHIPPED COMPLETE end-to-end (backend + frontend).** Session opened on stale docs: H6 v1 (newsletter AI co-pilot) had ALREADY SHIPPED in a separate chat before this session (Edge Functions newsletter_ai_generate v1, newsletter-image-search v1, ingest-pexels-asset v1 all ACTIVE; tables newsletter_ai_conversations + newsletter_ai_messages live with real data). convert-html-to-tiptap confirmed DELETED. PEXELS_API_KEY confirmed. **G3 + G9 SHIPPED:** App is pre-April-2026 Vite+React CSR SPA; G3 rendering-strategy decision RESOLVED = Option A (Lovable native platform prerendering for verified crawlers + markdown surface for AI engines — both confirmed PASSING via Lovable Discoverability scan + LinkedIn Post Inspector reading real per-article meta). Per-article SEO meta was already injected client-side by NewsletterArticle.tsx via setPageMeta. NEW RPC list_public_published_articles() (SQL/STABLE/SECDEF, anon+authenticated+service_role grants, filters status=published AND archived_at IS NULL AND gate=public AND published_at<=now()) is the single source of truth. THREE NEW public Edge Functions (verify_jwt:false, render live from the RPC, fail-soft): newsletter-sitemap (application/xml, STATIC_ROUTES + live article URLs), newsletter-feed (application/rss+xml, RSS 2.0), newsletter-llms (text/plain, llms.txt). All verified live returning the one published article + correctly excluding the archived one. robots.txt wired with Sitemap: + LLMs: lines; index.html head wired with RSS <link rel=alternate>. Both verified live after Cole published. **STANDING REMINDER (also memory): the STATIC_ROUTES list inside newsletter-sitemap/index.ts is HARDCODED. Article URLs are fully automatic, but when Cole adds a new public marketing page, STATIC_ROUTES must be manually updated (~10-sec edit + redeploy). Surface this on any newsletter/sitemap/SEO/new-marketing-page work.** Final STATIC_ROUTES: /, /services, /our-approach, /products, /pricing, /podcast, /contact, /newsletter, /privacy, /terms, /cookies, /international-privacy. **G8 COMPLETE (a + b + c):** Consent model LOCKED (Cole): users span US+EU+UK+Canada → strictest-fits-all = explicit opt-in, demonstrable consent, NO silent auto-subscribe; becoming a paid user does NOT create newsletter consent; one unified newsletter_subscribers list. (Claude flagged not-a-lawyer; real compliance review warranted before EU/CA scale.) Email format: summary + opening (first 4 TipTap text blocks) + Read-full-article button + compliant footer (physical address 8735 Dunwoody Place #STE R Atlanta GA 30350 + working per-recipient unsubscribe). **G8a (dispatch engine):** migration session104_g8a_dispatch_schema (newsletter_settings singleton, newsletter_dispatches, email_logs +dispatch_id/opened_at/clicked_at); Edge Function newsletter-dispatch (suppression by status=confirmed, per-send unsubscribe token Option 3 reusing unsubscribe_from_newsletter unchanged, per-recipient email_logs tagged newsletter); resend-webhook v4→v5 (additive opened/clicked mapping); frontend SendDispatchButton + DispatchHistoryPanel + DispatchEngagementStats + AutoSendToggle. **G8b (in-product opt-in):** migration session104_g8b_in_product_optin_rpcs (opt_in_to_newsletter upsert-by-email with bounced/complained NO-auto-resurrect, opt_out_of_newsletter token-free, get_my_newsletter_subscription; authenticated+service_role grants); frontend settings toggle (NotificationSettings.tsx) + UNCHECKED signup checkbox (SignUp.tsx) + 24h-TTL localStorage intent flushed in useAuth SIGNED_IN (isolated fire-and-forget). **G8c (scheduled publish + auto-dispatch):** FOUND + FIXED latent gap — scheduling existed (schedule_article) but NOTHING published due articles. Stages: scheduled_by_user_id column; schedule_article captures it; _snapshot_article_version scheduled-actor fallback; process_due_scheduled_articles() (per-article exception block, mirrors publish_article, mode-automatic returns dispatch_ids); newsletter-dispatch v1→v2 ADDITIVE dual-auth (x-dispatcher-secret service path OR super-admin JWT, verify_jwt flipped to false, perimeter re-verified); cron_publish_and_dispatch_due_articles() + cron job every 15 min; frontend manual-publish auto-dispatch in runTransition (gated prevStatus draft/scheduled — re-publish SUPPRESSED). **KEY LESSONS:** (1) Edge Function _shared module under MCP deploy tool: import as ./_shared/ NOT ../_shared/ (tool co-locates bundled files under source/). (2) newsletter_articles CHECK newsletter_articles_scheduled_consistency requires status=scheduled → scheduled_for>now(); publish UPDATE passes because status leaves 'scheduled'; CHECK only on write. (3) super_admin_audit_log is immutable (DELETE blocked by trigger) — one test audit row remains from G8c verification, harmless. **Resend dashboard (Cole-configured):** click tracking ON, open tracking intentionally OFF (Resend flags Not Recommended; opened_at stays empty by choice), email.clicked added to resend-webhook subscriptions. OPEN OPTIONAL (deferral, needs DNS): custom tracking subdomain. **Session 104 close artifacts:** this v112 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v106, session-104-to-105.md handoff. *v111 - Session 103 CLOSE — **PTP PDF export rebuild FINALIZED + PTP narrative-status root cause diagnosed and fixed + super-admin coachee report-access feature SHIPPED.** This session was the continuation/finalization of the PTP PDF rebuild that ran across the prior long-lived troubleshooting chat ("Troubleshooting video access permissions for certification cohort"); that chat carried font infrastructure (`pdfFonts.ts` base64 Poppins/Montserrat lazy-loaded), cover-page vector rebuild, async generator conversion, four-row narrative fetch rewrite, split-pair Combined handling (`isSplitPairCombined` guard, dimension-score merging, unified interpretation map), new section renderers, modal restructure, and data-correctness fixes across Prompts 1 through 2.7. **PTP PDF final polish SHIPPED (two rounds, both personal + combined verified).** Round 1 confirmed holding: action-plan dimension pill above title, first dimension box full-width + size-to-content, Reward chart no longer colliding with Assessment Responses, impact labels spaced, facet/question blocks held together. Round 2 final fixes: (a) section-header keep-with-next made universal in `sectionHeading` (default reserve = `MIN_BLOCK_SPACE` 30mm); the real orphan fix was reducing `renderFacetScoreTable`'s over-reservation from `checkPageBreak(12 + facets.length*7)` to `Math.min(facets.length,3)*7` so "Driving Facet Scores" header lands with its first rows; the originally-proposed Fix-1a (lowering the default reservation) was DROPPED because it would have weakened the default below 30mm. (b) `renderFacetBarChart` rewritten to fit-to-page: row height clamped `LEGIBILITY_FLOOR=3.0mm` to `MAX_ROW_H=7.0mm`, oversized charts split across consecutive pages with BALANCED bar counts (`nPages=ceil(rowCount/rowsPerPage)`, `barsPerPage=ceil(rowCount/nPages)`, `rowHeight=min(MAX_ROW_H, availableHeight/barsPerPage)`); the 81-bar combined All Facets chart now splits ~41/40 across two pages instead of overflowing. Real available chart height ~234mm (NOT the ~250 first estimated), which drove the balanced-split refinement; the 65-bar Threat chart stays single-page. Margins left UNCHANGED (widening worsens orphans/splits). PDF generator file is `src/lib/generateResultsPdf.ts`. **PTP narrative-status root cause CORRECTED + fixed.** Initial hypothesis (frontend writes `narrative_status`) was WRONG — Lovable's codebase search found ZERO frontend writes. Confirmed by reading `calculate-scores` Edge Function: row inserts as `'generating'`, then a fan-out of ~12 `generate-facet-interpretations` invokes (4 calls × 3 contexts for `both`) runs under `EdgeRuntime.waitUntil`; the wrapper sets `finalStatus = failures.length===0 ? 'ready' : 'failed'`. The narrative (`generate-report`) and the 89-entry `facet_insights_all` (`generate-all-facets`) write on SEPARATE fire-and-forget paths, so ONE fan-out rejection flips a fully-populated report to `failed`. `retry-ptp-narratives` only NULLs the status (regenerates nothing when content exists). Proof: Ryan Carey's row was never retried, still `failed`, yet had full narrative (23K chars) + 89-element `facet_insights_all` array — identical content to Ben Westfall's clean (NULL) row. **Fix = Option 2 (read-time reconciliation), SHIPPED via Lovable.** `usePtpNarrativeStatus` in `MyResults.tsx` now also reads `ai_narrative` + `facet_insights_all_total` and the `facet_insights_all` row's `facet_data` array length, treating a row as `ready` when content is present regardless of the flag (only ever flips TOWARD ready; never hides a renderable row). **Reconciliation predicate correction caught mid-build:** `facet_insights_all` is ONE `facet_interpretations` row whose `facet_data` is a JSONB array of 89 elements, NOT 89 rows — the first spec used a row count (`count >= total`) which is always 1 and never fires; corrected to read `facet_data` array length. Stuck rows (Ryan Carey, Jasper Morgan) now self-heal on view; no bulk status reset needed. Option 1 (server-side terminal-status fix in `calculate-scores`) DEFERRED as defense-in-depth carryforward. **Super-admin coachee report-access SHIPPED (additive, no regression).** Backend-first: applied + verified RLS policy `assessment_responses: super admin can read all` (`FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (current_user_account_type() = 'brainwise_super_admin')`) — the last table gap (the other three report tables already had it); additive OR-combined, user/coach/service-role access unchanged; confirmed each completed coach PTP has 89 readable response rows. Frontend: NEW `src/pages/super-admin/CoachReport.tsx` (thin two-level page — lists the coach's own completed PTP via direct `assessment_results` query by `user_id`, reusing the paired-PTP collapse logic that already exists in ClientResults' direct-by-user_id branch; renders `<MyResults isCoachView adminView targetUserId={coachUserId} preSelectedAssessmentId={assessmentId} coachUserId={superAdminId} permissionLevel="full_results" />`, skipping the `coach_clients`/`permissions` resolver). NEW route `/super-admin/coach-report/:coachUserId` wrapped `<RoleGuard allowedRoles={["brainwise_super_admin"]}><SuperAdminSessionProvider>` exactly like every other `/super-admin/*` route. NEW "View PTP Report" button in `CoachManagement.tsx` Active Coaches Actions cell (predicate `coach.certifications.some(c => c.certification_type==='ptp_coach' && (c.status==='in_progress'||c.status==='certified'))`), added inside a wrapper `<div className="flex gap-2">` so the existing `<TableCell>` line stayed byte-for-byte unmodified, with the existing "Mark Certified" button copied in verbatim. PTP-only list filter kept (page scoped to PTP debriefs). **NEW `adminView?: boolean` prop on `MyResults` (default false).** Required because the coach-view share filter (`if (isCoachView && coachUserId && shareWithCoach === false)`) empties the list for certification coaches — trainees with `share_results_with_coach=false` and zero `coach_clients` rows. `adminView` guards that filter (`!adminView`) and the `setShareWithCoach` line; the `clientName` fetch is kept so the header name shows. Verified exactly four `MyResults` call sites (App.tsx my-results owner view, SharedResults.tsx:272 peer share, ClientResults.tsx:587 coach→client, CoachReport.tsx) and ONLY CoachReport passes `adminView` — so default-false guarantees no regression BY CONSTRUCTION, independent of inventory completeness. SharedResults peer path already bypasses the share filter via missing `coachUserId` (filter short-circuits on falsy `coachUserId`), so the `!adminView` addition does not affect it. Debrief gate (`if (!isCoachView ...)` populating `debriefPendingIds` from `coach_clients_client_view.results_released`) is also owner-side-only and already bypassed by coach views; both share-filter and debrief gate are frontend-only presentational gates, not security boundaries. Cole checked for regression (including the real coach→client share-hiding path) and found none. **Impersonation cleanup:** cleared a stuck super-admin impersonation session (Cole impersonating Ryan Carey, observe mode) by setting `ended_at=now()`, `end_reason='manual'` on the `impersonation_sessions` row; `end_reason` CHECK whitelist is `manual|timeout|forced` (`manual_admin_cleanup` rejected first). The coachee-report feature removes the need to impersonate for debriefs, closing this stuck-session failure mode for that workflow. **Process notes Session 103:** (a) Backend-first discipline held — every claim Lovable made (calculate-scores fan-out ownership, the four MyResults call sites, the `coach.certifications` shape, the App.tsx route-wrap pattern, the share-filter and debrief-gate guards) was verified against live source via raw GitHub pull + grep and Supabase MCP before approving. (b) "Additive" plans twice required touching one existing file (the reconciliation hook; the `adminView` guard) because the reuse target carried a hidden owner-view assumption; both handled via default-preserving optional changes with explicit regression-test gates. (c) The fan-out is failing cohort-wide (4 of 5 completions; ~1.6s `narrative_completed_at` = fast rejection, not timeout; `admin.functions.invoke` + service-role JWT under signing-keys mode is the prime suspect) — pre-warm is effectively dead, every first view does on-demand section generation. (d) PDF export reads cached sections at export time, so exporting before lazy on-view generation completes could drop per-context sections — confirm before relying on cohort exports. **Session 103 close artifacts:** this v111 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v105 (adds the `assessment_responses` super-admin RLS policy, CoachReport feature, `adminView` MyResults prop, `usePtpNarrativeStatus` reconciliation, and the confirmed data-model facts), session-103-to-104.md handoff. Three markdown files for Cole to upload manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root). **Session 104 opens on: the newsletter SEO/AEO/RSS pass (G3 + G8 + G9 combined) — Cole's stated next focus.** G3 = newsletter article SSR/meta-tags/structured-data (the deferred SSR rendering-strategy decision must be made here); G8 = internal subscriber inclusion in dispatch (who-counts/consent/dispatch-path sub-decisions still open); G9 = RSS feed Edge Function at `/newsletter/feed.xml`. Estimated 2-4 sessions; confirm actual H6 v1 ship state at session open before assuming G-work starts clean. Carryforward (not blocking G-work): Option 1 `calculate-scores` fan-out terminal-status fix, the fan-out cold-fail root-cause investigation, and the PDF-export-cold-cache confirmation.*

*v110 - Session 102 CLOSE — **Group H Cycle H5 + Follow-up SHIPPED end-to-end. BrainWise HTML Authoring Spec produced as standalone deliverable. H6 v1 scope fully locked.** All three deliverables verified by Cole's smoke test on `ptp_test_article.html` (Group H canonical reference, 522 lines): eyebrow + deck + lede + byline + numbered section rules (01-04) + pullquote + callout + domain grid with 5 rows + index row with TRI/RSI cards + footer-meta tag strip all imported as correctly-styled BrainWise nodes. Smoke test PASSED. **H5 Cycle 1 (atom simples) SHIPPED — 20 files modified, 2 skipped.** Modified: Audio, AuthorBio, Chart, CodeDiff, CTA, Callout, Embed, FooterMeta, Footnotes, FurtherReading, ImageCompare, Math, Poll, Pullquote, RelatedArticles, StatCallout, SubscribeBlock, Terminal, Definition mark, FootnoteRef mark. Skipped (Lovable pushback accepted as architecturally correct): ImageGallery, StatGrid — wrapper-only fallback rules would fail ProseMirror content-expression coercion (`newsletterImage+`, `newsletterStatCallout+`) silently because the wrappers expect specific child node types and external markup doesn't produce them; children reparent naturally to the parent block flow without the wrapper, which is acceptable behavior. Image.ts excluded from H5 entirely — image import flow is handled upstream by `rewriteImgsToSyntheticFigures` in ImportHtmlModal.tsx (Session 101 H4 architecture). Embed deviation: `twitter` and `loom` map to `provider: "generic"` since EmbedProvider TypeScript union is `"youtube" | "spotify" | "vimeo" | "generic"` and was not extended. The `blockquote.twitter-tweet` rule was dropped from spec since it would require provider union extension. FootnoteRef cross-element lookup (Path 1) shipped: `sup > a[href^="#fn-N"]` rule walks `el.ownerDocument.getElementById(id)` to read the footnote body content from a separate footnotes block — first H5 example of cross-element parseHTML semantics. **H5 Cycle 2 (composites) SHIPPED — 3 files modified, 7 skipped with §151 doc-comment markers.** Shipped: Checklist (parent + ChecklistItem child), Citations (parent + CitationEntry child), Disclosure (parent + DisclosureSummary child). Skipped (schema-blocked, documented in source with §151 markers): DomainGrid, FourColumn, IndexRow, KeyMoments, StepList, ThreeColumn, TwoColumn. Two/Three/FourColumn parents have EXACT-count content expressions (no `+`) making them genuinely impossible to satisfy from external markup via parseHTML alone — `newsletterTwoColumnPane newsletterTwoColumnPane` (no plus quantifier) requires precisely two panes, no more no less, and external markup can't be coerced into that shape without preprocessing. StepList has a two-layer fail: parent strict content expression PLUS Step requires `heading block*` which means first child must be a heading; external step content rarely leads with a heading. **H5 Follow-up cycle 3 SHIPPED — 5 files, includes 2 REVERSALS of Cycle 2 skip decisions.** After Cole's smoke test exposed gaps on the canonical test article: (1) Pullquote.ts added `div.pullquote`, `div[class~="pullquote"]` matches (Cycle 1 missed these); (2) FooterMeta.ts added `div.footer-meta`, `footer.footer-meta`, `div[class~="footer-meta"]` matches (Cycle 1 missed these); (3) SectionRule.ts `div.section-rule` getAttrs now reads visible `.section-rule-num` and `.section-rule-title` children when canonical `data-*` attrs are absent, and auto-promotes `style` attr to `"numbered"` when a visible number marker is present (preserves "01" / "02" / "03" / "04" markers from the test article); (4) **DomainGrid.ts REVERSED from Cycle 2 skip** — added parent (`div.domain-grid`, `section.domain-grid`) and child (`div.domain-row`) fallback rules with TEXT_NODE-filtered label and count_value parsing from rendered DOM text; paired-rule pattern satisfies the `newsletterDomainRow+` strict content expression because both parent AND child fallbacks fire together; (5) **IndexRow.ts REVERSED from Cycle 2 skip** — added parent (`div.index-row`) and child (`div.index-card`) fallback rules with `.tri → navy` and `.rsi → orange` accent_color inference from CSS class names. **§151 amendment locked Session 102: "wrapper-strict-child" class of composite nodes is NOT universally unfixable.** Paired-rule patterns (both parent + child class fallbacks shipped together at priority 51) DO satisfy strict content expressions of the form `newsletterChildType+`, because ProseMirror runs both rules together during parse and the child matches feed the parent's content expression. The genuinely-unfixable cases are narrower than Cycle 2 assumed: only (a) exact-count content expressions without `+` quantifier (`A A`, `A A A`, `A A A A`) and (b) heading-first content expressions (Step's `heading block*` requirement). The Cycle 2 skip set should be re-examined for any node whose parent expression uses `child+`; paired-rule pattern likely applies. Remaining genuinely-blocked nodes: TwoColumn, ThreeColumn, FourColumn (exact-count), StepList (heading-first). KeyMoments may be re-evaluable. **BrainWise HTML Authoring Spec produced as standalone deliverable** at `/mnt/user-data/outputs/BRAINWISE_HTML_AUTHORING_SPEC.md` (796 lines). Complete authoring contract for LLMs producing BrainWise-importable HTML: 24 supported import patterns documented with class names and example markup, all 12 inline marks covered, 13 import-friendly block patterns with class names and internal selectors detailed, documented limitations for 7 schema-blocked composites, anti-patterns section, optional CSS template for preview rendering, pre-output checklist. Verified against shipped state of all 37 nodes + 10 marks via direct source inspection. Key authoring constraints documented: H1 must be dropped from body content (StarterKit configured with `levels: [2, 3, 4]` only, H1 is article title field), URLs must pass `isSafeHttpUrl()` validation (no `javascript:`, `data:`, etc.). This spec will be embedded as the system prompt in the H6 v1 `newsletter_ai_generate` Edge Function to ensure Claude-generated newsletter HTML imports cleanly via the H4 pipeline. **`convert-html-to-tiptap` Edge Function STILL ACTIVE — manual Cole-side deletion required.** D6=a (locked Session 101) called for deletion after Cole confirmed ≥1 successful real-article import via the new path; smoke test on `ptp_test_article.html` confirmed this. Function ID `20fb6ba1-cfcf-4d27-bd0c-f0296d982f06`, slug `convert-html-to-tiptap`, version 3, ezbr_sha256 `ffe538f1f4ecd886efed9ef83b5d955d0cf4e4b3bf9bd468153d153e0ac569a5`. Supabase MCP exposes list/get/deploy for Edge Functions but NOT delete; deletion must happen via Supabase Dashboard → Edge Functions → Delete OR via Supabase CLI (`supabase functions delete convert-html-to-tiptap`). **Cole action item carried to Session 103 open.** `import-html-images` v1 remains ACTIVE (function ID `53d32929-6e0d-4c0c-9e7c-479dcc544d04`, ezbr_sha256 `bb2fd15e894b9910628d6c0d573dd1ce180a6bb3ea23517fce58665e96e9de6c`). **H6 v1 scope LOCKED for Session 103 execution after G3/G8/G9 sequencing decision.** Cole's Session 103 sequencing call (locked): finish H6 first (the AI co-pilot integration), then do G3/G8/G9 combined as one SEO/AEO/RSS pass. **H6 v1 deliverables (Path A — full chat-based co-pilot, not modal-based one-shot):** Persistent chat panel in `/super-admin/newsletter/<articleId>` editor sidebar; SQL migration creates `newsletter_ai_conversations` + `newsletter_ai_messages` tables with RLS; NEW Edge Function `newsletter_ai_generate` (Class A super-admin gated, calls Anthropic API with BrainWise HTML Authoring Spec embedded as system prompt, accepts conversation thread + new user message + current article state, returns generated/modified HTML); NEW Edge Function `newsletter-image-search` (Class A super-admin gated, calls Pexels `/v1/search` API, returns 3-5 candidates with photographer name + alt + photographer_url + photo_page_url metadata); NEW Storage bucket `newsletter_ai_attachments` for chat file uploads; selection-aware editing via TipTap selection API + ProseMirror position-based splice (highlight text in editor → "Rewrite with Claude" → Claude returns HTML for just that range → spliced back into doc at exact position); file uploads to chat (PDF via pdf-parser, .docx via mammoth, plain text, images, 10MB ceiling); image search via Pexels with auto-attribution; **image attribution architecture locked Option B (Cole's Session 102 call): NEW `attribution: string | null` attribute added to `newsletterImage` TipTap node (default null), renderHTML appends `<p class="newsletter-image__attribution">` child element when attribution non-null, CSS treats it muted + smaller + italic; auto-populated as `Photo by [Name] on Pexels` with photographer profile link + photo page link when image comes from Pexels API path; the existing `caption` attribute stays purely editorial and is unchanged**; Opus/Sonnet toggle in chat header for cost/quality balance; generated content lands in existing ImportHtmlModal preview state for review before commit (no surprise inserts to editor). **PEXELS_API_KEY already in Supabase Edge Function secrets (Cole confirmed Session 102 close).** Anthropic API integration already in place (used by 20+ existing Edge Functions). Pre-flight verification at H6 session open: confirm PEXELS_API_KEY secret name matches what Edge Function will read via `Deno.env.get("PEXELS_API_KEY")`. **H6 v2 (generation fallback) DEFERRED INDEFINITELY — decision criteria documented**: only build v2 if Pexels coverage gaps emerge with 3+ articles where author wanted an image Pexels couldn't provide. If shipped, prefer Replicate (Flux Schnell, ~$0.003/image) over OpenAI DALL-E since Cole declined OpenAI dependency. Estimated H6 v1 effort: **4-6 sessions, 10-16 Lovable cycles, 1 SQL migration, 2-3 new Edge Functions**. Larger than originally-spec'd H6 (which was a one-shot modal-based generation) because Cole chose Path A (full chat-based co-pilot) over Path B (phased rollout). **Pexels attribution rule (legal vs API terms layered)**: Pexels License legally requires NO attribution (CC0-like terms); Pexels API Terms require attribution as condition of API usage. Standard format `Photo by [Photographer Name] on Pexels` with photographer profile link + photo page link. Pexels API response includes all needed metadata fields. Rate limit 200 requests/hour + 20,000/month free tier (more than sufficient for newsletter cadence); unlimited if attribution properly displayed (built-in benefit of compliance). **Session 103 sequencing (Cole's call): H6 v1 FIRST, then G3+G8+G9 combined SEO/AEO/RSS pass.** Group H formally closes when H6 v1 ships. G3 = newsletter article SSR/meta-tags/structured-data infrastructure (deferred Session 94 to "post-G6 combined G3+RSS pass"); G8 = internal subscriber inclusion in dispatch (deferred from Group G); G9 = RSS feed Edge Function at `/newsletter/feed.xml` (deferred Session 94 to G3+RSS combined pass to avoid URL-change debt). G3+G8+G9 combined pass estimated 2-4 sessions, sequenced after H6 v1 completes. **Process lessons locked Session 102:** (a) Lovable pushback discipline working — Cycle 1 EmbedProvider union limit + Cycle 2 wrapper-only content-expression coercion caught correctly. Cycle 2 SECOND pass also correctly identified DomainGrid + IndexRow as REVERSIBLE skips after seeing Cole's specific test article ("paired-rule pattern satisfies strict content expressions"). (b) Smoke test on real article before declaring cycle done is mandatory discipline — Cycle 1 + Cycle 2 alone would have shipped a deceptively-incomplete H5; smoke on `ptp_test_article.html` exposed the gap that triggered the Follow-up cycle. (c) API keys never pasted in chat; Cole sets in Supabase Edge Function secrets directly. (d) Claude image generation is genuinely impossible (Anthropic doesn't make an image-generation product); this is a real product limitation not a routing question. (e) Architecture-reference §151 expanded with paired-rule amendment — the original locked wording overgeneralized; the corrected wording distinguishes exact-count content expressions (genuinely unfixable) from `child+` quantified content expressions (paired-rule fixable). **Session 102 final ship totals**: H5 Cycle 1 (1 Lovable cycle, 20 files), H5 Cycle 2 (1 Lovable cycle, 3 files), H5 Follow-up (1 Lovable cycle, 5 files), BrainWise HTML Authoring Spec (Claude-side authoring, no Lovable), H6 v1 scope locking (Cole-side decisions Path A + Pexels-only + Option B attribution). 0 backend migrations, 0 Edge Function deploys, 0 §-rule corrections beyond the §151 amendment. **Carryforward deferred items unchanged from Session 101 except**: (a) `convert-html-to-tiptap` deletion newly surfaces as Session 103 OPEN action item for Cole (manual Dashboard/CLI step); (b) H6 v1 newly added as primary Session 103 work; (c) G3+G8+G9 combined SEO/AEO/RSS pass newly sequenced as Session 103-post-H6 focus. All other Session 101 deferred items unchanged: (i) Poll NodeView phantom audit-row fix (5-line post-hydration first-render ref skip), (ii) Masthead/FooterMeta published_label cosmetic fix, (iii) StatCard grid responsive variant restoration in ImportHtmlModal SuccessView (`grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3`), (iv) results_available notification firing point wiring (Phase 10 polish), (v) AIRSA facet-interpretation generation gap investigation, (vi) coach_messages + messaging subsystem, (vii) Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, (viii) §110 cert-path enrollment asymmetry from Session 84, (ix) H2-MIG-9e SVG bucket tightening, (x) content_assets_archive_reason_check whitelist extension, (xi) newsletter-categories admin UI, (xii) related-article RPC gate-relaxation, (xiii) AuthorBio Option 2 placeholder for unbound external author bios. **Session 102 close artifacts**: this v110 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v104 (adds §151 amendment + Session 102 ship narrative + H6 v1 scope reference), session-102-to-103.md handoff, AND the BrainWise HTML Authoring Spec at `/mnt/user-data/outputs/BRAINWISE_HTML_AUTHORING_SPEC.md` (796 lines, standalone reference doc, target home is internal-docs repo as `brainwise-html-authoring-spec.md`).
