*v107 - Session 100 OPEN — H2-FE-Pass 8 (Poll node) scoped. Pre-Lovable recon discovered three corrections to the Session 99 handoff that materially shape Session 100's plan: (1) **Poll backend is already complete** — newsletter_polls + newsletter_poll_votes tables + 5 RPCs (create_poll, update_poll, archive_poll, vote_on_poll, get_poll_results) all shipped Session 96-97 H2-MIG-7 and end-to-end smoke-tested. The handoff's "needs precursor H2-MIG-9 list_admin_polls RPC" refers only to a hypothetical admin picker; given the schema scopes polls to article_id + node_id with NOT NULL FK to newsletter_articles, polls cannot be reused cross-article, so list_admin_polls is unnecessary. Confirmed via live SQL: `list_admin_polls` does NOT exist in pg_proc; the 5 voting/lifecycle RPCs all exist. **Q3=A locked: no list_admin_polls migration.** (2) **C3=A authenticated-only voting is already locked at the SCHEMA level**, not a Session 100 decision — newsletter_poll_votes.PK is (poll_id, voter_user_id), there is no voter_session_id column. The Session 100 opener's Path B (anonymous voting via session_id) would require dropping the PK, adding a column, rewriting vote_on_poll and get_poll_results for dual-aggregate paths — a future initiative, not a 1-cycle ship. Plan 1 honored. (3) **tiptapDocToPlainText.ts does not exist** — searched src/components/newsletter/tiptap/, src/components/newsletter/editor/, src/lib/, and the full tiptap module index; no file exists, no consumer references it. The Session 99 handoff's "deferred across 3+ passes now, must ship in Session 100" describes a phantom file. Current search uses `list_admin_newsletter_articles` ILIKE against `title + excerpt` only; body-text indexing has no consumer today. **D1=b locked: drop tiptapDocToPlainText.ts from H2-FE-Final cleanup scope; carry as a future initiative ("body-text search/excerpt indexing — needs consumer + extractor + RPC scoping; not a cleanup task") on the deferred list.** **Architectural correction to Q2 made during recon**: get_poll_results returns {found:false} for non-published articles, so editor preview CAN'T use it during authoring. Q2 revised to **Q2=A' (editor renders question/options from local NodeView state; reader-NodeView calls get_poll_results)** — the SubscribeBlock pattern (P7a), not the RelatedArticles pattern (P7b). Same `.newsletter-poll--*` CSS classes for visual parity per §144. **Q1=A enforcement**: AdminNewsletterArticle.tsx mounts NewsletterEditor with `articleId={articleId ?? ""}` in create mode (editor mounts before first save). PollNodeView reads articleId via useNewsletterEditorContext() and renders "Save the article first to add a poll" placeholder if empty; once articleId is non-empty, the NodeView calls create_poll on first user Save and writes back the returned poll_id via updateAttributes ({poll_id}) — Audio's null-asset_id → upload → updateAttributes pattern, adapted. Single attr on the atom node: poll_id (uuid|null). **Pattern decision: Poll is the first H2 node where the durable data lives in a DB row, not in TipTap node attrs.** The doc serializes only poll_id; question/options/style/votes_visible/is_locked all live in newsletter_polls. **Single Lovable cycle planned** with everything bundled: Poll.ts schema, PollNodeView (editor), PollReaderNodeView (reader, calls get_poll_results, vote_on_poll on click), buildExtensions.ts add (1 import + 1 registration), NewsletterEditor.tsx EDITABLE_NODE_OVERRIDES + import, NewsletterSlashMenu.tsx INTERACTIVE entry (3rd position after CTA + SubscribeBlock), NewsletterArticle.tsx reader render switch (case after newsletterFootnotes), NewsletterBubbleMenu.tsx blockedParents add, types.ts NewsletterPollAttrs interface + CustomNewsletterNode union row, tiptap/index.ts export, newsletter-prose.css Poll selectors append + consolidation pass (D2=a folded in). **Reasons strings**: create_poll uses `Created poll in newsletter article (article: <id>)`; update_poll uses `Updated poll in newsletter article (article: <id>)`. **Options shape**: [{id, label}] arrays where id is crypto.randomUUID(); generated once on option-add, never re-generated (so existing votes by option_id remain valid). **NodeView UX**: empty article placeholder | question input + options list (add/remove/edit option label with id stable) + style picker pill (buttons|bars) + votes_visible toggle + locked toggle | "Saving poll…" overlay during create_poll | once poll_id set, options-list inputs become read-only IF any votes exist (NodeView surfaces update_poll's 22023 error to user as toast and reverts input). **Reader UX**: get_poll_results loaded via useQuery keyed on poll_id; renders question, options as buttons (style=buttons) or labeled bars (style=bars); on click, vote_on_poll then invalidate the query to re-fetch results; shows percentage bars after vote IF votes_visible=true, else "Thanks for voting" without numbers; locked polls render but disable buttons; archived polls render nothing (found:false → null). **Auth states for reader**: anonymous user clicking vote button → vote_on_poll raises 42501, surface as inline "Sign in to vote" CTA; user who already voted → buttons show vote-confirmation state via user_vote field on results payload. **D2=a locked**: while appending Poll CSS, audit newsletter-prose.css for duplicate selectors across P6a/P6b/P7a/P7b/P7c appends; consolidate any obvious duplicates. Folded into the Poll Lovable prompt. **Pre-Lovable recon complete; drafting Pass 8 prompt. **H2-FE-Pass 8 SHIPPED Session 100 (single Lovable cycle, clean).** Type-check clean — verified by Claude via `npx tsc -b --noEmit` after pulling main locally (EXIT=0, zero output). 3 new files + 8 modified files exactly per spec. Counts verified post-ship: EDITABLE_NODE_OVERRIDES grew 32→33; INTERACTIVE slash category grew 2→3 (CTA + SubscribeBlock + Poll); reader render-switch grew 5→6 cases; Vote icon used (BarChart3 collision avoided per StatGrid precedent); newsletter-prose.css Poll block has 16 selector instances at EOF starting line 2136. **Implementation pattern locked**: Poll is the first H2 node where durable data lives in a DB row (`newsletter_polls`), not in TipTap node attrs. The doc serializes only `poll_id`; question/options/style/votes_visible/is_locked all live in DB. PollNodeView has a Path A / Path B split internally — Path A (poll_id null) seeds local state and offers a Create poll CTA calling create_poll; Path B (poll_id set) hydrates via direct table reads against newsletter_polls + newsletter_poll_votes (both RLS-permitted for super-admin per verified policies), then debounced update_poll on edits. Reader NodeView calls get_poll_results, never reads tables directly. Vote click on reader maps 42501 → inline signin CTA, 23505 → silent refetch, success → refetch. **Phantom audit-row concern flagged but deferred (Cole's call)**: the post-hydration useEffect re-triggers an update_poll call with the just-hydrated values, writing a no-op audit row each time an editor opens an existing poll. Five-line fix possible via post-hydration first-render ref skip; deferred to future patch since audit-log pollution is harmless and the fix's own cycle cost > current pain. Watchpoint, not a blocker. **CSS consolidation pass (D2=a) was a no-op**: Lovable scanned newsletter-prose.css for duplicate selectors emerged across P6a/P6b/P7a/P7b/P7c appends, found no obvious duplicates, left file untouched per spec instruction "if no duplicates surface, leave the file unchanged in that part — don't reorganize for the sake of reorganizing." Confirmed Session 95's CSS-append discipline has held across 5 passes. **Backend smoke-test deferred to Session 101 (Cole's call: ship type-check-clean, skip live smoke, move to H2-FE-Final).** Acceptable risk profile: every code path in PollNodeView + PollReaderNodeView has been read and verified against locked RPC signatures + RLS policies + type-check. No runtime surprises expected. **H2-FE-Final SHIPPED Session 100 (zero Lovable cycles).** All three originally-scoped items resolve as: (1) buildExtensions.ts ordering + completeness check — audited post-Pass 8 ship via Python script reading the file: 63 names registered, every imported node/mark is in the array (zero orphans), every locally-defined extension (NewsletterCodeBlock, NewsletterTable) is registered, every external library extension (StarterKit configures, TextStyleWithFontSize, Link, Placeholder, TableRow/Header/Cell, the 33 H2 nodes + 10 H2 marks) is accounted for; ordering is StarterKit base → custom configures → custom blocks → marks, no reorder needed; **file is clean, zero edits**; (2) tiptapDocToPlainText.ts extensions — D1=b at session open: deferred as future initiative ("body-text search/excerpt indexing — needs consumer + extractor + RPC scoping; not a cleanup task") since the file doesn't exist and has no consumer; (3) newsletter-prose.css final cleanup — folded into Pass 8 via D2=a, Lovable scanned and found zero duplicates emerged across P6a/P6b/P7a/P7b/P7c appends, file untouched per spec. **Result: H2-FE-Final required ZERO additional Lovable cycles — Pass 8 ship completed H2-FE entirely.** **GROUP H CYCLE H2 = COMPLETE.** All 8 frontend passes shipped: Pass 1-2-3-5 (Session 98) + Pass 6 (P6a + P6b, Session 99) + Pass 7 (P7a + P7b + P7c, Session 99) + Pass 8 (Session 100). Across H2: 33 net new H2 nodes registered, 10 new marks registered, 8 backend migrations (Session 99 batch), 1 Edge Function redeploy (request-asset-upload v5→v6), 2 §-rule corrections (§136 column count 7→8, §142 column-name correction), 1 §-rule lock (§147 SQL RPC signature changes require Edge Function audit). **Session 100 retrospective lessons**: (a) **Always verify what the handoff claims about backend state before scoping a new feature** — Session 100 opener said H2-MIG-9 list_admin_polls was a prerequisite; live SQL recon revealed the entire Poll backend already shipped Session 96-97 H2-MIG-7 (5 RPCs, 2 tables, all smoke-tested) and list_admin_polls was unnecessary given the article-scoped FK design. Time-saver: ~1-2 cycles. (b) **The phantom-file pattern is real** — Session 100 opener listed tiptapDocToPlainText.ts as "deferred across 3+ passes, must ship"; the file does not exist in the codebase and has no consumer (search uses ILIKE on title+excerpt only). Carrying nonexistent work forward as "deferred" is itself a defect; the diagnosis must include a presence check before scoping. Locked as a process discipline going forward: when a handoff describes "deferred file X", verify file X exists before scoping work on it. (c) **Lovable Credit Conservation Protocol held**: 1 Lovable cycle for H2-FE-Pass 8 (Poll node), 0 cycles for H2-FE-Final (everything verified or deferred), backend SQL/RLS verification done first via Supabase MCP before drafting prompt. (d) **Pre-Lovable file pulls saved a real bug**: Lovable's slash menu icon choice — when I drafted the prompt I picked `BarChart3` initially, but pulling NewsletterSlashMenu.tsx imports showed BarChart3 was already used by StatGrid; corrected to `Vote` before prompt sent. Prevented a two-items-share-an-icon UX collision. (e) **Direct table reads in editor NodeViews are legitimate** when the canonical RPC gates on article published status — Pattern documented: get_poll_results returns {found:false} for draft articles, so editor hydration must use direct table reads via super-admin RLS (verified policy newsletter_polls_super_admin_all permits SELECT). This is the first H2 node where the durable data lives in a DB row outside the doc; future similar patterns should follow this precedent. **Group H next: Cycle H3 — server-side hydration scope.** AuthorBio and other reader NodeViews currently render full client-side; H3 work involves SSR-hydration for empty skeleton renders (FooterMeta auto-render-from-article-fields hook, AuthorBio reader-path Mode 2 hydration). Distinct cycle from H2. **Session 100 closeout ready.***

*v109 - Session 101 CLOSE — **Group H Cycle H4 SHIPPED. H5 gap discovered + audited; H5 cycle deferred to Session 102 with full inventory in hand.** **H4 Edge Function `import-html-images` v1 DEPLOYED** via Supabase MCP — function ID `53d32929-6e0d-4c0c-9e7c-479dcc544d04`, verify_jwt:true per §107, ezbr_sha256 `bb2fd15e894b9910628d6c0d573dd1ce180a6bb3ea23517fce58665e96e9de6c`, 521 LOC at `/home/claude/h4/import-html-images-index.ts`. SSRF defense includes IPv4 private ranges + 127.x + 169.254.x + 172.16-31.x + 192.168.x + 0.x + cloud metadata; IPv6 ::1, ::, fc00::/7, fe80::/10, IPv4-mapped (`::ffff:a.b.c.d`); `.local` mDNS blocked; manual-redirect fetch with per-hop URL re-validation (max 5 hops); schemes restricted to http/https. Limits: 30 images/request, 10MB per image (Content-Length pre-check + streamed byte cap), 8s per-image timeout via AbortController, 120s aggregate phase deadline (under Supabase 150s wall clock), worker-pool concurrency=5. MIME allowlist: jpeg/png/webp/gif (svg+xml dropped for layer-2 alignment with uploadUserAsset). Class A auth (`auth.getClaims` + super-admin gate via users.account_type lookup). Auth-layer + gateway curl tests run from Supabase MCP environment (no super-admin JWT available there, so content-layer tests deferred to live modal smoke): OPTIONS preflight returns 204 with all 3 CORS headers; GET returns 401 from gateway (`UNAUTHORIZED_NO_AUTH_HEADER`); anon JWT as bearer returns 401 from function (`{"error":"invalid_jwt"}`); bogus token returns 401 from gateway (`UNAUTHORIZED_INVALID_JWT_FORMAT`); no Authorization header returns 401 from gateway; no apikey + Authorization-only returns 401 from function (gateway accepts apikey-absent if Authorization is valid signed JWT — apikey is NOT a hard gateway requirement, Authorization IS). Cole's reconciliation pushback that "apikey may be enforced at gateway layer before function code runs" was empirically confirmed and is the correct gateway-vs-function diagnostic frame for future curl-test interpretation. **H4 frontend refactor SHIPPED via Lovable single cycle.** Commit `5288c63` "Refactored client-side HTML import" — 651 → 846 LOC on `src/components/newsletter/editor/ImportHtmlModal.tsx`, +195 LOC delta. Zero other files touched (verified directly: Image.ts unchanged, package.json byte-identical, convert-html-to-tiptap Edge Function source untouched, buildExtensions.ts untouched, no new deps). `generateJSON` imported from `@tiptap/core` (transitive via `@tiptap/starter-kit@^3.23.0`; the `@tiptap/html` fallback in spec was not needed). 8-step pipeline: DOMParser parse → collect `<img>` srcs + dedupe → split data: vs network → client preflight count vs 30-image limit → POST `import-html-images` (only if network URIs > 0) with subLabel "Fetching N image(s)…" → merge data-URI failures into resolutions as synthetic `scheme_rejected` → `rewriteImgsToSyntheticFigures` mutates parsedDoc replacing every `<img>` (or its enclosing non-newsletter `<figure>`) with `<figure data-newsletter-image="true" data-width="inline" data-asset-id="…">` OR `data-import-failed-src="…"` shell preserving alt + figcaption text → `generateJSON(parsedDoc.body.innerHTML, buildExtensions({editable:false}))` → build ConversionResponse with summed stats including data-URI count. Synthetic-figure helper handles three cases: skip-if-already-wrapped (round-trip case), enclosing-figure replacement (Substack/Medium pattern), bare-img replacement. Lovable's pre-build review caught a critical bug in the original spec's §3 step 5 plan (in-place attr rewrite on `<img>` would produce ZERO newsletterImage nodes because Image.ts parseHTML matches ONLY `figure[data-newsletter-image]` with no bare-img fallback) — synthetic-figure helper is the corrected approach. Lovable shipped one unprompted improvement (module-scope `ImageResolution` interface, used by both helper signature and resolutions map — better hygiene than spec). Type-check verified clean: `npx tsc -b --noEmit` EXIT=0 with zero output after `npm ci` + `npm install --no-save vitest` (vitest test-runner types not in npm ci's prod deps; once installed, no errors anywhere in src/ tree). Post-build verification via raw GitHub pull at HEAD `5288c63`: 0 `convert-html-to-tiptap` references in the modal, 1 `generateJSON` import, helper function present at line 174, runConversion replaced lines 296-476. **SuccessView StatCard grid changed 4-col → 3-col, "Tags dropped" removed** (no longer in ConversionResponse.stats); `f.tag_name` line removed from failures list. Minor cosmetic regression flagged: Lovable used plain `grid-cols-3` instead of `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3` so narrow-mobile shows 3 cards cramped in one row vs old stacked layout — not a blocker. **Soak fallback intact**: `convert-html-to-tiptap` v2 remains ACTIVE (ezbr_sha256 `ffe538f1f4ecd886...` unchanged from Session 95 deploy), untouched by Lovable, untouched by Claude. **D6=a locked**: delete `convert-html-to-tiptap` in Session 102 after Cole confirms ≥1 successful real-article import through the new path during soak. **H4-as-shipped behaves architecturally as designed but exposed a pre-existing import-fidelity gap**: Cole's smoke-test import of `ptp_test_article.html` (the Group H spec's canonical reference article, 522 lines, 0 images) produced correct results for eyebrow + deck + lede (parseHTML rules with class fallbacks) but FAILED for domain grid, index cards, callout dark box (parseHTML rules require `data-newsletter-*` attribute sentinels; external class-based markup doesn't match). Cole's pushback was specific: "the domain grid did not render for the five domains and the boxes for the indexies did not render and the callout dark box didn't render." This is the H5 gap — NOT an H4 regression. Root cause is upstream and structural: most H2 parseHTML rules across Sessions 96-100 were written for round-trip optimization (newsletter authors save + reload their own content, which always carries `data-newsletter-*` sentinels from renderHTML) but were never extended with class-based or tag-based fallback selectors for external content ingestion. **H5 pre-audit completed in-session via Python script** (`/home/claude/h4/audit_parsehtml.py`) reading every node + mark file's `parseHTML()` block, classifying each selector as round-trip-only (data-attr or BW-namespaced class) or import-friendly (generic class/tag external sources emit). Results: **47 node schemas analyzed across 37 node files (10 are composite parent+child pairs in single file). 41 schemas are ROUND-TRIP ONLY — need H5 import-fallback work. 6 schemas are already import-friendly**: Aside (`aside:not([data-newsletter-callout])` + `div.aside` + `div.sidebar`), Byline (`div.byline` + `p.byline` + `address.byline` + class~ patterns), Eyebrow (`div.eyebrow` + `p.eyebrow` + `[class~="eyebrow"]` + `[class~="kicker"]` + `[class~="category"]`), Lead (`p.deck` + `p.lede` + `p.lead` + `p.standfirst` + `[class*="lead-paragraph"]`), Masthead (`div.masthead` + `div.topbar` + `header.publication`), SectionRule (`hr.section-rule` + `hr.dot-divider` + `div.section-rule` + `div.section-break`). **Marks**: 10 schemas analyzed, 2 round-trip only (Definition, FootnoteRef), 8 import-friendly. **The 41 round-trip-only node schemas needing H5 import-fallback** (full list saved at `/mnt/user-data/outputs/H5_AUDIT_RESULTS.txt`): Audio, AuthorBio, CTA, Callout, Chart, Checklist (×2: parent + item), Citations (×2: parent + entry), CodeDiff, Disclosure (×2: parent + summary), DomainGrid (×2: parent + row), Embed, FooterMeta, Footnotes, FourColumn (×2: parent + pane), FurtherReading, Image, ImageCompare, ImageGallery, IndexRow (×2: parent + card), KeyMoments (×2: parent + moment), Math, Poll, Pullquote, RelatedArticles, StatCallout, StatGrid, StepList (×2: parent + step), SubscribeBlock, Terminal, ThreeColumn (×2: parent + pane), TwoColumn (×2: parent + pane). The 7 nodes Cole's specific test article hit (Byline, SectionRule, DomainGrid+Row, IndexRow+Card, Callout, Pullquote, FooterMeta) are a strict subset of the 41 — Cole's H5 scope decision was C (full 33-node audit before scoping Lovable cycle) which the script delivered. **H5 cycle plan deferred to Session 102 open**: scope-and-cycle-count decision will be made at Session 102 open from the audit data, not now. Likely shape: parseHTML extension on each round-trip-only node adding generic-class or tag-based fallback rules at priority 51 (one above default 50 per §144 convention) WITHOUT removing or weakening the canonical `data-newsletter-*` rule. Pattern example for DomainGrid: `{ tag: 'section[data-newsletter-domain-grid]' }` stays as canonical match; NEW `{ tag: '[class~="domain-grid"]', priority: 51 }` added as import fallback. Composite parents (DomainGrid, IndexRow, Citations, Checklist, FourColumn, KeyMoments, StepList, ThreeColumn, TwoColumn, Disclosure) require both parent + child schemas to be extended. Important: `getAttrs` callbacks must handle missing `data-*` attrs by reading from visible content (e.g., DomainRow's number+label+desc+count read from rendered text children rather than `data-number/data-label/...` attrs which won't exist on external content). The existing renderHTML stays unchanged in every case — H5 is import-side only, round-trip output unaffected. **Process discipline reinforced**: "parseHTML rules optimized for round-trip do NOT automatically import; verify import paths separately when shipping schema." Candidate §-rule §151 locked in arch-ref v103 below. **Phantom-file lesson revisited and partially reversed**: Session 100 retrospective lock said `tipTapDocToPlainText.ts` doesn't exist anywhere in src/. Investigation Session 101: the file DOES exist at `src/components/newsletter/versions/tipTapDocToPlainText.ts` (3.4KB, two consumers — ImportHtmlModal.tsx imports it at line 51, used as `firstNWords(tipTapDocToPlainText(result.tiptap_doc), 200)` for preview text). Session 100 retrospective searched `src/components/newsletter/tiptap/`, `src/components/newsletter/editor/`, `src/lib/` but did NOT search `src/components/newsletter/versions/`. The phantom-file pattern lesson stands in principle — verifying file existence before scoping work is right — but the specific empirical claim that the file was a phantom was wrong. D1=b from Session 100 (drop tipTapDocToPlainText.ts from H2-FE-Final cleanup scope as a future initiative) reached the correct outcome via the wrong reasoning: no consumer of body-text indexing exists today, so no work needed, BUT the file itself is real and is in active use for ImportHtmlModal preview text. New discipline: when verifying file existence, grep the entire `src/` tree, not just the directories that "should" contain it. **Session 101 final totals**: 1 backend Edge Function deploy (import-html-images v1, new), 0 backend SQL migrations, 1 Lovable build cycle (ImportHtmlModal refactor), 1 audit script run (H5 pre-audit), 0 mid-apply backend bug fixes. Both Edge Functions ACTIVE: import-html-images v1 (new), convert-html-to-tiptap v2 (soak fallback, untouched). **§-rule observations Session 101 (folded into arch-ref v103)**: §150 NEW locked (SSRF defense in HTTP-fetcher Edge Functions must use `redirect: "manual"` with per-hop URL re-validation; `redirect: "follow"` is vulnerable to DNS-rebinding via redirect to private IP; old convert-html-to-tiptap v2 had this vulnerability since it used implicit follow-redirects, import-html-images v1 explicitly opts out). §151 NEW locked (parseHTML rules optimized for round-trip do NOT import; verify import paths separately when shipping schema). §107 + §142 + §147 + §144 all referenced + held without exception. **Carryforward to Session 102 deferred list**: (a) **H5 cycle scope decision + planning** — primary Session 102 work; audit data at `/mnt/user-data/outputs/H5_AUDIT_RESULTS.txt`; (b) delete convert-html-to-tiptap Edge Function per D6=a after Cole confirms ≥1 successful real-article import via new path; (c) Poll NodeView phantom audit-row fix; (d) Masthead/FooterMeta published_label cosmetic fix; (e) results_available notification firing point wiring; (f) AIRSA facet-interpretation generation gap investigation; (g) coach_messages + messaging subsystem; (h) Action-Oriented Voice Redesign; (i) §110 cert-path enrollment asymmetry from Session 84; (j) H2-MIG-9e SVG bucket tightening; (k) content_assets_archive_reason_check whitelist extension; (l) newsletter-categories admin UI; (m) related-article RPC gate-relaxation; (n) StatCard grid responsive variant restoration in ImportHtmlModal SuccessView (cosmetic only, narrow-mobile import preview cards stack better with `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3` than plain `grid-cols-3`).***

Live disk inventory found 33 edit NodeViews already shipped (Sessions 98-100) and 6 reader NodeViews shipped selectively for runtime-behavior cases only (Image: asset_id→URL resolution; Embed: provider+id→iframe src; SubscribeBlock: live SubscribeForm mount; RelatedArticles: get_related_articles_by_* RPC; Footnotes: doc walker aggregation; Poll: get_poll_results RPC). The remaining H3 scope reduced to two items both wired in this single cycle: (a) AdminNewsletterArticle.tsx sidebar additions for 7 article-level fields (eyebrow_text, is_issue_based, issue_label, masthead_publication, masthead_logo_glyph, default_layout_width, theme_variant); (b) NewsletterArticle.tsx reader auto-render logic. **Pre-Lovable backend recon verified all prerequisites**: all 7 newsletter_articles columns exist with sensible defaults; upsert_article RPC v26 already accepts all 7 (frontend caller hardcoded them to null until this cycle); get_article_for_reader RPC payload already includes all 7 fields for both 'granted' and 'paywall' access paths (no Edge Function or RPC work needed); CHECK constraints lock the enums — default_layout_width ∈ {standard,wide,narrow}, theme_variant ∈ {default,editorial,minimal,technical}; live data confirms 'editorial' theme + 'wide' layout already in use (3 'standard'+'default' rows, 1 'wide'+'editorial' row). **Design calls made pre-Lovable**: A (layout-width enum from DB CHECK), B (full 4-variant theme dropdown not MVP subset), C (bounded suppression: first-2 / last-2 doc nodes inspected for auto-render-target match, permits mid-article use of same node types for editorial purposes), D (reader-only auto-render, no editor preview), E (single bundled Lovable cycle), F (two cards — Issue metadata + Layout & theme), G (is_issue_based hides issue_label when false), H (rendering order: Masthead first / Eyebrow second / body / FooterMeta / AuthorBio). **Pattern locked**: auto-render of synthetic TipTap nodes from article-level fields via bounded-suppression doc inspection — inspect only first N / last N doc nodes for matching auto-rendered node types — to avoid duplicating author-inserted instances while permitting same-type-node use elsewhere in the document. Useful precedent for any future "article metadata → reader render" features. **Reader DOM hooks added but no CSS responses yet**: data-theme-variant + data-layout-width attrs on the `.newsletter-prose` wrapper around EditorContent. CSS for the 4 theme variants and 3 layout widths deferred to future cycle — values persist + DOM exposes them, ready for design-system work to hook on without further frontend changes. **Known cosmetic gap (deferred follow-up)**: publishedLabel passed as null per spec; article.published_at is available in the GrantedArticle payload and could be formatted (via existing date-fns helpers elsewhere in codebase) for Masthead date_label + FooterMeta published_label. *v106 - Session 99 CLOSE — **H2-FE-Pass 6 = COMPLETE.** Bundles P6a + P6b shipped. P6a (FooterMeta + Citations + FurtherReading) shipped first half of session; P6b (AuthorBio) shipped after backend avatar infrastructure was put in place. **P6a SHIPPED — 3 of 4 nodes.** Type-check clean. FooterMeta (atom, 3 attrs: tags[]/issue_label/published_label, manually-entered for P6a — auto-render-from-article-fields hook is H3 scope), Citations (composite parent + entry child: parent attrs style/title, child attrs link only with content:"inline*", position-based CSS-counter auto-numbering via counter-reset/counter-increment on parent ol + ::before content:counter(citation) on entries — NO `number` attr on child, sets the auto-renumber-on-reorder precedent that Pass 7 FootnoteRef + Footnotes will ride on), FurtherReading (atom with entries[] {title,url,source?,description?} + optional title — full Byline dnd-kit repeater pattern with 4-input EntryRow + linkMode for URL validation). 4 NodeViews (FooterMeta, CitationsParent, CitationEntry, FurtherReading) all wired in NewsletterEditor.tsx EDITABLE_NODE_OVERRIDES per §146. 3 new slash items appended to END of EDITORIAL category (after Byline + existing Callout/Pullquote): FooterMeta (Tags icon), Citations (BookMarked icon), FurtherReading (BookOpen icon). parseHTML priorities: 60 on Citations parent + entry (beats StarterKit orderedList/listItem on bare ol/li), 51 on FooterMeta + FurtherReading (future-defensive on footer/section). All selectors bare-tag + [data-*] attr per §145. blockedParents extended with newsletterFooterMeta + newsletterFurtherReading (atoms, no text selection); newsletterCitations + newsletterCitationEntry intentionally NOT blocked (entries hold inline content needing bubble menu for bold/italic/link marks). Slash menu insert for Citations seeds one child entry with single-space text content (cursor needs landing spot; empty inline* would be schema-valid but UX dead). FurtherReading entries default [] with NodeView useEffect auto-inserting one empty entry on mount (Byline auto-insert pattern). Lovable deviation accepted: NodeView files placed in src/components/newsletter/tiptap/nodeviews/ (existing 22-NodeView convention) NOT src/components/newsletter/editor/nodeviews/ (prompt suggested) — §146 covers wiring location not file location; file-location convention candidate for §147 lock at session close. NodeViewContent `as` prop type escape `as any` used for `<ol>` and `<span>` matches existing CodeBlockNodeView precedent (TipTap library type restricts `as` to "div"). **Pattern locked Bundle P6a: CSS counters drive position-based auto-numbering for ordered composites — counter-reset on parent ol, counter-increment + ::before on child li, parent data-style attr selects between "numbered" (`counter(citation) ". "`) and "bracketed" (`"[" counter(citation) "] "`).** No JS positional logic needed; reorder/delete renumbers automatically via standard DOM CSS counter rendering, identical behavior in editor and reader paths. **Pass 7 FootnoteRef + Footnotes will reuse this technique for footnote numbering** — same counter-reset/counter-increment pattern with different selectors. Pass 6 remaining: AuthorBio (Bundle P6b — depends on users.bio column shipped H2-MIG-6 Session 96; backend ready, frontend pending recon). **P6b scope expanded mid-session to include full avatar infrastructure (Cole's call to ship β not defer):** D1=A (single user_id FK per AuthorBio block + multi-block support for multi-author articles), D2=C (read-only bio display with explicit edit-toggle calling update_user_bio), D3=A (live resolve at read time via new get_newsletter_author_bio RPC), D4=full avatar infrastructure with NEW user-avatars bucket (public-read, owner-folder writes, super-admin writes, service_role_all = 5 policies). **Four migrations planned (one more than original draft after pre-flight DB recon surfaced orphan-sweep parent enumeration gap)**: H2-MIG-9b (schema: users.avatar_asset_id + content_asset_refs.user_id + DROP+ADD CHECK constraint extended from 7 to 8 parents + CREATE user-avatars bucket + 5 storage.objects RLS policies + **bundled fix: 4 missing newsletter-article-images super-admin INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/SELECT policies mirroring lesson-assets pattern**), H2-MIG-9c (DROP+CREATE request_asset_upload adding p_user_id param + user mode in 5 ladders, NEW set_user_avatar RPC mirroring update_user_bio gate, INSERT user_avatar_set into super_admin_action_types per §142 — note §142 column-list is stale, actual booleans are requires_mfa+requires_justification+is_mutation+denylist_during_impersonation not the names §142 documents; correction lands at session close), H2-MIG-9d (DROP+CREATE reap_pending_uploads extending Sweep 2 parent enumeration from 7 to 8 — adds user_id OR branch with EXISTS users WHERE u.id = car.user_id AND u.deleted_at IS NULL. Without this migration, every user avatar would be auto-reaped 24h after upload because Sweep 2 explicitly enumerates each parent FK type in an OR EXISTS chain and an unenumerated parent reads as "no live parent" = orphan. Mandatory before 9b is safe to ship.), H2-MIG-9a (NEW get_newsletter_author_bio anon-callable read RPC returning {user_id, display_name, bio, avatar_asset_id, avatar_url} — public bucket means raw public URL not signed). **ALL FOUR APPLIED + SMOKE-TESTED Session 99 mid-session.** Order of apply: 9b → 9d → 9c → 9a (each depends on prior). **One mid-apply bug caught:** initial set_user_avatar draft INSERTed directly into wrong audit-log table name (super_admin_action_log doesn't exist; actual is super_admin_audit_log) with wrong column names (actor_user_id should be super_admin_user_id, target_user_id should be affected_user_id, session_id NOT NULL was missing entirely). Fix: rewrote set_user_avatar to call public.log_super_admin_action helper RPC verbatim mirroring update_user_bio pattern (which was the locked D2 parity reference but wasn't pulled body-first before drafting). Audit row capture verified set+clear paths. Lesson locked Session 99: when drafting an RPC meant to mirror an existing one, pg_get_functiondef the existing body BEFORE drafting, not after smoke-test catches the drift. Pre-flight recon caught H2-MIG-9d need (Lovable's hypothesis "sweepable via generic asset_id join" was wrong — Sweep 2 enumerates parents explicitly) and §142 column-name staleness (rule documents requires_reason/requires_target/is_destructive/surfaces_in_recent_actions but actual columns are requires_mfa/requires_justification/is_mutation/denylist_during_impersonation; mirror user_bio_updated row exactly). One latent issue carried forward to deferred list: content_assets_archive_reason_check whitelist has 10 values but none for user-avatar-specific lifecycle reasons (used manual_archive for smoke-test cleanup; whitelist may need extension if user-avatar-specific reasons surface later). **Backend ready for Lovable P6b cycle.** **Edge Function v5 → v6 DEPLOYED Session 99 mid-session** as additional follow-up: `request-asset-upload` v5 source did not forward `p_user_id` to the SQL RPC despite v5's `RequestBody` interface only knowing 7 parents (last addition was `newsletter_article_id` in Session 95). Without this update, every avatar upload from `uploadUserAsset` would fail at runtime — the SQL RPC sees `p_user_id = null`, no parent classifies, raises `asset_must_scope_to_parent_or_library`. v6 adds `user_id?: string | null` to `RequestBody` interface and `p_user_id: body.user_id ?? null` to the `.rpc()` call. Strictly additive: every v5 param preserved verbatim, every v1-v5 caller (omitting user_id) keeps working unchanged. verify_jwt:true preserved per §107. ezbr_sha256 `4e4489fcbbfc...` → `5a5772923f54...`. **Pattern locked Session 99: when a SQL RPC signature changes, the Edge Function body that wraps it must be audited and redeployed even if the change is just an additive param — Edge Functions don't auto-pick-up RPC signature changes. Pulled the source via get_edge_function BEFORE drafting v6 per Lovable Credit Conservation Protocol diagnose-before-prescribe. This is precisely the Session 95 Bug 1 pattern (convert-html-to-tiptap v1→v2 dropped `p_reason` after finalize_asset_upload signature changed) which should have made me check the Edge Function side BEFORE applying H2-MIG-9c — caught now before any user-facing call, but a beat late.** **Bundle P6b SHIPPED — H2-FE-Pass 6 final node, AuthorBio.** Type-check clean per harness. 3 new files + 7 modified, all per plan with zero deviations: NewsletterAuthorBio atom (single attr user_id, parseHTML priority 51 on aside[data-newsletter-author-bio], emits empty skeleton aside for H3 hydration); AuthorBioNodeView with 3-mode internal state machine — Mode 1 user picker (initials-only candidate-list reusing existing `["newsletter-author-candidates"]` React Query key for cache dedup across AdminNewsletterArticle.tsx and the NodeView), Mode 2 resolved author card (avatar + name + bio + "Change author" link + hover-reveal avatar upload), Mode 3 bio edit toggle (pencil opens textarea + Save/Cancel calling update_user_bio with reason `Edited via newsletter author bio block (article: <id|"editor">)`); uploadUserAsset.ts new helper (5MB ceiling, image MIMEs excluding svg+xml per Lovable's client-side defense choice, routes via request-asset-upload Edge Function v6 with user_id body param + ref_field "avatar", does NOT call set_user_avatar after upload because RPC Ladder 6 user branch already writes users.avatar_asset_id direct-column). Slash item appended to END of EDITORIAL category after FurtherReading (UserCircle icon). NodeAuthorBioEdit wired in NewsletterEditor.tsx EDITABLE_NODE_OVERRIDES per §146. newsletterAuthorBio added to blockedParents in NewsletterBubbleMenu.tsx. CSS appended to newsletter-prose.css reader-path styles. articleId surfaced via existing useNewsletterEditorContext() hook (Lovable correctly identified this established React context pattern instead of digging editor.options.editorProps). `Json` return type from get_newsletter_author_bio required `as unknown as AuthorBioData | null` two-step cast — same TipTap-style type-escape pattern P6a used for NodeViewContent `as` prop on `<ol>`/`<span>`. Reader-path hydration script for the empty aside skeleton remains H3 scope — editor renders Mode 2 full card, reader renders empty skeleton until hydration ships. **H2-FE-Pass 6 = COMPLETE: FooterMeta + Citations (composite) + FurtherReading + AuthorBio.** **Deferred forward-compat carried forward to closeout:** (1) candidateAuthors query can be extended to include avatar_asset_id for picker-row avatars once roster grows beyond ~20 users or search combobox is needed — not blocking; (2) user-avatars bucket policy permits image/svg+xml but uploadUserAsset client-side helper drops it for XSS defense — layer-2 alignment gap, optional tightening migration H2-MIG-9e (would remove svg+xml from bucket allowed_mime_types) deferred to session close decision; (3) §147 candidate rule: "SQL RPC signature changes require Edge Function audit" — pattern observed in Session 95 Bug 1 + Session 99 v5→v6 deploy; (4) informal TypeScript type-escape pattern (`as any` for TipTap NodeViewContent `as` prop; `as unknown as T` for PostgREST `Json` return casts) is emerging convention worth noting in arch-ref but not yet §-locked. **Session 99 totals through P6b ship:** 2 Lovable build cycles (P6a + P6b) + 4 backend migrations (H2-MIG-9b, 9d, 9c, 9a) + 1 Edge Function redeploy (request-asset-upload v5→v6) + 1 mid-apply backend bug fix migration (set_user_avatar audit-log column mismatch caught at smoke test). **Bundle P7a SHIPPED — H2-FE-Pass 7 partial (4 of 6 nodes).** Type-check clean. 4 new schema units (CTA atom + SubscribeBlock atom + Disclosure composite parent+summary child + Definition mark), 3 new editor NodeViews + 1 new reader-path NodeView (SubscribeBlockReaderNodeView wrapping live G6 SubscribeForm component — NOT modifying SubscribeForm itself, additive reader-side wrapper only), 8 file edits. NEW slash menu category INTERACTIVE introduced between MEDIA and LAYOUT (category enum is now BASIC|EDITORIAL|MEDIA|INTERACTIVE|LAYOUT|TECHNICAL — 6 categories). CTA in INTERACTIVE with Megaphone icon, SubscribeBlock in INTERACTIVE with Mail icon, Disclosure in LAYOUT with ChevronDown icon. Definition is mark-only — no slash item; bubble menu BookMarked trigger pre-fills from `editor.getAttributes("definition")` matching Abbr precedent. **Two design refinements locked Session 99 P7a:** (1) Disclosure native click-collapse SUPPRESSED in editor via onClick preventDefault on summary in NodeView; allowed in reader path because reader doesn't mount NodeViews (only Image/Embed/SubscribeBlock are reader-wrapped). The "Open/Closed by default" pill in NodeView controls is the only path to mutate default_open state in the editor. (2) CTA tracking_id ships as opaque data-tracking-id attr with inline NodeView helper text "Analytics consumption coming in a later release" — sets correct author expectations since no reader-path consumer exists today. **Lovable deviation from plan accepted as cleaner-than-planned:** CTA NodeView preview button calls the real `.newsletter-cta--primary/secondary/ghost` reader CSS classes instead of a forked editor-class system. Works because `.newsletter-prose` wraps the editor body, so reader CSS inherits. Visual parity guaranteed by construction. Pattern locked: when an editor NodeView needs preview chrome that should match reader visuals, reuse the reader CSS classes via the `.newsletter-prose` container rather than forking a parallel editor-only class system. **SubscribeForm not modified** — D2 A3 disabled-look-alike preview is implemented as static skeleton inside SubscribeBlockNodeView (cream wrapper + disabled email input + disabled orange Subscribe button + greyed Turnstile placeholder + "Preview only — not interactive" badge). Reader path's SubscribeBlockReaderNodeView renders `<SubscribeForm source={"newsletter-article-"+articleId} variant="inline" />` — full real interactive form. parseHTML priorities 60 on all 4 scoped selectors (`a[data-newsletter-cta]` beats Link mark on bare `<a>`; `div[data-newsletter-subscribe-block]`; `details[data-newsletter-disclosure]`; `summary[data-newsletter-disclosure-summary]`; `span[data-newsletter-definition]`). Disclosure parent content spec is "newsletterDisclosureSummary block+" (not block*) — enforces at least one body block after the summary. blockedParents extended with newsletterCta + newsletterSubscribeBlock (atoms, no inline text); newsletterDisclosure parent + newsletterDisclosureSummary intentionally NOT blocked so authors can apply bold/italic/link/definition marks to summary text via bubble menu. **H2-FE-Pass 7 remaining: P7b (RelatedArticles — backend coupling, needs filter-shape work on list_admin_newsletter_articles RPC, potentially H2-MIG-10 prequel), P7c (FootnoteRef + Footnotes — paired mark + block, auto-numbering complexity spike, CSS counter precedent from P6a Citations applies). H2-FE-Pass 8 (Poll node, needs precursor H2-MIG-9 list_admin_polls RPC) and H2-FE-Final remaining.** **P7b backend SHIPPED Session 99 mid-session:** 3 migrations applied (H2-MIG-10a-1 + H2-MIG-10a-2 + H2-MIG-10b). H2-MIG-10a-1 creates `newsletter_categories` table (id, slug w/ slug-format CHECK, display_name, sort_order, created_at, archived_at) + RLS (anon SELECT active; super-admin reads all) + adds `newsletter_articles.category_id` FK column (nullable, ON DELETE SET NULL) + index on category_id + 4 RPCs (`list_active_newsletter_categories` anon-callable, `create_newsletter_category` + `update_newsletter_category` + `archive_newsletter_category` super-admin gated, all SECDEF, all §140 triple-grants, all calling `log_super_admin_action` helper) + 3 action_type rows (`newsletter_category_created` / `_updated` / `_archived`) under **`content_authoring`** category — initial draft used `newsletter_management` which CHECK constraint `super_admin_action_types_category_check` rejected, surfacing the §142 / §99 whitelist-CHECK pattern again; rolled back the txn cleanly and re-applied with the proper category value. Existing whitelist categories: `impersonation, org_management, user_management, admin_role_management, contract_management, content_authoring, platform_observability, usage_management, mfa_management, audit_reporting` — `content_authoring` is the closest fit semantically and required no schema change. H2-MIG-10a-2 rewrites 4 function bodies for category propagation: **upsert_article** DROP+CREATE (25→26 args, adds `p_category_id uuid` positioned between `p_tags` and `p_reason`; validates against active newsletter_categories before INSERT/UPDATE; persists category_id; emits category_id in before/after audit payloads; §140 triple-grants reapplied); **list_admin_newsletter_articles** DROP+CREATE (5→6 args, adds `p_category_filter uuid DEFAULT NULL`; LEFT JOIN newsletter_categories for denormalized category_display_name + category_slug in returned payload; filter clause added to both count and page CTEs); **get_article_for_reader** CREATE OR REPLACE (signature unchanged, payload extended with category_id + category_slug + category_display_name lookup via LEFT JOIN gated on archived_at IS NULL); **preview_article_as_viewer_class** CREATE OR REPLACE (same payload extension as get_article_for_reader). H2-MIG-10b creates 3 anon-callable SECDEF RPCs for RelatedArticles reader-path resolution: `get_related_articles_by_tags(p_source_article_id, p_tag_match_mode 'any'|'all', p_max_count)` — uses `na.tags && v_source.tags` for ANY mode (overlap intersection count for ordering) and `v_source.tags <@ na.tags` for ALL mode (source tags fully contained in candidate); `get_related_articles_by_category(p_source_article_id, p_max_count)` — same-category match, gates on source category archive state (returns empty + `source_category_archived: true` flag if source's category is archived); `get_related_articles_by_ids(p_article_ids uuid[], p_max_count)` — preserves source array order via `unnest WITH ORDINALITY`. **All 3 related-article RPCs filter to `gate = 'public'` only** (subscriber_aggregate + plan_tier articles excluded from results) — deliberate design call to avoid leaking paywalled article existence/metadata to non-subscribers via "you might also like" cards on public reader pages. Can be relaxed later by allowing all gate values + adding members-only badge in reader card. **§147 candidate validated again**: convert-html-to-tiptap Edge Function audited via get_edge_function BEFORE H2-MIG-10a-2 applied — does NOT call upsert_article (only converts HTML to TipTap JSON and returns; frontend calls upsert_article separately). DOES call request_asset_upload with v5 16-arg shape which still works because new `p_user_id` param defaults to NULL. No Edge Function changes needed for H2-MIG-10 series. **CRITICAL Lovable cycle prerequisite**: frontend `upsert_article` callers MUST be updated to pass `p_category_id` (or null) before they can save articles — old 25-arg signature is gone (DROP+CREATE), so any existing caller would fail with "function not found" until updated. This will be a prominent flag in the P7b Lovable build prompt. **Pre-flight discipline observed**: all four function bodies retrieved via `pg_get_functiondef` and the upsert_article signature confirmed via `pg_get_function_arguments` BEFORE drafting migration SQL. Mirrored existing user_avatar_set action_type row shape exactly per §142 column discipline. **Pass 7 (CTA, SubscribeBlock, RelatedArticles, Disclosure, Definition, FootnoteRef+Footnotes), Pass 8 (Poll node, needs precursor H2-MIG-9 list_admin_polls RPC), and H2-FE-Final (wireup cleanup) remain.** Realistic call: P6b is the last bundle of Session 99 given the time spent on the avatar infrastructure pivot. Pass 7-8-Final land in Session 100. Deep recon via Lovable surfaced: (a) finalize_asset_upload is generic over parent FK columns (no DROP+CREATE needed there — one migration saved); (b) request_asset_upload super-admin gate is universal across all parent types — self-service avatar upload would deviate from established model so D4 revised to super-admin-only RPC path with owner-folder storage RLS shipped for forward compat only; (c) no reusable user-picker exists in codebase — AuthorBio NodeView uses candidate-list pattern matching existing AdminNewsletterArticle.tsx authors picker (cmdk combobox deferred until author roster grows); (d) uploadNewsletterAsset is newsletter-only with hardcoded articleId — fork to new uploadUserAsset.ts (not extend); (e) walker family unaffected since user_id is in node attrs not asset refs (avatar asset_id lives on users.avatar_asset_id + content_asset_refs row, not in doc body); (f) AuthorBio is FIRST user-bound image upload in codebase. **newsletter-article-images RLS policy gap discovered mid-recon (finding #4) — verified via DB query: bucket has only 2 policies (public_select + service_role_all), missing 4 authenticated super-admin INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/SELECT policies that lesson-assets and lesson-thumbnails have. Investigation confirmed: not an active bug — uploads work today because request-asset-upload Edge Function generates pre-signed URLs server-side using service-role credentials, and storage validates uploads against the URL HMAC signature not against RLS. Proof: 8 objects in bucket all have owner=NULL (service-role uploads, not authenticated-JWT uploads). However represents real defense-in-depth gap: if Supabase tightens default tus behavior to require RLS even with signed URLs (security update), bucket breaks while lesson buckets keep working; any future direct-write path bypassing request_asset_upload would behave unpredictably; storage-layer owner audit is absent. Fix is small (4 policies, ~20 lines SQL mirroring lesson-assets verbatim) — folded into H2-MIG-9b to ship in same migration as user-avatars policy creation rather than deferring. **Bundle P7b SHIPPED Session 99 (two passes — pass 1 hit Lovable time budget mid-cycle with admin form/list + reader switch + CSS items 1-5 incomplete; pass 2 finished them all). Type-check clean — verified by Claude via `npx tsc -b --noEmit` after pulling main locally (EXIT=0, zero errors). 5 new files + 9 file edits. NewsletterRelatedArticles atom with 3-mode internal state machine (by_tags / by_category / manual). RelatedArticlesNodeView reuses `useNewsletterImageUrl` hook for cover thumbnails (cheap, cached), dnd-kit FurtherReadingNodeView pattern for manual-mode reorderable selected-list, picker Dialog reusing `list_admin_newsletter_articles` (D4 — drafts visible to author, public+published filter applies only at reader path), live preview via per-mode `get_related_articles_by_*` RPC. RelatedArticleCardView shared between editor preview and reader path. RelatedArticlesReaderNodeView wired in NewsletterArticle.tsx render switch via `addOptions()` injecting `sourceArticleId: article.id` — mirrors SubscribeBlockReaderNodeView precedent (P7a). NewsletterEditorContext extended additively with `tags?: string[] | null` + `categoryId?: string | null` (optional fields, P6b AuthorBioNodeView only destructures `articleId` so unaffected — confirmed via repo inspection). AdminNewsletterArticle.tsx draft state extended with `category_id` + `tags`, load query selects them, `<select>` for category populated by `list_active_newsletter_categories` query keyed `["newsletter-categories-active"]`, Tags input (comma-separated) added in same Category & Tags card, `<NewsletterEditor tags={draft.tags} categoryId={draft.category_id} />` prop wiring forwards to context. AdminNewsletter.tsx admin list extended with Category badge column reading `category_display_name` from list_admin_newsletter_articles row. `p_category_id` added to upsert_article RPC call (positioned via named-arg semantics — Postgres named args mean position in JS object doesn't matter for routing). newsletter-prose.css P7b block appended at EOF with data-attr scoped selectors per §145. **Bundle P7c SHIPPED Session 99 (single pass, clean).** Type-check clean — verified by Claude via `npx tsc -b --noEmit` after pulling main locally (EXIT=0, zero errors). 5 new files + 6 file edits. **Architecture Option C locked Session 99**: refs own the text via `footnote_text` attr; Footnotes block is a render-time aggregator that walks `editor.state.doc.descendants` and collects FootnoteRef marks in document order, dedupes by mark identity (mark === prevMark reference check across adjacent text nodes), subscribes to `editor.on("transaction")` and bumps a counter via `useState` to re-walk on changes (precedent: NewsletterFloatingPlus.tsx line 78). useFootnotesAggregator hook shared between editor FootnotesNodeView and reader FootnotesReaderNodeView — single source of truth for the walk logic. No stored IDs, no sync, no orphans by construction. **CSS counter alignment achieved via two-counter mechanism**: inline refs use custom `footnote-counter` (counter-reset on `.newsletter-prose`, counter-increment on `[data-newsletter-footnote-ref]`, displayed via `::after` pseudo-element with `counter(footnote-counter)`). Footnotes block uses native `<ol>` list-style decimal. Both walk document order so numbers align by construction regardless of Footnotes block placement (top, middle, or end of article). TipTap renders one DOM `<span>` per contiguous mark run, so CSS counter increments once per ref naturally. **D4-a locked**: marked text stays visible + superscript number appears via `::after` (academic citation style). Bubble menu integration mirrors Definition mark precedent (P7a) exactly: Mode union extended with `{ kind: "footnote_ref"; footnote_text: string }`, default-branch trigger button rendered as `<sup>¹</sup>` text glyph (no lucide icon was a clean match), `renderBody()` case opens 3-row textarea + Apply/Remove/Cancel with Cmd/Ctrl+Enter Apply shortcut (Lovable deviation accepted as precedent for future multi-line-input bubble menu modes — plain Enter would prevent newlines in multi-line footnote bodies). Slash item appended to END of EDITORIAL category after RelatedArticles (BookMarked icon). NodeFootnotesEdit wired in NewsletterEditor.tsx EDITABLE_NODE_OVERRIDES per §146. `newsletterFootnotes` added to blockedParents (atom with no inline content). FootnotesReaderNodeView (default export — matches sibling reader NodeViews convention) wired in NewsletterArticle.tsx render switch after `newsletterRelatedArticles` case. newsletter-prose.css P7c block appended with all data-attr scoped selectors per §145. **CSS counter scoping safety verified**: only 3 `.newsletter-prose` containers exist in codebase (NewsletterArticle.tsx line 471, NewsletterEditor.tsx line 408, ImportHtmlModal.tsx line 647) — none nested. **H2-FE-Pass 7 = COMPLETE: CTA + SubscribeBlock + Disclosure + Definition (P7a) + RelatedArticles (P7b) + FootnoteRef + Footnotes (P7c). 6 of 6 nodes shipped.** **§-rule observations Session 99 (folded into arch-ref v101):** §147 candidate rule locked ("SQL RPC signature changes require Edge Function audit" — pattern observed across Session 95 Bug 1 + Session 99 Edge Function v5→v6 deploy); §142 column-name list correction (actual booleans are requires_mfa + requires_justification + is_mutation + denylist_during_impersonation, NOT the requires_reason/requires_target/is_destructive/surfaces_in_recent_actions names §142 originally documented); §136 column-count update (content_asset_refs now has 8 parent FKs after H2-MIG-9b added user_id, was 7); §144 selector specificity pattern locked (when an editor NodeView needs preview chrome matching reader visuals, reuse the reader CSS classes via the `.newsletter-prose` container rather than forking a parallel editor-only class system — P7a CTA pattern); informal TypeScript type-escape patterns documented (`as any` for TipTap NodeViewContent `as` prop on `<ol>`/`<span>`; `as unknown as T` two-step cast for PostgREST `Json` return types). **Session 99 final ship totals: 5 Lovable build cycles effective (P6a + P6b + P7a + P7b two-pass + P7c single-pass), 8 backend migrations (H2-MIG-9b + 9d + 9c + 9a + 1 mid-apply set_user_avatar fix + H2-MIG-10a-1 with 1 CHECK-constraint mid-apply fix + 10a-2 + 10b), 1 Edge Function redeploy (request-asset-upload v5→v6). H2-FE-Pass 6 + Pass 7 both COMPLETE. Two §-rule corrections + 1 candidate §147 rule.** **Remaining for Group H Cycle H2 (Session 100 opening):** H2-FE-Pass 8 (Poll node — needs precursor H2-MIG-9 list_admin_polls RPC), H2-FE-Final (wireup cleanup: buildExtensions.ts final pass + tiptapDocToPlainText.ts extensions + final newsletter-prose.css cleanup). **Deferred items carried forward to Session 100 deferred list:** (1) optional H2-MIG-9e to drop image/svg+xml from user-avatars bucket allowed_mime_types for XSS layer-2 alignment (uploadUserAsset client filters SVG already; bucket policy still permits — gap is harmless today but a defense-in-depth alignment opportunity); (2) candidateAuthors query can be extended to include avatar_asset_id for picker-row avatars when author roster grows past ~20 users; (3) content_assets_archive_reason_check whitelist extension if user-avatar-specific lifecycle reasons surface (used manual_archive for smoke-test cleanup); (4) admin UI for managing newsletter_categories (`/super-admin/newsletter-categories` management page) deferred — Cole will seed categories via direct RPC call when needed; (5) _walk_tiptap_for_image_asset_refs walker mismatches newsletterImage node type (carried from Session 98); (6) related-article RPCs filter to `gate = 'public'` only — paywalled articles excluded from "you might also like" cards. Can be relaxed later by allowing all gate values + adding members-only badge in reader card; (7) PostgreSQL named-arg semantics make order of params in `.rpc()` JS object irrelevant — useful pattern observation but already implicit, no §-rule needed.***

*v104 - Session 98 CLOSE — Group H Cycle H2 Frontend Passes 2 + 3 + 5 SHIPPED + H2-MIG-8 backend migration SHIPPED. 9 Lovable build cycles + 1 backend migration + ~9 recon round-trips, all type-check clean. One new standing rule locked (§146). Two new top-level packages installed (@tiptap/extension-code-block ^3.23.0, @tiptap/extension-table 3.23.6). 15 new node files, 10 new NodeView files. One new slash menu category "TECHNICAL". **Bundle A2a-1**: StepList+Step + Checklist+ChecklistItem composite pairs (Step uses content:"heading block*" for editable title via real h4; ChecklistItem has data-checked round-trip + ChecklistItemNodeView with clickable checkbox; both parents use priority:60 on ol/ul to beat StarterKit per §144). **Bundle A2a-2**: DomainGrid+Row + IndexRow+Card (DomainRow atom 7 attrs with NodeView 3-column grid + tag chip styling scoped per D-A5a-1; IndexCard atom 5 attrs with locked accent_color palette enum matching A1a per D-A5a-2). **Bundle A2b**: ThreeColumn+Pane + FourColumn+Pane + ImageGallery + StatGrid — completes H2-FE-Pass 2 (8 composite pairs total). Per recon confirmed ProseMirror permits dual role (block-group member + named child of another node) so Gallery and StatGrid reuse existing newsletterImage/newsletterStatCallout atoms without schema conflict. First H2 bundle to ship without touching EDITABLE_NODE_OVERRIDES. **Bundle A3a**: 5 refinements (Image lightbox+lazy_load, Embed aspect_ratio, Callout with_icon, StatCallout trend, Pullquote alignment). Pattern locked: surface refinements in existing NodeView chrome where present (pill bar / dropdown / dialog / inline); when no chrome exists (Pullquote), introduce NEW hover-strip pill bar modeled on Image's convention. **Bundle A3b**: TwoColumn (gap) + KeyMoments (numbered + accent_color) + codeBlock (filename + highlight_lines) — completes H2-FE-Pass 3. @tiptap/extension-code-block installed; StarterKit.configure({codeBlock:false}) verified to entirely omit CodeBlock (source-checked); NewsletterCodeBlock = CodeBlock.extend({addAttributes(){return{...this.parent?.(), filename, highlight_lines}}}). Re-exported from buildExtensions.ts for §146 NodeView wiring. CodeBlockNodeView is the first NodeView shipped via Option-W2 wiring (extending StarterKit-bundled node by name). Visual line painting for highlight_lines deferred to phase 2 — no syntax-highlighting library installed. **H2-MIG-8** applied to svprhtzawnbzmumxnhsq as session98_h2_mig_8_newsletter_audio_imagecompare_ref_field_support — three changes: (a) request_asset_upload RPC expanded newsletter ref_field whitelist from 3 to 6 (added inline_audio + inline_image_compare_before/after); replaced image-only asset_kind gate with ref_field-correspondence check, (b) storage.buckets newsletter-article-images expanded allowed_mime_types from 4 to 11 (added image/svg+xml + image/avif to align with RPC + 5 audio MIMEs) and file_size_limit raised from 10MB to 100MB, (c) §140 triple defensive grant pattern applied. Pre-flight recon caught two blockers (hardcoded image-only gate inside RPC + bucket-level MIME/size restrictions). Latent issue surfaced (not fixed): _walk_tiptap_for_image_asset_refs walker targets node->>'type'='image' not newsletterImage; _rebind_newsletter_article_asset_refs RPC unused in current flow. Flagged as future cleanup, doesn't block A5c/A5d. **Bundle A5a**: Math + Terminal + CodeDiff + Chart (Pass 5 Tier 1 schema-only-rendering). 4 atoms with text-input NodeView editors. Terminal uses BylineNodeView's full dnd-kit repeating-row pattern with idsRef parallel UUID array + flushDebounce. JSON-via-data-attribute serialization for Terminal's commands array matches Byline's entries pattern. No rendering libraries installed (KaTeX/chart.js/highlighters all deferred to phase 2 per spec §4.6 + §4.7). New slash category "TECHNICAL" with 4 items (Math/Terminal/Code diff/Chart). **Bundle A5b**: Table install (Pass 5 Tier 2). @tiptap/extension-table 3.23.6 installed. 4 extensions registered standalone (Table, TableRow, TableHeader, TableCell). NewsletterTable configured with resizable:false (no column resize chrome) + class:"newsletter-table". No StarterKit collision (verified). Bubble menu shouldShow relaxed to allow collapsed caret inside table; default-mode body appends conditional 8-button table action row when isInsideTable. 8 buttons: addRowBefore/After, deleteRow, addColumnBefore/After, deleteColumn, toggleHeaderRow, deleteTable. mergeCells/splitCell skipped per D-A5b-3. Slash menu Table item in TECHNICAL category seeds 3x3 with header row. No new NodeViews and no EDITABLE_NODE_OVERRIDES additions (extension-table ships its own contentEditable behavior). **Bundle A5c**: Audio (first storage-coupled node in Group H). Leverages H2-MIG-8 backend. Generalized uploadNewsletterImage → uploadNewsletterAsset({kind, file, articleId, refField?, onProgress?}) with per-kind MIME/size/refField lookup tables; AssetKind="image"|"audio". Old uploadNewsletterImage.ts deleted. useNewsletterImageUrl → useNewsletterAssetUrl aliased (same signed URL flow works for both kinds since H2-MIG-8 keeps both in same bucket). NewsletterAudio atom 4 attrs (asset_id, title, duration_seconds, transcript_url). AudioNodeView mirrors ImageNodeView's upload dropzone pattern + native <audio controls> + auto-duration detection via loadedmetadata event with duration===Infinity seek-to-end fallback per recon answer (e). MEDIA slash item with Music icon. **Bundle A5d**: ImageCompare — completes H2-FE-Pass 5. Most novel interaction work in Group H to date. NewsletterImageCompare atom 5 attrs (before_asset_id, after_asset_id, before_label, after_label, default_position default 50 per D-A5d-3). ImageCompareNodeView combines TWO upload dropzones (each uploading via uploadNewsletterAsset with explicit refField inline_image_compare_before/after) + draggable slider divider. Drag implementation uses onPointerDown + setPointerCapture + window-level pointermove/pointerup with cleanup — deviates from codebase's onMouseDown convention because drag interactions need touch parity (mobile editor usage); existing onMouseDown selection-guard usages unaffected. Full keyboard a11y: role="slider", aria-valuemin/max/now, arrow-key handling with shift for 10x step, Home/End for 0/100. Position state committed on drag-end and on each key press (not on every drag-move). Reader rendering static at default_position per D-A5d-2 (no hydration script). CSS uses --ic-position variable set inline via style attribute on viewport div; clip-path on after image creates the masking effect. MEDIA slash item with SplitSquareHorizontal icon. **H2-FE-Pass 2 = COMPLETE** (8 composite pairs), **H2-FE-Pass 3 = COMPLETE** (8 refinements), **H2-FE-Pass 5 = COMPLETE** (7 interactive/data nodes including Table). **§146 LOCKED**: Newsletter TipTap schema module is headless; React NodeViews wired via .extend({addNodeView}) in NewsletterEditor.tsx + EDITABLE_NODE_OVERRIDES array. Discovered during A2a-1 plan review when initial build prompt incorrectly wired addNodeView() on a Node.create() definition; corrected before any code shipped. Pattern was implicit in NewsletterEditor.tsx header comment but not yet §-numbered. Pairs with §143 — §143 dictates timing (atom+NodeView same cycle), §146 dictates wiring location (editor module not schema module). **New deferred item carried forward to Session 99**: _walk_tiptap_for_image_asset_refs walker mismatches newsletterImage node type — cleanup needed before any future code starts relying on the rebind path. **Session 99 opens on H2-FE-Pass 6** (FooterMeta, AuthorBio, Citations, FurtherReading — AuthorBio depends on users.bio column shipped H2-MIG-6 last session). **Pass 7 remaining:** P7b (RelatedArticles, backend coupling), P7c (FootnoteRef + Footnotes, complexity spike).

*v103 - Session 96-97 CLOSE — Group H Cycle H2 Backend SHIPPED (7 migrations + types regen + end-to-end smoke test) + Group H Cycle H2 Frontend Pass 1 + Pass 4 SHIPPED across three Lovable cycles (Bundle A1a + A1b-1 + A1b-2). Cole runtime-verified at end of Session 97 — "all is there." Remaining H2 frontend: Passes 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, Final. Six new standing rules locked (§140-§145), one staleness correction surfaced (§136). (1) **H2 BACKEND SHIPPED — 7 migrations + types regen.** `session96_h2_mig_1_newsletter_articles_visual_vocabulary_fields` added 8 new columns (`eyebrow_text` text, `is_issue_based` boolean default false, `issue_label` text, `masthead_publication` text, `masthead_logo_glyph` text, `default_layout_width` text CHECK in 'narrow|standard|wide' default 'standard', `theme_variant` text CHECK in 'default|minimal|bold' default 'default', `tags` text[] default '{}') + 4 CHECK constraints + GIN index on `tags` + extended `source_type` CHECK to include `'html_import_v2'`. Per-element tag length CHECK dropped at apply time — Postgres forbids subqueries in CHECK constraints; validation moved to RPC body. §141. `session96_h2_mig_2_upsert_article_visual_vocabulary_params` + `_2b_revoke_public` expanded `upsert_article` from 17 to 25 params (8 new params with COALESCE defaults). Discovered Supabase's default `EXECUTE TO PUBLIC` re-applies on DROP+CREATE; `REVOKE FROM anon` alone insufficient. Complete pattern locked: `REVOKE FROM PUBLIC; REVOKE FROM anon; GRANT EXECUTE TO authenticated, service_role`. §140. `session96_h2_mig_3_get_article_for_reader_visual_vocabulary_payload` extended granted + paywall reader payloads with 8 new fields (tags in paywall per C1=A). `session96_h2_mig_4_list_admin_newsletter_articles_visual_vocabulary` extended super-admin list row. `session96_h2_mig_5_preview_article_as_viewer_class_visual_vocabulary` extended super-admin preview path. `session96_h2_mig_6_users_bio_column_and_super_admin_update_rpc` added `users.bio` (nullable text, no length cap) + `update_user_bio(p_user_id, p_bio, p_reason)` RPC + `user_bio_updated` action_type (category='user_management'). Existing "users: update own safe fields" RLS policy uses allow-by-omission immutable-fields model — bio auto-editable by row owner. §142 surfaced: `super_admin_action_types` INSERT requires 9-column set (action_type, category, description NOT NULL + tier nullable + 4 booleans); always query `information_schema.columns` BEFORE drafting INSERT. `session96_h2_mig_7_newsletter_polls_tables_rls_rpcs_action_types` added `newsletter_polls` + `newsletter_poll_votes` tables + 6 RLS policies + 5 RPCs (create_poll, update_poll, archive_poll, vote_on_poll, get_poll_results) + 3 action types. P1=B locked: options edits BLOCKED once votes exist. P2: question edits allowed after votes. C3=A: authenticated-only voting via PK constraint (poll_id, voter_user_id). `session96_h2_noop_types_regen` triggers types.ts regeneration via `COMMENT ON SCHEMA public IS 'public'` per §111. (2) **H2 BACKEND END-TO-END SMOKE TESTED.** Smoke article `ed18f133-e0b3-4e1c-85ec-7bcfcc84a21b` with all 8 H2 fields → published → `get_article_for_reader` round-tripped all 8 fields cleanly. Poll lifecycle: created `dff82ab4-cc01-4ce9-ae26-97b558a59d6a` → voted "protection" → results showed `{protection:1}`, `user_vote='protection'` when authed, `null` for anon → P1 B block verified (options edit attempt after vote raised, options preserved) → P2 question edit succeeded with votes existing → double-vote rejected by PK → archived poll → `get_poll_results` returned `{found:false}`. Bio RPC: set + cleared. All fixtures cleaned up. 8 audit rows persist with `H2 *` reason prefix. (3) **BUNDLE A1a SHIPPED — H2-FE-Pass 1 partial (3 of 6 nodes) + H2-FE-Pass 4 (all 8 marks).** Type-check clean. 11 new files + 6 modified. New nodes Eyebrow.ts, Lead.ts, Aside.ts in `src/components/newsletter/tiptap/nodes/` — all content-editable in DOM, no NodeView needed. Eyebrow rendered as `<p>` (better semantic than spec's `<div>`). New marks SmallCaps/Superscript/Subscript/Underline/Highlight/Keyboard/Abbr/Accent in new `src/components/newsletter/tiptap/marks/` directory — all custom Mark.create(), no `@tiptap/extension-*` packages added. Highlight scoped to `mark[data-newsletter-highlight]` (no bare `mark` to avoid collision with Accent's `mark.accent`); Accent rules priority 60 beats Highlight; Underline excludes `[class~="underline"]` (Tailwind utility class collision). Callout.ts parseHTML modified to add `priority: 51` on `aside[data-newsletter-callout]` as belt-and-suspenders against Aside's `aside:not([data-newsletter-callout])`. §144 + §145 locked. `marketing-tokens.css` extended with 22 new tokens under `/* Group H newsletter visual vocabulary tokens */` block: `--bw-mono-font` (JetBrains Mono), `--bw-mustard #8a6400` (darker than spec's #7a5800 for AA contrast), editorial label sizing tokens, stripe widths, section rule margins, dropcap, 6 accent color aliases, 3 content-width tokens (640/780/920 narrow/standard/wide — defined but unused this cycle; `.newsletter-prose` continues using `--container-sm` = 720px; width swap deferred to H3-NV-Auto). `newsletter-prose.css` extended with BEM rules for 3 nodes + 8 marks. NewsletterSlashMenu.tsx got 3 new EDITORIAL items (Eyebrow/Lead/Aside) with explicit content arrays in insert payloads. NewsletterBubbleMenu.tsx got 7 mark toggle buttons; Abbr opens inline title input following linkMode pattern; pre-existing "Lead" toggle button removed (newsletterLead block node replaces) but TextStyleWithFontSize extension itself preserved (still used by lesson-blocks editor). Tiny unblock in AdminNewsletterArticle.tsx: 8 new H2 RPC params passed as nullish defaults so typecheck passes; no UI behavior change. Underline collision: extended declared `Commands.underline` shape to include `setUnderline`/`unsetUnderline` to match a pre-existing module augmentation elsewhere in the codebase. (4) **BUNDLE A1b-1 SHIPPED — H2-FE-Pass 1 (3 more of 6 nodes).** Type-check clean. Atom nodes SectionRule.ts + Masthead.ts shipped + their NodeViews. SectionRule parseHTML uses `priority: 60` on scoped `hr.section-rule`, `hr[data-numbered]`, `hr.dot-divider` selectors to beat StarterKit's HorizontalRule on bare `hr` — and does NOT register a bare `hr` selector so HorizontalRule continues unaffected. Masthead atom with 4 attrs (publication/issue_label/date_label/logo_glyph). NodeViews: SectionRuleNodeView (style picker pills + conditional inputs per style + live preview) + MastheadNodeView (4 inline inputs + curated glyph dropdown with 8 glyphs ▲ ◆ ● ■ ★ ✦ ◊ ▼). NewsletterBubbleMenu.tsx refactored from 4-useState mode chain to single discriminated union `Mode = {kind:"default"|"link"|"abbr"|"accent"|"highlight", ...}`. All existing Link and Abbr behavior preserved. Escape useEffect closes any open submenu. Accent picker submenu (3 rows: 6 colors / 3 styles / 2 weights + Apply/Remove/Cancel) replaces A1a's "click applies orange/plain/normal default" behavior. Highlight picker submenu (single row of 5 colors, click-to-apply) replaces A1a's "click applies yellow default". Both pickers prefill from `editor.getAttributes(markName)`. `blockedParents` extended with `newsletterSectionRule`, `newsletterByline` (forward-looking for A1b-2), `newsletterMasthead`. No reader NodeViews for atoms — `renderHTML` is sufficient (no async resolution needed unlike Image's asset URL resolution); D10=A locked. 2 new slash items: SectionRule → BASIC, Masthead → LAYOUT. CSS for both nodes appended to newsletter-prose.css using new tokens from A1a. (5) **BUNDLE A1b-2 SHIPPED — H2-FE-Pass 1 (final node).** Type-check clean. Byline.ts atom with `entries[]` (`{text, bold, link}` array) + `separator_style` (dot/pipe/slash). Module-scoped `parseBylineHtml(el)` helper walks child nodes, accumulates text + bold/link state per segment, splits on separator chars (·, •, |, /). parseHTML rules for `[data-newsletter-byline]`, `div.byline`, `p.byline`, `address.byline`, `[class~="byline"]`, `[class~="author-meta"]`. BylineNodeView.tsx — full-fidelity per D3=A. Three components in one file: BylineNodeView parent (~120 LOC) + EntryRow (~80 LOC) + LinkEditor (~30 LOC). Pattern adopted verbatim from existing `src/components/super-admin/lesson-blocks/block-forms/ListBlockForm.tsx`: `@dnd-kit/sortable` with `useSortable` per row + `PointerSensor` with `activationConstraint: {distance: 4}` (codebase convention, not 5) + `crypto.randomUUID()` per entry in `idsRef` parallel array (NOT in doc JSON — ephemeral UI state) + `arrayMove` on dragEnd + GripVertical spreads `{...attributes} {...listeners}` (NOT `data-drag-handle` — that's the node-level handle outside the entry list) + always-keep-one-entry invariant (removing last entry replaces it with empty). Per-entry link mode follows linkMode pattern — clicking link icon expands inline URL input below entry, Enter applies, Escape cancels, empty trims to unset. Link-editing tracked by `linkEditingId: string | null` (UUIDs survive reorder). Auto-insert one empty entry on first mount via useEffect (Q6=C) — slash insert seeds `entries: []`; NodeView fills with one empty entry. Debounced text commits 300ms; `flushDebounce()` called on `onDragStart` to prevent post-drag debounce race. Top-of-NodeView separator picker (3 small ·, |, / buttons). CSS for Byline appended using mono font + editorial label tokens + hairline borders + empty-state placeholder. 1 new slash item: Byline → EDITORIAL (icon `User`). (6) **§136 STALENESS CORRECTION — `content_asset_refs` has 7 nullable parent FKs, not 8 as documented.** G7 podcast scope was closed in Session 94 per Session 91 Anchor RSS pull architecture; no `podcast_episode_id` column exists. Arch-ref v99 fixes this. (7) **Session 98 opens on Bundle A2 = H2-FE-Pass 2** — 8 composite parent+child node pairs (DomainGrid+DomainRow, IndexRow+IndexCard, StepList+Step, Checklist+Item, ThreeColumn+Pane, FourColumn+Pane, ImageGallery, StatGrid). Pattern reference: existing TwoColumn/TwoColumnPane and KeyMoments/KeyMoment pairs use the same defining/isolating + intentionally-not-block-group child pattern. Estimate 1-2 Lovable cycles (16 node files + slash entries + CSS); likely splits A2a+A2b. (8) **Carryover deferred items unchanged from Session 95 except:** Author bio edit UI now backend-ready (H2-MIG-6 added `users.bio` + `update_user_bio` RPC; frontend pending, not blocking). All other Session 95 deferred items unchanged. (9) **Session 96-97 close artifacts:** this v103 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v99 (adds §140-§145 + Session 96-97 ship narrative + §136 staleness correction), session-96-to-97.md handoff. Three markdown files for Cole to upload manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root). **Sessions 96 + 97 happened in one continuous chat per Cole's preference to push through after backend was clean. Next session number = 98.***

*v102 - Session 95 CLOSE — Group G newsletter platform shipped end-to-end. G4-0 (shared TipTap module), G4-A (editor primitive), G4-B (list + detail pages), G4-C (HTML import modal with file upload + paste paths), G5 (version history + diff viewer), G6 (public reader with archive + article + subscribe + confirm + unsubscribe pages + paywall card with subscribe/upgrade/sign-in paths) all SHIPPED. Two real bugs surfaced during end-to-end test and FIXED: (Bug 1) Edge Function `convert-html-to-tiptap` v1 called `finalize_asset_upload(p_asset_id)` with single arg but actual RPC signature is `(p_asset_id uuid, p_reason text)` — PostgREST returned PGRST202 "function not found" because no single-arg overload exists; Edge Function v2 passes p_reason. (Bug 2) After "Import into editor" click, editor surface stayed empty because TipTap's `useEditor` only consumes `initialContent` on mount and ignores subsequent prop changes; fix added `forwardRef` + `useImperativeHandle` on NewsletterEditor exposing `setContent(next)` that calls `editor.commands.setContent(next, { emitUpdate: true })`; AdminNewsletterArticle.tsx now calls `editorHandleRef.current?.setContent(newBody)` in onImported handler. Both bugs RESOLVED. End-to-end test verified: drop branded HTML file → Edge Function returns 2 images succeeded + 28 tag drops (mostly `<span>` unwraps which are expected) → preview pane renders in BrainWise typography → click Import → editor fills with converted content → auto-save fires → version landmark created. (1) **G4-0 SHIPPED — Shared TipTap extensions module.** 11 files: 9 custom node schemas (`Image`, `Callout`, `StatCallout`, `Embed`, `Pullquote`, `TwoColumn`, `TwoColumnPane`, `KeyMoments`, `KeyMoment`), `buildExtensions` factory accepting `{editable, placeholder}` options, `buildEmbedSrc` helper for YouTube/Vimeo/Spotify provider routing. Image.ts later patched in G4-C to add `import_failed_src` attr that NodeView reads to render re-upload affordance. (2) **G4-A SHIPPED — Editor primitive.** 14 files: 9 NodeViews with edit affordances, `uploadNewsletterImage` helper with module-Map cache + in-flight dedupe via `useNewsletterImageUrl` hook, NewsletterToolbar with `imageInputRef.open()` exposure, NewsletterBubbleMenu using BubbleMenuPlugin + registerPlugin (v3 pattern), NewsletterSlashMenu via @tiptap/suggestion + tippy.js with 16 commands × 4 categories, NewsletterFloatingPlus with rAF-throttled selectionUpdate. Dependencies added: `@tiptap/extension-bubble-menu@3.23.1`, `@tiptap/suggestion@3.23.6`, `tippy.js@6.3.7`. (3) **G4-B SHIPPED — List + detail pages.** Routes wired: `/super-admin/newsletter` + `/super-admin/newsletter/:articleId`. Create mode shows placeholders rather than mounting half-functional editor. (4) **G5 SHIPPED — Version history + diff viewer.** 7 files using `diff@7.0.0` for inline word-level diff. Day-grouped list with collapsed draft runs. `flushSave` before opening sheet errors but still opens the sheet. (5) **G6 SHIPPED — Public newsletter reader.** 9 new files plus 3 modifications. PaywallCard handles 3 states: subscriber_required (inline subscribe form reveal), plan_tier_required (View pricing link), Already subscribed (Sign in with redirect param). Turnstile site key `0x4AAAAAADVBROvQ5jLUUIxJ` loaded via module-level promise. 4 marketing routes ordered specific-before-generic. (6) **G4-C SHIPPED — Paste/upload HTML import modal.** File drop + paste textarea + 5-phase state machine + raw `fetch` with AbortSignal (supabase-js `functions.invoke` lacks AbortSignal support) + preview reusing G6 reader NodeViews + `truncateDocByWords` preserving block boundaries.
