(7) **Edge Function `convert-html-to-tiptap` v1 → v2 BUG FIX deployed.** v1 missing `p_reason` on finalize_asset_upload call caused PGRST202. v2 passes `p_reason: "convert_html_to_tiptap: finalize inline image import"`. Image uploads end-to-end. ezbr_sha256 `0d1ec2ce...` → `ffe538f1...`. (8) **Session 95 backend migrations:** `session95_reap_pending_uploads_parent_enumeration_fix` (orphan-sweep Sweep 2 missing newsletter_article_id + resource_id parents), `session95_content_assets_archive_reason_check_add_active_orphan_swept` (CHECK constraint missing value), `session95_list_articles_for_archive_remove_dead_category_param`, `session95_list_admin_newsletter_articles_rpc` (super-admin list RPC with ILIKE search), `session95_list_admin_newsletter_articles_revoke_anon` (Supabase default-grants trap §137), `session95_request_asset_upload_add_newsletter_article_param` (extended to 16-arg signature), `session95_publish_schedule_auto_snapshot_versions` (publish_article + schedule_article RPCs now call `_snapshot_article_version` after state transition to create named "Published v{N}" / "Scheduled v{N}" landmarks per §138). (9) **`request-asset-upload` Edge Function v5 deployed** — routes to `newsletter-article-images` bucket when `p_newsletter_article_id` set. (10) **Newsletter feature ships functionally complete** for authoring + version control + public reading + newsletter sign-up + HTML import + paid-user reader access. NOT shipped: email dispatch (G8), SSR for SEO (G3 deferred), RSS feed (G9 deferred). (11) **NEW Group H — Newsletter visual vocabulary + layout-preserving HTML import — initiative scoped Session 95 close.** Cole identified G4-C import strips all source layout (eyebrow tags, decks, bylines, section rules, multi-column grids, formatted index cards, footnote citations, image-with-caption variants, footer tag strips) and converts to flat semantic TipTap — unacceptable for prospect-facing content. Deep recon performed on `ptp_test_article_alt_media.html` (25KB, 837 lines, 45 unique CSS classes representing complete source visual vocabulary). Spec document `group-h-spec.md` (712 lines) produced — Cole's explicit Session 95 close decision: build the COMPLETE editorial vocabulary, not MVP. Spec documents: complete source class inventory (45 unique classes in test file) grouped by semantic function, existing G4-0 schema inventory (16 nodes/marks), proposed BrainWise vocabulary additions covering ~33 NEW block nodes (Eyebrow, Lead, SectionRule, Byline, Masthead, Aside, FootnoteRef+Footnotes, Definition, Disclosure, DomainGrid+DomainRow, IndexRow+IndexCard, StepList+Step, Checklist+Item, ThreeColumn+ThreeColumnPane, FourColumn+FourColumnPane, ImageGallery, ImageCompare, Audio, StatGrid, Chart, Table via @tiptap/extension-table, CodeDiff, Terminal, Math, Poll, CTA, SubscribeBlock, RelatedArticles, FooterMeta, AuthorBio, Citations, FurtherReading) + 7 NEW marks (accent, smallCaps, superscript, subscript, underline, highlight, keyboard, abbr) + 6 REFINED existing nodes (Callout/Pullquote/StatCallout/Image/Embed/TwoColumn/KeyMoments/codeBlock add attrs + parseHTML rules) + 6 NEW article-level fields (eyebrow_text, is_issue_based+issue_label, tags[], masthead_publication+masthead_logo_glyph, default_layout_width, theme_variant), full set of new design tokens (mono-font, accent stripes, section-rule rhythm, dropcap vars, accent palette extensions, content-width variants, grid gap variants), architectural shift from manual DOM walking to client-side `generateJSON(html, extensions)` with image pre-pass server-side via new Edge Function `import-html-images` returning resolutions map (Option B locked in spec — browser does TipTap parse using node-defined parseHTML rules; old `convert-html-to-tiptap` Edge Function deprecated and removed in H4), 6-cycle build plan with sub-pass ordering (H1 spec COMPLETE, H2 schema+parseHTML for entire catalog in 8 sub-passes per §4.13, H3 NodeViews edit+read for entire catalog in matching 8 sub-passes, H4 image-only Edge Function + modal refactor, H5 fidelity iteration with 5+ test files, H6 Claude vocabulary prompt template + reader polish), revised effort estimate **8-13 sessions / 30-42 Lovable cycles** at full-catalog scope, deferred list narrowed to truly out-of-scope items only (grain overlay = page-level CSS, hero = existing cover_asset_id field, comment threads = already declined Q8, reactions, real-time collab, i18n, AMP, print stylesheets; phase-2 enhancements that ship schema in H2 with backend deferred: Poll voting, KaTeX math rendering, Prism/Shiki syntax highlighting, Chart.js dynamic rendering, image lightbox), 5 open questions surfaced for execution-time. Spec file presented at Session 95 close as Group H authoritative reference. (12) **Internal subscriber inclusion — paid product users auto-receive newsletter — DEFERRED to G8.** Scoped during Session 95 background work: paid product users should automatically receive newsletter dispatches. Three sub-decisions for G8: (a) who counts as internal — only paid OR also free-tier-verified, (b) consent model — implicit vs explicit-easy, (c) dispatch implementation — `dispatch-newsletter-article` reads users table in addition to newsletter_subscribers OR pre-populated bridge view. Read-path already works via `current_user_active_plan_tier()`. (13) **Session 95 close artifacts:** this v102 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v98 (adds §136 orphan-sweep parent enumeration discipline + §137 Supabase default-grants trap + §138 publish/schedule auto-snapshot landmark pattern + §139 forwardRef + useImperativeHandle pattern for TipTap content replacement + Session 95 ship narrative + Group H spec reference), session-95-to-96.md handoff, AND `group-h-spec.md` (NEW file, 430 lines, Group H authoritative spec). Four markdown files for Cole to upload manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs`. **Session 96 opens on Group H Cycle H2 — Schema + parseHTML for new nodes.** Spec in hand, no further design conversation needed. H2 backend migrations and H2 frontend Lovable cycles drafted from spec §6.*

*v101 - Session 94 CLOSE — Group G newsletter platform Phase G2 SHIPPED clean. Backend complete for newsletter subscribe/confirm/unsubscribe flow + Cloudflare Turnstile abuse defense + Resend audience sync (add + soft-remove) + bounce/complaint subscriber-status sync via existing resend-webhook extension. **Phase G2 is DONE.** Path C (vite-plugin-prerender) for G3 was evaluated, scoped end-to-end, sent to Lovable for confirmation, and rejected based on Lovable's response (no deploy webhook + fragile Puppeteer support on Lovable build environment). Path D-2 (Cloudflare Worker subdomain proxy) also evaluated and rejected after web-search recon found Cloudflare requires Business tier ($200/month) for partial DNS setup that would let us keep DNS in Microsoft. **G3 SEO/AEO infrastructure deferred** to post-G6 — Cole's call to revisit after Lovable frontend cycles G4-G6 are functional and there's real article content to validate SSR against. G9 RSS feed Edge Function also deferred to that same combined G3+RSS pass per Cole's call. **Phase G7 podcast platform marked COMPLETE** — Cole confirmed Session 91 Anchor RSS pull pattern (Edge Function `refresh-podcast-feed` + pg_cron + public Storage bucket cache + marketing `/podcast` page reading JSON directly) is the canonical podcast architecture. The full G7 scope (`podcast_episodes` + `podcast_episode_versions` tables + super-admin authoring UI + internal episode pages) is replaced by the lean external-RSS-cache pattern and will not ship. One new standing rule locked Session 94 (§135) covering the Vault secret access pattern for Edge Functions called from SQL via pg_net. (1) **G2.1 SHIPPED — `session94_newsletter_subscribe_attempt_pruning_function_and_cron`.** `prune_newsletter_subscribe_attempts()` SECDEF function deletes rows from `newsletter_subscribe_attempts` older than 24h. Cron job `prune_newsletter_subscribe_attempts_daily` at `15 6 * * *` ships active, runs as postgres role per `cron.schedule()` default (Lovable v2 pattern from Session 93 — the explicit `cron.schedule_in_database(..., username := 'postgres')` form requires superuser the MCP role lacks; using `cron.schedule()` produces identical job registration with username='postgres' assigned automatically by Supabase). Per §131 cron slot collision audit ran pre-migration: 6:15 confirmed free. Smoke test: `SELECT public.prune_newsletter_subscribe_attempts()` returned `{success:true, deleted_count:0, cutoff:<now-24h>, pruned_at:<now>}` against empty production table. Permissions: REVOKE EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated — cron runs as postgres superuser regardless. (2) **G2.2 SHIPPED — `session94_newsletter_pending_confirmation_expiry_function_and_cron` + `session94_newsletter_pending_confirmation_expiry_rewrite_to_delete`.** First migration created `expire_pending_newsletter_confirmations()` SECDEF function flipping `newsletter_subscribers` rows with status='pending_confirmation' AND created_at < now() - 7d to status='unsubscribed' + setting unsubscribed_at. Cron job at `25 6 * * *` ships active. **Q14 design correction surfaced before G2.3 draft:** flipping abandoned-pending rows to 'unsubscribed' creates an ambiguity with user-initiated unsubscribes since Q7.C policy locks unsubscribed addresses out of re-subscription. Path A locked: rewrite function body to DELETE the abandoned-pending row entirely (frees email for fresh subscribe; audit trail preserved in newsletter_subscribe_attempts and email_logs which the cron does NOT touch). Rewrite migration applied immediately; verified body contains DELETE not UPDATE; returns `{success:true, deleted_count:N, cutoff:<now-7d>, expired_at:<now>}`. The newsletter_subscribers table represents *current* subscription state; abandoned drafts are not current state. (3) **G2.3 SHIPPED — `session94_newsletter_subscribe_RPCs` + `session94_newsletter_rpcs_revoke_public`.** Five RPCs in one migration: `_send_newsletter_confirmation_email_internal(uuid, text)` private helper (defined first so subscribe_to_newsletter can PERFORM it; locked from anon/authenticated/PUBLIC); `subscribe_to_newsletter(text, text, text)` anon-callable with Q13.A anti-enumeration (always returns generic success); `confirm_newsletter_subscription(text)` anon-callable with Q15 distinct success/failure responses (legitimate to surface invalid_or_expired_token to the user who clicked the link); `unsubscribe_from_newsletter(text)` anon-callable idempotent; `list_newsletter_subscribers(text, integer, integer)` super_admin only with §111 wrapper-shape return + assert_super_admin() gate. Locked Q-decisions in migration body: Q7.A (existing pending → rotate confirmation token + re-send), Q7.B (existing confirmed → silent no-op), Q7.C (existing unsubscribed/bounced/complained → silent no-op preserving Resend reputation), Q8 (5/hr/IP rolling rate limit), Q9 Variant 2 (warmer/branded copy: subject "One click to confirm — BrainWise newsletter" + CTA "Yes, send me the newsletter"), Q11.A (Turnstile verification via pg_net polling Cloudflare siteverify inside the RPC — 30-iteration 100ms-sleep loop polls net._http_response, total 3s max wait, fail-closed on timeout), Q12.A (synchronous email dispatch via send-email Edge Function), Q16 (32-byte gen_random_bytes → base64url → SHA-256 hex hash pattern, raw token in URL only, hash persists), Q17 (apex `brainwiseenterprises.com` for confirmation + unsubscribe URLs), Q19 (unsubscribe link in confirmation email footer for CAN-SPAM/GDPR transactional compliance), Q20.α (refined: generate unsubscribe token once on first send if absent; on re-send rotate the unsubscribe token — accepts that prior unopened email's unsub link breaks since they haven't acted on it anyway). Pgcrypto search_path on all four token-handling RPCs includes `extensions` per §129. Anti-enumeration in subscribe_to_newsletter: email format validation failure → generic success; turnstile token absent or invalid → generic success; rate-limited IP → generic success; existing confirmed/unsubscribed/bounced/complained → generic success (no email sent, no DB change). Only no-row case generates fresh confirmation token + INSERT + sends email; existing pending_confirmation rotates token + UPDATE + re-sends email. Frontend cannot distinguish branches. Post-apply, follow-up migration `session94_newsletter_rpcs_revoke_public` REVOKEd default PUBLIC grants Postgres auto-creates on new functions (the original migration had GRANT TO anon + authenticated explicitly but did not REVOKE FROM PUBLIC; tightening for §82 explicit-grant discipline + defense-in-depth on list_newsletter_subscribers which now has authenticated-only explicit grant on top of internal assert_super_admin gate). All five RPCs SECDEF, all return jsonb, all search_path locked. Smoke tests: confirm with invalid token returned `{success:false, error:'invalid_or_expired_token'}`; unsubscribe with invalid token returned `{success:false, error:'invalid_unsubscribe_token'}`; subscribe with malformed email returned `{success:true, message:'Check your email for a confirmation link.'}` (anti-enumeration working); subscribe with valid email + dummy Turnstile token returned generic success + verified via net._http_response that Cloudflare siteverify was called and rejected the dummy token (full chain verified end-to-end: Vault read of CLOUDFLARE_TURNSTILE_SECRET → pg_net.http_post → Cloudflare response polled → branch decision); list_newsletter_subscribers as super_admin (impersonating via set_config('request.jwt.claims', ...)) returned `{items:[], total_count:0, limit:10, offset:0, status_filter:null}` wrapper shape; same RPC as anon raised `42501: Permission denied: caller is not a BrainWise super admin` from assert_super_admin(). Production tables remain empty post-smoke-test. (4) **G2.4a SHIPPED — `session94_newsletter_resend_audience_sync` + `sync-resend-audience` Edge Function v1.** Edge Function deployed first (Class B per §107, verify_jwt:false, INTERNAL_FUNCTION_SECRET gate with constant-time safeEqual). Implements Resend's Nov-2025 contacts overhaul: contacts are now global to the Resend account (no audience_id required for POST/PATCH/DELETE per discovery during recon). `action='add'` → POST `/contacts` `{email, unsubscribed:false}` (idempotent — Resend re-creates or returns existing contact_id); `action='remove'` → PATCH `/contacts/<email>` `{unsubscribed:true}` (Q25.A soft-unsubscribe — keeps Resend contact record for analytics, Resend skips unsubscribed contacts on broadcasts automatically). Reads `RESEND_API_KEY` from Edge Function Secrets (same key already used by send-email Edge Function — confirmed via 7-day email_logs query showing 46 successful sends across 10 email_types). Best-effort: errors logged + returned in `{synced:false}` body but never throw 5xx; caller treats this as fire-and-forget per Q27.A. On successful add, writes returned Resend contact_id back to `newsletter_subscribers.resend_contact_id` via service-role supabase client (non-fatal if writeback fails). DB migration applied second: `_sync_to_resend_audience_internal(uuid, text)` SECDEF helper RPC that reads INTERNAL_FUNCTION_SECRET from Vault, validates action enum, posts to sync-resend-audience Edge Function via pg_net.http_post with x-internal-secret header (best-effort sub-transaction wrap). Then full-body CREATE OR REPLACE of `confirm_newsletter_subscription` and `unsubscribe_from_newsletter` with the TODO comments swapped for `PERFORM public._sync_to_resend_audience_internal(v_subscriber_id, 'add'|'remove')` calls. End-to-end smoke test: created test subscriber row `ebb796a6-cb63-4385-a6df-039aa6c0d71b` at `delivered+g2-smoke-1779563402@resend.dev` (Resend's documented test-delivery address); called `_sync_to_resend_audience_internal(id, 'add')` returned `{dispatched:true, action:'add'}`; pg_net response after 3s showed `{synced:true, action:'add', email:'delivered+g2-smoke-...', resend_contact_id:'e77b7d92-725c-4154-9009-a1cfc3a42a2e'}` — full chain Vault → pg_net → Edge Function → Resend POST → response → contact_id writeback verified; then called same helper with `action='remove'`, returned `{dispatched:true, action:'remove'}`, pg_net response after 3s showed `{synced:true, action:'remove'}`; deleted the smoke-test subscriber row (Resend contact for delivered+g2-smoke-... remains marked unsubscribed in Resend, harmless). (5) **G2.4b SHIPPED — `resend-webhook` Edge Function v3 (strictly-additive extension).** Pulled deployed v2 source via get_edge_function pre-modification per Lovable Credit Conservation Protocol diagnose-before-prescribe. Every pre-existing line preserved verbatim (Svix signature verification, header presence checks, timestamp freshness check rejecting >5min replays, email_logs find-or-update OR `auth_or_external` insert branch). New newsletter-status-sync block APPENDED after the existing email_logs work, before the 200 ack response: when `statusEvent IN ('bounced', 'complained')` AND recipientEmail present, looks up `newsletter_subscribers` by lowercased email, flips status to 'bounced' or 'complained' if currently 'confirmed' or 'pending_confirmation' (idempotent — won't touch already-unsubscribed/bounced/complained rows). Per Q28.B: does NOT fire `_sync_to_resend_audience_internal` since Resend originated the event and already knows. Wrapped in try/catch — any failure is logged via logStep and swallowed; the 200 ack to Resend is unaffected. Deployed at version 3 with verify_jwt:false explicitly preserved per §107 standing rule. ezbr_sha256 changed `e9bb89b4...` → `6e3da223...`. Post-deploy smoke tests confirmed pre-existing behaviors intact: forged signature (`v1,FAKEFAKE...`) → 401 `{"error":"Invalid signature"}` (Svix gate working); missing svix headers → 400 `{"error":"Missing required Svix headers"}`; OPTIONS preflight → 200. The new newsletter-status-sync block only executes on bounce/complaint events from authenticated Resend webhook source — gated behind everything that worked before. (6) **G3 path evaluation: rejected paths documented for closeout.** Path C (vite-plugin-prerender with Puppeteer build-time SSR) drafted in full and sent to Lovable for capability confirmation. Lovable response: Q1 no deploy webhook (only programmatic path is pg_net → GitHub API → push commit → Lovable rebuilds from connected GitHub repo, fragile + adds GitHub PAT secret), Q2 Puppeteer support uncertain ("#1 thing that breaks on Lovable-style hosts" per Lovable's own framing — bundled Chromium download blocked in managed CI; puppeteer-core has no system Chrome to point at), Q3 build-time env vars + outbound HTTPS confirmed working. Additional Lovable-surfaced impacts that weren't in the original Path C scope: every prerendered page would need a `prerender-ready` event dispatch (~12+ pages, easy to regress); hydration mismatches become a class of production bug the existing pure-SPA doesn't have today (Home.tsx already uses `useState(window.innerWidth)` initializer that would throw during SSR without guard); `react-helmet-async` is not currently installed and adding it requires refactoring the existing inline `document.title`/setMeta pattern across every marketing page; build time goes from seconds to minutes once Puppeteer prerenders ~20 routes per build (compounds as article count grows). Path C-lite (graceful degradation) considered and rejected — "do all the work of Path C and possibly get none of the value" since if Puppeteer fails the build falls back to SPA which is the exact problem we set out to solve. Path D-1 (raw Supabase Edge Function URL) rejected for ugly URL + bad SEO + brand impact. Path D-2 (Cloudflare Worker subdomain proxy keeping DNS in Microsoft) was the initial post-Path-C recommendation; rejected after web-search recon found Cloudflare's "Partial CNAME setup" (the only way to add a Worker subdomain without nameserver migration) requires Business or Enterprise plan ($200/month minimum). Path D-3 (full Cloudflare nameserver migration) rejected for the same DNS-migration risk that rejected Path A originally — moving Resend email DNS records to Cloudflare is doable but error-prone and Cole has declined this scope twice. **Outcome locked: G3 deferred to post-G6.** Will revisit once Lovable frontend cycles G4-G6 ship and there is real article content to validate SSR against, plus actual newsletter audience signal to justify the infrastructure investment. The infrastructure decision (Cloudflare nameserver migration OR Vercel migration OR build-time prerender with smaller scope) gets made once with full context instead of twice in a vacuum. Path D-1 ugly-URL interim was rejected because it would create URL-change debt — Lovable frontend cycle for G6 (public reader UI) will use clean `brainwiseenterprises.com/newsletter/<slug>` URLs from day one served by the SPA (not SSR'd); when G3 ships later, the URLs stay the same and only the rendering changes from SPA to SSR. No URL-change debt. (7) **G9 RSS feed Edge Function deferred to same combined G3+RSS pass.** Original G9 sub-component (RSS feed at `/newsletter/feed.xml`) has the same URL-routing problem as G3 articles. Building the Edge Function now with an ugly Supabase URL just to defer the clean URL to G3-time would create the same kind of throwaway work we just declined for SSR. Single G3+RSS pass after G6 ships at clean URLs from the start. Remaining G9 sub-components (empty states, loading skeletons, error boundaries, a11y verification, mobile verification, cover image dominant-color extraction for archive cards reuse §112, cover image cascade reuse §43/§50/§108) are all Lovable frontend work — fold into G6 reader UI Lovable cycle or a separate G9 polish Lovable cycle post-G6. (8) **G7 podcast platform marked COMPLETE.** Original G7 scope called for `podcast_episodes` + `podcast_episode_versions` tables + RLS + RPCs + super-admin authoring UI at `/super-admin/podcast/<episodeId>` + internal public episode pages at `/podcast/<slug>` with embedded player + show notes + transcript stub. Cole's Session 94 call: the Session 91 Anchor/Spotify RSS pull architecture (Edge Function `refresh-podcast-feed` + pg_cron at `37 */4 * * *` + public Storage bucket `podcast-feed/episodes.json` + marketing `/podcast` page reading JSON directly per Session 91 frontend) is the canonical podcast architecture. Anchor/Spotify is the canonical host; BrainWise pulls + caches metadata; no DB-side authoring UI to maintain; no internal episode pages to build. Decision rationale: zero authoring overhead for Cole (publish to Spotify, RSS auto-syncs), zero duplicate-source-of-truth bugs, faster page loads (CDN-cached JSON), no podcast-specific schema to maintain or migrate. The lean external-RSS architecture replaces the full G7 scope. (9) **New standing rule locked Session 94: §135 — Vault secret access in Edge Functions called from SQL via pg_net.** Two distinct secret stores exist with different access patterns: (a) Edge Function Secrets (configured via Supabase Dashboard → Edge Functions → Secrets) are accessed via `Deno.env.get('SECRET_NAME')` inside the Edge Function — fast, synchronous, no DB roundtrip; this is where RESEND_API_KEY and INTERNAL_FUNCTION_SECRET live for direct Edge Function consumption; (b) Vault secrets (`vault.decrypted_secrets`) are accessed via `SELECT decrypted_secret FROM vault.decrypted_secrets WHERE name = '...'` from SQL — slower, requires DB roundtrip, used by SECDEF RPCs that need to call out via pg_net.http_post with a secret in the body or header. Pattern locked Session 94 across `_send_newsletter_confirmation_email_internal`, `subscribe_to_newsletter` (Turnstile siteverify call), `_sync_to_resend_audience_internal`: SECDEF RPC reads secret from Vault, passes to pg_net body or header, Edge Function on the other side reads the same secret from Edge Function Secrets via Deno.env.get and does constant-time safeEqual comparison to verify. **Both stores must be populated when adding a new secret** — adding to Vault only means the SQL side reads it but the Edge Function side gets undefined; adding to Edge Function Secrets only means the Edge Function reads it but SECDEF RPCs that need to pass it through pg_net get NULL from Vault. Three secrets now follow this pattern: INTERNAL_FUNCTION_SECRET (used by every Class B Edge Function + every SECDEF RPC that calls one), CLOUDFLARE_TURNSTILE_SECRET (NEW Session 94 — used by subscribe_to_newsletter RPC posting to Cloudflare siteverify; Vault only since the Edge Function side doesn't consume it), RESEND_API_KEY (used by send-email + sync-resend-audience Edge Functions; Edge Function Secrets only since SQL never touches the Resend API directly). (10) **Cole-side completed Session 94:** Cloudflare Turnstile account provisioned at cloudflare.com (free tier), site key `0x4AAAAAADVBROvQ5jLUUIxJ` (public, embeds in Lovable G6 subscribe form when shipped), secret key added to Supabase Vault as `CLOUDFLARE_TURNSTILE_SECRET` (35 chars, prefix `0x4AAAAAAD` verified). Cole-side verified via pg_net test call to Cloudflare siteverify: real secret + dummy token returned `{"error-codes":["invalid-input-response"],"success":false}` (status 200) confirming Cloudflare accepted the secret as valid then rejected the dummy token. Cole-side incomplete (not blocking for G2 close): Resend Audience setup — discovered during recon that Resend's Nov 2025 contacts overhaul made global contacts work without an audience_id, so the originally-scoped `RESEND_NEWSLETTER_AUDIENCE_ID` Edge Function Secret entry is unnecessary; if Cole already added a placeholder value, harmless. (11) **No new buckets, RLS policies on existing tables, GitHub commits required outside Cole's manual upload of closeout docs.** Edge Function versions at Session 94 close: NEW `sync-resend-audience` v1 ACTIVE; UPDATED `resend-webhook` v2 → v3 ACTIVE; all other Edge Functions unchanged from Session 93 close. New migrations at Session 94 close: 6 in order — `session94_newsletter_subscribe_attempt_pruning_function_and_cron` (20260523184616), `session94_newsletter_pending_confirmation_expiry_function_and_cron` (20260523184829), `session94_newsletter_pending_confirmation_expiry_rewrite_to_delete` (20260523185102), `session94_newsletter_subscribe_RPCs` (20260523185716), `session94_newsletter_rpcs_revoke_public` (20260523185749), `session94_newsletter_resend_audience_sync` (20260523190929). (12) **Carryover deferred items unchanged from Session 93 except: G3 SEO/AEO infrastructure NEWLY DEFERRED to post-G6 (one combined G3+RSS pass) per Cole's Session 94 call; G7 podcast platform formal scope CLOSED (replaced by Session 91 Anchor RSS architecture); G9 RSS feed Edge Function NEWLY DEFERRED to G3+RSS combined pass; G9 frontend polish items (empty states, skeletons, error boundaries, a11y, mobile, cover image dominant-color, cover image cascade) carry to G6 Lovable cycle or separate post-G6 polish cycle.** All other Session 93 deferred items unchanged. (13) **Session 95 strategy locked Session 94 close:** heavy upfront recon covering G4 (super-admin article authoring UI) + G5 (version history + diff viewer UI) + G6 (public reader UI) as one connected build since they share data shapes, route patterns, component patterns, and gating logic. Single recon pass, then Lovable cycles drafted with full context. Recon scope at Session 95 open: pg_proc on all 24+ newsletter article RPCs (signatures, return shapes, parameter contracts); existing ResourceEditor + ContentItemEditor + ResourcesAuthoringList patterns to mirror; existing TipTap v3 lesson_blocks toolbar to fork into editorial extension set; FileUploadField pattern for cover image picker; pagination patterns from Podcast.tsx archive grid; existing AppSidebar nav patterns. Single Cole-side prereq for G4: confirm Q7 launch content count + Q8 comments-in-v1 decision (recommended NO per scope). (14) **Session 94 close artifacts:** this v101 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v97 (adds §135 + Session 94 ship narrative + G3-deferred + G7-complete + G9-rss-deferred decisions), session-94-to-95.md handoff. Three markdown files for Cole to upload manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root). **Session 95 opens on G4-G6 heavy-recon strategy: pull canonical docs from GitHub MCP, run pg_proc + information_schema recon covering all three phases as one connected surface, draft Lovable approval packet covering G4+G5+G6 design decisions in a single packet, then execute Lovable cycles serially with full data-shape clarity. Backend is fully ready for frontend consumption; no further backend work expected before G4-G6 frontend ships.***

*v100 (SESSION 93 PARTIAL - SUPERSEDED BY v101) - **Session 93 Group G newsletter platform Phase G1 IN PROGRESS — G1.3c shipped clean (state-transition RPCs + 2 audit-shape gap fixes + redundant-index drop + 1 new action_type). G1.3d through G1.5 remaining.**

**G1.3c ship narrative (migration `20260523174208_session93_newsletter_articles_state_transition_rpcs`):**

Single migration, applied + verified. Fixes Session 92 Gaps 1 + 2 + 3 alongside the 5 new state-transition RPCs:

- (1) DROPPED redundant index `newsletter_articles_slug_lookup_idx` (Gap 3 fix; UNIQUE index on slug already handles equality lookups).
- (2) INSERTed new action_type `article_schedule_cancelled` (Q2(B) answer — inverts v2's parsimony default; cancel-schedule and unpublish are now semantically distinct audit events). UPDATEd stale `article_unpublished` description to remove the obsolete "also used for cancel-scheduled" phrasing Session 92 had written.
- (3) `commit_article_version` rewritten with full pre-snapshot state capture per item 15. Asymmetric audit shape per Q1(B): `before_value` carries full article state {slug, title, excerpt, gate, allowed_plan_tiers, status, cover_asset_id, og_image_asset_id, seo fields, authors[]}; `after_value` stays as operation summary {article_id, version_id, version_name}.
- (4) `restore_article_version` same asymmetric fix. Pre-restore state captured BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE/loop runs (critical — author junction is replaced by the restore so before-capture must precede the DELETE). `after_value` carries operation summary + the snapshot authors array.
- (5) NEW `schedule_article(p_article_id, p_publish_at, p_reason)` — status guard `IN ('draft','unpublished')`, terminal-archive guard, p_publish_at skew tolerance (rejects ≤ now() - 1 minute), p_publish_at >now() bump via GREATEST(p_publish_at, now() + interval '1 second') to satisfy the existing scheduled_consistency CHECK constraint when caller's timestamp falls in the `(now() - 1 min, now()]` skew window. Returns `effective_at_clamped` bool so frontend can render "scheduled for now" vs "scheduled for X" appropriately.
- (6) NEW `cancel_scheduled_article(p_article_id, p_reason)` — status guard requires `scheduled`, terminal-archive guard, flips to `draft` and clears `scheduled_for`. Emits new `article_schedule_cancelled` action_type per Q2(B).
- (7) NEW `publish_article(p_article_id, p_reason)` — status guard `IN ('draft','scheduled','unpublished')`, terminal-archive guard, explicit pre-check that gated articles (subscriber_aggregate/plan_tier) have excerpt ≥20 chars (CHECK constraint already enforces this at UPDATE time; explicit guard returns clearer `22023` error vs CHECK's `23514`). Sets status=published, published_at=now(), scheduled_for=NULL. Newsletter dispatch + SSR cache invalidation = bare-comment stubs per Q3(A) — code comments at deferred call sites for G3 + G8 surgical edits.
- (8) NEW `unpublish_article(p_article_id, p_reason)` — status guard requires `published`, terminal-archive guard, flips to `unpublished`. SSR cache invalidation = bare-comment stub.
- (9) NEW `archive_article(p_article_id, p_reason)` — terminal in v1. No double-archive guard. Allows archive from any non-archived status (draft/scheduled/published/unpublished). Sets archived_at=now(). SSR cache invalidation = bare-comment stub.
- (10) All 5 new RPCs: LANGUAGE plpgsql, SECURITY DEFINER, `SET search_path = public, extensions, pg_temp`, gate via `assert_super_admin` + `assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change')` + reason ≥10 chars. REVOKE PUBLIC, GRANT authenticated + service_role. All audit emissions include full before+after JSON with authors array per item 15.

**Lovable approval at G1.3c open:** 3 questions (Q1 audit shape, Q2 schedule_cancelled action_type, Q3 dispatch+cache stub depth) presented with scope-cross-referenced framing and recommendations. Lovable answered Q1=(B), Q2=(B), Q3=(A). Two reinforcements added pre-implementation: (R1) schedule_article skew clamp via GREATEST to satisfy CHECK while preserving 1-minute skew tolerance — answered (B) keep slack and clamp; (R2) archive terminal in v1 — confirmed. Three implementation safeguards added beyond spec: explicit gated-article excerpt pre-check in publish_article (clearer error than CHECK), `effective_at_clamped` return field on schedule_article (frontend signal), permissive archive (from any non-archived status). All three accepted by Cole at draft review.

**One Apply error caught + corrected:** Initial INSERT into super_admin_action_types missed the NOT NULL `description` column (Session 92 inventory in handoff did not surface this column; my recon query went to `pg_proc` not `information_schema.columns`). Re-applied successfully with description added + the stale `article_unpublished` description update bundled in. **Lesson candidate for §131 or new §-number: always query `information_schema.columns` on the target table before drafting any INSERT migration, especially for tables not directly modified in current session's prior work.**

**Smoke test (Path B - minimal):** Single DO block exercised 6 invariants on a fixture article slug `g1-3c-smoke-state-transitions` (id `58adbd26-...`):

- T1: schedule with far-future timestamp returns `effective_at_clamped=false`, status=scheduled. PASS.
- T2: schedule with skew-window timestamp (now() - 30 sec) returns `effective_at_clamped=true`, scheduled_for > now(). PASS.
- T3: schedule with far-past timestamp (now() - 2 min) raises 22023 with "must be in the future" message. PASS.
- T4: archive succeeds, archived_at populated. PASS.
- T5: schedule on archived article raises 22023 with "archived — cannot transition. Archive is terminal in v1." message. PASS.
- T6: double-archive raises 22023 with "already archived" message. PASS.

Audit-row shape verification: both `before_value` and `after_value` contain `authors` array on `article_scheduled` and `article_archived` rows (item 15 ✓); `effective_at_clamped=true` only on the clamped schedule row (clamp signal ✓). Fixture article DELETEd post-test; cascade clean (0 articles, 0 authors, 0 versions remaining). 6 immutable audit rows persist correctly prefixed `'G1.3c smoke test - <step>'` per §130 candidate convention.

**EXACT STATE OF DB AT END OF SESSION 93 G1.3c:**
- Tables: unchanged from Session 92 close (5 newsletter tables, all empty).
- Functions: 9 from Session 92 + 5 new from G1.3c = 14 newsletter-related functions total. Two Session 92 functions (`commit_article_version`, `restore_article_version`) rewritten with asymmetric audit shape.
- Action_types: 10 from Session 92 + 1 new (`article_schedule_cancelled`) = 11 newsletter action_types. One description (`article_unpublished`) updated.
- RLS policies: unchanged (12 newsletter policies).
- Indexes: -1 (slug_lookup_idx dropped); no new indexes this migration.
- Smoke-test audit rows in production: Session 92's 5 + Session 93's 6 = 11 total, all reason-prefixed and identifiable.
- Storage buckets, Edge Functions, cron jobs: unchanged (none yet for newsletter).

**G1.3d ship narrative (migration `20260523175312_session93_newsletter_articles_read_rpcs`):**

Single migration, applied + verified clean on first try (no aborts this time — pre-migration column-recon discipline applied per lesson candidate from G1.3c apply error). Resolves Session 92 Gap 5 and ships all 5 read RPCs in one atomic migration:

- (1) **Gap 5 resolution: ADD COLUMN `newsletter_subscribers.linked_user_id` (Q4(B) Lovable-locked).** Nullable uuid REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL. Populated at confirm-time when confirming viewer is authenticated; NULL for anon subscribers. Future-proof against users.email rotation. COMMENT documents the multi-subscription edge case for G2 `confirm_newsletter_subscription` to address (when auth.uid() already has a confirmed row: reject or merge — deferred decision).
- (2) **Partial index `newsletter_subscribers_linked_user_id_idx` WHERE `linked_user_id IS NOT NULL AND status = 'confirmed'`** per Lovable R2 reinforcement (status predicate folded into partial index since every read-path query pairs them — smaller index, faster gate check on hot path).
- (3) **`get_article_for_reader(p_slug text)`** — anon-callable SECDEF STABLE, search_path=public,pg_temp. ONE param per v2 N3 (viewer identity from `auth.uid()`). Returns wrapper `{access:'granted'|'paywall'|'not_found', article, authors[, paywall_reason]}` per Lovable R3 vocabulary lock. Gate eval: public always granted; subscriber_aggregate granted if confirmed-subscriber (linked_user_id match OR lower(email) fallback per R1) OR `current_user_active_plan_tier() IS NOT NULL`; plan_tier granted if viewer_tier ∈ allowed_plan_tiers. Paywall payload exactly: `{id, slug, title, excerpt, cover_asset_id, published_at, gate, allowed_plan_tiers}` + `authors` (display-name-only) + `paywall_reason ∈ ('subscriber_required','plan_tier_required')` per R4 contract. NO body_tiptap leakage on paywall. Granted payload carries body_tiptap + full author records (user_id + display_name + order).
- (4) **`preview_article_as_viewer_class(p_article_id uuid, p_viewer_class text)`** — super_admin only (`assert_super_admin` gate, no impersonation gate since read-only). 5-value whitelist (`anon|subscriber|base|premium|individual`). Mirrors `get_article_for_reader` gate-eval logic against simulated viewer state. Echoes `viewer_class` in response so frontend can render "Preview as: [class]" banner. Ignores `published`/`archived` state intentionally (super admin needs to preview drafts and scheduled articles during authoring).
- (5) **`list_articles_for_archive(p_gate_filter text DEFAULT NULL, p_category_id uuid DEFAULT NULL, p_limit integer DEFAULT 12, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)`** — anon-callable SECDEF STABLE. Q5(A) show-with-previews semantics: returns all published non-archived articles paginated; each row carries `access_state` ∈ `'granted'|'paywall'` per per-viewer gate eval. Wrapper `{items, total, limit, offset}` per §111. Three defenses: (a) `p_gate_filter` whitelist NULL or one-of-3-valid-gates (anti-enumeration per Lovable Q5 followup); (b) `p_limit` clamped via `GREATEST(1, LEAST(p_limit, 50))` per R5 anti-exfiltration cap; (c) `p_category_id` accepted for forward-compat with categories landing in G2/G4 (unused for v1, no error if non-NULL). Single pass viewer-state resolution (subscriber check + plan_tier lookup) once per call, reused across all rows. Paywall row payload byte-identical to `get_article_for_reader` paywall branch per R4.
- (6) **`list_article_versions(p_article_id uuid)`** — super_admin only. Wrapper `{items, total, capped}` per §111. Capped at 200 versions (returns `capped: true` if total > 200; non-blocking, version-history UI in G5 can show "showing latest 200" if hit). Each item: version_id, version_number, version_type, version_name, restored_from_version_id, created_by_user_id, created_by_display_name (left-joined from users), created_at, title_snapshot, body_preview (first 200 chars of `body_tiptap::text` for diff-viewer preview).
- (7) **`get_article_version(p_version_id uuid)`** — super_admin only. Full version row including body_tiptap and metadata_snapshot (which carries the historical authors array per Session 92's _snapshot_article_version pattern).
- (8) GRANTs: get_article_for_reader + list_articles_for_archive to anon + authenticated + service_role (public reader paths). preview + list_versions + get_version to authenticated + service_role (assert_super_admin gates internally). All REVOKE PUBLIC first.

**Lovable approval at G1.3d open:** 2 questions (Q4 subscriber-user linking, Q5 archive gate-filter semantics) with 3-point scope cross-references and recommendations. Lovable confirmed Q4=(B) + Q5=(A) with 5 reinforcements (R1-R5) and 1 question-back about email lowercase enforcement. Question-back answered via DB recon: `newsletter_subscribers_email_lowercase CHECK` already shipped Session 92; subscribers side guaranteed lowercase; users side has no such constraint, so fallback JOIN explicitly lowercases users.email side (R1 honored). One forward-compat note documented: G2 confirm_newsletter_subscription must decide multi-subscription reject-or-merge policy (multi-row edge case in Lovable Q4 point 2). All 5 R1-R5 reinforcements folded into migration body. Three Cole-confirmed implementation calls: preview ignores published/archived state, list_article_versions capped at 200 with capped flag, all author display_name reads use `users.full_name`.

**Smoke test (Path B - minimal extended for read RPCs):** Single DO block exercised 14 invariants on 3 fixture articles (public + subscriber_aggregate + plan_tier=[premium,individual]):
- T1: public article get_article_for_reader returns granted with body_tiptap and authors. PASS.
- T2: subscriber_aggregate as anon returns paywall + subscriber_required + NO body_tiptap leak (R4 contract holds). PASS.
- T3: plan_tier as anon returns paywall + plan_tier_required. PASS.
- T4: nonexistent slug returns `{access: 'not_found'}`. PASS.
- T5: list_articles_for_archive as anon returns wrapper shape with ≥3 rows + mixed access_state (public granted + gated paywall). PASS.
- T6: list_articles_for_archive with bad p_gate_filter raises 22023 (anti-enumeration guard). PASS.
- T7: p_limit=10000 clamped to 50 (R5 anti-exfiltration cap). PASS.
- T8: p_gate_filter='public' returns only granted rows. PASS.
- T9: preview_article_as_viewer_class(sub_agg, 'anon') returns paywall. PASS.
- T10: preview(plan_tier=[premium,individual], 'premium') returns granted. PASS.
- T11: preview(plan_tier=[premium,individual], 'base') returns paywall (base not in allowlist). PASS.
- T12: preview bad viewer_class raises 22023. PASS.
- T13: list_article_versions returns {items, total, capped} wrapper. PASS.
- T14: get_article_version returns full body_tiptap + metadata_snapshot with authors. PASS.

Fixtures DELETEd post-test. Cascade clean. 6 new audit rows persist from fixture setup (3 article_created + 3 article_published, all reason-prefixed 'G1.3d smoke test - ...'). Read RPCs themselves emit no audit rows by design. Total smoke-test audit rows across both sessions: 17 (Session 92: 5, Session 93: 12).

**EXACT STATE OF DB AT END OF SESSION 93 G1.3d:**
- Tables: unchanged from Session 92 close + 1 new column on newsletter_subscribers (linked_user_id).
- Functions: 9 from Session 92 + 5 G1.3c + 5 G1.3d = 19 newsletter-related functions total.
- Action_types: 11 (unchanged from G1.3c close).
- RLS policies: 12 (unchanged).
- Indexes: 1 dropped (slug_lookup_idx in G1.3c) + 1 added (linked_user_id_idx in G1.3d) = net 0.
- Smoke-test audit rows in production: 17 total (5 Session 92 + 12 Session 93), all reason-prefixed.
- Storage buckets, Edge Functions, cron jobs: still none for newsletter.

**G1.3e ship narrative (migration `20260523175845_session93_newsletter_draft_pruning_function_and_cron`):**

Single migration applying:
- (1) `prune_newsletter_draft_versions()` zero-arg SECDEF plpgsql function, search_path=public,pg_temp. Per article: identify draft version rows older than 7 days, rank `ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY article_id ORDER BY created_at DESC)`, DELETE rows where rn > 20 (keeping top-20-most-recent per article). Non-draft version types (named_revision/scheduled/published/restored_from) retained forever. Returns wrapper `{deleted_count, articles_touched, ran_at}` for cron observability. REVOKE PUBLIC, GRANT service_role + postgres.
- (2) Cron job `prune_newsletter_draft_versions` registered at `35 6 * * *` (non-colliding slot per Session 92 Discovery 2). **Shipped DISABLED** (`active = false`) per Lovable v2 item 3 — Cole monitors draft accumulation for 2 weeks before enabling. Comment in migration documents the enable command (`SELECT cron.alter_job(jobid, active := true)`).

**Apply error caught + corrected (second such error this session):** First apply attempt failed at `UPDATE cron.job SET active = false WHERE jobname = '...'` with `42501 permission denied for table job`. The migration role doesn't own `cron.job` directly. Re-applied successfully using `cron.schedule_in_database(name, schedule, command, database, username, active)` which has an `active boolean` parameter — registers the job in disabled state in a single function call without needing direct UPDATE on `cron.job`. **Lesson candidate for §-number at session close: pg_cron migrations must use `cron.schedule_in_database()` for disable-at-ship pattern, never follow-up `UPDATE cron.job`. The function API has appropriate permissions; the table-level UPDATE does not.** Pairs with the G1.3c lesson candidate (always query schema before INSERT). Both are recon-discipline rules.

**Smoke test:** Single zero-arg call to `prune_newsletter_draft_versions()` against currently-empty `newsletter_article_versions` table. Returns `{ran_at: <timestamp>, deleted_count: 0, articles_touched: 0}`. Confirms function callable end-to-end with correct wrapper shape, no permission errors, no SECDEF issues. Cron job remains active=false; no scheduled run will occur until Cole enables. No new audit rows (function emits no log_super_admin_action — it's an internal maintenance cron, not a super-admin action).

**EXACT STATE OF DB AT END OF SESSION 93 G1.3e:**
- Tables, RLS, action_types, indexes: unchanged from G1.3d close.
- Functions: +1 new = 20 newsletter-related functions total.
- Cron jobs: +1 new (`prune_newsletter_draft_versions` at 35 6 * * *, active=false). First newsletter cron job to ship. Two more (`prune_newsletter_subscribe_attempts` at 6:15, `expire_pending_newsletter_confirmations` at 6:25) owed in G2.
- Smoke-test audit rows: 17 (unchanged from G1.3d — G1.3e emits no audit rows).
- Storage buckets, Edge Functions: still none for newsletter.

**G1.4 ship narrative — TWO migrations applied this phase (the second a fix-up for whitelist omission caught by smoke):**

**Migration 1: `20260523181119_session93_newsletter_articles_inline_asset_refs`** (748 lines)

**Lovable approval at G1.4 open:** Q6 raised with three coherent paths (A bucket-only, B content_assets-integrated, C hybrid) plus four implementation sub-questions (Q6.1-Q6.4). Pre-Lovable recon discovered: (a) existing `_walk_block_config_for_asset_refs` walker is structurally lesson_block-only and can't be surgically extended for TipTap (handoff/scope assumed it could); (b) v2 spec for `convert-html-to-tiptap` Edge Function had inline images going straight to Storage bypassing `content_assets` — meaning scope's "extend content_asset_refs to 7-way" assumed an integration that wasn't fully specified. Cole locked path (B): inline images flow through `content_assets` with `attrs.asset_id` as canonical TipTap node reference, never `src`. Lovable confirmed Q6.1-Q6.4 + 5 reinforcements + 1 question-back (FK setup on `content_asset_refs.asset_id`). Recon confirmed FK is `ON DELETE RESTRICT`, archive flows are soft via `archived_at`, mirroring `_cascade_archive_asset_refs_for_content_item` is correct.

**What landed in migration 1:**

- (1) Schema extension: ADD COLUMN `content_asset_refs.newsletter_article_id uuid REFERENCES newsletter_articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`. DROP + recreate `content_asset_refs_exactly_one_parent` CHECK as 7-way. Partial index `WHERE newsletter_article_id IS NOT NULL AND archived_at IS NULL`.
- (2) `_walk_tiptap_for_image_asset_refs(p_body_tiptap jsonb)` — recursive CTE over TipTap node tree. Returns `TABLE(out_ref_path text, out_asset_id uuid)`. Image nodes with `attrs.asset_id` matching UUID pattern → emit row with path like `body.image[2.1]`. Image nodes with missing/non-uuid asset_id → silently skipped (per Lovable R-Q6.2a). Walker name encodes v1 scope per Lovable R-Q6.3.
- (3) `_cascade_archive_asset_refs_for_newsletter_article(p_article_id, p_caller_id, p_archive_reason)` — exact mirror of `_cascade_archive_asset_refs_for_content_item` adapted to newsletter parent. Archives refs first, then for each affected non-library asset where `_asset_active_ref_count = 0`, archives the asset via `_archive_asset_internal`.
- (4) `_rebind_newsletter_article_asset_refs(p_article_id, p_caller_id)` — Rebind owns ALL article refs (cover + og + inline). Three sources, one function. Phase 1 builds desired-set from cover_asset_id column + og_image_asset_id column + walker output; for each desired ref does FK-safety pre-check (skip with NOTICE if asset not active per Lovable R-Q6.2b) then B-2 rebind on `(asset_id, newsletter_article_id, archived_at IS NULL)` UPDATE-or-INSERT. Phase 2 archives refs whose asset_id is no longer in desired set. Returns `{refs_created, refs_rebound, refs_archived}`.
- (5) Integration into 4 existing RPCs (full function body replacements via CREATE OR REPLACE):
  - `upsert_article`: rebind call BEFORE audit emission per Lovable R-Q6.4. Audit `after_value` includes `asset_refs` summary.
  - `auto_save_article`: rebind call after UPDATE; no audit to coordinate with.
  - `restore_article_version`: rebind BEFORE audit emission; `after_value` includes `asset_refs`.
  - `archive_article`: cascade-archive call BEFORE audit emission. **Fixes §43 gap from G1.3c**: original archive_article shipped without cascade integration. Migration body comment calls this out explicitly.

**Migration 2: `session93_newsletter_articles_inline_asset_refs_archive_reason_whitelist`** (fix-up)

**Apply error caught by smoke test (third such error this session):** G1.4 migration 1 shipped clean (`success: true`). Smoke test then failed at T7 (archive_article cascade) with `23514 content_assets_archive_reason_check` violation. The CHECK constraint whitelists 8 archive_reason values; my cascade function passed `'newsletter_article_archived'` which was not in the whitelist. Same §99 discipline that applies to action_types: every flow that writes to a whitelisted column must add its value in the same migration. Re-applied as fix-up migration 2, extending the whitelist to 9 values.

**Lesson candidate for §-number at session close (third recon-discipline rule this session, all related):** ALL of these belong to the same family — query the schema/constraint state before drafting any migration that writes whitelisted values, calls cron-restricted functions, or INSERTs into tables not freshly authored. The pattern across all three:

- G1.3c apply error: missed NOT NULL `description` column on `super_admin_action_types` INSERT
- G1.3e apply error: tried to UPDATE `cron.job` table (no permission); needed `cron.schedule_in_database(active => false)`
- G1.4 smoke error: missed whitelist update on `content_assets.archive_reason` CHECK

All three preventable by pre-migration recon. Standing rule candidate: **"Before drafting any migration that INSERTs/UPDATEs/calls into a table not freshly authored in the current session, query the schema first (information_schema.columns for nullability/defaults, pg_constraint for CHECK predicates, pg_proc for available functions)."**

**Smoke test (G1.4 full lifecycle, 8 invariants):**

Fixture: 1 article, 1 library asset (cover), 2 throwaway non-library assets (inline body images). Tests:

- T1: Create article with cover + 2 inline images → 3 active refs total. PASS.
- T2: Rebind result reports `refs_created=3`. PASS.
- T3: `ref_field` paths correct: `cover_asset`, `body.image[1]`, `body.image[2.1]`. PASS.
- T4: Edit body to remove second inline image → 2 active refs + 1 archived ref. PASS.
- T5: Rebind result reports `refs_archived=1`. PASS.
- T6: Idempotent re-save (same body) → `refs_rebound=2, refs_created=0` (B-2 rebind preserves rows, no churn). PASS.
- T7: Archive article → cascade archives 2 remaining refs + non-library asset_a (only ref archived); library cover asset stays active (protected by `is_library_asset = true` filter). PASS.
- T8: Walker tolerance — body with 3 image nodes (1 with `src` only, 1 with non-uuid asset_id, 1 with valid uuid) → only 1 ref created, malformed nodes silently skipped. PASS.

6 new audit rows from smoke (article_created + article_saved×2 + article_archived×2 + 1 more — covering both fixture article and tolerance article lifecycle). All reason-prefixed `'G1.4 smoke test - ...'`. Fixture cascade verified: library asset remained active, non-library throwaway assets correctly archived.

- Tables: unchanged from G1.3e close (no new newsletter tables; just `content_asset_refs` extended with newsletter_article_id column).
- Functions: +3 new (`_walk_tiptap_for_image_asset_refs`, `_cascade_archive_asset_refs_for_newsletter_article`, `_rebind_newsletter_article_asset_refs`) + 4 RPC replacements = 23 newsletter-related functions total.
- Action_types: 11 (unchanged).
- RLS policies: 12 (unchanged).
- CHECK constraints: `content_asset_refs_exactly_one_parent` now 7-way; `content_assets_archive_reason_check` now 9-value whitelist.
- Indexes: +1 new (`content_asset_refs_newsletter_article_idx`).
- Cron jobs: 1 (unchanged from G1.3e).
- Smoke-test audit rows: 23 total Session 93 (G1.3c: 6, G1.3d: 6, G1.4: 6 + 5 from fixture-prep auto_saves not yet counted exactly).
- Storage buckets, Edge Functions: still none for newsletter.

**G1.5 ship narrative (migration `20260523181555_session93_newsletter_article_images_bucket`):**

Single migration applying the final piece of Phase G1 backend lift. No Lovable approval needed — every aspect locked by scope §G1.5 + v2 §G1.5 with no design ambiguity. Pre-migration recon (applying the recon-discipline lesson from earlier this session) confirmed `podcast-feed` bucket Session 91 precedent + no naming collision + correct INSERT signature into `storage.buckets`.

- Bucket `newsletter-article-images` created with `public=true`, 10MB ceiling, MIME allowlist `image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, image/gif`. ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency.
- Two §82-clean RLS policies on `storage.objects`:
  - `newsletter_article_images_public_select` TO public, FOR SELECT, USING `bucket_id = 'newsletter-article-images'`
  - `newsletter_article_images_service_role_all` TO service_role, FOR ALL, USING + WITH CHECK on bucket_id

Public-tier classification per §84 — promotional content, world-readable, no PII. Bytes served via Supabase Storage CDN. Used by `convert-html-to-tiptap` Edge Function (G2) rehosting external images at paste-time, native TipTap editor image-upload toolbar (G4), and public reader page (G6). No smoke test needed — bucket existence + 2 policies are statically verifiable; actual reads/writes get exercised in G2 onward.

**§-numbered standing rule candidates from Session 93 (Cole locks at session close in architecture-reference v96 entry):**

- §129 candidate from Session 92: SECDEF functions calling pgcrypto must include `extensions` in search_path
- §130 candidate from Session 92: Smoke-test audit rows are unavoidable + must be self-identifying via reason prefix
- §131 candidate from Session 92: pg_cron slot collision discovery before scheduling new jobs
- **§132 candidate from Session 93 (NEW):** Pre-migration schema recon discipline — before drafting any migration that INSERTs/UPDATEs/calls into a table not freshly authored in current session, query (a) `information_schema.columns` for nullability/defaults, (b) `pg_constraint` for CHECK predicates, (c) `pg_proc` for available function signatures. Caught 3 apply errors in Session 93 alone; all preventable.
- **§133 candidate from Session 93 (NEW):** TipTap node asset reference contract — newsletter inline image nodes MUST carry `attrs.asset_id` referencing a `content_assets` row; `src` is derived at render time, never canonical. Locks Lovable's Q6.2 architecture decision into a discoverable rule for future authors (G2 Edge Function, G4 native upload, G6 reader render path all honor this).
- **§134 candidate from Session 93 (NEW):** pg_cron disable-at-ship pattern — use `cron.schedule_in_database(name, schedule, command, database, username, active => false)` rather than `cron.schedule()` + `UPDATE cron.job SET active = false`. The UPDATE path requires elevated permissions not available to migration roles; the function API has appropriate permissions.
