*v99 (SESSION 92 PARTIAL - SUPERSEDED BY v100) - **Session 92 Group G newsletter platform Phase G1 backend lift PARTIAL — 5 of approximately 8 migrations applied + verified. Context exhausted; next session resumes at G1.3c.**

**Decisions locked Session 92 open (before any SQL ran):**
- Q7: 3 articles at launch, mixed gates (1 public + 1 subscriber_aggregate + 1 plan_tier), pulled from Cole's existing LinkedIn long-form posts via Paste-HTML authoring path (Q2 Path X from scope). BrainWise becomes canonical; LinkedIn posts get edited at launch to add "this article has moved to [BrainWise URL]" note as funnel. No fake test-article seed content. If 3 real articles not ready by launch, ship archive empty with "Newsletter launches soon — subscribe to be notified" empty state.
- Q8: Defer comments to v2. No comments table, no moderation surface in v1. Engagement paths: existing contact form, inline subscribe form on every article, reply-to-newsletter via Resend inbound on a `newsletter@mail.brainwiseenterprises.com` address routed to Cole's inbox (G8).
- SSR: Vercel migration locked. Cole's Vercel account already created. Phase G3 sequenced AFTER G4/G5 (revised order G1 → G2 → G4 → G5 → G3 → G6) per Lovable v2 review item 13 — DNS cutover risk window stays closed during authoring UI development.
- NEW decision (overrides scope §6 non-goal "multi-author byline"): Multi-author bylines in v1. Cole and Phil Dixon (both currently `brainwise_super_admin`) author together from launch. Junction table `newsletter_article_authors` with `UNIQUE (article_id, author_order)`. Either listed author can edit. Edge case: junction row persists if author loses super_admin status (authorship is historical). Author bios deferred to v2; ship v1 with just names. Co-author changes audit-logged via existing `article_saved` action_type with `before_value`/`after_value` both ALWAYS including full author array (per Lovable v2 item 15).
- Authoring capabilities locked: Paste-HTML path sanitizes via dompurify allowlist (`p, h2-h4, ul, ol, li, strong, em, a[href only], blockquote, code, pre, hr, br, img[src/alt/width/height only], figure, figcaption`); strips all `style`/`class`/`id` attributes and all `on*` handlers; branding applied at render time via TipTap renderer component mappings. External `<img>` URLs detected at paste-time, downloaded by `convert-html-to-tiptap` Edge Function, uploaded to `newsletter-article-images` Storage bucket (public-tier per §84, owed in G1.5), src rewritten to BrainWise-hosted URL. Iframe allowlist for trusted video providers: `youtube.com/embed/*`, `youtube-nocookie.com/embed/*`, `player.vimeo.com/video/*`. Native TipTap editor toolbar: bold, italic, underline (off by default), strikethrough, inline code, H2/H3/H4 (H1 reserved for article title at template level), bulleted+numbered lists, link, blockquote, image upload, embed (YouTube/Vimeo URL), code block (lowlight syntax highlighting), horizontal rule, undo/redo. Deferred to v2: tables, footnotes, custom callouts, drop caps/pull quotes, author-uploaded native video.

**Lovable v2 review nits resolved at migration-write time (3 of 3):**
- N1 (`pgcrypto` schema-qualification): VERIFIED `pgcrypto` v1.3 installed in `extensions` schema (not `public`). Confirmed via `pg_extension` query before any migration ran. Pattern locked: SECDEF functions calling `digest()` or `gen_random_bytes()` use `SET search_path = public, extensions, pg_temp`. No `CREATE EXTENSION` line needed. Applied in G1.2b (import_newsletter_subscribers_bulk uses both digest and gen_random_bytes).
- N2 (CHECK constraint placement + tier allowlist): All CHECK constraints written as named `CONSTRAINT name CHECK (...)` clauses at end of column list. Added `newsletter_articles_allowed_tiers_valid_values CHECK (allowed_plan_tiers <@ ARRAY['base','premium','individual']::text[])` — array-subset operator prevents garbage tier strings.
- N3 (drop `p_viewer_user_id` footgun): Confirmed at design time, will be reflected in G1.3d when `get_article_for_reader` is written (NOT yet shipped). Signature will be `get_article_for_reader(p_slug text)` — viewer identity always from `auth.uid()`. `preview_article_as_viewer_class` becomes the only way for super-admin to simulate different viewer.

**Pre-migration architectural discoveries Session 92:**
- `pgcrypto` v1.3 lives in `extensions` schema (not `public`). pg_cron v1.6.4 lives in `pg_catalog`. pg_net v0.20.0 lives in `extensions`.
- Existing precedent for digest()/gen_random_bytes() calls is mixed: `corporate_employee_choose_individual` uses `search_path=public, extensions, auth`; `peer_access_request_create` uses `search_path=public` (risky — relies on default search_path resolving extensions). NEW pattern locked for G1 onward: explicit `extensions` in search_path.
- Existing pg_cron job COLLISION at slot `20 6 * * *`: `process_due_scheduled_assignments_daily` already runs at 6:20 (touches scheduled_assignments, user_curriculum_assignments, user_module_assignments, coach_certifications, coach_mentor_assignments, super_admin_audit_log). Newsletter cron jobs rescheduled to NON-colliding minutes: `prune_newsletter_subscribe_attempts` at 6:15, `expire_pending_newsletter_confirmations` at 6:25, `prune_newsletter_draft_versions` at 6:35. Working sets are disjoint (newsletter_* tables only), no shared lock contention with assignments cron.
- `super_admin_audit_log` has an immutability trigger `block_audit_log_mutations()` that blocks UPDATE and DELETE. Smoke-test audit rows persist permanently. Convention adopted: all smoke-test RPC calls use `reason` prefix `"G1.X smoke test - <detail>"` so test rows are self-identifying. Five smoke-test audit rows remain in production audit log after Session 92 (acceptable — they're correctly labeled).
- `super_admin_audit_log.action_type` has HARD FK to `super_admin_action_types.action_type`. Confirms §99 discipline: action_types MUST be INSERTed in same migration as RPCs that emit them, BEFORE the RPC body can be called. Verified at deploy time — every G1 migration that adds RPCs emitting new action_types INSERTs those action_types in the same migration.
- JWT context test pattern: `set_config('request.jwt.claims', json_build_object(...)::text, false)` for session-local context (true is transaction-local and breaks across MCP statements). Used in DO blocks for multi-step smoke tests.
- `current_user_account_type()` reads `auth.uid()` directly — has `SET search_path TO 'public', 'auth', 'pg_temp'`, SECURITY DEFINER, STABLE.
- `assert_super_admin()` raises 42501 with message `'Permission denied: caller is not a BrainWise super admin'` when `current_user_account_type() IS DISTINCT FROM 'brainwise_super_admin'`.
- `assert_impersonation_allows(p_action_category)` denylist includes `permission_change` for `account_type` modifications. Newsletter authoring RPCs use `permission_change` as the action category (treated as content-management surface; the denylist behavior is appropriate — impersonating a super admin shouldn't allow article authoring under their identity).
- `log_super_admin_action(p_target_user_id, p_target_org_id, p_action_type, p_before, p_after, p_reason, p_mode)` is the canonical audit insert path. Returns the inserted audit row id (uuid). Implements scheduled-assignment-actor GUC bypass for cron context.

**MIGRATIONS SHIPPED + VERIFIED THIS SESSION (5):**

(1) **`20260523170352_session92_current_user_active_plan_tier_helper`** APPLIED + VERIFIED. Creates `public.current_user_active_plan_tier()` — zero-arg, returns text, LANGUAGE sql, SECURITY DEFINER, STABLE, `SET search_path = public, pg_temp`. Returns `subscription_tier` from `public.users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND subscription_status = 'active' AND subscription_tier IN ('base','premium','individual') LIMIT 1`. Returns NULL for anon, NULL for inactive subscriptions, NULL for users with no subscription. GRANTed EXECUTE to authenticated + anon + service_role. REVOKEd from PUBLIC. Six-check verification PASSED: signature shape, grants, anon returns NULL, active base subscriber `cf0fece6-af64-481e-beb3-4c806c86bd8f` (synthetic-ic-1@brainwise.test) returns `'base'`, active premium subscriber `d7fe0e36-a215-45de-bef0-cae65cb7c4d2` (test@gmail.com) returns `'premium'`, inactive subscription `3f336270-469e-4a08-858c-a0317640c832` (demo-director-4@brainwise.test, base:inactive) returns NULL.

(2) **`20260523170706_session92_newsletter_subscribers_table_and_rls`** APPLIED + VERIFIED. Creates two tables (no RPCs in this migration):
   - `public.newsletter_subscribers` (16 columns): id uuid PK gen_random_uuid, email text NOT NULL, status text NOT NULL, confirmation_token_hash text (UNIQUE, NULL allowed), confirmation_token_expires_at timestamptz, unsubscribe_token_hash text (UNIQUE, NULL allowed), confirmed_at timestamptz, unsubscribed_at timestamptz, source text, referrer_url text, consent_evidence text, resend_contact_id text, created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). Named CHECK constraints: `newsletter_subscribers_email_format` (length > 0 AND email regex), `newsletter_subscribers_email_lowercase` (email = lower(email)), `newsletter_subscribers_status_valid` (5-value whitelist), `newsletter_subscribers_email_unique`, `newsletter_subscribers_confirmation_token_hash_unique`, `newsletter_subscribers_unsubscribe_token_hash_unique`. Four indexes: `newsletter_subscribers_confirmed_idx` (partial WHERE status='confirmed'), `newsletter_subscribers_confirmation_token_hash_lookup_idx` (partial WHERE hash IS NOT NULL), `newsletter_subscribers_unsubscribe_token_hash_lookup_idx` (partial WHERE hash IS NOT NULL), `newsletter_subscribers_pending_expiry_idx` (partial WHERE status='pending_confirmation'). Trigger `trg_newsletter_subscribers_touch_updated_at` BEFORE UPDATE calls `_touch_newsletter_subscribers_updated_at()`. RLS enabled. Two §82-clean policies: `newsletter_subscribers_super_admin_all` TO authenticated USING (EXISTS users super_admin), `newsletter_subscribers_service_role_all` TO service_role USING (true) WITH CHECK (true). NO anon/authenticated public read — all public-facing operations go through SECDEF RPCs (lands G2).
   - `public.newsletter_subscribe_attempts` (3 columns): id uuid PK, ip_address inet NOT NULL, attempted_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). One index: `newsletter_subscribe_attempts_ip_attempted_idx` on (ip_address, attempted_at DESC). RLS enabled. Two §82-clean policies: super_admin_all + service_role_all. For per-IP rate limiting in G2 anon-callable subscribe RPC (5 attempts/hour/IP).
   - Verification PASSED: 16 + 3 columns present, 6 + 1 named constraints fire correctly on bad data (rejected: `not-an-email` via email_format, `Test@Example.com` via email_lowercase, `bogus_status` via status_valid; valid `'pending_confirmation'` insert succeeds), 4 RLS policies in place, 4 indexes built.

(3) **`20260523170911_session92_newsletter_subscriber_bulk_import_and_action_types`** APPLIED + VERIFIED. INSERTs 2 action_types in same migration as RPC that emits them per §99: `newsletter_subscriber_imported_bulk` and `newsletter_subscriber_unsubscribed_by_admin`. Both `category='content_authoring'`, `requires_justification=true`, `denylist_during_impersonation=true`, `is_mutation=true`, `tier=NULL`, `requires_mfa=false`. ON CONFLICT (action_type) DO NOTHING for idempotency. Creates `public.import_newsletter_subscribers_bulk(p_subscribers jsonb, p_reason text) RETURNS jsonb` — LANGUAGE plpgsql, SECURITY DEFINER, `SET search_path = public, extensions, pg_temp` (per N1 resolution; uses `gen_random_bytes(32)` and `digest(text, 'sha256')`). Gate: `assert_super_admin()` + `assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change')` + reason ≥10 chars. Validates `p_subscribers` is jsonb array, cap 500. Per-row error isolation via BEGIN/EXCEPTION. Per row: `lower(trim(email))`, requires non-empty `consent_evidence` per Lovable v2 item 8 (else row fails with error='consent_evidence_required'), generates raw token via `translate(encode(gen_random_bytes(32),'base64'),'+/=','-_')`, stores SHA-256 hash via `encode(digest(raw_token,'sha256'),'hex')` (64-char hex output), inserts with `status='confirmed'`, `confirmed_at=now()`. Returns raw token ONCE in results array for caller to use in email dispatch — never re-derivable from DB. Single audit row covers bulk operation via `log_super_admin_action` with `after_value` containing `{requested, succeeded, failed, imported_emails}`. REVOKE from PUBLIC, GRANT to authenticated + service_role. Verified: bulk import as Cole (super_admin `<super-admin-uuid>`) with 2 rows (1 valid + 1 missing consent_evidence) returned `{requested:2, succeeded:1, failed:1}`; landed row had `status='confirmed'`, `confirmed_at` populated, `unsubscribe_token_hash` length=64 (SHA-256 hex), `confirmation_token_hash IS NULL`, `consent_evidence` captured. Audit row created with correct FK chain. Non-super-admin caller `cf0fece6-...` rejected at `assert_super_admin` gate (42501), zero leak. Smoke-test subscribers fixture DELETEd post-test; audit row remains per immutability (reason `'G1.2 smoke test of bulk import RPC after fresh deploy'`).

(4) **`20260523171213_session92_newsletter_articles_tables_rls_action_types`** APPLIED + VERIFIED. Creates 3 tables + INSERTs 8 action_types in same migration per §99:
   - `public.newsletter_articles` (23 columns): id uuid PK, slug text, title text, excerpt text, body_tiptap jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, body_html_rendered text, cover_asset_id uuid REFERENCES content_assets(id), gate text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'public', allowed_plan_tiers text[] NOT NULL DEFAULT ARRAY[]::text[], status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft', scheduled_for timestamptz, published_at timestamptz, source_type text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'native', word_count integer, read_time_minutes integer, seo_title text, seo_description text, og_image_asset_id uuid REFERENCES content_assets(id), canonical_url text, created_by_user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), archived_at timestamptz. **NOTE: `is_current_published` column intentionally NOT present per Lovable v2 review N4 — status enum encodes the state.** 10 named CHECK constraints (all at end of column list per N2): `newsletter_articles_slug_unique`, `newsletter_articles_slug_format` (length>0 AND `^[a-z0-9-]+$`), `newsletter_articles_title_nonempty`, `newsletter_articles_gate_valid` (3-value), `newsletter_articles_status_valid` (5-value), `newsletter_articles_source_type_valid` (2-value), `newsletter_articles_allowed_tiers_match_gate` (using `cardinality()` per item 5), `newsletter_articles_allowed_tiers_valid_values` (`<@ ARRAY['base','premium','individual']` per N2), `newsletter_articles_excerpt_required_for_gated` (excerpt IS NOT NULL AND length≥20 when gate IN subscriber_aggregate/plan_tier per item 10), `newsletter_articles_scheduled_consistency` (scheduled status requires scheduled_for > now() — KNOWN: cron-driven UPDATE flips status away from scheduled before time elapses; if cron lags and someone manually edits a stale-scheduled row the CHECK would block — narrow edge case, accepted for v1), `newsletter_articles_published_consistency` (published status requires published_at IS NOT NULL). 6 indexes: `newsletter_articles_pkey`, `newsletter_articles_slug_unique` (UNIQUE), `newsletter_articles_published_idx` (partial), `newsletter_articles_gate_published_idx` (partial), `newsletter_articles_slug_lookup_idx` (REDUNDANT with slug_unique — see FOLLOW-UP), `newsletter_articles_status_idx`, `newsletter_articles_scheduled_for_processing_idx` (partial). RLS enabled. 3 §82-clean policies: super_admin_all TO authenticated, service_role_all TO service_role, `newsletter_articles_public_read` TO anon,authenticated USING (`status='published' AND archived_at IS NULL AND gate='public'`). Gated articles (subscriber_aggregate, plan_tier) require RPC mediation per §116 (lands G1.3d as `get_article_for_reader`).
   - `public.newsletter_article_authors` (3 columns): article_id uuid REFERENCES newsletter_articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, author_user_id uuid REFERENCES users(id), author_order integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0. PRIMARY KEY (article_id, author_user_id) + named `newsletter_article_authors_order_unique UNIQUE (article_id, author_order)` per Lovable v2 item 6. Index on author_user_id. RLS enabled, 3 policies: super_admin_all, service_role_all, `newsletter_article_authors_public_read` TO anon,authenticated joined to publicly-readable articles.
   - `public.newsletter_article_versions` (12 columns): id uuid PK, article_id uuid REFERENCES newsletter_articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, version_number integer, version_type text NOT NULL, version_name text, body_tiptap jsonb NOT NULL, title_snapshot text NOT NULL, excerpt_snapshot text, metadata_snapshot jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, restored_from_version_id uuid REFERENCES newsletter_article_versions(id), created_by_user_id uuid REFERENCES users(id), created_at timestamptz. 3 named CHECKs: `_version_type_valid` (5-value), `_per_article_unique UNIQUE (article_id, version_number)`, `_restored_consistency` (restored_from version has restored_from_version_id, others have NULL). 3 indexes: pkey, `newsletter_article_versions_article_created_idx`, `newsletter_article_versions_draft_pruning_idx` (partial WHERE version_type='draft' per Lovable v2 item 3 retention pruning). RLS enabled, 2 policies (super_admin_all, service_role_all) — NO public read (version history is authoring-internal).
   - Trigger `trg_newsletter_articles_touch_updated_at` BEFORE UPDATE calls `_touch_newsletter_articles_updated_at()`.
   - INSERTed 8 action_types per §99, all `category='content_authoring'`, `requires_justification=true`, `denylist_during_impersonation=true`, `is_mutation=true`: `article_created`, `article_saved`, `article_version_committed`, `article_scheduled`, `article_published`, `article_unpublished`, `article_restored_from_version`, `article_archived`. ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency.
   - Verification PASSED: all 3 tables exist, 23+3+12 columns, 10 CHECKs on articles fire on bad data (rejected: `'Bad Slug With Spaces'` slug_format, gated article without excerpt, `ARRAY['premium','BOGUS_TIER']` valid_values, `plan_tier` gate with empty allowed_plan_tiers match_gate), valid public article INSERT succeeds. Author UNIQUE order fires on second insert with same `(article_id, author_order)` returning 23505 unique_violation. All 8 RLS policies in place, all action_types present. Smoke fixture article DELETEd (slug `g1-3a-smoke-valid`); cascade verified clean.

(5) **`20260523171529_session92_newsletter_articles_write_rpcs`** APPLIED + VERIFIED (with known gap — see FOLLOW-UPS). Creates 5 SECURITY DEFINER functions, all `SET search_path = public, extensions, pg_temp`:
   - **`_snapshot_article_version(p_article_id uuid, p_version_type text, p_version_name text DEFAULT NULL, p_restored_from_version_id uuid DEFAULT NULL) RETURNS uuid`** — internal helper. Loads article, builds authors jsonb via `jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object('user_id', author_user_id, 'order', author_order) ORDER BY author_order)` per Lovable v2 item 7, builds metadata_snapshot with `{authors, gate, allowed_plan_tiers, cover_asset_id, og_image_asset_id, seo_title, seo_description, canonical_url, source_type, word_count, read_time_minutes}`, computes next version_number, INSERTs row, returns version id. REVOKE PUBLIC, GRANT service_role only (NOT authenticated — only callable from other SECDEF RPCs and service_role).
   - **`auto_save_article(p_article_id, p_body_tiptap, + 7 optional metadata DEFAULT NULL params) RETURNS jsonb`** — silent auto-save per Lovable v2 item 3. Gate: assert_super_admin + assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change'). Touches body + curated metadata subset only (NOT gate/authors/status/scheduling/cover/og_image/source_type). UPDATE uses COALESCE so NULL params preserve existing values. Calls `_snapshot_article_version(_, 'draft', NULL, NULL)`. **DOES NOT call log_super_admin_action — no audit row.** GRANTed authenticated + service_role.
   - **`upsert_article(17 params) RETURNS jsonb`** — explicit save (create or update). Gate: assert_super_admin + assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change') + reason ≥10 chars. Branches on `p_article_id IS NULL` → create with `article_created` audit, else update with `article_saved` audit. At create, defaults `p_author_user_ids` to `[caller]` if NULL or empty. At update, captures before_value with authors array via jsonb_agg (per Lovable v2 item 15). If `p_author_user_ids IS NOT NULL`: validates cardinality ≥ 1, DELETEs all current author rows for the article, INSERTs new rows with `author_order = idx`. Captures after_value with authors. Calls `_snapshot_article_version(_, 'draft', NULL, NULL)`. Audit row written via log_super_admin_action with full before/after JSONs. GRANTed authenticated + service_role.
   - **`commit_article_version(p_article_id, p_version_name, p_reason) RETURNS jsonb`** — Gate: assert_super_admin + assert_impersonation_allows + reason ≥10 + non-empty version_name. Calls `_snapshot_article_version(_, 'named_revision', p_version_name, NULL)`. Audit row written. **GAP: `before_value = NULL` on the audit row, `after_value` contains only `{article_id, version_id, version_name}`. Does NOT include current article state with authors for auditor reconstruction. Inconsistent with Lovable v2 item 15 discipline — FOLLOW-UP for next session.**
   - **`restore_article_version(p_version_id, p_reason) RETURNS jsonb`** — Gate: assert_super_admin + assert_impersonation_allows + reason ≥10. Loads version row. Validates `metadata_snapshot.authors` is non-empty array (raises 22023 if missing). UPDATEs article body + metadata fields from snapshot. DELETEs current author rows. Loops `jsonb_array_elements(authors)` from snapshot, INSERTs new author junction rows per Lovable v2 Q-A (restoring brings back historical author roster even if those users have since lost super_admin status). Calls `_snapshot_article_version(_, 'restored_from', NULL, p_version_id)`. Audit row written. **GAP: `before_value = NULL` on audit row. Should include pre-restore article state with authors. FOLLOW-UP for next session.**
   - Verification PASSED end-to-end on smoke article slug `g1-3b-smoke-test-article` (id `78a8a0c5-ea5f-445e-92b3-2246f6793180` — now DELETEd, cascade clean): create → commit named revision → auto-save (silent, NO audit row created) → explicit upsert (article_saved audit created with both before_value.authors AND after_value.authors populated per item 15) → restore to named revision. Final article state confirmed body + title + word_count rolled back to v2 (named_revision) snapshot. Version sequence: v1=draft, v2=named_revision (name 'First named revision'), v3=draft (from auto_save), v4=draft (from upsert), v5=restored_from (with restored_from_version_id pointing at v2). All 5 versions had `metadata_snapshot.authors` populated with `[{order:0, user_id:1d14e510...}]`. Smoke-article DELETE cascade clean (0 articles, 0 authors, 0 versions remaining). 4 smoke-test audit rows remain (article_created, article_version_committed, article_saved, article_restored_from_version, all reason-prefixed 'G1.3b smoke test - ...').

**EXACT STATE OF DB AT SESSION 92 CONTEXT-EXHAUSTION:**
- Tables created (5): newsletter_subscribers, newsletter_subscribe_attempts, newsletter_articles, newsletter_article_authors, newsletter_article_versions. All EMPTY (zero rows).
- Functions created (9): current_user_active_plan_tier(), _touch_newsletter_subscribers_updated_at(), _touch_newsletter_articles_updated_at(), import_newsletter_subscribers_bulk(jsonb,text), _snapshot_article_version(uuid,text,text,uuid), auto_save_article(uuid,jsonb,text,text,text,text,text,int,int), upsert_article(17 params), commit_article_version(uuid,text,text), restore_article_version(uuid,text).
- Triggers created (2): trg_newsletter_subscribers_touch_updated_at, trg_newsletter_articles_touch_updated_at.
- Indexes created: subscribers (5: pkey + 4 partial/conditional), subscribe_attempts (2: pkey + ip), articles (7: pkey + slug_unique + 4 conditional + status + scheduled), authors (3: pkey + author + order_unique), versions (3: pkey + article_created + draft_pruning).
- Action_types INSERTed (10): newsletter_subscriber_imported_bulk, newsletter_subscriber_unsubscribed_by_admin, article_created, article_saved, article_version_committed, article_scheduled, article_published, article_unpublished, article_restored_from_version, article_archived.
- RLS policies created (13): subscribers (2), subscribe_attempts (2), articles (3 inc public_read), article_authors (3 inc public_read), article_versions (2).
- Smoke-test audit rows remaining in super_admin_audit_log (5, immutable, all reason-prefixed 'G1.X smoke test'): newsletter_subscriber_imported_bulk, article_created, article_saved, article_version_committed, article_restored_from_version. Identifiable + acceptable per audit immutability discipline.
- NO Storage buckets created Session 92. `newsletter-article-images` bucket NOT YET CREATED — owed in G1.5.
- NO Edge Functions deployed Session 92.
- NO cron jobs registered Session 92. The 3 newsletter cron jobs (`prune_newsletter_subscribe_attempts` at 6:15, `expire_pending_newsletter_confirmations` at 6:25, `prune_newsletter_draft_versions` at 6:35) NOT YET REGISTERED — owed in G1.3e and G2.
- `content_asset_refs` extension to 7-way parent NOT YET APPLIED — owed in G1.4.

**MIGRATIONS NOT YET APPLIED — REMAINING G1 WORK FOR NEXT SESSION (in order):**

(A) **`session92_newsletter_articles_state_transition_rpcs`** (G1.3c) — Create 5 RPCs: `schedule_article(p_article_id, p_publish_at, p_reason)`, `cancel_scheduled_article(p_article_id, p_reason)`, `publish_article(p_article_id, p_reason)`, `unpublish_article(p_article_id, p_reason)`, `archive_article(p_article_id, p_reason)`. All SECURITY DEFINER, search_path=public,extensions,pg_temp, gate via assert_super_admin + assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change') + reason ≥10. publish_article must trigger background dispatch via pg_net.http_post to `dispatch-newsletter-article` Edge Function (lands G8) for gated articles — for v1 just log the intent and return; the actual Edge Function ships in G8. publish/unpublish/archive must invalidate SSR cache (lands G3). For G1.3c, just write the state transitions + audit rows; cache invalidation and dispatch trigger are stubs returning success. **Per item 15 discipline: capture before_value with full article state including authors on EVERY audit emission. Apply this discipline to commit_article_version and restore_article_version (see FOLLOW-UP below) in the same migration to fix the gap from G1.3b.**

(B) **`session92_newsletter_articles_read_rpcs`** (G1.3d) — Create 5 read RPCs:
   - `get_article_for_reader(p_slug text) RETURNS jsonb` — anon-callable SECDEF. Drops `p_viewer_user_id` param per Lovable v2 N3. Viewer identity always via `auth.uid()`. Resolves subscriber status via JOIN to users.email (NOTE: this requires the user to have signed up for newsletter using the same email as their account — confirm UX flow in G2; alternative is matching on user_id directly if subscribers table gets a `linked_user_id` column). Returns `{access:'granted', article, authors}` or `{access:'paywall', article:{id,slug,title,excerpt,cover_asset_id,published_at,gate,allowed_plan_tiers?}, authors, paywall_reason:'subscriber_required'|'plan_tier_required'}`. The 3-state gate evaluation: public always granted; subscriber_aggregate granted if confirmed_subscriber OR `current_user_active_plan_tier() IS NOT NULL`; plan_tier granted if `current_user_active_plan_tier() = ANY(allowed_plan_tiers)`. Anon and authenticated grants.
   - `preview_article_as_viewer_class(p_article_id uuid, p_viewer_class text) RETURNS jsonb` — super_admin only, simulates `get_article_for_reader` output for given viewer class (`'anon'`, `'subscriber'`, `'base'`, `'premium'`, `'individual'`). Reimplements gate logic against simulated viewer state — does NOT modify DB.
   - `list_articles_for_archive(p_gate_filter text DEFAULT NULL, p_category_id uuid DEFAULT NULL, p_limit integer DEFAULT 12, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0) RETURNS jsonb` — anon-callable. Returns published articles respecting gate (anon sees public only with full body; auth-no-sub sees public + subscriber_aggregate previews; auth-subscriber sees public + subscriber_aggregate full + plan_tier previews; plan-tier holders see all). Wrapper shape `{items, total, limit, offset}` per §111 type discipline.
   - `list_article_versions(p_article_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb` — super_admin only. Returns `{items: [{version_id, version_number, version_type, version_name, restored_from_version_id, created_by_user_id, created_at, body_preview_first_200_chars}]}`.
   - `get_article_version(p_version_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb` — super_admin only. Returns full version row including body_tiptap and metadata_snapshot.

(C) **`session92_newsletter_draft_pruning_function_and_cron`** (G1.3e) — Create `prune_newsletter_draft_versions()` function per Lovable v2 item 3 retention: per article, identify draft versions older than 7 days, rank by created_at DESC, keep top 20, delete the rest. Register pg_cron job `prune_newsletter_draft_versions` at `35 6 * * *` (non-colliding slot per Session 92 discovery). Test by inserting fixture draft rows >7 days old, running function manually, confirming top-20 retention. **Cron schedule can ship disabled initially — Cole monitors draft accumulation for 2 weeks before flipping cron on.** Document the disable/enable toggle in the migration.

(D) **`session92_content_asset_refs_extend_newsletter_article`** (G1.4) — Extend `content_asset_refs` to 7-way parent by ADD COLUMN `newsletter_article_id uuid REFERENCES newsletter_articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`. DROP CONSTRAINT `content_asset_refs_exactly_one_parent` (current 6-way), ADD CONSTRAINT with 7-way CASE-WHEN. CREATE INDEX `content_asset_refs_newsletter_article_idx` (partial WHERE newsletter_article_id IS NOT NULL). Extend `_walk_block_config_for_asset_refs` walker function in same migration with newsletter_article parent mode following §72 surgical-edit pattern (diff against live function definition, no full rewrite).

(E) **`session92_newsletter_article_images_bucket`** (G1.5) — Create Storage bucket `newsletter-article-images` (public=true, 10MB ceiling, mime allowlist `image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, image/gif`). §82-clean policies: `newsletter_article_images_public_select` TO public bucket-scoped, `newsletter_article_images_service_role_all` TO service_role. Used by `convert-html-to-tiptap` Edge Function (lands G2/G4 for image re-hosting at paste-time) and by native TipTap editor image upload toolbar (G4).

**FOLLOW-UP FIXES OWED IN G1.3c (not blocking next session start):**
- `commit_article_version` should populate `before_value` with full article state including authors before the snapshot completes. Currently writes `before_value = NULL`. Inconsistent with Lovable v2 item 15. Fix in G1.3c migration by adding a "capture pre-snapshot state" block at the top of the function using the same pattern as `upsert_article`.
- `restore_article_version` same gap. Currently writes `before_value = NULL`. Fix in G1.3c by capturing pre-restore state (current article body + authors) before the UPDATE.
- `newsletter_articles_slug_lookup_idx` is REDUNDANT with `newsletter_articles_slug_unique` (the UNIQUE constraint creates a unique btree index that handles slug lookups). Drop the redundant index in G1.3c or G1.3e migration. Both are non-blocking; production usage will pick the unique index automatically.

**ARCHITECTURE NOTES FROM SESSION 92 (consider for next architecture-reference version §-numbered rule):**
- Pattern candidate (§129): SECDEF functions calling pgcrypto functions (digest, gen_random_bytes) must `SET search_path = public, extensions, pg_temp` because pgcrypto lives in `extensions` schema in Supabase. Skipping `extensions` works only if extensions schema is in the default search_path before SECDEF locks it — risky pattern (`peer_access_request_create` precedent does this and works today but is fragile to future search_path policy changes). Explicit inclusion is the safe pattern.
- Pattern candidate (§130): Smoke-test audit rows are unavoidable when testing super_admin RPCs because `super_admin_audit_log` is immutable (`block_audit_log_mutations` trigger). Convention: all smoke-test RPC calls prefix `p_reason` with `'G<phase>.<step> smoke test - <detail>'` so test rows are self-identifying. Five such rows added Session 92, all correctly labeled.
- Pattern candidate (§131): pg_cron job slot collision discovery via `SELECT jobname, schedule FROM cron.job` BEFORE planning new job schedules. Existing `process_due_scheduled_assignments_daily` at `20 6 * * *` would have collided with Session 92 originally-planned newsletter slots; rescheduled to 6:15/6:25/6:35. Discipline: query cron.job first when adding cron-driven features.

**Carryover NOT touched Session 92 (still owed):**
- og-image canonical PNG upload (Session 91 fast-follow, unchanged)
- AIRSA facet-interpretation gap, messaging subsystem, module reorder gap, MFA trusted-device feature, editor thumbnail-loss-on-republish hardening, coach-paid invitation email verification, `create-checkout` graceful-degradation hardening, AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine, Session 71 anon EXECUTE audit, post-launch `coach_clients_client_view` SECDEF refactor, `results_available` NAI/AIRSA/HSS coverage, `users.last_active_at` infrastructure, `user_ui_preferences` dedicated table migration, bulk Phase 11 v2, /super-admin/coaches consolidation, Members Surface v2 polish, button labeling pattern, legacy 2-arg `search_impersonation_targets` cleanup, Mentor Portal v2 MQ-1 through MQ-4.

*v97 - Session 89 CLOSE — Phase 10 Round 6 (Notification surfaces polish) + Round 8 expanded scope (Mentor Portal Cycles B/A/C/D) FULLY SHIPPED. **Phase 10 Rounds 6 + 8 are DONE.** What started as "single small Lovable cycle for `/mentor` polish" expanded into 4 frontend cycles after Cole's bug report on the WrittenSummary review surface surfaced two deeper opportunities (iteration history + per-mentor feedback templates), plus one strategic addition (cumulative progress + mentor notes for a richer trainee detail surface). Session 89 ran one backend session (3 migrations) before any frontend cycle began — backend-first discipline held the floor across all four cycles. One new standing rule locked Session 89: §128 (Radix Collapsible state-driven child visibility requires `className="group"` on Root + `group-data-[state=X]:hidden` on children — bare `data-[state=X]` reads from self, not parent, and silently fails). (1) **Round 6 (Notification surfaces) SHIPPED in a single Lovable cycle.** 6 Y-findings closed across NotificationBell, NotificationDropdown, NotificationsList, NotificationSettings. Recon was light because the subsystem shipped clean in Session 84/86 with proper types + a11y from day one — confirmed the resources-subsystem-as-positive-example pattern from Session 86 Round 5 carries to other recently-built surfaces. (2) **Backend session for Round 8 expanded scope.** Three migrations applied and smoke-tested before any Lovable cycle began: `session89_get_content_item_for_viewer_written_submissions_plural` (singular `written_submission` field migrated to plural `written_submissions` array via surgical splice on `pg_get_functiondef` output, ASC by iteration_number — load-bearing for the iteration history rendering in cycle B); `session89_feedback_templates_table_and_rpcs` (table + 5 RLS policies + 3 RPCs `list_feedback_templates` / `upsert_feedback_template` / `delete_feedback_template`, per-mentor private templates scoped by `panel_type` enum); `session89_mentor_trainee_notes_table_and_rpcs` (table + 5 RLS policies + 2 RPCs `list_mentor_trainee_notes` / `upsert_mentor_trainee_note`, notes attached to `coach_mentor_assignments.id` via FK ON DELETE CASCADE). All RLS uses inline `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND account_type = 'brainwise_super_admin')` per the codebase's established pattern; Lovable's initial plan-doc had proposed `has_role()` which doesn't exist in the codebase — caught and corrected during the backend session before Lovable cycles started. Architecture decision Option W locked: Cole (super-admin) self-assigns as mentor via the existing `assign_mentor` flow when he needs to use the notes panel for a trainee; no special "super-admin proxy-write path" needed. Notes RPCs work uniformly when Cole has a real `coach_mentor_assignments` row. Notes deletion uses direct table `.delete()` from frontend per RLS-handles-auth pattern; no dedicated delete RPC. (3) **Cycle B (WrittenSummary version history + ContentItemArtifactPanel bug fix) SHIPPED.** 2 files, +93 lines. WrittenSummaryReviewPanel.tsx grew from 187 → 268 lines (Cycle B) → 280 lines (after Cycle A's a11y additions). Added: `WrittenSubmission` type, local `formatDateTime` helper, Collapsible primitive import, iteration history rendering ASC by iteration_number with `isLatest` flag, mentor actions gated by `latest && latest.review_decision === null`. ContentItemArtifactPanel.tsx fix: latent bug where `submission.length` returned `"undefined"` and `{submission ?? "(empty)"}` rendered `[object Object]` — Lovable's Pass 2 review caught the `data-[state=X]` vs `group-data-[state=X]` Radix mistake before ship (would have silently broken the collapse-on-preview pattern). The Radix Collapsible group pattern became §128. Cole visually verified the iteration history rendering on `/super-admin/members` MemberDrawer Learning tab after RPC return shape confirmed (1 production submission, iteration=1, char_count=103). (4) **Cycle A (mentor portal a11y + palette + Retry polish sweep) SHIPPED.** 5 files, +87 lines. 14 Loader2 spinners + 1 Upload icon got correct Pattern 1a/1b a11y treatment (1a = standalone loading block → container gets `role="status"` + `aria-label="Loading"` when no text OR text label when text-bearing, Loader2 gets `aria-hidden="true"`; 1b = inline spinner inside action button → Loader2 gets `aria-hidden="true"` only, button label conveys state). Bonus: CircleCheck on SkillsPracticeReviewPanel line 318 also got `aria-hidden="true"` as part of Pattern 2 palette migration. 7 raw Tailwind palette literals migrated to brand-token inline styles (`color-mix(in oklab, var(--bw-X) N%, white)` + token color). MentorPortal.tsx gained module-level `pendingActionPillStyle` constant. MentorTraineeDetail.tsx `statusBadgeClass` helper split into `statusBadgeClass` (returns `"border-transparent"` only) + new `statusBadgeStyle(status)` 3-branch helper. SkillsPracticeReviewPanel.tsx CircleCheck green migrated to `var(--bw-forest)`. Visible state changes per §120 actor-identity palette: `certified` badges now render forest green (was purple); `revoked` badges now render destructive-tinted (was orange). 6 Retry buttons added to error branches (rosterQuery for MentorPortal page-level error, stateQuery for per-row expand and MentorTraineeDetail, detailQuery for the 3 review panels). Zero new imports across all 5 files. (5) **Cycle C (per-mentor feedback templates) SHIPPED.** 9 files (5 new + 4 modified). New: `src/types/feedback-templates.ts` (16 lines, 3 interfaces with §111 wrapper-unwrap discipline), `src/hooks/useInsertAtCursor.ts` (41 lines, caller-owns-ref contract with rAF cursor restoration — establishes the codebase's first insert-at-cursor pattern), `src/components/mentor/FeedbackTemplatePicker.tsx` (152 lines, Popover + Command with cmdk fuzzy search on `${name} ${text}`, "Templates ▾" trigger + optional "Save as template…" sibling button, "Manage templates →" link to `/mentor/feedback-templates`), `src/components/mentor/SaveAsTemplateDialog.tsx` (144 lines, shadcn Dialog, dual-mode create/edit, error-code → friendly toast mapping for `name_too_long` / `text_too_long` / `name_required` / `text_required`, invalidates `["feedback_templates"]` query key on success, char counters on both fields 200/5000), `src/pages/mentor/FeedbackTemplates.tsx` (214 lines, mirrors NotificationSettings layout `max-w-2xl mx-auto p-4 md:p-6 space-y-4`, two TemplateSection siblings one per panel_type, edit/delete icon buttons with aria-label, shadcn AlertDialog for delete confirmation). Modified: WrittenSummaryReviewPanel (picker above both Textareas, 2 refs + 2 insertion hooks + saveDialogTarget state, single SaveAsTemplateDialog at end keyed off saveDialogTarget), SkillsPracticeReviewPanel (picker only above revisionComment Textarea, 1 ref + 1 hook + saveDialogOpen state), App.tsx (new route `/mentor/feedback-templates` MentorGuard-wrapped between `/mentor/trainee/:traineeId` and `{/* Admin */}` comment), AppSidebar.tsx (mentorItem single splice → mentorItems array spread, second nav entry "Feedback Templates" with MessageSquare icon — zero new lucide imports). Critical implementation pattern: Lovable rendered picker+textarea as siblings within the EXISTING `<div className="space-y-2">` parent containers rather than wrapping in a `<>...</>` Fragment as I had proposed in the prompt — same DOM structure, no extra nodes, natural gap from existing space-y. Better than spec. Cole verified full create/use/edit/delete flow + cross-panel isolation + focus restoration after insert. New: `src/types/mentor-trainee-notes.ts` (2 interfaces), `src/components/mentor/MentorTraineeCumulativeProgress.tsx` (183 lines, pure derivation from `stateQuery.data` walking `assignments[].modules[].items[].completion` + `module_assignments[]`, 4 stat tiles in 2x2/md:1x4 grid with Items completed showing completion %, Curricula, Certifications, Last activity; "Pending mentor actions" card with conditional list items in module-level `pendingPillStyle` amber/mustard pills per §120; local `StatTile` helper not extracted; zero new queries beyond what MentorTraineeDetail already fetches), `src/components/mentor/MentorTraineeNotesPanel.tsx` (347 lines, self-contained, `resolveAssignmentId(mentorRelationships, callerUserId)` helper filters by `mentor_user_id === user.id` and prefers active `ended_at === null` falling back to most-recently-ended assignment via assigned_at descending sort, three early-exit branches in order: no-assignment empty state with CTA to `/super-admin/members?userId={traineeId}` for the assign-mentor flow / loading state / error state with retry button, composer Textarea + char count + Save button with optional italic "Your mentorship with this trainee has ended" hint when isActive false, notes list with inline edit pattern — pencil icon flips note into Textarea + Save/Cancel buttons no modal, delete via direct `supabase.from("mentor_trainee_notes").delete().eq("id", ...)` per RLS-handles-auth pattern, shadcn AlertDialog confirmation for delete, `useAuth()` from `@/hooks/useAuth` for `user.id` per useUserProfile.tsx canonical pattern). Modified: MentorTraineeDetail.tsx (added Tabs/TabsContent/TabsList/TabsTrigger imports + CumulativeProgress + NotesPanel imports, activeTab useState default "progress", mentorRelationships array extraction alongside existing certifications extraction, identity Card structurally simplified to CardHeader only, page-level loading/error states moved between identity Card and Tabs section, three TabsContent panels each wrapping content in `<Card><CardContent className="pt-6">`, Cycle A `statusBadgeStyle` + `statusBadgeClass` helpers preserved verbatim, Cycle A retry button + Loader2 a11y preserved at page-level location). Zero deviations from spec on Cycle D — cleanest of the four cycles. No URL persistence for active tab in v1 (useState only with default "progress"); URL-bookmarkable tab state deferred to a future polish cycle. (7) **One new standing rule locked Session 89:** **§128 — Radix Collapsible state-driven child visibility requires `className="group"` on Root + `group-data-[state=X]:hidden` on children.** Discovered during Cycle B Lovable Pass 2 review. The bare `data-[state=X]:hidden` Tailwind variant reads the state from the element it's applied to, NOT from a Radix parent — so a child of Collapsible.Root marked `data-[state=open]:hidden` would silently fail to hide when the parent opens (the child has no state attribute of its own). The correct pattern mirrors the navigation-menu.tsx codebase precedent: `<Collapsible className="group">` propagates a `group` marker that lets descendants use `group-data-[state=X]:hidden` to read the Radix state from the marked ancestor. Without the marker, child visibility silently does nothing. Concrete: Cycle B's iteration history rendering uses `<Collapsible defaultOpen={isLatest} className="group">` with `<div className="text-sm ... group-data-[state=open]:hidden">` for the preview-in-Root pattern (preview visible when Collapsible is CLOSED, hidden when OPEN; full content rendered inside CollapsibleContent only when OPEN). Caught in plan-mode Pass 2 review before ship. (8) **DEFERRED items added Session 89:** **Mentor Portal v2 build queue (MQ-1 through MQ-4)**: keyboard-driven review queue (j/k navigation between submitted-for-review items), rubric-based scoring on SkillsPracticeReviewPanel (numeric 1-5 per criterion with weighted average displayed to trainee), timestamped media annotations (drop comments at specific timestamps in submitted video/audio), AI-assisted feedback suggestions via Anthropic API (mentor types initial reaction, AI proposes 2-3 elaborated feedback drafts mentor can pick or refine). All non-blocking, all "phase 2+ when usage data informs prioritization." **Cycle C cosmetic follow-ups (3 items rolled into a single Session 90 mini-cycle)**: MessageSquare → FileText (or Library) icon swap in AppSidebar for "Feedback Templates" entry to eliminate AI Chat icon collision; FeedbackTemplates.tsx h1 font-semibold → font-bold for NotificationSettings consistency; FeedbackTemplatePicker loading state regain `role="status"` + `aria-label="Loading templates"` attributes (single-line a11y fix). (9) **Carried forward unchanged to Session 90.** Phase 10 polish queue remaining: Round 7 (Coach surfaces — CoachClients/ClientResults/CoachInvoices; CoachInvoices touched Round 1 palette work, CoachClients touched Session 85 Bug 1, ClientResults touched Session 85 Bugs 4+5; all three have had narrow patches but not full polish passes). Then formal Group C closure. All other Session 88 deferred items unchanged (AIRSA facet-interpretation gap, messaging subsystem, module reorder gap, MFA trusted-device, editor thumbnail hardening, create-checkout graceful-degradation, coach-paid invitation email verification, AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema, Clarity Engine, Session 71 anon EXECUTE audit, post-launch coach_clients_client_view SECDEF refactor, results_available NAI/AIRSA/HSS coverage, Members Surface v2 polish 5 items, button labeling pattern inconsistency, legacy 2-arg search_impersonation_targets cleanup, users.last_active_at infrastructure, user_ui_preferences dedicated table migration, bulk role change / MFA reset / password reset / send message Phase 11 v2, /super-admin/coaches consolidation evaluation). (10) **Session 89 close artifacts**: this v97 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v93 (§128 Radix Collapsible group pattern + Session 89 ship narrative), session-89-to-90.md handoff. Markdown only. Cole uploads all three manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root); GitHub MCP write access verified STILL 403 at session close via test write on `/.write-test-session-89.txt` (`403 Resource not accessible by integration`). **Phase 10 Rounds 6 + 8 are fully done. Session 90 opens on Phase 10 Round 7 (Coach surfaces polish: CoachClients + ClientResults + CoachInvoices), then formal Group C closure.***



*v96 - Session 88 CLOSE — Phase 11 Members surface frontend FULLY SHIPPED end-to-end. **Phase 11 is DONE.** 12 Lovable prompts shipped across 8 cycles (1, 1.5 backend patch, 2a, 2b parts 1+2, 11.D, 11.D.2, 11.E, 11.E.2), 5 backend migrations applied + verified, full a11y + mobile audit pass via Chrome MCP + code-driven analysis. Surface is production-ready. Members at `/super-admin/members` replaces the deleted legacy Users + Learning Admin pages. Three new standing rules locked: §125 (DROP-then-CREATE discipline for additive PL/pgSQL params), §126 (Lovable can ship adjacent backend fixes when load-bearing), §127 (caller-owns-mutation + description slot for multi-step modals reusing single-step primitives). One process note documented (Chrome MCP cannot do CSS viewport emulation — code-driven analysis is the correct fallback for responsive testing). Session 89 opens on Phase 10 polish queue continuation: Round 6 (Notification surfaces), Round 7 (Coach surfaces), Round 8 (Mentor portal), then formal Group C closure. Round 4 (learning-admin tooling) no longer applies — Phase 11 deleted LearningAdmin entirely. (1) **Cycle 1 (skeleton) SHIPPED.** 13 new files + 2 modified shipped via Lovable. New: Members.tsx page, MembersTable.tsx, MembersFilterBar.tsx, MembersBulkActionsBar.tsx, MemberDrawer.tsx + 4 tab components (Learning/Coach/Assignments/Audit), ColumnVisibilityMenu.tsx, SavedViewsDropdown.tsx, types.ts, highlightMatch.ts, plus the routes. Modified: App.tsx (new routes), AppSidebar.tsx (Members entry added, legacy entries kept but renamed "Legacy: Users" / "Legacy: Learning Admin" with deprecation banners). (2) **Cycle 1.5 (backend patch, surfaced mid-cycle 1).** Members.tsx full-page route had a fallback `directMember` query that fetched `p_limit:1` with no narrowing and used client-side `arr.find(routeUserId)` — returned null for any user other than the alphabetical first. Fix path required a 15th param on `search_impersonation_targets` for by-id narrowing. Two migrations: `phase11c_cycle1_5_add_specific_user_id_param` (adds `p_specific_user_id uuid DEFAULT NULL` as the 15th param, creates new overload) and `phase11c_cycle1_5_drop_legacy_14arg_search_impersonation_targets` (drops the obsoleted 14-arg overload via DROP RESTRICT to eliminate ambiguous resolution that surfaced after the additive migration). 14-named-arg callers and 15-named-arg callers both resolve cleanly to the canonical 15-arg via default-fill. Legacy 2-arg `(p_query, p_limit) -> 6-col` overload untouched — non-overlapping named-args set, no collision; v2 cleanup deferred to post-launch when the 4 legacy callsites get cleared. **Lovable folded the cycle 1.5 fix into the cycle 2a prompt as a `directMember` query rewrite** rather than shipping it as a standalone hotfix — acceptable batching since the bug was non-blocking (table view worked; only the deep-linked URL path was broken). (3) **Cycle 2a (JustifiedActionDialog + 4 single-row actions) SHIPPED.** 1 new file (JustifiedActionDialog.tsx at `src/components/justified-action/`) + 4 modified shipped. The shared dialog uses the **caller-owns-mutation pattern** locked in §121 (Session 87): dialog provides visual + UX + justification field consistency + 10-char min validation + submitting state + error display + no-op handling (`changed: false` branch) + success toast; caller passes the mutation function as `onSubmit: (reason) => Promise<{changed, note?}>` and handles RPC selection + payload + post-action React Query invalidation. Four single-row actions wired through it: mentor toggle in drawer header (fixes the silent no-op bug from the deleted MentorRoleTab as side effect — `set_mentor_role` returns `{changed, before, after}` and the dialog now shows "No change" toast on `changed: false`), cert grant/revoke in Coach tab, completion mark in Learning tab, grant attempts in Coach tab. (4) **Cycle 2b parts 1+2 (bulk modals + chunking + Schedule + Import) SHIPPED.** 10 new files in `src/components/members/bulk/` (types.ts, BulkProgress.tsx, useBulkChunkRunner.ts, BulkAssignModal.tsx, BulkAssignMentorModal.tsx, BulkUnassignModal.tsx, BulkUnassignMentorModal.tsx, BulkOverrideCompletionModal.tsx, ScheduleAssignmentModal.tsx, BulkImportModal.tsx) + 3 modified files. Frontend chunking + progress UI for >50-user selections via `useBulkChunkRunner` hook with useRef-based cancel flag pattern (cancel sets the ref immediately; loop checks `cancelRef.current` between chunks and aborts cleanly without React state-update timing races). Each chunk's RPC commits independently — cancel does NOT roll back already-committed batches, post-cancel UI says "Cancelled. {N} of {total} processed." per §123 candidate from Session 87. BulkAssignMentorModal uses the per-trainee certification resolution flow via `get_mentorable_certifications` + `assign_mentor_pairs_bulk` (one mentor + array of `{trainee_user_id, certification_id}` pairs). ScheduleAssignmentModal: two-tab layout (Create new + Scheduled). BulkImportModal: download template → fill → upload → result flow via the existing `learning-admin-import` Edge Function. **Lovable correctly caught a spec error during cycle 2b**: Claude's spec said `is_archived: false` but the actual schema is `archived_at IS NULL`; Lovable used the correct schema without flagging it as a blocker, just shipped it right. Folded into §-numbered audit discipline. (5) **11.D (legacy file delete + brand tokens) SHIPPED.** 6 files deleted: LearningAdmin.tsx (2050 lines), Users.tsx, UserDetailsModal.tsx, CompletionControlTab.tsx, TraineeMultiSelect.tsx, learnerSearchShared.ts. Two routes `/super-admin/users` and `/super-admin/learning-admin` converted to bare `<Navigate to="/super-admin/members" replace />` — no RoleGuard wrap (improvement Lovable proposed; the destination route has its own guard, double-wrapping was unnecessary). AppSidebar legacy entries removed. `UserSearch` import removed but `GraduationCap` icon kept (still used by Mentor Portal). Brand tokens migrated: 4 files now use `color-mix(in oklab, var(--bw-forest) 12%, white)` etc per `index.css` `--bw-*` vars instead of raw Tailwind amber/emerald/teal classes. (6) **11.D.2 (Assignments tab wiring) SHIPPED.** MemberDrawerAssignments.tsx grew from a 43-line stub (cycle 1) to a 600-line component with 4 inline `*Launcher` sub-components (CertPathLauncher, CurriculumLauncher, ModuleLauncher, MentorLauncher). MemberDrawer.tsx got a one-line edit to pass `userId` + `fullName` + `setHasUnsavedChanges` to the tab. Launchers reuse the cycle 2b bulk RPCs with `p_user_ids: [oneUser]` — same backend surface, single-user payload. Mentor launcher uses 4-state render: loading / 0-certs-blocked / 1-readonly / 2+-dropdown (the trainee's cert count determines which UI shape; assign_mentor needs a specific cert axis, so 0 certs means the launcher refuses and 1 cert auto-resolves without picker). **Lovable correctly used `<span className="block">` wrappers inside DialogDescription** to avoid the invalid HTML nesting that would have surfaced as a React hydration warning — DialogDescription renders `<p>` and `<div>` cannot nest inside `<p>`. Pattern documented as §127. (7) **11.E (backend overload drop + dead-file cleanup) SHIPPED.** **Backend**: migration `phase11e_drop_legacy_search_impersonation_targets_2arg` DROP FUNCTION'd the legacy 2-arg overload from cycle 1.5 carryover. Smoke-tested clean: 14-named-arg and 15-named-arg call shapes both resolve correctly to canonical 15-arg via default-fill. **Frontend**: 1 new file `src/components/learning-admin/learning-tree-types.ts` (verbatim `MarkTier` + `MarkTarget` types extracted from the deleted CompletionConfirmDialog so AdminLearningTree could still import them after the dialog file was deleted), 2 modified (AdminLearningTree.tsx line 9 + MemberDrawerLearning.tsx line 5 — import path swaps from `./CompletionConfirmDialog` to `./learning-tree-types`), 2 deleted (CompletionConfirmDialog.tsx — replaced by JustifiedActionDialog wiring + ResultPanel.tsx — Members surface owns its own result rendering pattern now). Final `learning-admin/` folder contents: AdminLearningTree.tsx (still in use by MemberDrawerLearning as shared primitive), ContentItemArtifactPanel.tsx (used by AdminLearningTree for inline expansion of submission artifacts), learning-tree-types.ts (new types-only module). (8) **11.E.2 (a11y + mobile audit pass) SHIPPED.** Live audit run via Chrome MCP on Lovable preview deployment, authenticated as super admin. Visual inspection + JavaScript-based contrast measurement via canvas-pixel-extraction (`ctx.fillStyle = oklab(...)` then `getImageData` to read computed RGB values that match-on-paper to numeric strings the CSS engine reports) confirmed all status pills pass WCAG AA: Active 5.32:1, Certified 5.32:1, In Progress 5.93:1, Mentor 4.79:1 (marginal pass). My initial visual concern about green+teal pairing in the drawer header turned out to be a visual eye-trick — the rendered RGB confirms both Active and Certified are forest green (`#2D6A4F`), not teal. Cycle 11.D brand-token migration is contrast-compliant. **12 audit findings documented at `/home/claude/round-11e-audit/findings.md`** — 7 triaged into 11.E.2 scope, 5 deferred to a v2 polish ticket. The 7 fixes shipped: pagination chevron aria-labels (P0), MembersTable overflow-x wrapper (P0), mobile column-hiding ladder via responsive Tailwind classes (P0 — `hidden sm:table-cell` etc layered on top of `showCol()` user-toggle), drawer Active badge aria-label (P1), Mentor switch aria-label (P1), row actions aria-label including user name (P1), bulk modal DialogContent `w-[calc(100vw-2rem)] sm:w-full` for narrow-viewport sizing (P1, scoped to ScheduleAssignmentModal + BulkImportModal only). Lovable diagnostic verified all 7 findings clean against current main + confirmed the column-priority ladder is reasonable. Lovable build verified post-ship: 14 responsive class additions (7 heads + 7 cells), 18 `showCol()` calls preserved (9 heads + 9 cells layered correctly with user-toggle), only the two intended modals got the viewport-shrink class. **Chrome MCP audit lesson logged**: `resize_window` resizes the host browser window but does NOT change the CSS viewport (`window.innerWidth` stays at 2560 regardless). Mobile testing requires code-driven analysis of responsive Tailwind class usage in addition to in-browser inspection — process note, not §-numbered. (9) **5 backend migrations shipped this session, all live on `svprhtzawnbzmumxnhsq`.** `phase11c_cycle1_5_add_specific_user_id_param`, `phase11c_cycle1_5_drop_legacy_14arg_search_impersonation_targets`, `phase11e_drop_legacy_search_impersonation_targets_2arg`. (Two additional migrations were applied as part of the Session 87 backend phase: M1-M5 from v95 are referenced here as already-shipped baseline.) (10) **NEW standing rules locked Session 88.** **§125 — Adding optional params to existing PL/pgSQL functions requires DROP-then-CREATE, not CREATE-OR-REPLACE.** `CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION` only replaces functions with identical argument signatures. Adding a new optional param creates a NEW function and leaves the old in place. PostgreSQL then has two overloads with overlapping named-args sets, and PostgREST's overload resolution becomes ambiguous when a call comes in with the original arg count (planner cannot choose between exact match on old vs default-fill on new). Correct sequence: (a) CREATE OR REPLACE with new signature including optional param DEFAULT NULL — creates the new function alongside the old; (b) test new function in isolation; (c) DROP FUNCTION old signature RESTRICT — RESTRICT fails loud if anything depends on the old signature; CASCADE would silently chain into views or triggers, never use it for this pattern; (d) re-test: existing N-named-arg callers resolve to (N+1)-arg via default fill. Skipping step (c) leaves intermittent `42725 is not unique` errors at runtime. Pairs with §96 (CHECK constraint audit on RPC validation changes) — both reinforce that PostgreSQL function evolution touches multiple layers atomically. **§126 — Lovable can ship adjacent backend changes when load-bearing.** During cycle 1, Lovable proactively extended `search_impersonation_targets` from the existing 3-arg form to a 14-arg form to fix the directMember query bug — outside the prompt's stated frontend-only scope but minimum-coupled correct fix for the bug. Accepted. Pattern locked: future cycles include explicit "DO NOT modify any RPC" guardrail in frontend-only Lovable prompts where backend ownership must stay with Claude (e.g., when Lovable lacks Supabase MCP write access in the specific session, or when backward-compat verification is critical). When Lovable is permitted to ship adjacent backend, the prompt explicitly authorizes it and lists the exact RPC names that may be touched. Don't trust prompt-scope discipline alone to prevent adjacent edits — call out what's allowed and what isn't. **§127 — Caller-owns-mutation + description slot for multi-step modals reusing single-step primitives.** When a complex action surface (multi-target picker + extra fields + amber inline gate hint) needs to reuse an existing single-step primitive (JustifiedActionDialog), pass the multi-step content through the dialog's `description: React.ReactNode` slot rather than building a parallel multi-step dialog. The dialog primitive renders description content inside a `<p>` (shadcn DialogDescription wraps in `<p>`), so the multi-step content must use `<span className="block">` wrappers — NOT `<div>` — to avoid invalid HTML nesting and the React hydration warning that follows. Throw-then-mapError pattern for non-reason validation: the multi-step content sets state outside the dialog, and the dialog's `onSubmit` callback (which gets the reason string) reads that state and throws a typed error if any pre-condition isn't met; `mapError` translates the thrown error to user-facing copy. Concrete example: 11.D.2's MemberDrawerAssignments uses 4 launcher components, each wrapping JustifiedActionDialog with its own target picker + due date + amber inline gate hint rendered inside the description slot. (11) **DEFERRED Build Queue items added Session 88 (v2 polish for Members surface):** (a) **Members Surface v2 Polish — 5 deferred audit findings.** Mobile column-hiding refinement based on real analytics (current ladder is best-guess; revisit after collecting mobile super-admin usage data). Row hover-reveal opacity pattern keyboard discoverability (current: `opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100`; keyboard users can't trigger hover so the action button is invisible until they Tab to it). Mentor body text contrast bump from 4.79:1 marginal to 7:1+. Filter chip horizontal scroll on narrow viewports (currently wraps vertical, pushes table down). Page header "Bulk Import" + "Schedule" button stacking polish at narrow widths. (b) **Inconsistent button labeling pattern** (Toggle Sidebar, Log Out, drawer Close use label-on-inner-span instead of `aria-label` on button itself). Works functionally but inconsistent with rest of codebase. (c) **Legacy 2-arg `search_impersonation_targets(p_query, p_limit) -> 6-col` overload still live** — cycle 1.5 dropped only the 14-arg; the 2-arg remains because it has a non-overlapping named-args set with the canonical 15-arg. The 4 legacy callsites (Users.tsx + LearningAdmin.tsx 3 sites) were deleted in 11.D so the overload is dead code in the production app surface but still callable from psql. Cleanup deferred to a post-launch session (low priority, no security implication). (12) **Carried forward unchanged to Session 89.** Phase 10 polish queue remaining: Round 6 (Notification surfaces — bell/dropdown/`/notifications`/`/settings/notifications`; just shipped Session 84 so should audit clean), Round 7 (Coach surfaces — CoachClients/ClientResults/CoachInvoices; partially touched in Round 1 palette work), Round 8 (Mentor portal — `/mentor` routes; lowest blast radius, smallest user base). **Round 4 (learning-admin tooling) NO LONGER APPLIES** — Phase 11 deleted LearningAdmin entirely; Round 4 audit findings at `/home/claude/round4-recon/` are obsolete. Formal Group C closure after all polish rounds ship. Also still carried: AIRSA facet-interpretation generation gap (Session 84 deferred); messaging subsystem (prerequisite for `coach_messages` notification type); module reorder gap (Session 85 discovery — scope approved); MFA trusted-device feature (Session 84 logged); editor thumbnail-loss-on-republish hardening (Session 84 logged); coach-paid invitation email verification (Session 82 carryover); `create-checkout` graceful-degradation hardening (~60-day comp coupon recurrence); AIRSA Phases 3e-8; SOC 2 written policies; Action-Oriented Voice Redesign across six surfaces; pricing-reads refactor; corporate contract renewal schema change; Clarity Engine; Session 71 anon EXECUTE audit on 95 SECDEF functions; post-launch `coach_clients_client_view` → SECDEF RPC refactor; `results_available` NAI/AIRSA/HSS coverage (Session 86 carryover); `users.last_active_at` infrastructure (Session 87 v2); `user_ui_preferences` dedicated table migration (Session 87 v2); bulk role change / MFA reset / password reset / send message (Session 87 v2 deferred from Phase 11). (13) **Session 88 close artifacts**: this v96 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v92 (§125 DROP-then-CREATE for additive PL/pgSQL params, §126 Lovable adjacent backend authorization discipline, §127 caller-owns-mutation + description slot for multi-step modals; plus Chrome MCP CSS viewport limitation process note and Phase 11 ship narrative), session-88-to-89.md handoff. Markdown only — Session 74 decision. Cole uploads all three manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root); GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY. **Phase 11 is fully done. Session 89 opens on Phase 10 polish queue continuation: Round 6 (Notification surfaces) first as the lowest-risk audit since the notification subsystem just shipped Session 84/86, then Round 7 (Coach surfaces), then Round 8 (Mentor portal), then formal Group C closure.***


*v95 - Session 87 CLOSE — Phase 11 Members surface consolidation SHIPPED design phase + backend phase end-to-end; Round 4 polish-only approach ABANDONED mid-session in favor of full consolidation; 14 design decisions locked across two Lovable review passes; 5 backend migrations applied + verified with 4 verification tasks completed including coach gate audit; plan locked end-to-end ready for Phase 11.C cycle 1 (frontend skeleton) in Session 88. Session 87 was design-and-backend; no Lovable code generation this session (credit conservation discipline held). Phase 10 surface queue replaced by Phase 11 surface queue; Phase 10.D polish round 4 (learning-admin) audit findings preserved at `/home/claude/round4-recon/` for use in Phase 11.D polish pass. **Total estimate for Phase 11 end-to-end: 5-6 sessions from Session 87** (this session = design + backend; Sessions 88-91 = frontend cycles 1 / 2a / 2b / 11.D). (1) **Round 4 polish-only approach ABANDONED mid-session.** Session opened with Round 4 audit of `/super-admin/learning-admin` 4-tab page (Trainees, Assign/Unassign, Mentor Role, Completion Control). Audit produced 22 items (3 red, 11 yellow, 8 green) including the silent no-op bug in MentorRoleTab where `set_mentor_role` returns `{changed, before, after}` but the frontend code ignores it and shows "Mentor role granted/revoked" regardless. Cole's read after the audit: "this is clunky, not comprehensive enough." Pivot to full Phase 11 consolidation locked. Audit findings preserved at `/home/claude/round4-recon/` — they will be used during Phase 11.C migration of the underlying code into the new Members surface. (2) **Phase 11 Members surface — full design + scope.** Three super-admin surfaces consolidated into single Members page: `/super-admin/learning-admin` (2038-line file with 4 inline functions), `/super-admin/users` (278-line user-management page), `UserDetailsModal` (313-line coach-attempt grant modal). Structural problem in current state: five copies of essentially the same search-table-pagination-dropdown pattern across Users.tsx, TraineesTab, AssignUnassignTab's trainee picker, MentorRoleTab, and CompletionControlTab.LearnerPicker. Members surface collapses all five duplications into one data table + filter chips + side drawer + bulk actions toolbar — industry-standard pattern (Linear, Notion, Absorb, Docebo April 2026). Competitor recon at `/home/claude/internal-docs/learning-admin-competitor-recon.md` covered Cornerstone, Docebo, SuccessFactors, Absorb, LearnUpon, TalentLMS, 360Learning, Adobe Learning Manager, Linear, Notion, Rise/Articulate. Key patterns adopted: reports-first admin, row click → side drawer, roles as user properties (not tabs), saved views with persistence. (3) **14 design decisions locked.** Full decision log at `/home/claude/internal-docs/phase-11-learners-surface-decisions.md` (1262 lines after all corrections). Surface name: Members. Route: `/super-admin/members` + `/super-admin/members/:userId` (full-page drawer view). Single sidebar entry replaces both prior nav entries. 10 default columns: Name / Email / Account type / Mentor / Organization / Active assignments / Certifications / Status / **Last login** / Actions overflow ⋮. "Last login" rename from "Last active" — auth.users.last_sign_in_at semantics are sign-in-only, not active-session (column-header tooltip clarifies). Hybrid filters: 4 always-visible chips (Account type / Mentor / Status / Active assignments) + "More filters" overflow (Organization / Certifications / Last active range / Created range / Has supervisor). Default filter: Status=Active. Saved views with per-user persistence. Search: email + name + org (current `search_impersonation_targets` scope unchanged). Typeahead 250ms debounce min 2 chars composes with filters with match highlighting. Row click: side drawer (720px desktop / full-width below 1024px via shadcn Sheet native `className="w-full sm:max-w-[720px]"`) + "Open full view" escape to `/super-admin/members/{uuid}`. Drawer: sticky header (identity + Mentor toggle + Impersonate CTA + ⋮ overflow) + tabs (Learning default / Assignments / Coach conditional / Audit). Coach tab visibility: `is_practitioner_coach=true OR account_type='coach'`. Justified-action modal: NEW shared `JustifiedActionDialog` component using **caller-owns-mutation pattern** (Option B per Lovable's pass-2 recommendation; dialog provides visual + UX + justification-field consistency, caller passes mutation function as prop). Bulk actions toolbar v1: assign / unassign / schedule / override completion / export completion CSV. **Mentor bulk-assign INCLUDED in v1** with custom per-trainee certification resolution modal — the certification axis makes mentor-bulk-assign load-bearing for the operation to make sense (a mentor isn't generically "the mentor for these N people"; they're "the mentor for these N people on this specific certification program each"). Deferred to v2: bulk role change, bulk MFA reset, bulk password reset, bulk send message (prerequisite: messaging subsystem). Schedule modal: single modal with two tabs (Create new / Scheduled (N) with status filter chip). Bulk Import modal: current download-template → fill → upload → result flow preserved. (4) **Two Lovable review passes — both substantive engagement with real catches.** Pass 1 prompt at `/home/claude/internal-docs/phase-11-lovable-review-prompt.md` (274 lines, also at `/mnt/user-data/outputs/`). Pass 2 at `phase-11-lovable-review-pass2.md` (201 lines, also at outputs). Lovable engaged with concrete risks vs rubber-stamping. **Pass 1 adoptions (16 items):** Cycle 1 stubs completion actions instead of reusing CompletionConfirmDialog (avoids dual code path during cycle 1→2 gap). Drop migration 6 noop_types_regen (types regen happens automatically after migrations 4-5 land). DROP RESTRICT not CASCADE on search_impersonation_targets recreation. Disable mentor-bulk-assign in cycle 1, build as discrete piece in cycle 2b. Drawer-vs-full-page implementation rules: React Query key as pure function of userId, shared inner makes no scroll context assumptions, active tab in URL `?tab=learning`. ui_preferences jsonb contract documented as write-from-frontend / read-whole-blob-on-mount / never query into. Per-row overflow menu: `group-hover:opacity-100 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100` desktop hover touch always-visible. Lazy-render DropdownMenuContent (Radix default; don't pre-compute action lists per row). ContentItemArtifactPanel max-height fix: `max-h-[60vh] overflow-y-auto` + sticky internal header "Viewing: [name] [Collapse]" makes inline expansion viable. Drawer responsive via shadcn Sheet native (no manual breakpoint code). Saved views persist last-selected across sessions (`default_view` field set to last-active). "Last login" rename + column-header tooltip "Last password sign-in. Does not reflect active sessions." Frontend bulk-action chunking + progress UI for selections >50 users (UX improvement, not timeout safety). Legacy nav entries during cycle 1: renamed to "Legacy: Users" / "Legacy: Learning Admin" with deprecation banners on the pages themselves linking to specific equivalent locations. React Query key stable serialization helper (sorted keys) for the 10+ filter-dimension queries. 3-cycle frontend split (cycle 1 skeleton → 2a JustifiedActionDialog + single-row actions → 2b bulk modals + mentor-bulk-assign + audit polish) plus 11.D cleanup. **Pass 2 adoptions (5 spec refinements):** Filter chip + saved view detachment indicator is a leading `●` dot (VS Code/Figma convention), NOT italics (italics reads as "system view" not "dirty"). Discard with 3+ filter/column changes shows confirmation dialog "Discard {N} changes to '{view name}'?"; single-chip discard is silent. View-switch while detached uses three-button dialog [Cancel] [Discard] [Save and switch], not two-button (two-button forces context switch to save first). NO `system_default` field in stored jsonb (frontend constant; stale-snapshot risk + "Reset to default" semantics + smaller blob). ADD `"version": 1` field to `members` object in jsonb for future schema-migration detection. Coach gate audit EXPANDED to 4 checks (RPC body read + migrations grep for account_type.*coach + coach_certifications RLS inspection + useCoachData hook check). Legacy nav deprecation banners link to specific equivalent locations ("Completion controls have moved to Members → {user} → Learning tab."). JustifiedActionDialog caller-owns-mutation pattern explicitly chosen over discriminated union (avoids 400-line switch on action type; visual + UX + justification consistency is the shared value; mutations themselves are genuinely diverse and ownership-inverted in callers ages better). Cycle 2b escape hatch identified: CSV export + audit polish can move to 11.D if 2b overflows. (5) **Three pushbacks adopted by Lovable with evidence Cole provided.** Bulk RPC cap stays at 500 (verified Supabase project `SHOW statement_timeout` returns 2min, not Lovable's assumed 8s PostgREST cancellation; existing `assign_curriculum_bulk` etc all have c_max_bulk=500 and work in production). Frontend chunking is UX improvement (50-100 per batch with progress UI + cancel) NOT a timeout safety mechanism — call it out in code comments or the next person will conflate them. No new audit-log index needed (verified `idx_super_admin_audit_log_affected_user_created` already exists as partial composite on `(affected_user_id, created_at DESC) WHERE affected_user_id IS NOT NULL` — EXPLAIN ANALYZE confirmed planner picks it). "Last login" column rename instead of new `users.last_active_at` infra for v1 (Lovable accepted both as valid; Cole picked the simpler one). Lovable also added: cancel button on bulk-action chunking aborts the frontend loop, does NOT attempt to roll back committed batches (each chunk's RPC commits independently); post-cancel UI says "Cancelled. 230 of 500 processed." not "Cancelled. Nothing was changed." EXPLAIN ANALYZE on representative user in 11.B confirmed audit log index pick (execution time 0.051ms, no seq scan). (6) **Phase 11.B backend — 5 migrations applied + verified.** All five live on production. **M1 `add_users_ui_preferences_column`**: `ALTER TABLE public.users ADD COLUMN ui_preferences jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb;` + COMMENT documenting write-whole-read-whole contract. Smoke test passed: round-trip write+read+restore via `||` jsonb concatenation operator works cleanly. **M2 `extend_search_impersonation_targets`**: DROP RESTRICT then CREATE on 3-arg form → new 14-arg signature with 7 new returned columns (`is_mentor`, `active_assignment_count`, `certification_count`, `worst_certification_status`, `account_status`, `last_sign_in_at` from auth.users via SECDEF, `show_coach_tab`) and 11 new optional filter+sort params (all NULL-default so existing 4 callsites unchanged). Active assignment count = sum of active user_curriculum_assignments + active user_module_assignments + coach_mentor_assignments where trainee_user_id=u.id AND ended_at IS NULL (trainee side, not mentor side). worst_certification_status priority: revoked > in_progress > certified. Sort columns supported: name / email / account_type / organization / account_status / worst_certification_status / active_assignments / certification_count / last_login. Smoke tests passed: real cbastian search returns 3 rows with all new columns populated (Cole: is_mentor=true, active_assignment_count=1, certification_count=2, worst_certification_status='in_progress', show_coach_tab=true; Test Coach: show_coach_tab=true from account_type='coach'; Test Client 3: show_coach_tab=false individual account_type); filter by is_mentor=true returns 3 mentors (Cole + Test Coach Cert + Test Coach 1, matching audit count); composability (search 'test' + p_account_types=['coach'] + sort by name DESC) works correctly. `auth.users.last_sign_in_at` accessible via SECDEF; postgres role has SELECT on auth.users (verified by population in returned column). **M3 `create_list_user_audit_history_rpc`**: New SECDEF RPC `list_user_audit_history(p_user_id, p_limit=50, p_offset=0, p_categories[]=NULL)`. Returns audit_id, created_at, action_type, computed category (via CASE on action_type — role/completion/certification/permission/impersonation/other), actor_user_id, actor_name with 'Unknown admin' fallback for null actor_user_id, actor_email, actor_account_type, reason, before_value, after_value, detail, total_count via COUNT(*) OVER(). Smoke test on Cole returned 2 entries (mentor_role_changed→role + free_attempts_granted→certification, both actor=Cole). EXPLAIN ANALYZE confirmed planner picks `idx_super_admin_audit_log_affected_user_created` (Index Scan, 0.051ms execution, no seq scan, 3 buffer hits). Lovable's index concern resolved without new migration. **M4 `create_completion_bulk_rpcs`**: Three new SECDEF wrappers following `assign_curriculum_bulk` template. `set_content_item_completion_bulk(p_user_ids[], p_content_item_id, p_complete, p_reason)`, `set_module_completion_bulk(p_user_ids[], p_module_id, p_complete, p_reason)`, `set_curriculum_completion_bulk(p_user_ids[], p_curriculum_id, p_complete, p_reason)` — the curriculum variant resolves user→assignment_id internally, preferring active over completed, most recent assigned_at, per-user "no_curriculum_assignment_for_user" recorded as failure. Max 500 users per call. Each wrapper does BEGIN/EXCEPTION/END isolation per user, calls `assert_super_admin()` once at top, inherits impersonation gate from wrapped single-user RPCs (no double-gating). Returns standard `{operation, requested, succeeded, failed, results[]}` shape.
