Did NOT exercise as real bulk operations (would mark real content items complete); signature verification + structural match to `assign_curriculum_bulk` template sufficient at this phase. **M5 `add_completion_export_rpc`**: New SECDEF `get_user_completion_export(p_user_ids uuid[])` returning flat per-user-per-item rowset via UNION ALL across 4 tiers (cert_path from coach_certifications, curriculum from user_curriculum_assignments, module from user_module_assignments, content_item from content_item_completions). Columns: user_id, user_email, user_full_name, tier, target_id, target_name, parent_path (module breadcrumb for content_items via LEFT JOIN modules), status, started_at, completed_at, assigned_at. Max 500 users. Smoke test on Cole returned 2 cert_path rows (my_brainwise_coach certified + ptp_coach in_progress) + 1 curriculum row (PTP VILT 1 active) — expected shape. Frontend builds CSV from result. Final verification: 7-of-7 PASS check confirmed all deliverables present (M1 column, M2 14-arg signature, M3, M4a/b/c all three bulk RPCs, M5). (7) **Coach gate audit — 4 checks all clean.** Performed before M2 to ensure Coach tab plan holds. **Check 1**: `grant_additional_free_attempts` RPC body gates on `assert_super_admin + assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change')` only; does NOT require `account_type='coach'` on target. Cert row existence is sufficient. **Check 2**: `coach_certifications` RLS — three policies: service_role bypass + super_admin full access + `coaches read own (user_id = auth.uid())`. The "coaches read own" policy does NOT require `account_type='coach'`, so `is_practitioner_coach=true` users (including the 2 super_admins) can read their own cert rows. RLS is moot for drawer anyway since SECDEF bypasses RLS. **Check 3**: `pg_proc.prosrc ILIKE '%account_type%coach%'` returned 33 functions — most are content-fetching with coach-context permission checks, not gates affecting the Members Coach tab. Notable: `_sync_is_practitioner_coach_on_account_type` trigger auto-sets `is_practitioner_coach=true` when account_type becomes coach OR brainwise_super_admin — explains why all 10 coaches AND 2 super_admins have `is_practitioner_coach=true`. **Check 4**: `_sync_is_practitioner_coach_on_account_type` trigger body confirms the auto-sync. Coach tab visibility expression `is_practitioner_coach=true OR account_type='coach'` is equivalent to just `is_practitioner_coach=true` given the trigger — defensive OR doesn't hurt. **Conclusion**: plan holds without modification. Coach tab shows for `is_practitioner_coach=true OR account_type='coach'`; inside the tab, cert list shows for all; grant-attempts action enabled for all (RPC does not gate on account_type). (8) **Phase 11.C plan locked end-to-end — 3 frontend cycles + cleanup.** **Cycle 1 (next session)**: page + table + drawer skeleton with completion/bulk/role actions STUBBED. New files: `src/pages/super-admin/Members.tsx` (route handlers for `/super-admin/members` and `/super-admin/members/:userId`), `src/components/members/MembersTable.tsx` (data table + columns + multi-select + sort), `MembersFilterBar.tsx` (4 always-visible chips + More filters overflow + Saved views dropdown), `MembersBulkActionsBar.tsx` (sticky toolbar with stub action handlers), `MemberDrawer.tsx` (drawer shell with sticky header + tabs), `MemberDrawerLearning.tsx` (Learning tab migrating AdminLearningTree near-as-is, mark-complete STUBBED via "Available after next update" toast to avoid dual completion-confirm path), `MemberDrawerCoach.tsx` (Coach tab migrating UserDetailsModal coach section), `MemberDrawerAssignments.tsx` (Assignments tab reusing SingleUserAssignDialog patterns inline), `MemberDrawerAudit.tsx` (Audit tab loading `list_user_audit_history` and rendering timeline — no in-tab filter chips yet), `ColumnVisibilityMenu.tsx`, `SavedViewsDropdown.tsx`, `src/lib/highlightMatch.ts`. Modified files: `App.tsx` (new routes), `AppSidebar.tsx` (rename existing entries to "Legacy: Users" / "Legacy: Learning Admin" with deprecation banners on the pages themselves linking to specific equivalents, add single "Members" entry). **Cycle 2a**: NEW shared `JustifiedActionDialog` component at `src/components/justified-action/JustifiedActionDialog.tsx` using caller-owns-mutation pattern; API spec sketch: `{open, onOpenChange, title, description?, onSubmit: (reason) => Promise<{changed, note?}>, mapError?, successTitle, noopTitle?, confirmLabel?}` — dialog handles 10-char-min validation + submitting state + error display + no-op handling (`changed: false`) + success toast; caller handles RPC selection + payload + post-action React Query invalidation + post-success cleanup. Wire single-row actions through it: mentor toggle in drawer header (fixes silent no-op bug from MentorRoleTab as side effect), cert grant/revoke in Coach tab, completion mark in Learning tab, grant attempts in Coach tab. **Cycle 2b**: bulk modals (BulkAssignModal, BulkUnassignModal, BulkOverrideCompletionModal), BulkAssignMentorModal (custom per-trainee cert resolution modal), ScheduleAssignmentModal (two-tab Create/Pending), BulkImportModal (preserve current download-template/upload/report flow), audit tab filter chips + Load more pagination, bulk Export Completion CSV handler from `get_user_completion_export`, frontend chunking UX for >50-user selections with progress + cancel. Escape hatch: CSV export + audit polish can move to 11.D if 2b overflows. **11.D cleanup**: DELETE legacy files (LearningAdmin.tsx 2038 lines, Users.tsx 278 lines, UserDetailsModal.tsx 313 lines, CompletionControlTab.tsx, CompletionConfirmDialog.tsx, AdminLearningTree.tsx after migration into MemberDrawerLearning, TraineeMultiSelect.tsx, learnerSearchShared.ts), remove fallback nav/routes (or convert to redirects), brand color tokens for status pills in migrated AdminLearningTree (replace raw amber/emerald/purple Tailwind), a11y pass, mobile responsiveness pass. (9) **NEW Build Queue items added Session 87 (v2 deferred from Phase 11):** (a) **`users.last_active_at` infrastructure** — lightweight RPC on app load + column update to give honest "Last active" semantics (current "Last login" reads from auth.users.last_sign_in_at which only updates on password sign-in, not JWT refresh, so long-session users show stale data). Defer until Phase 11 ships and "Last login" tooltip semantics are operationally validated. (b) **`user_ui_preferences` dedicated table migration** — when 5+ admin surfaces have persisted prefs OR any query-into-preferences requirement emerges. Until then, jsonb-on-users column is fine. Migration is genuinely cheap when the day comes (one migration + one hook swap), so deferring is correct. (c) **Bulk role change** (deferred from Members v1 bulk-actions toolbar). (d) **Bulk MFA reset** (deferred from v1). (e) **Bulk password reset** (deferred from v1). (f) **Bulk send message** (deferred from v1; prerequisite is messaging subsystem). (g) **`/super-admin/coaches` consolidation evaluation** — Coach Management nav entry was noted as a third super-admin surface that *might* overlap with Members but was kept out of scope for Phase 11; revisit after Phase 11 ships to evaluate whether it absorbs into Members or stays standalone. (h) **`results_available` notification coverage for NAI / AIRSA / HSS** (carried from Session 86) — currently PTP-only because non-PTP instruments don't populate `facet_insights_all_total`. Wiring requires either per-instrument generation EFs maintaining the threshold OR a per-instrument completion contract. AIRSA facet-generation gap investigation (Session 84) is a prerequisite. (10) **Standing rules to consider for v91 architecture-reference:** Four candidates emerged. **§121 candidate — Caller-owns-mutation pattern for shared action dialogs**: when a "shared" dialog would otherwise require a discriminated union of action types (each with different RPC, payload, invalidation, error mapping), invert ownership: dialog provides visual + UX + justification-field consistency, caller passes mutation function as prop. Avoids ugly internal switches and ages better. Trade-off: callers carry slightly more post-action cleanup responsibility, but the cleanup is genuinely caller-specific (different RPCs invalidate different keys). **§122 candidate — jsonb-on-row prefs vs table-of-prefs decision criterion**: for per-user preferences scoped to a single admin surface, column-on-users jsonb is fine. Migrate to dedicated table when 5+ surfaces have persisted prefs OR any query-into-preferences requirement emerges. Document write-from-frontend / read-whole-blob-on-mount contract in column comment to prevent accidental GIN-indexing or jsonb_path_query in WHERE clauses. Include `"version": 1` field for future schema evolution detection. **§123 candidate — Bulk RPC cap vs frontend chunking discipline**: bulk RPC cap matches existing pattern in the codebase for consistency (current: 500). Frontend chunking + progress UI ships independently as UX improvement, not as a backend timeout workaround. Chunks of 50-100 are pragmatic; cancel aborts the loop on frontend (each chunk RPC commits independently). Post-cancel UI must be honest: "Cancelled. {N} of {total} processed." not "Cancelled. Nothing was changed." **§124 candidate — Saved view detachment pattern (Linear style)**: when a UI surface has both saved views and inline filter controls, chip toggles detach the active view into an unsaved state rather than overwriting it. Indicator: leading `●` dot before view name (VS Code/Figma convention). Discard requires confirmation when 3+ changes detected. View-switch while detached uses three-button dialog [Cancel] [Discard] [Save and switch]. Cole picks the right subset for v91 in closeout; some may be too Phase 11-specific to warrant a §-number and become process notes instead. (11) **Session 87 close artifacts**: this v95 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v91 (selected from candidates §121-§124 above), session-87-to-88.md handoff, plus 4 design-phase docs (learning-admin-competitor-recon.md, phase-11-learners-surface-decisions.md, phase-11-lovable-review-prompt.md, phase-11-lovable-review-pass2.md) for upload to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root). Markdown only — Session 74 decision. Cole uploads all manually; GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY. **Session 88 opens on Phase 11.C cycle 1 — draft Lovable prompt for page + table + drawer skeleton with completion/bulk/role actions STUBBED, legacy routes preserved with deprecation banners linking to specific equivalent locations. Reference phase-11-learners-surface-decisions.md as canonical source.***

*v94 - Session 86 CLOSE — Notifications Phase 3 closeout SHIPPED end-to-end and verified; Phase 10 polish rounds 1, 2, 3, and 5 SHIPPED (Round 4 deferred to Session 87 for fresh recon). `results_available` firing-point wired via Postgres-only watcher trigger (no Edge Functions touched), `is_v1_active` flipped to true, all fixtures cleaned up; state restored to baseline. Phase 10 status: 4 of 8 surfaces shipped this session; 4 remain for Session 87+ (Round 4 Learning-admin tooling, Round 6 Notification surfaces, Round 7 Coach surfaces, Round 8 Mentor portal). (1) **`results_available` firing point SHIPPED — Postgres-only watcher trigger.** Constraint locked by Cole: do not touch any existing Edge Functions. Recovered the Session 84 paths-A-and-B notes via `conversation_search` (canonical docs had logged "documented" without the specifics). Cole rejected both Path A (fire from `calculate-scores` at row-insert with "results are ready to view" wording) and Path B (rearchitect NAI/AIRSA section generation to fire on true completion); proposed instead a row-count watcher on `facet_interpretations`. Discovery via diagnostic SQL: per-facet content is NOT stored as one row per facet — it lives inside `facet_interpretations.facet_data` JSONB arrays (one row per section bucket like `facet_insights_all`, with the array carrying the per-facet entries). `assessment_results.facet_insights_all_total` already tracks the expected per-context completion count (89 for `context_type='both'`, 47 for paired `professional`, 42 for paired `personal`). The completion contract: `jsonb_array_length(facet_data) >= facet_insights_all_total` on the `facet_insights_all` row. Verified live across all 7 PTP rows where the column is populated — every one matches exactly. (2) **Lovable review pass incorporated.** Cole sent the spec draft to Lovable for safety review. Lovable verified the dedup unique index is `(user_id, notification_type, dedup_key) WHERE dedup_key IS NOT NULL` — per-user not global — so taker and coach can safely share the same `dedup_key` string. Accepted refinements: instrument_label lookup via `instruments.instrument_name` (not hardcoded), JSONB type guard (`jsonb_typeof = 'array'`) before `jsonb_array_length`, per-call `BEGIN/EXCEPTION` blocks to isolate taker vs coach failures, `IF NOT FOUND THEN RETURN NEW` orphan guards on both `assessment_results` and `assessments` lookups, defensive `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users ...)` checks before each `notify_user` call. Rejected: `IS NOT DISTINCT FROM` short-circuit on UPDATEs (would short-circuit valid INSERTs because OLD is NULL; dedup_key already handles idempotency). Locked: Option A for paired PTPs — one notification per half (per `assessment_result_id`); UI-side collapse from Session 85 Option C2 handles the user-facing single-entity view. (3) **Two migrations applied + verified.** `session86_results_available_watcher_trigger` created the SECDEF function `fire_results_available_on_full_facets()` and the `trg_results_available_on_full_facets` AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE trigger on `facet_interpretations` gated to `WHEN section_type = 'facet_insights_all'`. `session86_results_available_activate_v1` flipped `notification_types_catalog.results_available.is_v1_active = true`. Zero existing user triggers on `facet_interpretations` (no collision risk). The dry-run sequence (deploy trigger → verify → flip flag) is safe because `notify_user` short-circuits at the `is_v1_active` check returning `'type_not_v1_active'` without inserting any row. (4) **Five fixture-based tests, all passed.** Cole declined to take a real PTP through the system; tests run by directly inserting fixture rows that match the trigger's expected shape. Test 1 (dry-run, flag off): trigger fired, `notify_user` returned `type_not_v1_active`, zero notifications landed. Test 2 (flag on, taker only, `context_type='both'`, total=89, array_len=89): notification landed for taker with correct payload — `instrument_label="Personal Threat Profile"` resolved via `instruments` table lookup, not hardcoded fallback. Test 3 (flag on, coach-ordered via `ordered_by_coach_id=2f591eb3-...` Test Coach 2): both taker AND coach got notifications sharing the same dedup_key `results_available:<ar_id>` — validates the per-user unique index. Test 4 (idempotency): re-firing trigger via UPDATE on existing facet row produced zero duplicate notifications — both users remained at exactly 1 row for the shared dedup_key; `notify_user`'s `unique_violation` handler is doing its job. Test 5 (non-PTP gate): inserted NAI (INST-002) fixture with `facet_insights_all_total=25` and `array_len=25`; trigger fired, `instrument_id <> 'INST-001'` gate held, zero notifications landed. (5) **All fixtures cleaned up.** Four assessment_results, four assessments, four facet_interpretations rows, and three landed user_notifications all deleted in reverse-FK order. Baseline notification count restored exactly (`testclientbwe+employee@gmail.com` went from 10 → 10). Database state matches pre-Session-86 except for the new function, trigger, and flipped catalog flag. (6) **v1 coverage scope: PTP only.** NAI / AIRSA / HSS do not populate `facet_insights_all_total` (all NULL for those instruments); the trigger silently passes them. Wiring those instruments requires either their respective generation EFs populating `facet_insights_all_total` (or equivalent threshold column) OR a per-instrument completion contract. Both deferred — the AIRSA facet-generation gap investigation (Session 84 deferred item) is a prerequisite for AIRSA coverage. (7) **Standing rule candidate (§119): completion-watcher trigger pattern.** When a notification needs to fire at "true completion" of a server-side process that writes a deterministic completion threshold (count, status, etc.), prefer a Postgres trigger reading that threshold over editing the producing EF — provided the threshold column is already maintained by the EF and the completion contract is enumerable. Pattern: AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE trigger with WHEN-clause filter, SECDEF function checking threshold against array_length/row_count, dedup_key on `notify_user` for fire-once-per-target. No EF edits; deploy is reversible via DROP TRIGGER + flag flip. (8) **Notifications Phase 3 is now CLOSED.** All 18 catalog types have been reviewed; 17 are wired (16 active + `results_available` activated this session); 1 (`cert_path_deadline_approaching`) remains explicitly deferred as a v2 informational feature with no documented firing path. (9) **Phase 10 polish round 1 SHIPPED — My Learning tab + palette consistency pass.** Surface-at-a-time approach to Phase 10 to avoid blast-radius regressions. Audit produced 42 items across 7 categories (empty states, loading, error handling, brand styling, a11y, standing rules, data flow); 19 green, 15 yellow, 8 red. Spec sent to Lovable for safety verification — Lovable caught one real omission (ModuleDetail.tsx amber Clock icon adjacent to Tile.tsx amber pill — palette inconsistency if not bundled) and confirmed every spec item before any code was written. Cole found a second omission via independent verification — CoachInvoices.tsx "In Progress" badge also used amber. Two backend migrations applied + verified before any frontend: `session86_recommended_next_add_content_item_title` (added content_item_title to both `_compute_recommended_next_for_*` helpers, one-key surgical addition) and `session86_get_user_learning_state_add_polish_tile_fields` (added `estimated_minutes` and `instrument_codes` flat fields to assignment rows, `estimated_minutes` to module rows, all via surgical edit pattern from §-candidate). Verified live against Cheryl Kish's PTP cert path enrollment — `instrument_codes: ["INST-001"]`, `content_item_title: "VILT 1: Introduction & Orientation"` populated correctly. Regression test against zero-enrollment and multi-enrollment users passed. Frontend Lovable round: 6 files touched. **`MyLearningTab.tsx`** structural rewrite — inline TypeScript interfaces (`UserLearningState`/`LearningAssignment`/`ModuleAssignment`/`RecommendedNext`/`AvailableLearning`) replacing `as any` with `as unknown as <Interface>` boundary casts (§111 type discipline); Section component extended with `filteredEmptyText`/`isFiltered`/`enrollingId` to distinguish filter-empty from real-empty; empty-text copy fixes ("Browse All Available" → "Browse & Enroll" matching button label); per-entity enroll loading state with `inlineCtaLoading` prop preventing double-click; error card with Retry button rendering before loading/welcome shell; loading spinner with `role="status"` and aria-label; `hasAnyContent` welcome shell for first-time users; search input `aria-label="Search learning content"`; new Tile props passed from Section — `instrumentCodes` (cert_path entities only, from `entity.instrument_codes`) and `recommendedNextLabel` (compute-here IIFE with verb switch: `"Start with: …"` for not_started, `"Continue with: …"` for in_progress, hidden for completed; uses `content_item_title ?? module_name` for the readable title); render order locked as `!userId` → error → loading → welcome shell → normal. **`Tile.tsx`** (shared, blast radius 4 production surfaces) — in-progress pill background `var(--bw-amber)` → `var(--bw-teal)` semantic upgrade (warning token → info token per `marketing-tokens.css`); new optional `recommendedNextLabel` prop rendered between summary and metadata row in teal; new optional `inlineCtaLoading` prop disabling the CTA button and rendering a `Loader2` icon when loading; added `Loader2` to lucide-react imports. **`tileVariants.ts`** — JSDoc comment above `InstrumentCode` union noting source of truth (`instruments.instrument_id`) and instruction to extend the union when new instruments are added (INST-005+). **`ModuleDetail.tsx`** line ~447 — Clock icon for in-progress content items `var(--bw-amber)` → `var(--bw-teal)` (palette consistency with parent Tile). **`CoachInvoices.tsx`** line ~284 — "In Progress" status badge background `var(--bw-amber)` → `var(--bw-teal)`. Cole evaluated whether this badge should stay amber (fulfillment-status semantic of paid transaction, not learning progress) but decided teal is fine; semantic distinction noted but not enforced. **`WrittenSummaryViewer.tsx`** line ~56 — color UNCHANGED, comment added marking amber as intentional for pending-review state (waiting on mentor, not actively in-progress); palette polish passes must respect this. (10) **Process discipline pattern established for Phase 10:** Audit → triage with Cole → Lovable verification deep-dive → backend-first (if needed) → frontend Lovable round → Cole visual + functional verification → close round → next surface. Round 1 took the full pattern; subsequent rounds benefit from the established discipline. (11) **Palette audit lesson — process note, not a §-numbered rule.** Pattern-matching "find every `--bw-amber` usage" was the right tool to surface candidates but the wrong tool to decide which ones should flip to teal. Each candidate needed a one-line semantic read: actor identity matters more than label text. Two surfaces displaying "In Progress" can warrant different colors if one means "you are doing it" and the other means "someone else is doing it and you are waiting." Three candidates this round: Tile.tsx pill (flip — user is actively engaged), ModuleDetail Clock (flip — same actor as Tile), WrittenSummaryViewer (stay amber — pending-review, waiting on mentor). CoachInvoices was a third semantic again (fulfillment-status of paid transaction) — Cole decided teal is acceptable, judgment call not enforced. Discipline noted for future palette work. (12) **My Learning polish ROUND 1 CLOSED.** Standing rule candidates from this round: §111 type discipline reinforced (UserLearningState wrapper interface defined, `as unknown as` boundary cast pattern). Surgical-edit pattern (Session 85 candidate) reinforced — both migrations applied as surgical additions to existing functions, never rewrites. Frontend Lovable round used "read first, then write" discipline with explicit Section A (structural) vs Section B (surgical) separation in the prompt — no scope drift. (13) **Remaining Phase 10 surfaces, scoped for Sessions 86-87:** CertPathDetail.tsx (likely large file, careful read needed), CurriculumDetail.tsx, ModuleDetail.tsx (touched lightly this round — may not need full pass), ResourceGridTab.tsx + ResourceReader.tsx (resources subsystem), coach surfaces (CoachClients, ClientResults, CoachInvoices partially touched), learning-admin tooling (Completion Control, Assign Mentor Role tabs), mentor portal (`/mentor` routes), notification surfaces (just shipped Session 84 — should be clean). 7-8 candidate surfaces remaining. At one Lovable round per surface plus recon, may need to split between Sessions 86 and 87. (14) **Phase 10 polish round 2 SHIPPED — CertPathDetail.tsx.** Single-file frontend round; no migrations, no shared-file touches (Tile primitive inherited Round 1 changes via existing `detailPageMode={true}` curriculum tile usage). Audit produced 31 items: 8 red, 11 yellow, 12 green. Cole triaged 7 open decisions in real-time; Lovable's verification pass confirmed exact line-number anchors across all 11 implementation sections before any code was written, raised 3 non-blocking flags (the `as never` casts on RPC argument calls are input-side type suppression separate from the §111 output-side rule — left untouched; the try/finally wrap on handleEnroll reindents ~38 lines as whitespace churn — called out in prompt; §10 IIFE pattern flagged as alternative to hoisted const but consistency with Round 1's `recommendedNextLabel` pattern won — kept IIFE). Eleven implementation sections shipped in one Lovable cycle: §1 inline types (`RecommendedNext`/`DimensionCompetency`/`UserAssignment`/`Curriculum`/`CertificationPath`/`UserCertification`/`CertPathDetailResponse`/`EnrollPaymentRequired` union); §2 four `as any` boundary cast removals (main RPC return → `as unknown as CertPathDetailResponse`, derived state types annotated, enroll RPC response → `EnrollResponse` union with `(enrollResponse as EnrollPaymentRequired).status` narrow inside the if-condition, error object → `Error | null`); §3 loading-state a11y (`role="status"`, `aria-label="Loading certification path"`, `aria-hidden="true"` on Loader2); §4 error branch Retry button + `navigate(-1)` Back with `/resources` fallback; §5 enroll double-click protection (new `isEnrolling` state, `try/finally` wrap on `handleEnroll`, button `disabled={cta.label === "Enroll" && isEnrolling}` with conditional Loader2 spinner); §6 Resume CTA deep-link to `/learning/content-item/{recommendedNext.content_item_id}` (stale Group W TODO removed cleanly — viewer infrastructure had already shipped, TODO was misleading); §7 CTA decision-tree clarifying block comment (4-branch precedence enumeration); §8 normal-render Back button `navigate(-1)` with `/resources` fallback (matches error-branch Back); §9 hero h1 `line-clamp-2` (defensive against long cert path names); §10 missing-instruments specific copy (IIFE compute pattern matching Round 1, grammatical 0/1/2/3+ branching: "Take your NAI assessment" / "Take your NAI and AIRSA assessments" / "Take your PTP, NAI, and AIRSA assessments"); §11 brand glyph decorative comment above hero brain icon. Post-edit self-check confirmed: zero `as any` in file, exactly two `as unknown as` boundary casts (queryFn line 173, handleEnroll line 234), zero `TODO Group W`, no edits to any other file. File grew from 458 → 597 lines (added types, IIFE expansion, retry block, comment blocks). Cole's 11-point verification checklist all passed: type discipline, Resume deep-link, double-click guard, error retry, loading a11y, back button history, h1 line-clamp, missing-instruments copy, branch comment, brand glyph comment, regression (certified-view Review button, hero dominant color, dimension cards, curriculum tile navigation, paid-enrollment modal). **WCAG AA contrast audit on dimension card and brand color combinations LOGGED to build queue as Phase 11 item** (deferred from Round 2 R8); broader scope than just dimension cards — covers whole-app brand color contrast verification. **Round 2 CLOSED.** Round 3 next: CurriculumDetail.tsx + ModuleDetail.tsx (paired). (15) **Phase 10 surface queue order (locked):** 1) CertPathDetail [DONE Round 2], 2) CurriculumDetail + ModuleDetail paired [Round 3], 3) Learning-admin tooling (Completion Control + Assign Mentor Role), 4) ResourceGridTab + ResourceReader, 5) Notification surfaces (bell/dropdown/`/notifications`/`/settings/notifications`), 6) Coach surfaces (CoachClients/ClientResults/CoachInvoices), 7) Mentor portal (`/mentor` routes). Approach Option A locked: full discipline per surface (audit → triage → Lovable verification → backend if needed → frontend Lovable round → verify → close). (16) **Phase 10 polish round 3 SHIPPED — CurriculumDetail.tsx + ModuleDetail.tsx paired.** Two-file frontend round; no migrations, no shared-file touches. Audit produced 42 items: 16 red (8 per file, mirror pattern), 12 yellow, 14 green. Cole triaged 7 open decisions; Decision 4 (mentor-view CTA gating) verified unreachable in current UI — all three detail RPCs receive `p_user_id: userId` from `useAuth().user.id`, no production callsite passes a trainee's ID; deferred to build queue for when mentor "view trainee" surfaces ship. Lovable's pre-flight verification caught one audit miss: `prereqLabel` helper was classified as dead code but is called at line 367 in CurriculumDetail.tsx; corrected to retype-instead-of-delete (folds into §111). One more miss caught during execution: self-check #4 expected count was 2 but actual is 3 — module tile onClick in modules grid is a legitimate third site (`<Tile onClick={() => navigate('/learning/module/${m.module_id}')}>`), correctly unchanged by the round. Twenty-two implementation sections shipped in one Lovable cycle (11 per file, mirror structure): A1/B1 inline types (mirror interfaces — `RecommendedNext`/`ContentItemCompletion`/`ContentItem`/`ModuleCompletion`/`UserAssignment`/`ModuleRecord`/`ParentCertPath`/`ParentCurriculum`/`CurriculumRecord` or `ModuleRecord`/`CurriculumDetailResponse` or `ModuleDetailResponse`/`EnrollPaymentRequired`/`EnrollResponse` union), A2 `prereqLabel` retype (corrected from delete), A3/B2 `as any` removals (4 sites per file), A4/B3 loading-state a11y, A5/B4 error branch Retry button + `navigate(-1)` Back with `/resources` fallback, A6/B5 enroll double-click protection (try/finally lock + button spinner), A7 CurriculumDetail Resume deep-link to `/learning/content-item/{recommendedNext.content_item_id}` matching CertPathDetail pattern (skips module page entirely), B6 four stale Group W TODO removals in ModuleDetail (Review/Resume/Start branches + content item row onClick — all four already worked, comments were vestigial), A8/B7 CTA decision-tree clarifying block comments (6-branch precedence enumerations per file), A9/B8 normal-render Back button fallback, A10/B9 hero h1 `line-clamp-2`, A11/B10 brand glyph decorative comments, B11 content item row `aria-label="Open content item: {title}"` (a11y win unique to ModuleDetail). Post-edit self-check confirmed via independent grep: zero `as any` per file, exactly two `as unknown as` boundary casts per file, zero `TODO Group W`, three `/learning/module/` callsites in CurriculumDetail (Review + Start CTAs + Tile onClick — all correct). Files grew: CurriculumDetail 384 → 530 lines, ModuleDetail 469 → 624 lines (~30-38% each, consistent with type-block additions and error-card expansions). Cole's 25-point verification checklist all passed. **Mentor-view CTA gating LOGGED to build queue**: when mentor "view trainee's learning" UIs ship, all three detail pages (CertPathDetail/CurriculumDetail/ModuleDetail) will need CTA gating to hide self-enroll actions when `viewer_role !== 'self'`, plus a data-flow change to pass `p_user_id` as the trainee's ID. Not currently reachable; do not pre-build. (17) **Audit discipline process note for future rounds.** Session 86 surfaced a recurring pattern of audit misses: claiming code is dead/deferred/stale without grep-verifying the claim. Four instances this session: Round 2 found a stale Group W TODO comment Claude classified as deferred (viewers had already shipped); Round 3 audit classified `prereqLabel` as dead code without grepping for callsites (was called at line 367); Round 3 self-check #4 expected 2 matches for `/learning/module/` in CurriculumDetail (actual was 3 — missed the Tile onClick in modules grid). Each was caught by Lovable pre-flight or post-merge grep; recovery cost was one round trip per miss. Discipline locked for Round 4+: every numeric expectation in a self-check, every "dead code" judgment, every "stale TODO" classification gets a literal grep against the live file before claim is written. **Round 3 CLOSED.** Round 4 next: Learning-admin tooling (Completion Control tab + Assign Mentor Role tab). (18) **Phase 10 polish round 5 SHIPPED — ResourceGridTab.tsx + ResourceReader.tsx (resources subsystem).** Out-of-order execution per Cole's call — Round 4 (learning-admin) deferred to Session 87 because the resources subsystem is shorter and Session 86 was running long. Two-file frontend round, smallest of the session. Audit produced 22 items: 4 red, 7 yellow, 11 green. Key structural finding: **the resources subsystem was built with proper TypeScript interfaces from day one** (`src/components/resources/types.ts` exports `Resource`/`ResourceTab`/`GetUserResourcesResult`/`UpgradeEntityType`), so there are ZERO `as any` casts in either file and NO §111 violations to fix. This made the round substantially smaller than Rounds 2-3 — no inline interface block, no boundary-cast work. Seven implementation sections shipped in one Lovable cycle. **ResourceGridTab.tsx** (3 sections): A1 search input `aria-label="Search resources"`; A2 `try/catch` wrap on `handleFileDownload` (currently checked `error` and `data?.error` but didn't handle invoke-itself exceptions — async unhandled rejection on auth-token-expired edge cases now caught with destructive toast); A3 6-line decision-tree comment above `handleResourceClick` documenting the 5-branch routing precedence (locked → upgrade modal, video with asset → reader page, non-video with asset → file download, external link → window.open, inline content → reader page). **ResourceReader.tsx** (4 sections): B1 main loading state a11y (`role="status"`, `aria-label="Loading resource"`, `aria-hidden="true"` on Loader2); B2 VideoPlayer error retry — added `refetch` to `useResourceSignedUrl` destructure, added Retry button to error branch, also added matching a11y treatment to VideoPlayer's loading state (`role="status"`, `aria-label="Loading video"`, `aria-hidden` on Loader2); B3 resource-not-found Retry — added `refetch` to main useQuery destructure, expanded card copy to "Resource not found. If you expected to see this resource, it may have been moved or you may have lost access.", added Retry button inside the card; B4 Back button `navigate(-1)` with `/resources` fallback alignment applied to all three sites in ResourceReader (resource-not-found, resource-not-accessible, main render) — same pattern from CertPathDetail Round 2 now consistently applied across detail/reader surfaces. Post-edit self-check confirmed via independent grep: 3 `navigate("/resources")` calls in RR all inside `else` fallback branches, 3 `aria-label` matches across both files (Search input + main loading + VideoPlayer loading), 4 `refetch` matches in RR (2 destructures + 2 onClick callsites), 1 `try {` match in RGT. Files grew modestly: ResourceGridTab 195 → 210 lines (+15), ResourceReader 330 → 366 lines (+36). Cole's 13-point verification checklist all passed. **Round 5 CLOSED.** (19) **Phase 10 status as of session close**: 4 of 8 surfaces shipped (My Learning round 1, CertPathDetail round 2, CurriculumDetail+ModuleDetail paired round 3, ResourceGridTab+ResourceReader round 5). 4 surfaces remain for Session 87+: Round 4 (Learning-admin tooling — Completion Control tab + Assign Mentor Role tab; different patterns from the detail-page family, super-admin-gated, fresh recon needed), Round 6 (Notification surfaces — bell/dropdown/`/notifications`/`/settings/notifications`; just shipped Session 84 so should audit clean), Round 7 (Coach surfaces — CoachClients/ClientResults/CoachInvoices; partially touched in Round 1 palette work), Round 8 (Mentor portal — `/mentor` routes; lowest blast radius, smallest user base). Session 87 opens on Round 4 — recon-from-scratch since no prior session has audited the learning-admin tabs. (20) **New build queue items logged from Session 86 polish work:** (a) **Safari/mobile popup-blocker compatibility on resource file download flow** (Round 5 RGT-Y2 deferred) — `window.open` after `await` on signed-URL fetch may be blocked in strict browsers; needs production testing to confirm real-world incidence before fix is scoped. (b) **Phase 11 broader a11y pass for tap-target sizes and brand color contrast** (Round 2 R8 + Round 5 RR-Y3 deferred) — covers dimension card WCAG AA contrast verification, shadcn Button default h-10 vs WCAG 2.5.5 recommendation of 44×44, and whole-app brand-color contrast audit; scope a focused a11y session for it. (c) **Mentor "view trainee's learning" surfaces** (Round 3 Decision 4 deferred) — when designed, requires CTA gating on all three detail pages (CertPathDetail/CurriculumDetail/ModuleDetail) to hide self-enroll actions when `viewer_role !== 'self'`, plus data-flow change to pass `p_user_id` as the trainee's ID instead of the caller's. Not currently reachable via production UI; do not pre-build. (21) **Session 86 close artifacts**: this v94 build-queue entry (extended through items 14-20 with the four polish rounds), architecture-reference v90 (§119 completion-watcher trigger pattern, §120 palette color semantics actor-identity rule, plus Round 2/3/5 ship notes + audit discipline process note), session-86-to-87.md handoff. Markdown only — Session-74 decision. Cole uploads all three manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root); GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY. **Session 87 opens on: (1) Round 4 — Learning-admin tooling polish (Completion Control + Assign Mentor Role tabs, fresh recon); (2) Round 6 — Notification surfaces polish; (3) Round 7 — Coach surfaces polish; (4) Round 8 — Mentor portal polish; (5) formal Group C closure once all Phase 10 polish rounds ship.***

Group C closeable subject to remaining Session 86 priorities: Phase 10 polish pass, then formal Group C closure documentation.*

*v93 - Session 85 CLOSE — five production bug clusters SHIPPED end-to-end and verified; cohorts formally deferred indefinitely; module reorder gap discovered + scoped + deferred to a fresh session. Backend-first throughout. Six backend migrations applied + verified (one full-rewrite RPC attempt was rolled back and replaced with a surgical edit before any production traffic hit it), one new SECURITY DEFINER RPC created, multiple Lovable PRs (Cole runs all Lovable; GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY). Carried Session 84 priorities (notifications Phase 3, Phase 10 polish, Group C closeable) NOT advanced — Session 85 was a production bug cluster session; they carry forward to Session 86 unchanged. Cohorts formally confirmed not needed and marked deferred (Q11 scoped them as schema-seam only; v1 UI was always a non-goal; Group C is closeable without them). (1) **Bug 1 SHIPPED — coach actor-order gate fix (Caroline / Patrice / Ryan).** Three production coaches with `coach_certifications.status = 'in_progress'` (not yet `certified` but holding non-zero `free_assessment_uses` cert-pool credit) were blocked at three frontend gates in `src/pages/coach/CoachClients.tsx`. Existing gates only checked `certified`. Pure frontend fix via Lovable: introduced derived booleans `canOrderAssessment` (certified OR in_progress-with-pool) and `actorOnlyMode` (forces modal into actor-debrief flow when coach is not certified but has pool credit). Modal force-sets `isActorDebrief = true` on submit. Plus a toast typo fix ("3 actor debriefs" → "4"). No backend touched. Standing rule candidate (§114): UI cert-status gates must distinguish "no cert" from "in-progress with active cert-pool credit"; `canDoFeature = certified || (in_progress && hasActivePool)`. (2) **Bug 2 SHIPPED — module archive CHECK constraint extension.** Module-tier archive cascade was failing because `content_assets.archive_reason` CHECK did not whitelist `module_archived`, `curriculum_archived`, or `certification_path_archived`. Cascade attempted to write the reason, hit the CHECK, transaction rolled back. Migration `session85_content_assets_archive_reason_check_extend_tier_cascade_reasons` dropped the old constraint and recreated with the 8-value whitelist (3 new tier-cascade values added — `module_archived`, `curriculum_archived`, `certification_path_archived` — alongside the 5 pre-existing values `content_item_archived`, `lesson_block_replaced`, `replaced_by_author`, `manual_archive`, `orphaned_pending_expired`). Standing rule candidate (§115): tier-archive cascades must whitelist the tier's `archive_reason` in the CHECK before the cascade trigger reaches the row. (3) **Bug 3 SHIPPED — My Learning blank cert-path tile (Symptom 1 RPC + Symptom 2 RLS gap).** User reported a cert-path tile on My Learning rendering with no name, no description, orange-circle placeholder thumbnail. Two independent defects under one symptom. **Symptom 1 — `get_user_learning_state` RPC was not projecting cert-path tier's `name`/`description`/`thumbnail_asset_id` as flat columns.** First attempt fully rewrote the function and accidentally introduced a `GROUP BY` bug. Caught during verification (no production traffic hit it). Recovered via `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations` lookup of the Group Z m4 known-good source (version `20260516010830`), then applied a surgical edit. Three migrations: `session85_get_user_learning_state_project_flat_tile_fields` (BROKEN attempt), `session85_revert_get_user_learning_state_to_z_m4` (rollback to last-known-good), `session85_get_user_learning_state_add_flat_tile_fields` (surgical fix — LEFT JOIN to `certification_paths` in v_assignments CTE, flat name/description/thumbnail in v_assignment_rows via `COALESCE(cp_*, curriculum_*)`, same flat fields in v_module_assignment_rows). Lesson locked: surgical edits to complex RPCs with diff against live function definition; no full rewrites from memory (process discipline, not a §-numbered rule). **Symptom 2 — trainee tier-thumbnail RLS gap.** After Symptom 1 shipped, tile still rendered the placeholder. Root cause: `content_assets_trainee_read` RLS policy anchored its EXISTS chain on `content_items`. Tier thumbnails (cert_path, curriculum, module, resource) have `content_item_id IS NULL`. EXISTS returned false; trainees could not read thumbnail asset metadata even though bytes are public-tier per §84. Two migrations: `session85_align_content_asset_refs_trainee_read_status_filter` (`status = 'active'` → `IN ('active', 'completed')`) and `session85_create_get_thumbnail_urls_for_entities_rpc` (new SECDEF RPC `get_thumbnail_urls_for_entities(p_entity_type text, p_entity_ids uuid[])` returning `[{entity_id, asset_id, bucket, path, dominant_color}]`; branches per entity_type — cert_path/curriculum/module require `is_published = true AND archived_at IS NULL` and accept any authenticated user; resource branch mirrors `get_user_resources` is_visible rules with super_admin bypass). Frontend shipped via Lovable: new `resolveTierThumbnailUrls`/`resolveTierThumbnailRows` helpers in `src/lib/assetUrls.ts`, called from 5 sites — `MyLearningTab.tsx`, `ResourceGridTab.tsx`, `CertPathDetail.tsx`, `CurriculumDetail.tsx`, `ModuleDetail.tsx`. Standing rule candidate (§116): thumbnails are public-tier per §84; RLS on metadata when bytes are world-readable is the wrong protection model; SECDEF RPC enforcing "entity published AND not archived" at the API layer is the correct architecture. (4) **Bug 4 SHIPPED — coach can't see client results (Fix A + Fix B-Level-2 + Fix B-Level-3).** Cheryl Kish (`cf3ccc21-...`) could not load Edgar Vazquez Encarnacion's (`db586ec5-...`) PTP results — page flashed empty. Root cause: `src/pages/MyResults.tsx` line 437 queried `coach_clients_client_view`, which filters `WHERE client_user_id = auth.uid()` — correct for client callers, wrong for coach callers. Three related fixes shipped in one Lovable PR: **Fix A** — `MyResults.tsx` line 437: `.from("coach_clients_client_view")` → `.from("coach_clients")`; base table's RLS handles coach access correctly. **Fix B-Level-2** — `src/pages/coach/ClientResults.tsx` Level 2 `AssessmentList` data fetch: extended linked-id union to include `paired_assessment_id` so paired PTP sibling rows surface in the tile list. **Fix B-Level-3** — `CoachResultsView` permission resolver: `.eq("assessment_id", X)` → `.or(\`assessment_id.eq.${X},paired_assessment_id.eq.${X}\`)`; clicking a paired/personal tile no longer degrades to `score_summary`. Cheryl→Edgar verified end-to-end via impersonation. Edgar's `coach_clients` row: id `5ade7a72-3ce3-4bd5-a819-a04fdb07cf72`, `assessment_id = f0be3170-59d0-4aa2-8bd8-f9911bb35a16` (professional), `paired_assessment_id = 02079f1b-b119-4e73-a581-a678d1cdda38` (personal), `context_progress = 'both_done'`. Standing rule candidate (§117): `coach_clients_client_view` is for client callers exclusively; coach callers query the base table. (5) **Bug 5 SHIPPED — PTP paired-assessment UX consistency (Option C2).** Polish issue surfaced after Bug 4: Cheryl saw two visually identical "Personal Threat Profile / 5/14/2026" tiles; Edgar's own dropdown showed two entries "PTP Professional" / "PTP Personal". Same underlying data, inconsistent UX shapes across coach and client surfaces. Decision locked — **Option C2**: paired PTP collapses to ONE user-visible entity on BOTH surfaces. Coach tile labeled "Personal Threat Profile / Professional + Personal · {date}". Client dropdown shows ONE umbrella entry; if only paired PTP and no other instruments, dropdown hides entirely (the in-PTP tabs ARE the navigation). Lovable diagnose-then-implement cycle, two file changes. **`ClientResults.tsx` Level 2** — extended `AssessmentInfo` with `context_type`, `paired_assessment_id`, `instrument_id`; added `assessments` lookup for context info; grouping pass with mutually-paired check (prevents retake collapse); tile subtitle renders "Professional + Personal · {date}" for paired and "{Context} · {date}" for single; click handler always sends professional `assessment_id`. **`MyResults.tsx` dropdown** — extended fetch with `paired_assessment_id`; built `dropdownEntries` memo collapsing pairs; visibility gate switched from `assessments.length > 1` to `dropdownEntries.length > 1`; selected paired entry uses professional `result.id`. Did NOT touch `effectiveSelected`, PTP tabs, Combined synthesis, narrative providers, PDF export, narrative status polling, `ptpContextTab` initialization, AI chat session anchoring, or the "Complete other half" prompt. Mutually-paired check (each side's `paired_assessment_id` mirrors the other) protects against retake collapse — two professional retakes can never collapse. Standing rule candidate (§118): paired-PTP collapse uses mutually-paired check, not instrument equality. (6) **Cohorts FORMALLY DEFERRED INDEFINITELY.** Q11 scoped cohorts as schema-seam only — `cohorts` and `cohort_members` tables exist for forward compatibility; v1 UI was always a non-goal. Session 83 (`v91`) identified them as "NOT owed for Group C"; Sessions 84 and 85 carried "formally confirm cohorts are not needed and record it" as priority 1. Session 85 formally confirms: cohorts are deferred indefinitely. Group C is closeable without them. Re-opening requires a deliberate product decision, not implicit creep at Group C closeout. (7) **Module reorder gap DISCOVERED + scoped + deferred to its own session.** Backend RPC asymmetry surfaced during Session 85 recon: `reorder_content_items(p_module_id, p_ordered_item_ids[])` exists at the content-items tier, but no `reorder_curriculum_modules(p_curriculum_id, p_ordered_module_ids[])` and no `reorder_certification_path_curricula(p_cert_path_id, p_ordered_curriculum_ids[])`. Frontend: `CurriculumEditor.tsx` (~lines 40-110) renders the attached modules list with `display_order` as read-only text — no drag handle, no dnd-kit wiring. Same gap in `CertPathEditor.tsx` for attached curricula. `ModuleEditor.tsx` may also lack dnd-kit wiring on content items even though the backend RPC exists; needs verification. Scope approved — complete the pattern: build the two missing RPCs (same shape as the existing one, SECURITY DEFINER, super_admin gate), wire dnd-kit drag-and-drop in three editors. Estimated ~30 min backend + one full Lovable cycle on the three editor files. Deferred to its own session — non-trivial enough to deserve clean context, not tacked onto bug cluster work. (8) **RPC wrapper shape discipline (§111 reinforce).** Session 85 verified the discipline held across all backend work — every RPC returning a wrapper object had a proper TypeScript interface at the call site, never cast wrapper-to-array via `as unknown as`. Standing rule from Session 84 (§111) reinforced as operational. (9) **Surgical edit process discipline locked (candidate standing rule, not §-numbered).** When a non-trivial RPC needs a small change, the correct approach is a diff against the live function definition (read via `pg_get_functiondef` or migration history) and a surgical edit. Full rewrites from memory introduce regression risk. Session 85 hit this on `get_user_learning_state` and recovered cleanly via the migration history lookup. Discipline: show diff against current restored function before applying; never rewrite from memory. (10) **Pattern: production bug clusters batch effectively into one session.** Five bug clusters (Bugs 1-5) shipped sequentially in Session 85, each with its own diagnose-then-implement cycle through Lovable. The session was high-bandwidth because each bug had clear ownership (frontend-only vs migration-only vs both), independent verification paths, and no shared regression risk. Pattern reinforced: when production bugs queue up, batch them deliberately rather than interleaving with feature work. (11) **No Edge Functions deployed via MCP this session.** All backend work was migrations + one new SECDEF RPC. Edge Function GitHub-sync carryover per §92 unchanged from Session 84: `compute-dominant-color` v2, `finalize-asset-upload` v4, `stripe-webhook` v27, `identity-mutation` (deployed direct, never committed — Session 81 carryover), and `content-item-file-upload` v2 (Session 83 carryover). (12) **Carried forward unchanged to Session 86:** (1) notifications Phase 3 closeout — `results_available` firing-point wiring with paths A and B documented in Session 84 transcript but not yet executed; (2) Phase 10 polish pass; (3) Group C closeout after (1) and (2). Also still carried: AIRSA facet-interpretation generation gap (Session 84 deferred); messaging subsystem (prerequisite for `coach_messages` notification type); module reorder gap (Session 85 discovery — scope approved); MFA trusted-device feature (Session 84 logged); editor thumbnail-loss-on-republish hardening (Session 84 logged); coach-paid invitation email verification (Session 82 carryover); `create-checkout` graceful-degradation hardening (~60-day comp coupon recurrence); AIRSA Phases 3e-8; SOC 2 written policies; Action-Oriented Voice Redesign across six surfaces; pricing-reads refactor; corporate contract renewal schema change; Clarity Engine; Session 71 anon EXECUTE audit on 95 SECDEF functions; post-launch `coach_clients_client_view` → SECDEF RPC refactor. (13) Session 85 close artifacts: this v93 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v89 (§114 cert-status UI gate distinction; §115 tier-archive cascade reason whitelist; §116 thumbnails public-tier RPC pattern; §117 client-view-vs-base-table caller discipline; §118 paired-PTP collapse mutually-paired check; surgical-edit process discipline), session-85-to-86.md handoff. Markdown only — Session-74 decision. Cole uploads all three manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root); GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY. **Session 86 opens on: (1) notifications Phase 3 closeout — recover the `results_available` firing-point notes from Session 84 transcript and execute, then flip `is_v1_active = true`; (2) Phase 10 polish pass; (3) Group C closeout. After those three, Group C is closed.***

*v92 - Session 84 CLOSE — coach entitlement bugs (Cheryl phantom-credit + Patrice unreachable cert-pool gate) SHIPPED end-to-end; hero banner thumbnail redesign SHIPPED end-to-end with a CSS bug surfaced and fixed post-merge; MFA trusted-device feature LOGGED to queue; editor thumbnail-loss hardening LOGGED to queue. Backend-first throughout. Five backend migrations applied + verified, two Edge Functions deployed (`finalize-asset-upload` v4 + new `compute-dominant-color` v2), one Lovable PR for the coach entitlement frontend, one Lovable PR for the hero rewrite frontend, one Lovable hotfix PR for the CSS bug. Carried Session 83 priorities (notifications Phase 3, Phase 10 polish, Group C closeable) NOT advanced — Session 84 was a one-off fix session; they carry forward to Session 85 unchanged. (1) **Coach entitlement bugs SHIPPED + verified — Cheryl phantom-credit + Patrice unreachable cert-pool gate.** Two real bugs surfaced from production-account audits. **Bug A — Cheryl Kish phantom-credit:** Cheryl had `assessment_purchases` rows from PURCHASES she had made FOR clients via the coach-clients page; `InstrumentSelection.tsx:101` and `consume_assessment_purchase` RPC both read `user_id = me` with NO `coach_client_id` filter, so the rows she'd bought for clients were treated as her own free PTP credit. **Bug B — Patrice Love unreachable cert pool:** Patrice was certified and held a cert-pool credit (`coach_certifications.free_assessment_uses`), but no frontend or backend gate consulted that field — only the `assessment_purchases` table was checked. **Bug C — trigger over-decrement (found during fix design):** `link_assessment_to_coach_client` trigger branch (b) decremented `free_assessment_uses` on ANY self-completion, including paid purchases. (2) **Five backend migrations for coach entitlements + Edge Function update:** (a) `add_coach_client_id_to_assessment_purchases` — added `assessment_purchases.coach_client_id uuid` (nullable FK, indexed); backfilled 8 coach-bought rows. (b) `add_entitlement_source_to_assessments` — added `assessments.entitlement_source text` CHECK in (`free_cert_pool`, `paid_purchase`, `coach_paid_client`, `self_pay_coach_invite`). (c) `consume_assessment_purchase_filter_coach_client_id` — added `AND coach_client_id IS NULL` to all 5 SELECT blocks. (d) `link_assessment_to_coach_client_gate_cert_pool_decrement` — gated trigger branch (b) by `NEW.entitlement_source = 'free_cert_pool'` so paid completions never burn cert-pool credit. (e) `stripe-webhook` v27 — coach_order + coach_bulk_order branches now write `coach_client_id` to assessment_purchases. (3) **Cheryl back-seed.** `free_assessment_uses = '{"INST-001": 6}'::jsonb`, expiry `2026-09-17 12:55:11 UTC` (~120 days), status `certified` (unchanged), cert id `4fc432b9-a10c-4e1f-8a81-60221f013e5f`. Matches Patrice's pool shape; restores the 6 PTP credits Cheryl had legitimately accrued through the cert program. (4) **Frontend SHIPPED via Lovable PR for coach entitlements.** `assetUrls.ts` Step 6 (`.is("coach_client_id", null)` on InstrumentSelection purchases query); Step 7 (cert_certifications query added to `Promise.all`; eligibility matrix mirrors backend trigger); Step 8 (cert-pool branch at InstrumentSelection.tsx:516 between purchaseAccess and actorDebrief, label "Start Assessment (Coach Cert)" or "Continue Assessment"); Step 9 (13 handleSelect call sites tagged with correct `entitlementSource` — all 13 verified); Assessment.tsx (state slot, prop passthrough, onExit cleanup, autostart-from-Stripe tagged `paid_purchase` — bonus catch by Lovable); AssessmentFlow.tsx (stamps `entitlement_source` via fire-and-forget UPDATE with atomic `.is("entitlement_source", null)` guard in both Phase 1 init and `handleAcknowledgmentConfirm`; both write sites guard against EPN/manager paths). (5) **Hero banner thumbnail redesign SHIPPED end-to-end with CSS bug fix.** User reported hero banners on CertPath/Curriculum/Module detail pages were stretching small-square thumbnails with `bg-cover bg-center` on the wide 180/240/320px hero, producing distorted images. **Design locked:** solid background = dominant color extracted from thumbnail at upload time, with the orange BrainWise brain glyph (`/brain-icon.png`, verified 512×512 RGBA with ~80% transparent background) in the upper-right; diagonal navy gradient as fallback when no dominant color is set. (6) **Backend for hero redesign — three migrations + new Edge Function + backfill.** (a) `add_dominant_color_to_content_assets` — `content_assets.dominant_color text NULL` + comment. (b) New shared helper `/_shared_dominant_color.ts` using `jsr:@unpic/pixels@1.2.3` (pure-JS, no native deps — Deno/Supabase Edge Runtime reliable JPEG/PNG decoder). (c) New Edge Function `compute-dominant-color` v2 (id `08e5347c-7f4a-42d5-8e24-9a5766d7fdc3`, `verify_jwt:false`, Class B `X-Internal-Secret` auth). v1→v2: switched from public render URL to service-role `storage.download({transform:{width:16,height:16,resize:"cover"}})` for private bucket support. Idempotent (skips if `dominant_color` already set). Non-fatal failures return 200 with `{success:false, reason}` so a thumbnail upload never blocks on color extraction. (d) `finalize-asset-upload` v4 — fire-and-forget `compute-dominant-color` invocation on image uploads, catch-wrapped. (e) Backfill: 30 of 31 image assets populated. The one remaining NULL is orphan library asset `0400f749-30dd-4598-8e26-96150404e41f` (not referenced as thumbnail anywhere — no action needed). Examples: Cheryl's cert path thumbnail `2ca07d8b-ce7f-452d-9556-76df9936e0ff` = `#152c48` (navy); Foundations curriculum `#481a69` (purple); Debrief Practice curriculum `#4a1a76` (purple); all 19 PTP modules picked up appropriate colors. (7) **Frontend SHIPPED via Lovable PR for hero rewrite.** `src/lib/assetUrls.ts` exported new `ThumbnailMeta = { url: string; dominantColor: string | null }`, changed `resolveThumbnailUrls` return to `Promise<Map<string, ThumbnailMeta>>`, added `dominant_color` to the embedded `content_asset_versions` select with defensive `Array.isArray` unwrap. `src/hooks/useAssetResolver.ts` extracts `.url` when building the Record (public hook shape preserved as URL map). All 7 `.get()` call sites appended `?.url ?? null`: `MyLearningTab.tsx:117`, `ResourceGridTab.tsx:163`, `CertPathDetail.tsx:177+413`, `CurriculumDetail.tsx:150+341`, `ModuleDetail.tsx:174`. Three hero rewrites in `CertPathDetail.tsx`/`CurriculumDetail.tsx`/`ModuleDetail.tsx`: brain-icon `<img>` rendered with `pointer-events-none` + `top-4 right-4 h-12 w-12 md:h-14 md:w-14 opacity-90`; dropped `bg-cover bg-center`; `heroBackground` composed from `heroOverlay + dominantColor` (or `heroOverlay + heroFallback` diagonal navy gradient). (8) **CSS bug surfaced post-merge — bare hex colors are NOT valid in `background-image`.** User reported every hero rendered navy regardless of the extracted dominant color. Diagnosis: bare hex colors like `#481a69` are NOT valid values for CSS `background-image` — only `<url>` and `<gradient>` are accepted. The invalid declaration was being ignored entirely, leaving the transparent overlay gradient to show the underlying page navy background through it. **Fix shipped via Lovable hotfix PR:** split the composed value across two CSS properties — `backgroundImage` (gradient layers only) and `backgroundColor` (solid hex or `transparent`). `const heroBackgroundImage = heroMeta?.dominantColor ? heroOverlay : \`${heroOverlay}, ${heroFallback}\`; const heroBackgroundColor = heroMeta?.dominantColor ?? "transparent";` Style prop now passes both. User confirmed fix shipped and working — heroes render in their dominant color across all detail pages. (9) **MFA trusted-device feature LOGGED — not built this session.** User asked about extending 5-minute MFA freshness gate on impersonation. Investigation: `check_mfa_freshness` exists but only `impersonation-start` calls it (default 300s window); no other RPCs are gated. Build queue item: **Trusted Device feature for super-admin MFA step-up.** Build `trusted_devices` table (hashed token, fingerprint, label, expiry, revoked_at), `mint_trusted_device`/`check_trusted_device`/`revoke_trusted_device` RPCs, edit `impersonation-start` to read device cookie and bypass `check_mfa_freshness` on hit, "Manage trusted devices" settings page, auto-revoke on password/MFA changes, audit rows in `super_admin_audit_log`. Scope-adjacent: also cover `org_deactivation`, `user_pseudonymization`, other sensitive admin RPCs currently with no step-up. SOC 2 supporting feature. Estimated 1.5 sessions backend + 0.5 session frontend. (10) **Editor thumbnail-loss-on-republish hardening LOGGED — not built this session.** User reported thumbnail "disappeared" on a cert path. Audit log analysis (`super_admin_audit_log` action_type=`certification_path_updated`) reconstructed: May 19 17:02:17 — upload `7a4f6a68-...` succeeded; May 19 17:02:56 (+39s) — `publishing` action wrote `thumbnail_asset_id=null` (`before_value` had asset, `after_value` was null); the `_archive_thumbnail_ref_and_maybe_asset` cascade correctly archived the orphan asset. **Conclusion: cascade-archive system working correctly.** Bug is editor-side: `CertPathEditor` (and CurriculumEditor / ModuleEditor / ContentItemEditor / ResourceEditor — all share the FileUploadField + state + upsert pattern) loses local thumbnail state between upload and save/publish. Build queue item: **Editor thumbnail-loss-on-republish hardening.** Options: (a) On save, if `thumbnailAssetId` is null AND `initial?.thumbnail_asset_id` was not null, show confirmation dialog. (b) Refetch the row fresh before submitting, merge any field where the form would null out a previously-populated value. Investigate whether the editor's `initial` data refetches after upload-finalize Edge Function returns. (11) **Pattern reinforced — fast diagnostic path for "feature broken" reports.** When a feature renders wrong despite the data being correct in the DB, the standing diagnostic sequence is: (a) verify the data IS correct in the DB (`execute_sql`); (b) simulate the exact query the frontend runs against the deployed REST endpoint with a real auth token and inspect the response shape; (c) if response shape is correct, inspect the rendering layer for the bug. The Session 84 CSS bug was found at step (c) — backend was correct (verified Cheryl's cert path = `#152c48` in the DB, the frontend query returned the right shape, the `heroBackground` string was built correctly) but the resulting CSS string was invalid by spec. Diagnosis to fix was under 10 minutes once the sequence ran cleanly. (12) **Carried forward unchanged from Session 83 — Session 84 was a one-off fix session, priorities do not shift:** (1) formally confirm cohorts are not needed and record it; (2) notifications (Phase 3 + the Phase 10 notification UI surfaces — bell, dropdown, `/notifications`, `/settings/notifications`), backend-first; (3) deliberate Phase 10 polish pass on the new Group C screens. After those three, Group C is closeable. Also still carried: coach-paid invitation email verification (Session 82 carryover), `create-checkout` graceful-degradation hardening (~60-day comp coupon recurrence, Session 82 HIGH), AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine. (13) **Edge Function GitHub-sync carryover per §92 unchanged from Session 83 plus this session's three:** `compute-dominant-color` v2 NEW, `finalize-asset-upload` v4 UPDATED, `stripe-webhook` v27 UPDATED. (14) Session 84 close artifacts: this v92 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v88 (§110 entitlement_source pattern; §111 coach_client_id discriminator on assessment_purchases; §112 dominant_color pipeline + private-bucket service-role storage transform; §113 CSS `background-image` accepts only `<url>` and `<gradient>` — split-property pattern for gradient-over-solid-color), session-84-to-85.md handoff. Markdown only — Session-74 decision. Cole uploads all three manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root); GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY. **Session 85 opens on the unchanged Session 83 priorities: (1) confirm cohorts not needed and record; (2) notifications Phase 3 + Phase 10 UI surfaces, backend-first; (3) Phase 10 polish pass. After those three, Group C is closeable.***

*v91 - Session 83 CLOSE — super-admin manual completion control SHIPPED end-to-end; reverse-recount bug found during testing and FIXED; one Edge Function deployed to v2; full security-scan triage pass completed. Backend-first throughout. Five backend migrations applied + verified, one Edge Function v1->v2 deploy, one multi-part Lovable frontend feature, one Lovable frontend follow-up, nine security-scanner findings dispositioned. Priorities 2-6 not reached — the session budget went entirely to Priority 1, the bug it surfaced, and the security pass; they carry to Session 84. (1) **Super-admin manual completion control SHIPPED (Priority 1, carried from Session 82).** A super admin can mark any learning entity complete OR incomplete across four tiers — content item, module, curriculum, cert path — from the UI. An existing forward-only rollup trigger chain cascades completions upward; this feature adds the deliberate-override path. **Four design decisions locked before code:** (a) reverse cascade stops at the curriculum tier — a reverse that would drop a curriculum under an already-certified cert path is REFUSED entirely (`manual_incomplete_blocked_certified_cert_path`); (b) combined-cert cascade is out of scope by construction since reverse never reaches `coach_certifications`; (c) forward marks the full subtree complete, reverse is target-plus-ancestor recompute and never touches children; (d) forward CAN certify via rollup, reverse can NEVER un-certify — the cert tier reuses the existing `grant_certification`/`revoke_certification` RPCs. (2) **Migration 1 `manual_completion_control_action_types`** — three audit `action_type` rows in `super_admin_action_types`, per §99, INSERTed before the RPCs that emit them. (3) **Migration 2 `manual_completion_control_rpcs`** — three public RPCs (`set_content_item_completion(p_user_id, p_content_item_id, p_complete, p_reason)`, `set_module_completion(p_user_id, p_module_id, p_complete, p_reason)`, `set_curriculum_completion(p_assignment_id, p_complete, p_reason)`) plus three internal helpers. All SECURITY DEFINER, search_path locked, `assert_super_admin` + `assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change')` + reason >=10 chars, idempotent, audit-logged. The curriculum RPC is keyed per-assignment (`p_assignment_id`), not per-curriculum, because a curriculum can be assigned more than once. (4) **Migration 3 `manual_completion_helpers_revoke_authenticated`** — the three internal helpers made service_role-only (REVOKE from authenticated), defense-in-depth so only the public RPCs are the reachable surface. (5) **Migration 4 `manual_completion_reverse_recount_fix` — the reverse-recount bug fix (see item 7).** (6) **Migration 5 `storage_policies_content_and_skills_buckets` — the security-pass storage migration (see item 9).** (7) **BUG FOUND during testing + FIXED + verified — reverse-recount.** Testing the new feature on Test Coach 4 surfaced a real bug: the `set_module_completion` and `set_curriculum_completion` reverse paths hard-wrote a downgraded status (`not_started`/`active`) instead of recomputing the tier from its children, producing incoherent states (a `not_started` module sitting above six completed required items). **Decision locked — Option A:** module and curriculum status must ALWAYS be recomputed honestly from children, never hard-written; you change a parent\'s status by changing its children, not by overriding it. Migration `manual_completion_reverse_recount_fix` rewrote both reverse branches to recount inline; "mark incomplete" on a tier whose children are all complete is now a no-op returning `changed:false` plus a `note`. Test Coach 4\'s two corrupted rows were repaired to `completed`. The bug got through the original Migration 2 verification because the test matrix tested reverse-from-below but never reverse-at-the-tier — a test-matrix hole, now closed. (8) **Frontend SHIPPED via Lovable — the Completion Control tab + a follow-up.** A fourth "Completion Control" tab on `LearningAdmin.tsx`; a SEPARATE admin learning-tree component (NOT an edit of the shared `MentorProgressTree.tsx` — ~150 lines of duplication accepted to keep the mentor-portal blast radius at zero); an artifact inspector showing each of the eight content-item types\' real submission per a data-source table; a confirm dialog; impersonation-aware disabling of mark actions. New files under `src/components/learning-admin/`. The three `set_*` RPCs are called with an `as never` cast (not yet in generated `types.ts`). **Follow-up prompt:** `CompletionConfirmDialog` was discarding the RPC result and always showing "Marked complete/incomplete" — it now captures `data` and branches the success toast on `data?.changed === false` to a neutral "No change" toast carrying the backend `note`; the cert-path branch uses a strict `=== false` check and is unchanged. (9) **Edge Function `content-item-file-upload` v1->v2 deployed (verify_jwt:false preserved).** The `read` action now accepts an OPTIONAL `target_user_id`; when present and not equal to the caller it is passed to `get_content_item_for_viewer`, which gates on super_admin/mentor; absent, the function is byte-identical to v1. Reason: a super admin needed to view another learner\'s uploaded file in the new artifact inspector — v1 was hardwired to the caller\'s id. Verified by logic + probe (anon -> 401, super-admin resolves another user\'s item, non-privileged caller rejected). NOT yet verified by a live signed-URL browser round-trip — carried. CARRYOVER: v2 source owed to GitHub `cbastianBWE/brainwise-blueprint` at `supabase/functions/content-item-file-upload/index.ts`. (10) **Security-scan triage pass — three errors + six warnings dispositioned.** The Lovable scanner (flagged "Out of date") fired; Lovable did an independent diagnose-only pass; Claude verified every claim against the live DB; the two sides agreed a fix split — Claude does backend, Lovable does frontend. **E1/E3 (storage buckets with no policies) — REAL defense-in-depth gap, FIXED this pass.** `content-item-file-uploads` and `skills-practice-attachments` had zero `storage.objects` RLS policies. Not exploitable (RLS-on + no policy = default deny held; the Edge Functions use service-role and bypass storage RLS anyway), but a missing floor inconsistent with every other content bucket. Migration `storage_policies_content_and_skills_buckets` added eight path-segment ownership policies (four per bucket). Path layouts verified against live objects: content bucket `{contentItemId}/{userId}/{file}` -> user is segment 2; skills bucket `{contentItemId}/{role}/{userId}/{file}` -> user is segment 3, role is segment 2. Skills SELECT keys on segment 3 (a trainee may see both their own `trainee/` file and the `mentor/` feedback file under their own id — already an entitlement); skills write policies additionally require segment 2 = `trainee` so a trainee cannot write the mentor path. Behaviorally verified. Path-segment match was chosen over the scanner-suggested table join — simpler, no per-access subquery, consistent with the existing `departure-exports` policies. **E2 (quiz answers readable) — NOT a bug.** `quiz_answer_options` has only service_role + super_admin policies, no authenticated/public SELECT; `is_correct` is locked; the trainee path is `get_quiz_for_trainee`, which strips it. The scanner asked us to confirm a negative; confirmed. Mark fixed in scanner. **W1/W2/W3 (three XSS) — REAL, moderate severity (writers are super-admin-only), FIXED by Lovable.** `isSafeHttpUrl` in `src/lib/safeUrl.ts` is now the single allowlist used by all three href sites (`ExternalLinkViewer.tsx`, `ResourceReader.tsx` VideoEmbed, `BlockRenderer.tsx` button-block — the third found by Lovable\'s own audit) and both TipTap `Link.configure` sites; resource articles sanitized via DOMPurify with `USE_PROFILES:{html:true}`. **W4/W5/W6 (cohort/bulk-batch SELECT) — design-intentional/deferred per the Session 73 dispositions; no work.** PENDING for Cole: mark E2/W4/W5/W6 ignored in the scanner with rationale, then re-run the scan to confirm it clears. (11) **Q13 post-certification benefit hook — VERIFIED satisfied for v1.** `apply_post_certification_benefits(uuid)` exists as the v1 no-op placeholder the Group C scope (Q13) specifies — stamps `post_certification_benefit_applied_at`, idempotent, carries the marked TODO and the documented future fan-out, returns `hook_version:v1_noop`. `grant_certification` invokes it and logs the audit row. No Group C work owed here; the full benefit build stays deferred until subscription tiers are decided. (12) **Group C completion status mapped this session (against the 10-phase scope).** **Cohorts are NOT owed** — Q11 scoped cohorts as schema-seam-only (`cohorts`/`cohort_members` tables exist, which is what W5/W6 referred to); cohort UI was always a v2 non-goal. Phases 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 are done across Sessions 72-83. **Phase 3 Notifications is NOT done** — no read surface (bell, panel, `/notifications`, `/settings/notifications`). **Phase 10 Polish is NOT cleanly closed** — most cross-cutting items were absorbed per group as it shipped, but no deliberate Phase 10 pass has been done. To finish Group C: notifications then a Phase 10 polish pass. Caveat: phase status was read off build-queue summaries, not re-verified live. (13) **Thumbnail sizing answered.** One spec for all five entity types (cert paths / curricula / modules / content items / resources) because all flow through the single `Tile.tsx` primitive (§85): **1280x720, 16:9, JPG or WebP, subject centered with edge margin.** Each thumbnail renders two ways — as a Tile (`aspect-video` + `object-cover`) and as a detail-page hero (`bg-cover bg-center` banner, 320px tall desktop, full content width, dark gradient overlay). Both crop, so keep content centered and nothing important near top/bottom edges. 1280x720 is crisp at every tile size and at 2x on the hero, ~100-250KB at JPG q80. Content items and resources render tile-only; resources have no image hero. (14) **Deferred Build Queue items added Session 83:** (a) **Option B reverse-cascade-down** — marking a module/curriculum incomplete and optionally cascading the downgrade DOWN to children; Session 83 shipped only target-plus-ancestor recompute (Option A); the status-only-vs-full-wipe question for the child downgrade is still open. (b) **`revoke_certification` combined-cert audit** — confirm that revoking one component certification correctly handles the auto-derived Combined Certification; out of scope for the manual-completion feature by construction but worth an explicit check. (15) NO movement Session 83 on: notifications (Phase 3 — Session 84 priority), Phase 10 polish, coach-paid invitation email verification (carried Session 82 item, not reached), `create-checkout` graceful-degradation hardening (carried HIGH, not reached, still recurs ~60 days from the Session 82 comp-coupon recreation), AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine. Edge Function GitHub-sync carryover per §92 unchanged from Session 82 plus the new `content-item-file-upload` v2. (16) Session 83 close artifacts: this v91 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v87 (§108 private-content-bucket storage-policy floor; §109 derived-parent-status-is-recomputed-not-hard-written), session-83-to-84.md handoff. Markdown only — Session-74 decision. Cole uploads all three manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root); GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY. **Session 84 opens on: (1) formally confirming cohorts are not needed and recording it, (2) notifications — Phase 3 + the Phase 10 notification UI surfaces, backend-first, (3) a deliberate Phase 10 polish pass. After those three, Group C is closeable.***

*v90 - Session 82 CLOSE — Mentor-role feature SHIPPED end-to-end; actor-flow + coach-invitation email bugs FIXED. Backend-first throughout. Two backend migrations for mentor-role, two backend migrations for actor flows, one Edge Function path change verified, two Lovable prompts written for Cole to run (one mentor-role frontend, one combined actor-debrief + self-pay email frontend). Priority 2 (super-admin manual completion control) was NOT reached — the session budget went to Priority 1 plus actor-flow firefighting; it carries to Session 83 as priority 1. Priority 4 (notifications) did not start. (1) **Mentor-role model decision LOCKED — Model 1, standalone eligibility flag.** There was no standalone "mentor role" before this session — being a mentor was emergent from a `coach_mentor_assignments` row. Two models were presented: Model 1, a standalone `users.is_mentor` eligibility flag (schema change); Model 2, a cleaner UI over the existing `coach_mentor_assignments` flow (no schema change). Cole chose Model 1. Rationale: without an eligibility flag there is no clean way to gate the `/mentor` route or to enforce that only approved users can be assigned as mentors. This refines — does not contradict — the v84 "mentor is a relationship row" framing: the relationship rows still define WHICH trainees a mentor mentors; the new flag defines WHETHER a user is allowed to be a mentor at all. (2) **`list_mentor_trainees` confirmed leak-safe.** Read via `pg_get_functiondef` (it was NOT read in Session 81 — flagged carryover). Confirmed: a non-mentor caller gets `viewer_role:"none"` and an empty roster; no other trainee's data is exposed. No fix needed. (3) **Migration 1 — `users.is_mentor` column + helper, applied + verified.** New `is_mentor` boolean column, NOT NULL, default false. Backfilled `true` for the one existing approved mentor (Test Coach 1) and false for everyone else. A small read-helper added with clean grants (no anon/PUBLIC). Verified column, index, backfill scope, helper grants, and gate via separate `execute_sql`; no function overload created. (4) **Migration 2 — `set_mentor_role` RPC + `mentor_role_changed` audit action_type, applied + verified.** Per §99 the new action_type is INSERTed in the SAME migration as the RPC that emits it, or `log_super_admin_action` FK-fails. `set_mentor_role(p_user_id uuid, p_is_mentor boolean, p_reason text)` — one RPC handles BOTH grant and revoke via the boolean param; SECURITY DEFINER, `assert_super_admin` + `assert_impersonation_allows('permission_change')` + reason ≥10 chars; logs a single `mentor_role_changed` action with before/after JSON showing the transition; idempotent — a redundant call returns `changed:false` with no error. Returns jsonb `{user_id, is_mentor, changed}`. The `mentor_role_changed` action_type: category `admin_role_management`, tier2, requires MFA + justification, mutation, denylisted during impersonation — mirrors the `mentor_assigned` precedent. RPC grants restricted to `authenticated`/`service_role`, single signature. Positive end-to-end test: grant flipped the flag, the audit row landed (proving the §99 FK chain), the redundant-grant no-op returned `changed:false`, revoke restored a clean state — no residual test rows. (5) **`assign_mentor` now gates on `is_mentor`.** The existing `assign_mentor` RPC was updated so assigning a user as a mentor of a trainee requires that user to have `is_mentor = true`; an un-approved user surfaces the friendly `mentor_not_approved` error. (6) **Mentor-role Lovable prompt written (frontend-only, Cole runs it).** Six changes: surface `is_mentor` through `useUserProfile`; derive an `isMentor` boolean in `accountRoles.ts` (mirrors `isPractitionerCoach`); NEW `MentorGuard.tsx` (allows `isMentor || isSuperAdmin`); swap the two `/mentor` routes from `PractitionerCoachGuard` to `MentorGuard` in `App.tsx`; gate the "Mentor Portal" sidebar entry on real mentor status in `AppSidebar.tsx`; add an "Assign Mentor Role" tab to `LearningAdmin.tsx` (searchable user list, grant/revoke via `set_mentor_role` through a reason dialog). This FIXES the Session-81 logged BUG-HIGH "any coach can reach `/mentor`".
