(7) **BUG FIXED + verified — actor-debrief paywall.** A certification trainee who invites an "actor" (practice subject) to take the PTP — the actor reached the assessment page and was shown a payment dialog ("Your Coach Wants You to Take This"). An actor debrief is free, covered by the trainee's certification. Root cause: in `InstrumentSelection.tsx` the `selfPayCoachClientsRes` query selects `coach_clients` rows with `stripe_payment_intent_id IS NULL` and treats every such row as "client must self-pay" — but an actor-debrief invitation legitimately has a null payment intent (it never involves Stripe), so it was swept into `selfPayCoachInstrumentIds` and triggered the paywall. **`start_assessment` was read in full and has NO payment gate of its own** — the paywall is purely a frontend display decision; there is no backend security hole. Backend fix (applied + verified): `coach_clients_client_view` now exposes the `invitation_source` column via `security_invoker`; testactor3 reading their own row sees `invitation_source = 'actor_debrief'`, `stripe_payment_intent_id = NULL`. Frontend fix is a Lovable prompt routing `invitation_source = 'actor_debrief'` rows (UUID-keyed, matching the existing `selfPayCoachInstrumentIds` keying) to a free-access branch. (8) **BUG FIXED + verified — actor-debrief invitation email never sent.** `handleOrderActorDebrief` called the internal-secret-protected `send-email` Edge Function directly from the browser, which cannot supply `INTERNAL_FUNCTION_SECRET`. Fix (Option B, chosen over reproducing the email template as a PL/pgSQL literal): `create_actor_debrief_order` migrated to a 5-arg signature adding `p_email_html` (DEFAULT NULL, backward-safe) and now sends the email server-side via `net.http_post` to `send-email` with the Vault secret + `x-internal-secret` header, copying the `notify_user` pattern. The Vault secret was verified readable + non-empty first. The email send is wrapped in a `BEGIN...EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS` block so an email failure NEVER rolls back a valid order; outcome reported in the return JSON as `email_dispatched`. The old 4-arg overload was DROPPED so all calls (including the frontend's current 4-arg call) resolve to the 5-arg version. Functional test confirmed `email_dispatched:true` — the `net.http_post` fired with the internal secret. (9) **BUG FIXED + verified — self-paid coach invitation email never sent.** Same browser-cannot-call-`send-email` cause as (8), in `handleOrderClientPays`. Confirmed broken by Cole's test plus the absence of any `handleOrderClientPays` success row in `email_logs`. Fix: a NEW generic RPC `send_coach_invitation_email(p_to, p_subject, p_html, p_email_type)` does the server-side `net.http_post` with the Vault secret, role-gated to coach / practitioner-coach / super-admin (not a wide-open mailer); returns `{dispatched}`; does not raise on email failure. A combined Lovable prompt updates `handleOrderActorDebrief` (pass `p_email_html`, drop the `send-email` invoke) and `handleOrderClientPays` (call `send_coach_invitation_email` instead of `send-email`). (10) **Coach-paid invitation email — UNVERIFIED, carried.** Sent server-side from the `stripe-webhook` Edge Function, which is not in the repo and could not be read this session; testing it would require a live charge. It runs server-side so it likely does not have the (8)/(9) bug, but this is not confirmed. Carryover: read the `stripe-webhook` source next session and verify its email logic. (11) **`create-checkout` graceful-degradation hardening — known unfixed, recurs ~60 days.** `create-checkout` throws a 500 instead of degrading gracefully when a comp coupon goes bad (expires/archives/deletes). Works today — Cole recreated the comp coupon unlimited and tested coach-paid checkout end-to-end — but comp coupons expire by design (`redeem_by` 60 days out), so this exact failure recurs. Logged HIGH with a known recurrence date; carried to Session 83. (12) **`3 of 3 → 4 of 4` cap display fix** — Cole ran this Lovable prompt; counted done. (13) NO movement Session 82 on: super-admin manual completion control (Priority 2, NOT reached — Session 83 priority 1), notifications (Priority 4, NOT started), AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine. Carryover Edge Functions still owed to GitHub `cbastianBWE/brainwise-blueprint` per §92 unchanged from Session 81 (`identity-mutation`, `learning-admin-import` v1, `draft-text` v7, `get-content-item-video-url` v1, `content-item-ai-assist` v1, `skills-practice-attachment-upload` v1, `content-item-file-upload` v1, `get-lesson-block-asset-urls` v1, `create-checkout`/`customer-portal` drift). (14) Session 82 close artifacts: this v90 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v86 (§106 standalone-eligibility-flag pattern, §107 server-side email-sender RPC pattern), session-82-to-83.md handoff. Markdown only — Session-74 decision, no `.docx` from Claude beyond the project-knowledge copies. Cole uploads all three manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root); GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY. **Session 83 opens on super-admin manual completion control (the carried Priority 2 — mark any learning entity complete/incomplete, backend-first, reverse-cascade is the hard part), then coach-paid email verification, then `create-checkout` hardening, then notifications.***

*v89 - Session 81 CLOSE — Group V certification buildout reached partially; password reset production bug fixed; coach-enrollment RPC fixed (Issue 2); marquee verified end-to-end; certificate + marquee-overflow fixes prompt-ready. Session was Group V certification work plus heavy production-bug firefighting. Backend-first throughout; one backend migration, one Edge Function fix, no Lovable prompts shipped by Claude (Cole runs all Lovable prompts; GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY). (1) **Password reset production bug FIXED + verified live.** Real users clicking the reset link got "Auth session missing!". Root cause fully traced: the implicit auth flow puts recovery tokens in the URL hash; the `identity-mutation` Edge Function built a Supabase client with only an Authorization header — `getClaims` validates statelessly (works) but `updateUser` needs a real SESSION loaded via `setSession`, which was never called. Two-part fix: the frontend `src/lib/identityMutation.ts` now sends `access_token`+`refresh_token` in the request body, and the `identity-mutation` Edge Function calls `setSession()` before `updateUser`. The corrected Edge Function was deployed with the impersonation-gate code INLINED (no `_shared/` import — an earlier non-inlined version failed to deploy with a bundler "Module not found" error; §80 inline-divergence discipline). `src/pages/ResetPassword.tsx` was also hardened (three-state status, `onAuthStateChange` + `PASSWORD_RECOVERY` listener, `getSession` guards) — correct but not the root fix. Cole confirmed end-to-end in production. CARRYOVER: the `identity-mutation` Edge Function is NOT in the GitHub repo — deployed direct, never committed; needs a manual commit to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-blueprint` at `supabase/functions/identity-mutation/index.ts` using the inlined version. (2) **Issue 2 — coach-enrollment RPC FIXED + verified four ways (migration `enroll_path_attach_to_existing_cert_row`).** `enroll_user_in_certification_path` previously threw `user_already_has_active_certification_of_this_type` whenever a `coach_certifications` row of the same type already existed — but the coach-invite acceptance flow creates a bare cert row (no curriculum) on EVERY invite, by design, because `certification_type` drives assessment-ordering gating. That made it impossible to assign the cert path's curriculum via Learning Admin to any invited coach. The fix changes the idempotency guard from throw-always to **adopt-instead-of-throw**: if an `in_progress`/`certified` cert row of the type exists, ADOPT it (use its id, do not create a second row, do not modify it) and attach the cert path's published curricula to it, skipping any curriculum already actively assigned from `source='certification_path'` (dedup mirroring `assign_curriculum_directly` — there is no UNIQUE constraint on `user_curriculum_assignments` beyond the PK, so the dedup must be in code). The original error is preserved for the genuine-no-op case — an adopted row that already has every curriculum. Verified via impersonation-context tests as super admin: adopts an existing row (`cert_row_adopted:true`), attaches the curriculum, dedups on re-run, and still throws `user_already_has_active_certification_of_this_type` on a true duplicate. The coach-invite flow is UNTOUCHED — only the enrollment RPC changed; assessment-ordering gating is unaffected. (3) **Issue 1 — coach invite creates a bare cert row: CLOSED by decision, no fix.** The invite-acceptance flow creating a `coach_certifications` row is load-bearing — `certification_type` drives assessment-ordering gating. Fix B (decouple the invite from cert-row creation) was considered and REJECTED for exactly this reason. The bare cert row stays by design; the Issue 2 adopt-fix makes it workable. (4) **Issue 3 — "Enroll Now Pro" / PTP-Coach not showing as enroll-able on Browse & Enroll: CLOSED as working-as-intended, no fix.** Diagnosed to `list_available_learning`: it computes a cert path's `enrollment_status` by checking `coach_certifications` by `certification_type`, so any coach invited as `ptp_coach` already has an `in_progress` row → status `'enrolled'` not `'not_enrolled'` → the frontend `shouldShowEnrollButton` (in `src/components/resources/MyLearningTab.tsx`) correctly returns false. Cole's decision: this is CORRECT — coaches only get the `ptp_coach` cert row if they paid and were invited as a PTP coach, so showing them as already-enrolled on Browse & Enroll is right. No RPC change. (5) **Marquee test fixture BUILT, marquee VERIFIED end-to-end.** Migration `marquee_test_fixture_module_c_completions_v2` inserted 4 `content_item_completions` rows (status `completed`) for test coach `testclientbwe+testcoachcert@gmail.com` (UUID `b662077a-a293-442f-b6b2-929f2e1307a8`) — Test Video, Test Written Summary, Test External Link, Test Skills Practice — leaving Test File Upload Item (`18d03728-1186-4b97-a7c3-a030d6eb662d`) for UI completion. (First attempt failed: `skills_trainee_signed_off`/`skills_mentor_signed_off` are NOT NULL — fixed by passing `false` not NULL.) Cole completed Test File Upload in the UI; the certification marquee fired correctly end-to-end ("You're certified!" + certificate render). Note: the fixture rows were inserted via direct SQL, which fires the rollup trigger per-insert — the marquee fired correctly so the cascade works, but intermediate module/curriculum rollup-counter consistency was not separately verified (low risk, fixture-only). (6) **Certificate text positioning — diagnosed, fix prompt-ready, NOT shipped.** `src/components/certification/CertificateCanvas.tsx` draws the recipient name + date onto the template PNG. The recipient name is now correctly positioned. The remaining issue: "AWARDED ON" is BAKED INTO the template PNG and is off-center there (its center sits at fraction 0.4154 of image width, not 0.5); the code-drawn date currently tracks it at `cw * 0.415`. This cannot be fixed in code. Resolution chosen (Option A): Cole has a corrected template PNG with "AWARDED ON" re-centered to 0.5 — a Lovable prompt is ready that replaces `public/certificates/ptp-coach-certificate-template.png` with the new file (same path/name) and changes the date `fillText` x from `cw * 0.415` to `cw * 0.5` (y `ch * 0.689` unchanged). Caveat: the fix only fully works if the new PNG has "AWARDED ON" at exactly fraction 0.5. (7) **Marquee overflow — fix prompt-ready, Cole counts it fixed.** The certification-tier celebration dialog in `src/pages/learning/ContentItemViewer.tsx` was cut off at BOTH top and bottom — the dialog (orange header + full certificate canvas + action rows + footer) is taller than the viewport, and the shared `DialogContent` (`src/components/ui/dialog.tsx`) is vertically centered with no max-height, so it overflowed off both edges. Fix (scoped to the certification tier only, not the shared primitive): change the certification-tier `DialogContent` className from `"sm:max-w-3xl"` to `"sm:max-w-3xl max-h-[90vh] overflow-y-auto"`. Lovable prompt given; Cole said to count it fixed. (8) **Account states verified at close.** `testclientbwe+testcoachcert@gmail.com` (`b662077a-...`): cert row `588a4b8e-c6b3-4150-ad55-a97248ad291e` (`ptp_coach`, `in_progress`) + PTP VILT 1 curriculum assignment — COMPLETE, marquee tested on this account, no cleanup needed. `<redacted-email>` (`cf3ccc21-a7e4-4b1b-bd77-b06c8add736e`): production account, cert row `4fc432b9-...` (`ptp_coach`, **`certified`**), no curriculum assignments — she was granted the cert early for assessment-ordering and goes through the coursework later; the Issue 2 fix handles her safely (running her Learning Admin assignment will ADOPT her certified row, never modify it, and attach PTP curriculum). PENDING: Cole to run Cheryl's Learning Admin assignment to confirm Issue 2 from the UI. Test Coach Invite (`621fa236-...`): still has an orphan bare `ptp_coach` cert row, no curriculum — now workable via the Issue 2 fix. (9) **Mentor Portal access — confirmed bug, logged, NOT fixed.** Any coach can reach `/mentor` — the route is gated by `PractitionerCoachGuard` (coach/super-admin), not by actual mentor status. The data itself appears protected: `MentorPortal.tsx` calls `list_mentor_trainees`, and a non-mentor gets `viewer_role:"none"` + an empty-state card. NOT a data-leak emergency as far as the page code shows — but the route and the sidebar nav entry should be gated on real mentor status. A mentor = a user with an active `coach_mentor_assignments` row as `mentor_user_id` (`ended_at IS NULL`). CARRYOVER: `list_mentor_trainees` RPC itself was NOT read this session — confirm it is leak-safe next session. (10) **NEW feature scoped, not built — "Assign Mentor Role" Learning Admin tab.** Cole wants a third tab in Learning Admin next to Assign/Unassign: a searchable user list to assign a mentor role. OPEN DECISION for next session: there is no standalone "mentor role" today — "being a mentor" is emergent from having a `coach_mentor_assignments` row. Two models: (1) a standalone mentor-eligibility flag (schema change — new column/table, new RPC, the Mentor Portal guard checks the flag); or (2) just a cleaner UI over the existing `coach_mentor_assignments` trainee-assignment flow (no schema change). This ties together with item (9) — the tab is HOW you assign mentor status, the route/sidebar guard is WHAT it controls; same workstream. (11) Session 81 close artifacts: this v89 build-queue entry, architecture-reference v85 (§105), session-81-to-82.md handoff. Markdown only — Session-74 decision, no `.docx`. Cole uploads all three manually to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-internal-docs` (flat repo root); GitHub MCP is READ-ONLY. **Session 82 opens on the mentor-role workstream (the "Assign Mentor Role" tab + the Mentor Portal route/sidebar gating, with the standalone-flag-vs-UI-over-existing-flow decision to make first), THEN the actor flow work, THEN the notification work.***

*v88 - Session 80 CLOSE — Group C Phase 6 mentor portal SHIPPED + super-admin Learning Admin v1→v2 (scheduled assignment + Excel bulk import) SHIPPED, all verified. Backend-first throughout. Session opened on the deferred Phase 6 mentor-facing + super-admin-facing LMS UI (everything outside content authoring). Group V (the certification page) was Session 80 priority 2 and was NOT reached — it remains the only unfinished group in the X→Y→Z→V→W arc and is the Session 81 opener. (1) **Mentor portal SHIPPED (backend + 3 Lovable prompts, all verified).** A mentor is NOT an account_type — there is no `mentor` role. A mentor is defined entirely by a `coach_mentor_assignments` row; the account types remain super_admin, coach, company_admin, corporate_employee, individual, org_admin. Backend: a seven-part migration — `has_lms_permission` helper, new `skills_practice_iterations` table, a `skills_revision_requested` audit action_type, a rewrite of `mark_skills_practice_signoff`, a new `request_skills_revision` RPC, an extension of `get_content_item_for_viewer`, and `list_mentor_trainees`. Frontend Prompt 1 (mentor portal read-only) shipped `MentorProgressTree.tsx`, `MentorPortal.tsx`, `MentorTraineeDetail.tsx`; routes `/mentor` and `/mentor/trainee/:traineeId` under `PractitionerCoachGuard`; a sidebar "Mentor Portal" entry. Verified: roster loads, the lazy-loaded four-tier progress tree renders, assignments are keyed by `assignment_id` (so a curriculum assigned twice — e.g. PTP VILT 1 — correctly appears twice), null-safety holds. Prompt 2 (review drawer + panels + mentor upload) shipped `ReviewDrawer.tsx` (shadcn Sheet), `SkillsPracticeReviewPanel.tsx`, `LiveEventReviewPanel.tsx`, `WrittenSummaryReviewPanel.tsx`. All four write actions verified: skills sign-off, request-revision (reaches the trainee), mentor attachment upload, live-event attendance (`attended`/`completed`), written-summary approve. **Note on a non-bug:** an earlier-suspected skills sign-off "bug" was action-order, not a defect — a revision request resets the trainee's sign-off, so a subsequent mentor sign-off correctly leaves a `both_required` item in `revision_requested`. (2) **Learning Admin v1 SHIPPED then expanded to v2 mid-session.** Prompt 3 (Learning Admin v1) shipped `src/pages/super-admin/LearningAdmin.tsx` as a single-tab "Assignments" page (4-way type selector, bulk multi-select assign, cross-trainee unassign via `list_all_learning_assignments`) plus `src/components/learning-admin/TraineeMultiSelect.tsx`; route `/super-admin/learning-admin` + sidebar entry. Cole then expanded scope to a two-tab v2 redesign. (3) **Learning Admin v2 SHIPPED (Prompts C1 + C2 + a fix prompt, all verified).** Prompt C1 rebuilt `LearningAdmin.tsx` as a two-tab page — "Trainees" (an interactive user table reusing the `search_impersonation_targets` RPC and the `Users.tsx` pattern, with per-row ellipsis single-user assign actions) and "Assign / Unassign" (a guided flow with an operation toggle + type selector doing immediate assign/unassign) — and extracted `ResultPanel` into `src/components/learning-admin/ResultPanel.tsx`. Prompt C2 extended the Assign/Unassign tab with three things: a due-date field + a schedule-for-later branch (calls `create_scheduled_assignment` when a future date is set), an "Import from spreadsheet" card (SheetJS template download + file upload calling the `learning-admin-import` Edge Function), and a "Scheduled assignments" card (list + cancel). A subsequent fix prompt corrected two real issues — see (6). (4) **Backend for Learning Admin — all migrations applied + verified on `svprhtzawnbzmumxnhsq`.** Seven bulk-assignment RPCs (`assign_curriculum_bulk`, `assign_module_bulk`, `enroll_users_in_certification_path_bulk`, `assign_mentor_bulk`, `unassign_curriculum_bulk`, `unassign_module_bulk`, `unassign_mentor_bulk` — thin loops over the single-target functions, per-element exception isolation, 500-cap, return `{operation,requested,succeeded,failed,results[]}`). `get_content_item_for_viewer` gained a `written_submission` block (latest written submission's id/content/char_count/iteration_number/submitted_at + review fields). `list_all_learning_assignments()` — a super-admin SECDEF STABLE RPC returning three arrays (curriculum / module / mentor assignments), active rows only. **Part A — scheduled assignment:** a new `scheduled_assignments` table (15 cols, RLS super-admin-only, 3 indexes, named CHECK constraints incl. reason ≥10 chars and a status enum pending/processing/completed/partial/failed/cancelled); the **GUC-bypass pattern** (`app.scheduled_assignment_actor`) applied to five functions (`assign_curriculum_directly`, `assign_module_directly`, `enroll_user_in_certification_path`, `assign_mentor`, `log_super_admin_action`) so a cron context with no JWT can act — fail-closed preserved when the GUC is unset; `process_due_scheduled_assignments()` cron processor (per-row isolation); three management RPCs (`create_scheduled_assignment`, `cancel_scheduled_assignment`, `list_scheduled_assignments`); a pg_cron job `process_due_scheduled_assignments_daily` at `20 6 * * *`, active. **Part B — Excel bulk import:** a `learning-admin-imports` storage bucket (private, 10MB, xlsx/csv); `get_learning_import_reference()` RPC feeding the template's reference sheet; the `learning-admin-import` Edge Function (Class A, `verify_jwt:true`) — frontend parses xlsx with SheetJS, POSTs JSON rows, the function resolves names/emails to IDs (zero-match and ambiguous-match both fail the row), calls the bulk RPCs as the authenticated super admin, returns a per-row report. Cole verified the full bulk-import HTTP round-trip via a live UI test (one good row + one bad-email row → one succeeded, one failed). (5) **Mentor-fix RPCs SHIPPED + verified (migration `session80_v2_mentor_pair_resolution_rpcs`).** Immediate mentor assignment was broken since v1 — it passed `p_certification_id: null` and `assign_mentor` requires a real certification. Two RPCs fix it: `get_mentorable_certifications(p_mentor_user_id, p_trainee_user_id)` returns a trainee's certifications filtered to ones the mentor is qualified to mentor (qualification rule lifted verbatim from `assign_mentor`); `assign_mentor_pairs_bulk(p_mentor_user_id, p_pairs jsonb, p_reason)` takes an array of `{trainee_user_id, certification_id}` pairs, loops `assign_mentor`, per-row isolation, standard result shape. The fix prompt rebuilt the mentor assign flow to do per-trainee certification resolution: each selected trainee is resolved via `get_mentorable_certifications` — one qualifying cert auto-resolves, multiple shows a per-trainee picker, zero marks the trainee skipped — and Confirm calls `assign_mentor_pairs_bulk`. **Data finding that drove the design:** multiple certifications per person is COMMON, not rare (Cole Bastian and Test Coach 1 each hold two), so failing the multi-cert row was rejected in favour of the per-trainee picker; the picker pre-filters to certifications the selected mentor is qualified for so it never offers a pairing the RPC would reject. A trainee's certification is normally `in_progress` (a record is created at enrollment, `certified` only after completion) — `assign_mentor` requires the *mentor* to be certified but does NOT require the trainee's certification to be certified, so `in_progress` is the correct, valid state to mentor against. (6) **Bugs surfaced + fixed Session 80.** (a) Immediate mentor assign passed `null` certification — FIXED via the mentor-fix RPCs + fix prompt above. (b) The optional due-date field on immediate assignment accepted past dates (no frontend or backend guard) — FIXED in the fix prompt with `min={today}` on the input plus a client-side check before the RPC call. Note: the *scheduled* date was never affected — `create_scheduled_assignment` has a backend guard and was verified to reject past dates; only the separate `due_at` field lacked one. (7) **Logged followups added to the queue Session 80** (none blocking): skills-practice two-way commenting (trainee resubmission notes + mentor sign-off notes — backend-first when built); a platform-wide in-app notifications display surface (bell/panel) — multiple features now write notifications with no surface to read them; a "Viewer Smoke Test Quiz (DELETE ME)" test fixture is leaking into a real trainee's progress tree — clean up; `MentorProgressTree.tsx` uses raw Tailwind colors instead of semantic tokens — minor; the per-trainee mentor resolution fires one `get_mentorable_certifications` call per selected trainee — fine at current data volumes, watch if large multi-selects become common. (8) **Carryover Edge Functions still owed to GitHub `cbastianBWE/brainwise-blueprint`:** the five long-standing Session-77 functions (`draft-text` v7, `get-content-item-video-url` v1, `content-item-ai-assist` v1, `skills-practice-attachment-upload` v1, `content-item-file-upload` v1), the Session-79 `get-lesson-block-asset-urls` v1, this session's `learning-admin-import` v1, plus the `create-checkout` / `customer-portal` drift past last-committed v56/v41. Web-UI upload each via GitHub's "create new file" with the full slashed path (all are named `index.ts`). (9) NO movement Session 80 on: Group V certification buildout (priority 2, deferred — Session 81 opener), AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine. **Session 81 opens on Group V — the Certification page at `/certifications/<id>` with the Canvas PNG credential plus the deferred cert-path celebration marquee — which finishes the X→Y→Z→V→W arc.***

*v87 - Session 79 CLOSE — lesson_blocks trainee viewer SHIPPED end-to-end and verified. **Group W is COMPLETE** (lesson_blocks was the 8th and final content item viewer). Three backend changes + one multi-round Lovable frontend build + full live-DB verification. Two mid-session UX scope changes and one found-and-fixed pre-existing bug. (1) **Backend — three changes, all migrations applied and separately verified.** (a) Migration `add_cascade_to_complete_lesson`: added the `cascade` block (`public._compute_completion_cascade(v_caller_id, p_content_item_id)`) to `complete_lesson`'s return jsonb, bringing it in line with the six Session-78 completion RPCs (§101). (b) New RPC `get_lesson_block_assets_for_trainee(p_content_item_id uuid, p_user_id uuid DEFAULT NULL)` — migration `add_get_lesson_block_assets_for_trainee`. SECURITY DEFINER, access model mirrors `get_content_item_video_asset` (self with an active/completed curriculum assignment on a curriculum containing the parent module, OR mentor, OR super admin). Reuses the existing `_walk_block_config_for_asset_refs` helper. GRANT to `authenticated`+`service_role` only, anon REVOKEd. Decision settled the Session-78 open question: a parallel `_for_trainee` RPC was built rather than widening the super-admin-gated `get_lesson_block_assets` — the access models are different enough that one function with a role branch would have been less clear. (c) Migration `widen_lesson_trainee_read_policies_to_completed`: widened three RLS policies — `lesson_blocks_trainee_read`, `content_assets_trainee_read`, `content_asset_versions_trainee_read` — from `uca.status = 'active'` to `uca.status IN ('active','completed')` via in-place `ALTER POLICY` (Postgres rejects `CREATE OR REPLACE POLICY`). Required because the test trainee `+employee` is in a `completed` curriculum state; aligns these policies with `get_content_item_for_viewer`'s already-`('active','completed')` access model. (2) **New Edge Function `get-lesson-block-asset-urls` v1** — deployed `verify_jwt:false` (Class A custom auth, own `getClaims`), no `_shared/` imports so repo source and deployable source match. A JWT-bound client calls `get_lesson_block_assets_for_trainee` (the access check), then a service-role client signs the `lesson-assets` private-bucket paths. Returns `{assets:[{asset_id,signed_url,mime_type,asset_kind}],expires_in_seconds}`. Smoke-tested: no-auth → 401, OPTIONS → 200. The authenticated end-to-end signed-URL path is NOT exercised — the test lesson has no `lesson-assets` media; see test-coverage gaps below. (3) **Frontend — lesson_blocks trainee viewer SHIPPED via Lovable across multiple review rounds.** Five files at repo HEAD: `src/components/learning/viewers/LessonBlockViewer.tsx` (NEW, the paged-section viewer); `src/hooks/useLessonBlockAssets.ts` (NEW, trainee asset resolver calling the new Edge Function, the SCORM/API export seam); `src/hooks/useCompletionReporter.ts` (MODIFIED — added `reportProgress(rpcName, rpcArgs)`: a raw RPC call with no query invalidation, no cascade read, no `isReporting` flip, alongside the unchanged `reportCompletion`; the SCORM/API export seam for incremental progress); `src/pages/learning/ContentItemViewer.tsx` (MODIFIED — added `case "lesson_blocks"`, destructured/passed `reportProgress`); `src/components/super-admin/lesson-blocks/BlockRenderer.tsx` (MODIFIED — surgical edits, NOT a fork). (4) **ARCHITECTURE DECISION — Session-78 'separate renderer' decision AMENDED.** The Session-78 locked decision was a fully separate from-scratch `LessonBlockRenderer.tsx` sharing only CSS. **Overridden Session 79:** the trainee viewer REUSES the authoring `BlockRenderer.tsx` via a new `mode="trainee"` prop plus two optional props (`onBlockComplete`, `savedProgress`) that no-op in editor mode. Rationale: reuse guarantees pixel-identical trainee/authoring output and removes the visual-drift risk that a separate renderer carries; the orchestration concern that motivated the separate-renderer decision is handled by the `mode` prop and the no-op props rather than by code duplication. The authoring AI tools (`src/components/super-admin/lesson-blocks/ai-pane/`) are untouched and remain trainee-inaccessible (route guard `RoleGuard allowedRoles=["brainwise_super_admin"]` on the authoring route + super-admin-gated RPCs) — trainees correctly have no AI authoring surface. The lesson_blocks viewer has no trainee-facing AI feature in v1 (per §3.8 "no distinctive feature for v1"). (5) **§3.8 DEVIATION 1 — paged-section progression (Model B), replacing single-scroll.** The §3.8 spec describes one continuously-scrolled lesson. Cole changed this mid-session: the lesson renders ONE section at a time; Continue/Previous step linearly; the sidebar TOC jumps to ANY section (no locking — navigation is fully open); only final lesson completion is gated. Section boundaries are authored `button_stack` blocks containing an `action_type:"continue"` button — these delimiter blocks are CONSUMED (not rendered), the viewer's own Continue button replaces them. A lesson with zero continue-delimiter blocks renders as a single section (graceful fallback). The `content-item-viewer-scope.md` §3.8 text should be updated to match the shipped paged-section Model B design. (6) **§3.8 DEVIATION 2 — completion gate is `allGatedComplete && allSectionsVisited`.** The §3.8 spec gated `scroll_and_checks` mode on scroll-position-reaches-bottom. That latch-prone flag was replaced: `visitedSections` is a `Set<number>` seeded `{0}` and updated on every section change; the lesson completes only when every gating-required interactive block is complete AND every section has been visited. Furthest-position write is monotonic via a `furthestSectionRef` (the debounced `upsert_lesson_progress` early-returns when the current section index is behind the furthest; resume sets the ref to the resumed index). (7) **BUG FOUND + FIXED — match-question right-column not shuffled (pre-existing, NOT a Session-79 regression).** In `MatchTrainee` (inside `BlockRenderer.tsx`), `rightsSorted` used `[...pairs].sort((a,b)=>a.client_id…)` — a deterministic `client_id` sort, not a shuffle; with sequentially-authored `client_id`s the right column rendered in authored order, making every match question trivially solvable by row position. Pre-existing authoring-side bug, surfaced by the trainee reuse. Fixed: `rightsSorted` replaced with `useMemo(() => stableShuffle(pairs, \`${blockClientId}:${question.client_id}:match-right\`), [pairs, blockClientId, question.client_id])`; `blockClientId` threaded into `MatchTrainee` from `KnowledgeCheckRender`. The `:match-right` seed suffix avoids collision with the ranking/timeline shuffle seeds. The fix is in the shared `BlockRenderer.tsx`, so it also corrects the authoring preview. (8) **Other bugs found + fixed during the Lovable review rounds** (all corrected before close, none logged as carryover): knowledge_check completion was gated on `allCorrect` (a perfect score) — a one-way-door bug where a trainee unable to ace a check could never complete the lesson/module/cert; fixed to gate on `allAttempted` (every question Checked once, right or wrong). sessionStorage was removed from all four interactive renderers — `lesson_block_progress.completion_data` (DB) is the single source of truth; each renderer seeds in-memory `useState` lazily from a new `savedProgress` prop and a `completionFiredRef` suppresses re-firing the completion effect on already-complete blocks. Re-attempt was a no-op until the block-progress query was made attempt-scoped (`.eq("attempt_number", currentAttempt)`, `attempts_count` added to the query key, `handleReattempt` invalidates the content-item-viewer query first so `attempts_count` refreshes before the progress query re-runs). (9) **Testing — verified live against the DB.** Published test lesson: content_item `32e0e966-4cb8-4e8b-abf8-5617de346f59` ("Test Lesson Blocks Item") in Test Module C, 28 active blocks (0-27), 14 block types, `lesson_completion_mode=explicit_continue`, `is_required=false`, exactly ONE continue-delimiter `button_stack` → splits into 2 sections. Cole exercised the full UI. Backend post-run: `content_item_completions` = `completed` with `completed_at`, `lesson_furthest_continue_client_id` and `lesson_last_block_id` populated; `lesson_block_progress` attempt 1 = 6 `completed` rows all with `completion_data`, attempt 2 (re-attempt exercised) = 8 `completed` rows — attempts cleanly isolated by `attempt_number`. The celebration modal correctly did NOT fire: the content item is `is_required=false`, so completion yields only a `content_item`-tier cascade, which the chrome suppresses by design (the modal fires only for module/curriculum/certification tiers). (10) **Test-coverage gaps (carryover for future verification):** the authenticated signed-URL asset path on `get-lesson-block-asset-urls` was not run end-to-end (the test lesson has no `lesson-assets` media); the celebration modal was not run on a real module/curriculum/cert cascade (cascade plumbing on `complete_lesson` is verified by the function definition, but modal-fire on a real tier-2+ event is verified-by-design only); no `scroll_and_checks`-mode lesson fixture exists (only `explicit_continue` was tested); the mentor-role viewer path (`viewerRole === "mentor"`) is untested; the test lesson has only one section delimiter, so paging is a thin stress test. (11) **`create-checkout` integrity check (carry-forward concern):** the live function is v59 and does not use a `coach_user_id` Stripe metadata field at all (it sets `user_id`, `instrument_ids`, `checkout_type`, coach-order client fields, Pattern C comp flags). The known-fragility note about `create-checkout` losing `coach_user_id` does not apply to v59 — not a regression; the Session-79 Lovable run did not touch `supabase/functions/`. The repo copy being behind is the pre-existing v56→v59 drift (still owed — see carryover). (12) **Carryover still owed:** five Session-77 Edge Functions remain uncommitted to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-blueprint` under `supabase/functions/` — `draft-text`, `get-content-item-video-url`, `content-item-ai-assist`, `skills-practice-attachment-upload`, `content-item-file-upload`; plus `create-checkout` v59 and `customer-portal` v44 are drifted past last-committed v56/v41. Session 79 added `get-lesson-block-asset-urls` v1 — also owed to the repo. (13) NO movement Session 79 on: AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine. SCORM/API export remained a stretch goal and was not reached — the export seams (`useLessonBlockAssets`, `reportProgress`) shipped as part of the viewer; export tooling stays post-launch. **Session 80 opens on the mentor UI + super-admin UI for all LMS surfaces outside authoring (the deferred Phase 6 mentor portal plus super-admin LMS oversight), followed by the full certification buildout (Group V — the Certification page at `/certifications/<id>` with the Canvas PNG credential, plus the deferred cert-path celebration marquee).** Build Queue post-launch fast-follow added Session 79: drag-and-drop matching for knowledge_check `match` questions (§3.2 keeps tap-to-pair for v1 — this is a planned enhancement, not a bug).*

*v86 - Session 78 CLOSE — Group Z completion-modal arc finished; cascade refactor shipped; BUG-8/9/10 closed or partially closed. (1) **Cascade refactor SHIPPED (backend + frontend).** Two migrations to `svprhtzawnbzmumxnhsq`: `add_compute_completion_cascade_helper` (new SECDEF STABLE `_compute_completion_cascade(p_user_id, p_content_item_id)` returns jsonb `{tier, entity_id, entity_name}` — `tier` is the highest tier of {certification|curriculum|module|content_item|null} that transitioned to completed within a 5-second `completed_at` window, distinguishing a fresh transition from a stale completion; REVOKE PUBLIC/anon, GRANT authenticated+service_role) and `add_cascade_to_completion_rpcs` (all six completion RPCs — `confirm_external_link`, `record_video_progress`, `submit_file_upload`, `submit_written_summary`, `mark_skills_practice_signoff`, `submit_quiz_attempt` — got an additive `cascade` block in their return jsonb; each an identical-signature CREATE OR REPLACE, no overloads, all existing keys preserved). The three rollup triggers and `get_user_learning_state` were NOT modified — rollups run synchronously in-transaction so the RPC's final RETURN reads post-rollup state. Frontend Lovable prompt: `useCompletionReporter` deletes `collectCompleted` + `fetchLearningState` + the two-snapshot diff and reads `result.cascade` directly. `submit_written_summary` in `review` mode writes `submitted_for_review` → cascade tier null → no modal until mentor approval (Phase 6); `auto` mode completes normally; single helper handles both. (2) **BUG-8 RESOLVED, BUG-9 RESOLVED, BUG-10 PARTIALLY RESOLVED** — see the BUG entries below for detail. BUG-10's "course-level modal does not fire" diagnosis: NOT a code bug, NOT cascade-detection failure — a test-fixture gap (the trainee's PTP VILT 1 assignment was a `direct_assignment` with `certification_id=NULL` and the trainee had zero `coach_certifications` rows, so cert-path completion had never occurred server-side; the cert-path rollup trigger correctly early-exits unless `source='certification_path' AND certification_id IS NOT NULL`). Fixed by enrolling `+employee` in PTP-Coach (replicated `enroll_user_in_certification_path`'s INSERTs via SQL since the function is `auth.uid()`-gated). (3) **Four-tier celebration modal SHIPPED (partial).** Module tier (green-check, Next + Back-to-cert-path, 4s auto-dismiss) and curriculum tier (stronger, dismiss-required) fully built. Certification tier ships as an interim celebration Dialog ("You're certified!", Award icon, dismiss-required) with a STUBBED placeholder block ("Your certificate and sharing options will be available soon") — NO Download/Share buttons. The full cert-path marquee (certificate preview + Download + Share-with-coach + Share-on-LinkedIn) is deferred to Group V — it depends on a certificate credential artifact that does not exist yet. (4) **BUG-9 file_upload — no Edge Function code change needed.** `content-item-file-upload`'s `finalize` action already spreads the full RPC result (`{...rpcData, ok:true}`) so it auto-forwards `cascade`; only frontend work was an `onCascade` callback in `FileUploadViewer` reading `fin.data.cascade`. (5) **Two affordances SHIPPED:** "Completed [date]" badge on external_link/file_upload/live_event cards in `ModuleDetail.tsx` (`get_module_detail` already returns `completion.completed_at` — no backend change); "More content below" scroll affordance in `ContentItemViewer.tsx` (measures the inner scroll container, not window). (6) **Two new Build Queue items added** (Cole request): [MEDIUM] inline preview window for the file_upload viewer (frontend-only, renders the existing signed URL by file type); [MEDIUM] inline preview for the external_link viewer (OG-unfurl card needs a new backend Edge Function — recommended — vs frontend-only iframe embed often X-Frame-Options-blocked). See "Build Queue items added Session 78" below. (7) **X→Y→Z→V→W arc status corrected.** Literal arc definition (build-queue v79): X = tile primitive + Resources shell; Y = My Learning + All/Coach Resources tabs; Z = detail pages + completion modal sequence + Restart/Review/Request-Access stubs; V = Certification page at `/certifications/<id>` with Canvas PNG; W = the 8 content item viewers. **X ✅, Y ✅, Z ✅** (detail pages Session 76, completion modals Session 78). **W IN PROGRESS** — chrome + 7 of 8 viewers built/verified (video, written_summary, external_link, quiz, skills_practice, file_upload, live_event); the lesson_blocks viewer is the 8th and only one remaining. **V NOT STARTED.** The viewers (W) were built ahead of V — a sequencing deviation, not a problem. To finish the arc: the lesson_blocks viewer (finishes W) + the whole Certification page (V). (8) **All tests PASSED:** cascade-refactor regression (module modal still fires after `collectCompleted` deletion), badge, scroll affordance, External Link as non-final item (no modal), File Upload as final item (cert modal fires; server reconfirmed all four tiers flipped in one transaction). Module-tier and curriculum-tier modals in ISOLATION are not testable in this fixture (PTP VILT 1 has one required module so any module completion cascades to cert) — module/curriculum branches were code-read instead. (9) **Edge Function GitHub sync still owed (Carryover from Session 77):** five Session-77 Edge Functions are NOT committed to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-blueprint` — `draft-text` v7, `get-content-item-video-url` v1, `content-item-ai-assist` v1, `skills-practice-attachment-upload` v1, `content-item-file-upload` v1. `content-item-file-upload` needs NO v2 (cascade pass-through works via the existing `{...rpcData}` spread) — just the commit. Also flagged: `create-checkout` is live at v59 and `customer-portal` at v44 — both have moved past their last-committed versions (Session 73 committed v56/v41), separate pre-existing drift to reconcile. (10) NO movement Session 78 on: AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine, Phase 6 mentor portal.*

*v85 - Session 77 CLOSE — Group Z content item viewers built (chrome + 7 of 8 viewers; lesson_blocks deferred to Session 78). Viewer chrome + 7 of 8 viewers shipped + verified. (1) **Step 1 SHIPPED + verified: unified viewer chrome + video/written_summary/external_link viewers.** Chrome at `/learning/content-item/:contentItemId` (`ContentItemViewer.tsx`), `ViewerProps` contract, `useCompletionReporter`/`useAssetResolver` hooks. Backend: M1 `get_content_item_for_viewer` read RPC, M2-M5 (video_ai_summary column, external_link_reflection_text, confirm_external_link reflection text, video asset resolver), `draft-text` v7 + `get-content-item-video-url` v1 Edge Functions. (3) **Step 3 SHIPPED + verified: skills practice viewer (trainee-facing).** Backend: M8 (3 skills revision columns), M9 (M1 surfaces revision fields + signoff timestamps), M10/M10b (`skills_mentor_attachment_url` column + M1 surfaces it), M11 `set_skills_practice_attachment` RPC. New `skills-practice-attachments` bucket (private, 200MB). New `skills-practice-attachment-upload` Edge Function v1 (Class A, role-parameterized trainee/mentor — built for both from the start; mentor role has no UI until Phase 6). FileUploadField is super-admin-only and could not be reused for a trainee — trainee upload subsystem built net-new. (4) **Step 5 SHIPPED + verified: file_upload + live_event viewers.** `submit_file_upload` and `mark_live_event_attendance` RPCs pre-existed. New `content-item-file-uploads` bucket (private, 500MB) + `content-item-file-upload` Edge Function v1 (Class A, enforces per-item `file_upload_max_bytes` / `file_upload_allowed_extensions`). live_event viewer is trainee read-only — attendance marking is a mentor/super-admin action (Phase 6). (5) **Step 4 (lesson_blocks viewer) DEFERRED to a fresh session** — needs its own recon (block-type rendering, the block read RPC). `lesson_blocks` stays on the placeholder. (6) **Three viewer-gap bugs logged: BUG-8/9/10.** BUG-8 (HIGH): celebration cascade modal never fires — `collectCompleted` in `useCompletionReporter.ts` reads `node.status` where the real status is nested in `module_completion.status` / `assignment_status` / `completion.status`; fix drafted as Lovable Prompt 5. BUG-9 (MEDIUM): file_upload completes via the Edge Function not `reportCompletion`, so no cascade modal even after BUG-8 fix. BUG-10 (MEDIUM): the celebration modal is one generic Dialog, not the four-tier treatment scope §2.10 specifies. (7) **NEW Build Queue items added Session 77:** (a) **[HIGH] Cascade refactor — completion RPCs return cascade directly.** Replace the fragile client-side before/after snapshot diff in `useCompletionReporter` with cascade info returned from the completion RPCs themselves (they already compute module/curriculum/cert rollup via Postgres triggers). Deletes `collectCompleted` + `fetchLearningState` + 2 RPC round-trips per action, and fixes BUG-9 (file_upload) for free. The contained BUG-8 fix is the interim step. (b) **[MEDIUM] Four-tier celebration treatment (BUG-10).** Per-tier modal treatment per scope §2.10: module auto-collapse 3-5s, curriculum dismiss-required + learning summary, cert_path full-screen marquee with certificate credential preview + Download + Share-with-coach + Share-on-LinkedIn. Currently one generic Dialog. (c) **[MEDIUM] "Last completed [date]" badge** on external_link / file_upload / live_event content_item cards in the module detail page (scope §2.10) — committed, not shipped; lives in module detail page, outside the viewer prompts. (d) **[MEDIUM] "More content below" affordance** (down-chevron + fade gradient at viewport bottom) on viewer surfaces when content extends past the fold (scope §2.10 pro-pattern) — committed universally, not shipped in any viewer prompt. (e) **[MEDIUM] Video viewer — `react-player` migration + mux support.** Scope §3.1 specified installing `react-player` to wrap all 5 source types uniformly. What shipped: `supabase_storage` via native `<video>` (complete); `youtube_unlisted`/`vimeo`/`cloudflare_stream` via hand-rolled iframe embeds (work, but missing the §3.1 variable-speed and 3-sec-back-step player controls); `mux` is NOT played — renders an HLS `.m3u8` link only (unimplemented). Migrate to `react-player` to unify all five and gain the missing controls. Launch impact depends on which source types are actually published (Cole to confirm). (f) **Video seek-skip** — a trainee can scrub native `<video>` to the end and completion fires. Anti-skip / seek-prevention deferred (Cole-approved Step 1). (g) **Quiz UI upgrades** (Cole's future feedback): selected answer fills the whole card box (not just outline); per-question feedback surfacing as the trainee answers. Post-v1 polish. (h) When restart-with-attempts schema ships, `ai_assist_used_at` allowance should reset per module attempt. (8) **Full Phase 6 mentor-phase plan** — to be written into the session handoff: mentor portal UI; mentor skills sign-off + "Request revision" (the M8 revision columns are the schema seam, no writer yet); mentor attachment upload (the `skills-practice-attachment-upload` mentor role + `set_skills_practice_attachment` mentor path already exist — Phase 6 needs only the UI); the `TODO Phase 3` notification wiring in `mark_skills_practice_signoff`; live_event mentor attendance marking UI; M1 should also surface `live_event_marked_by` for the mentor view; super admin gets the mentor portal and sees ALL trainees. (9) **GitHub sync owed at close:** 5 Edge Functions to commit to `cbastianBWE/brainwise-blueprint` — `draft-text` v7, `get-content-item-video-url` v1, `content-item-ai-assist` v1, `skills-practice-attachment-upload` v1, `content-item-file-upload` v1. NO movement Session 77 on: AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine.*

*v84 - Session 76 CLOSE — Group Z content item viewer infrastructure scoped + partially shipped. Quiz authoring shipped end-to-end (5 RPCs + 7 audit action_types + frontend Prompt 1 SHIPPED + verified). Quiz viewer backend RPCs shipped + verified (`get_quiz_for_trainee`, `get_quiz_attempt_results`). Quiz viewer frontend prompt DRAFTED but DEFERRED — does not send until Prompt 0 (unified viewer chrome) ships first. Comprehensive content item viewer scope doc written covering all 8 viewers with locked decisions, completion-RPC gap matrix, per-viewer feature specs, chrome contract, and competitor recon synthesis. Two new standing rules locked (§99 FK lookup tables for auditable actions, §100 structured response shapes for client-side rendering). (1) **Quiz authoring backend SHIPPED (5 RPCs + audit action_types).** `upsert_quiz_question(p_id, p_content_item_id, p_question_type, p_question_text, p_question_image_url, p_display_order, p_points, p_explanation, p_reason)`, `archive_quiz_question(p_id, p_reason)`, `reorder_quiz_questions(p_content_item_id, p_ordered_ids uuid[], p_reason)` — exact-set validation, `upsert_quiz_answer_option(p_id, p_question_id, p_option_text, p_option_image_url, p_is_correct, p_match_pair_key, p_display_order, p_reason)`, `archive_quiz_answer_option(p_id, p_reason)`. All SECDEF + search_path locked + REVOKE anon / GRANT authenticated+service_role per §82. 7 new entries in `super_admin_action_types` (quiz_question_created/updated/archived, quiz_questions_reordered, quiz_answer_option_created/updated/archived) — required because `super_admin_audit_log.action_type` is an FK lookup table, not a CHECK constraint (lesson learned, becomes §99). End-to-end smoke test verified all error paths fire correctly (parent_item_not_quiz, option_text_or_image_required, match_pair_key_required, ordered_ids_count_mismatch, already_archived). (2) **Quiz viewer backend SHIPPED (2 RPCs + 1 patch).** M1 `get_quiz_for_trainee(p_content_item_id)` — returns content_item wrapper + questions + options shaped for trainee. Strips `is_correct`/`match_pair_key`/`explanation` unless `quiz_show_correct_mode='always'`. For match types splits options into `prompts` (authored order by match_pair_key) + `answers` (server-shuffled via `ORDER BY random()`). Includes `last_attempt` + `best_score_pct` + `ever_passed` + `attempts_count`. Validates content_item is quiz, not archived, parent module published (super admin bypasses published check). M2 `get_quiz_attempt_results(p_attempt_id)` — post-submit reveal RPC. Authorization: caller owns attempt OR mentor of trainee OR super admin. Reveal logic per quiz_show_correct_mode: `never`→false, `after_each_attempt`/`always`→true, `after_pass`→true only if ever_passed. When revealing, re-derives per-question correctness from `quiz_attempts.answers` jsonb using SAME scoring rules as `submit_quiz_attempt` (per-question all-or-nothing, select_all requires exact set match, match types require every pair). M2b patch fixed `ORDER BY display_order` outside `jsonb_agg` bug in the no-reveal branch. End-to-end smoke verified: trainee read works, is_correct stripped (3 different orderings across 3 calls confirm shuffle), `always` mode reveals is_correct, `after_each_attempt` reveals everything post-submit with per-question correctness matching submit RPC exactly, `after_pass`+failed attempt → reveal=false / options=null / is_question_correct=null (correct security behavior). Test quiz `0e365d0e-81e6-4d28-a0fe-ccd749714a9d` left in place on Test Module C, 4 questions, 1 attempt logged (`cffd2c4d-aaf4-4637-b1ee-77171251ee1d`, 50%, failed). (3) **Quiz authoring frontend SHIPPED (Prompt 1, Cole reported "the author UI for quiz landed fine").** New page `/super-admin/content-authoring/quizzes/:contentItemId` mirrors LessonBlocksEditor pattern. New components: `QuizQuestionsEditor.tsx`, `QuestionCard.tsx`, `MultipleChoiceOptionsEditor.tsx`, `TrueFalseOptionsEditor.tsx`, `MatchOptionsEditor.tsx`, `useQuizAuthoring.ts` hook. ContentItemEditor.tsx: "Edit quiz questions" button added (mirrors "Edit lesson blocks" pattern, ExternalLink icon per Cole's nit on Lovable's plan, question count badge). App.tsx route wired. 4 question types exposed: multiple_choice (2-5 options, exactly one correct), true_false (fixed 2 options auto-seeded), select_all (2-6 options, ≥1 correct), match_definition (2-6 pairs, text-only both sides). `match_picture` NOT exposed (schema CHECK still permits it; UI hides it pending image upload work). All options text-only. Per-card save flow with reason modal (min 10 chars). Reorder on drag-end. Per-option archive with inline reason. Match pair storage convention: prompt row `display_order=0, is_correct=false`; answer row `display_order=1, is_correct=true`; shared `match_pair_key`. Reused from KnowledgeCheckBlockForm: SortableChoice, ChoicesEditor, TrueFalseEditor, MatchEditor (text-only variant), drag infrastructure, confirm-on-type-switch pattern, RichTextEditor. (4) **Quiz viewer frontend prompt DRAFTED but DEFERRED (Prompt 2).** Staged at `prompts/prompt-2-quiz-viewer.md`. Explicitly marked at top: "DO NOT SEND THIS PROMPT YET — depends on Prompt 0". Drafted to capture context while fresh; sends after Prompt 0 (unified content item viewer chrome) ships and verifies. Plugs into chrome's `item_type === 'quiz'` branch — does NOT ship as standalone page. Chrome contract documented: viewer receives `contentItem` row, `reportCompletion(payload)`, `useCompletionReporter()` hook, `useAssetResolver()` hook, `navigateToNextItem()` from chrome. Viewer state machine: loading → intro → in_progress (one-question-per-screen) → submitting → summary. Per-question advance via "Save and continue" button (path b — no auto-advance even in `always` mode; extra Submit-this-question step in `always` mode for feedback before continuing). Match question UX: tap-prompt-then-tap-answer pairing (NOT drag-and-drop in v1) with brand-color pair chips (Navy → Teal → Orange → Purple → Green → Mustard cycle, max 6 chips matches max 6 pairs). Failure tone sober (no red backgrounds). Defensive `match_picture` handling: renders stub, lets trainee skip. Edge cases: zero-question quiz, network failure on submit, quiz archived between get and submit, already-passed retake (backend no-downgrade rule). (5) **CONTENT ITEM VIEWER SCOPE DOC SHIPPED — comprehensive lock for all 8 viewers.** File: `content-item-viewer-scope.md`. Sections: §1 gap matrix (verified live), §2 locked decisions (sequencing, ambition tier, SCORM/API export path, BlockRenderer reuse, quiz match_picture defer, match pair storage, reveal RPC shape, feedback mode mapping, authoring v1 scope), §3 per-viewer feature specifications (8 viewers × intro/flow/distinctive/edge cases/out-of-scope), §4 backend work needed for build completion, §5 chrome contract (Prompt 0 specification), §6 competitor recon synthesis (Brilliant, Coursera, CABEM, anti-patterns), §7 per-viewer post-launch fast-follows, §8 cascade-collapse contract (Session 72 lock), §9 test fixture state, §10 out-of-scope items, §11 document authority. **Completion RPC gap matrix verified live:** Video ✅ `record_video_progress`, Quiz wrapper ✅ `submit_quiz_attempt`, Quiz Q&A ✅ Session 76 shipped, Written ✅ `submit_written_summary`, Skills ✅ `mark_skills_practice_signoff`, External link ✅ `confirm_external_link`, File upload ❌ GAP (no completion RPC — need `submit_file_upload_completion`), Live event ✅ `mark_live_event_attendance` (verified live, previously thought missing), Lesson blocks ✅ `upsert_lesson_block_progress`. **Net backend gap before viewer build completes:** `get_content_item_for_viewer` unified read RPC (needed for Prompt 0), `submit_file_upload_completion` (file upload viewer), widen `get_lesson_block_assets` for trainee access (lesson blocks viewer), additive migration `content_item_completions.external_link_reflection_text` (external link distinctive feature), Resend cron integration (live event distinctive feature). (6) **Viewer ambition Tier 3 locked — distinctive features per viewer.** Video: AI summary card at end (Anthropic infra). Quiz: none — compete on smoothness. Written: AI starter prompt suggestion (draft-text infra). Skills: mentor "Request revision" status. External link: optional reflection prompt after return (additive column). File upload: none. Live event: pre-event reminder email (Resend cron). Lesson blocks: none — SCORM/API export abstractions ARE the long-term distinction. (7) **Quiz match_picture DEFERRED to post-launch FF.** Path C locked. Reason: `content_asset_refs` has no `quiz_question_id` or `quiz_answer_option_id` columns, so existing FileUploadField + `request-asset-upload` Edge Function cannot link uploads to quiz rows. Edge Function flagged as fragile from past sessions — extending deserves own backend-first work block. Build queue item: schema migration (add 2 nullable FK columns), Edge Function extension to accept new ID params, FileUploadField prop extension, viewer support for match_picture. (8) **Five Group Z Lovable prompts SHIPPED earlier in Session 76 (before the viewer scoping work):** cert path detail page, dimension card color refactor, curriculum detail page, module detail page. All four routes live at `/learning/cert-path/:certPathId`, `/learning/curriculum/:curriculumId`, `/learning/module/:moduleId`. Hero band patterns reusable for `/learning/content-item/:contentItemId` (Prompt 0 in Session 77). (9) **NEW Standing rules locked Session 76:** **§99 — FK lookup tables must be audited alongside CHECK constraints when adding auditable actions.** Surfaced during quiz authoring RPC build. `super_admin_audit_log.action_type` is an FK to `super_admin_action_types`, NOT a CHECK. Every new auditable action_type must INSERT into that lookup table before the RPC succeeds. Pattern: when adding RPC that calls `log_super_admin_action(action_type=>'foo')`, audit `super_admin_action_types` for the entry, INSERT if missing in the SAME migration. Pairs with §96 (relax-RPC-validation requires matching CHECK-constraint audit). Both reinforce that validation lives at multiple layers and ALL must be touched atomically. Standing query: `SELECT * FROM super_admin_action_types WHERE action_type = 'foo';` before any new audit-emitting RPC. **§100 — Structured response shapes for client-side rendering simplification.** When a single SQL-driven response shape can produce client-side bugs around how to interpret/filter the data, refactor to a structured shape with explicit named arrays per logical role. Example from `get_quiz_for_trainee`: instead of returning a flat `options` array for match questions and forcing client to separate `display_order=0` (prompts) from `display_order=1` (answers), return `prompts` + `answers` as separate arrays. Server-side shuffle for `answers` removes need to expose `match_pair_key` to client. Net: one shape ambiguity removed, one security boundary made structural. Apply this pattern when SQL response shape forces client to make role-decisions based on data values rather than data structure. (10) **NEW Build Queue items added Session 76:** (a) **Prompt 0 — unified content item viewer chrome (HIGH PRIORITY, scope doc §5).** Page at `/learning/content-item/:contentItemId`. Chrome owns route + breadcrumbs + header band + completion-cascade modal + abstraction hooks (`useCompletionReporter`, `useAssetResolver`). Plug in 3 simplest viewers (external_link, video, written_summary) as proof-of-concept. Pre-work: build `get_content_item_for_viewer` unified read RPC. (b) **Prompt 2 — quiz viewer frontend (drafted, deferred — sends after Prompt 0).** File `prompts/prompt-2-quiz-viewer.md`. (c) **`submit_file_upload_completion` RPC** — needed before file upload viewer build. Validates content_item is file_upload type, content_asset exists and is owned by caller, updates content_item_completions with file metadata, marks complete, triggers Postgres rollup. (d) **Widen `get_lesson_block_assets` for trainee access** OR build parallel `get_lesson_block_assets_for_trainee` — needed before lesson blocks viewer build. Currently super-admin-only. (e) **Additive migration: `content_item_completions.external_link_reflection_text text NULL`** + `confirm_external_link` accepts optional `p_reflection_text` — external link distinctive feature. (f) **Cron + Resend integration for live_event reminder emails** 1 hour pre-event — live event distinctive feature. Pattern already exists for other reminder emails, reuse infrastructure. (g) **Quiz match_picture authoring + viewer + image uploads on quiz prompts/options (POST-LAUNCH FF).** Requires: schema migration (add `quiz_question_id` + `quiz_answer_option_id` nullable FK columns to `content_asset_refs`), Edge Function extension (`request-asset-upload` accepts new ID params), FileUploadField prop extension, expose match_picture in question type Select, viewer support for image rendering. (h) **Per-viewer post-launch FFs** (full list in scope doc §7): video captions/transcripts, AI wrong-answer feedback for quiz, autosave drafts for written summary, mentor rubric scoring for skills, time-on-external for external link, mentor in-app review for file upload, Zoom auto-attendance for live event, SCORM/API export tooling for lesson_blocks. (11) **Sequencing locked for Session 77:** Open with drafting Prompt 0 (unified chrome). Pre-work backend RPC `get_content_item_for_viewer` first, then frontend prompt. Verify chrome with simpler viewers (external_link, video, written_summary). After Prompt 0 ships+verifies, send drafted Prompt 2 (quiz viewer). Then remaining per-type prompts in order: Prompt 3 (skills practice), Prompt 4 (lesson blocks — largest scope), Prompt 5 (file upload + live event paired). Backend prep work for prompts 3-5 can run parallel to viewer prompts. (12) **Test fixture state at Session 76 close:** Test quiz `0e365d0e-81e6-4d28-a0fe-ccd749714a9d` on Test Module C, 4 questions (mc Q1 "What is 2+2?", tf Q2 "Sky is blue", sa Q3 "Which are prime?", match_definition Q4 with 3 pairs Cat→Feline / Dog→Canine / Fish→Aquatic). Mode `after_pass` (last set during smoke test). Pass threshold 60%. 1 attempt logged: `cffd2c4d-aaf4-4637-b1ee-77171251ee1d` (50%, failed). Edge Function versions unchanged from Session 75 close. *v83 - Session 75 CLOSE — Resources flow functional verification + detach/duplicate workstream SHIPPED end-to-end + 10 bugs fixed + Content Authoring restructure & Rise first-class scoping deferred to future sessions. 11+ Supabase migrations, 8 Lovable prompts, 1 frontend hotfix migration, ~95% functional verification on Group Y complete. Session 76 opens with pre-build decision lock on the two scope docs (Content Authoring restructure Part 1 + Rise as first-class authoring Part 2), then either ships those or pivots to Group Z (detail pages) per locked X→Y→Z→V→W arc. (1) **Resources flow bugfixes — 6 fixed via mixed backend/frontend work.** Bug 1: `get_resource_content_asset` legacy `content_type IN ('worksheet','template')` guardrail blocked guide/article/video file downloads — fix removed type guardrail, all 7 content_types now route through SECDEF RPC. Bug 2: `assetUrls.ts` PGRST201 FK ambiguity (content_assets→content_asset_versions has 2 FKs) — fix explicit FK name `!content_asset_versions_asset_id_fkey!inner`. Bug 3: `request_asset_upload` v5 — content-type-aware asset_kind (was hardcoded `'document'`, blocked video files >50MB) — fix branches on `content_type IN ('video','image','audio')`. Bug 4: ResourceEditor structural XOR violation — separate URL vs File state could exist simultaneously — fix mode selector radio (article/video) with mutual-exclusion via state reset on mode switch. Bug 5: Friendly filenames on downloads — `get-resource-signed-url` v2 with `as_attachment: boolean` body parameter, sets `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<original>"` via Supabase Storage transform. Bug 6: Video files routed to download instead of inline player — ResourceGridTab `handleResourceClick` now branches on `content_type === 'video' && content_asset_id != null` → inline player route. (2) **Locked-tile defaulting bug FIXED (BUG-X1).** `MyLearningTab.tsx` used `locked={!entity.is_accessible}` which treated undefined as locked (RPC `is_accessible` field is optional on some entity types) — fix `locked={entity.is_accessible === false}`. Defensive pattern: treat undefined as accessible (fail-open for UI tile rendering, fail-closed enforcement remains backend RLS). (3) **Detach + Duplicate workstream SHIPPED end-to-end (backend + frontend).** Backend: 2 detach RPCs (`detach_curriculum_from_certification_path`, `detach_module_from_curriculum`) — hard delete link rows (user enrollments preserved, Option A locked), clear sibling prerequisite_*_id references first to avoid FK violations. 3 duplicate RPCs (`duplicate_certification_path`, `duplicate_curriculum`, `duplicate_module`) + 2 internal helpers (`_duplicate_module_children`, `_duplicate_curriculum_full`) — deep copy with **assets SHARED by reference (not duplicated)**, new entities start `is_published=false`, slug+name editable in dialog. Map-tracking via `v_module_id_map` jsonb (object, NOT array — caught and fixed `jsonb_array_length` bug mid-test for cert path duplicate). 5 new super_admin_action_types: `curriculum_detached_from_path`, `module_detached_from_curriculum`, `certification_path_duplicated`, `curriculum_duplicated`, `module_duplicated`. Frontend: X icon detach buttons next to edit pencils across CertPathEditor/CurriculumEditor/ModuleEditor, Duplicate button next to Archive on each editor, both with reason field min 10 chars, success toasts, post-action navigation. (4) **Paid enrollment modal SHIPPED.** Replaces prior toast "Payment flow coming soon" with proper modal: PaidEnrollmentNudgeModal.tsx, title "Ready to learn more about {entityName}?", body explains paid enrollment, primary CTA routes to `/products#certifications`, secondary "Cancel". Triggered when `self_enroll_*` RPC returns `payment_required` response. (5) **Browse & Enroll relabel SHIPPED.** "All Available" → "Browse & Enroll" across MyLearningTab. "Standalone Curricula" → "Curricula". "Standalone Modules" → "Modules". Empty state copy updated. Smart-default toggle preserved (Enrolled view first if user has any active assignments, Browse & Enroll otherwise). (6) **Video content_item chicken-and-egg + stale-state FIXED.** Two distinct bugs surfaced when authoring a video content_item using a `supabase_storage` source: (a) Chicken-and-egg: ContentItemEditor `perTypeValid` required `videoSourceId` to be non-null at save time, but `video_sources` row could only be created AFTER content_item existed (FK requires content_item_id). Fix: relaxed `perTypeValid` for `supabase_storage` source type — videoSourceId can be null at initial save, video_sources row created in second-save flow after content_item id exists. CHECK constraint `content_items_video_required` relaxed to allow NULL video_source_id when video_source_type='supabase_storage'. `upsert_content_item` RPC updated: `v_video_source_id := NULLIF(p_type_config->>'video_source_id', '')`. (b) Stale state on source-type switch: ContentItemEditor Select `onValueChange` for video source type was not clearing `videoSourceId` state — switching from YouTube to supabase_storage left stale YouTube id in state, caused subsequent save to fail validation. Fix: `onValueChange` clears `videoSourceId` and related state when source type changes. (7) **TEST VERIFICATION — ALL 18+ scenarios PASSED end-to-end.** All 7 resource content types (article URL, article file, guide, video URL, video file, worksheet, template); Coach Resources tab; file downloads with friendly filenames; external link tile behavior; locked tile overlay (plan_tier=premium for base-tier user `testclientbwe+coupontest@gmail.com` promoted to active/base for test); free/individual/base-tier visibility; inline video player for file-based videos; YouTube embed for URL-based videos; paid enrollment modal triggers correctly with correct entityName + routing; Browse & Enroll label fix; locked-tile defaulting fix; free cert path self-enroll via PTP-Coach; smart-default Enrolled vs Browse & Enroll; search filter; library-pull for video into content_item (clunky workaround flow — chicken-and-egg + stale-state bugs surfaced during this test, both NOW FIXED); detach curriculum from cert path; detach module from curriculum; duplicate module (3 content_items + 28 lesson_blocks + 5 ci asset_refs + 1 module asset_ref copied, **assets SHARED by reference verified via SQL** — same 4 asset_ids referenced by both source and duplicate); duplicate curriculum; duplicate cert path (after `jsonb_array_length` fix). Group Y functional verification ~95% complete (only paid cert path full Stripe integration deferred). (8) **Content Authoring restructure + Rise first-class SCOPED — full scope doc shipped.** Cole identified two UX gaps in Content Authoring page during Session 75: (a) tree navigation is the only way to find/edit entities — flat search is missing, multi-parent relationships display as duplicates in tree, no surface for recent/draft/published filtering; (b) Rise lessons (lesson_blocks) buried 3 layers deep — must create module → create content_item type='lesson_blocks' → click Edit lesson blocks to reach the actual Rise editor. Frontend recon: ContentAuthoring.tsx is 33KB, ~80% tree logic + ~20% editor routing, would be heavy rewrite. _shared.tsx exports TreeNode + NodeTypeIcon (mostly reusable as EntitySearchResult). Backend recon: `content_items.module_id` is NOT NULL — Rise first-class requires schema migration OR a hidden "lesson library" module hack. 9 RPCs reference `content_items.module_id`: `_duplicate_curriculum_full`, `_duplicate_module_children`, `_rollup_content_item_to_module`, `_rollup_module_to_curriculum`, `get_user_learning_state`, `reap_pending_uploads`, `reorder_content_items`, `request_asset_upload`, `upsert_content_item`. Full scope doc at `/home/claude/internal-docs/scope-content-authoring-restructure.md` (437 lines) covers: Part 1 (UI restructure, replace tree with action sidebar + tile/carousel main area + EntityTileSection/EntitySearchDialog/CreateSidebar new components, 4 open design Qs, 6-8 hours cost, 2 focused sessions); Part 2 (Rise first-class with 3 architecture options scoped — Option A decouple lesson_blocks from content_items entirely, Option B nullable module_id only for lesson_blocks **(RECOMMENDED v1)**, Option C hidden "lesson library" module hack; schema migrations, 8 RPC updates, 2 new RPCs `attach_lesson_to_module`/`detach_lesson_from_module`, frontend components, 7-9 hours cost, 2 focused sessions). Sequencing recommendation: Part 1 first (establishes tile surface), Part 2 second (plugs into it). DO NOT do both same session (14-17 hours combined). (9) **NEW Build Queue items added Session 75:** (a) **Content Authoring page restructure (HIGH PRIORITY, scope doc).** Replace left tree with action sidebar (Create / Find-edit / Duplicate / Recent), main area as tile/carousel grouped by entity type with parent context badges + filter chips. Editors unchanged. Pre-build decisions required from Cole. Pointer to scope doc. (b) **Rise lesson as first-class authoring entity (HIGH PRIORITY, scope doc).** Surface in sidebar directly, detach from forced module nesting, attach to module separately. Schema option A vs B vs C decision required. Pointer to scope doc. (c) Library badge count UX polish on FileUploadField empty state (deferred — minor). (d) Cert path tiles in Browse & Enroll should preview embedded curricula (post-Group Z work — wait for detail pages to exist first). (e) `get_user_learning_state` should add `is_accessible` to assignments long-term — current defensive frontend fix in MyLearningTab is correct but cleaner if backend projects it explicitly. (f) Interest opt-in CTA + email-to-support + `paid_enrollment_interest_leads` table (deferred — captured in paid modal scope but not built; for marketing follow-up). (g) `detach_lesson_from_module` RPC (when Part 2 ships — currently no need). (h) Investigate whether other RPCs need the same CHECK constraint audit pattern reinforced this session (relaxing RPC validation without also auditing matching table-level CHECK constraints). (10) **NEW Standing rules locked Session 75:** **§96 — When relaxing RPC validation, audit table-level CHECK constraints in same migration.** Lesson learned from Bug 7e/7d: `upsert_content_item` was updated to accept NULL video_source_id for supabase_storage source, but the table CHECK constraint `content_items_video_required` was missed in initial migration — required follow-up migration to relax constraint, broke first save attempt. Standing query: `SELECT pg_get_constraintdef(oid) FROM pg_constraint WHERE conrelid = 'table_name'::regclass AND contype='c';` before any RPC validation change. **§97 — Detach pattern: hard delete link rows, not soft archive.** UNIQUE constraints `(certification_path_id, curriculum_id)` and `(curriculum_id, module_id)` would block reattach if soft-deleted via archived_at. User enrollments preserved (Option A locked Session 75) by NOT cascading detach to user_curriculum_assignments / user_module_assignments. Clear sibling prerequisite_*_id references first in detach RPC to avoid FK violations. **§98 — Duplicate pattern: deep copy with assets SHARED by reference, new entities start unpublished.** Storage-efficient (same asset bytes referenced by multiple content_items). New entities `is_published=false` forces author review before exposure. Slug + name editable in dialog. Internal helper RPCs `_duplicate_module_children(p_source_module_id, p_target_module_id, p_caller_id)` and `_duplicate_curriculum_full(p_source_curriculum_id, p_new_slug, p_new_name, p_caller_id)` reusable, not exposed via grant. Use jsonb map (object, not array) to track source_id → target_id for cross-reference resolution within the same duplicate transaction. (11) **Sequencing decision for Session 76:** Two valid paths. (Path A — Content Authoring restructure + Rise first-class) ship the two scope docs over 4-5 sessions. Higher author velocity gain. Requires Cole's pre-build decisions first (Part 1 Q1/Q2/Q4/Q5 + Part 2 architecture A/B/C + detach semantics + publish state). (Path B — Group Z detail pages) continue locked X→Y→Z→V→W arc. Detail pages for cert path / curriculum / module with completion modal sequence + stubs for Restart/Review/Request Access. Cole picks Session 76 open. Group Y functional verification at session close is ~95% complete. PTP-Coach is PAID (toggled during paid modal test, set price back to NULL if needed). `testclientbwe+coupontest@gmail.com` (`ab99d5de-7a07-4bcb-88ac-8e7b3ead4ae3`) is active+base (revert to inactive+base if cleanup desired). Premium-Only Locked Tile Test resource (`43f46e7f-2581-44b2-aa6e-d4de09dfabef`) exists as test fixture. Test duplicate module created during verification (`498f4422-dbb4-4221-abc9-91d39b9b20a5`) archived for cleanup. Edge Function versions at Session 75 close: `get-resource-signed-url` v2 ACTIVE (added `as_attachment` boolean param + Content-Disposition transform), `request_asset_upload` (RPC) v5 ACTIVE (content-type-aware asset_kind), all other Edge Functions unchanged from Session 74 close.
