*v82 - Session 74 CLOSE — Group Y (My Learning + Resources tabs) SHIPPED end-to-end backend + frontend. Locked Group C completion arc order remains X (Session 72) → Y (this session) → Z → V → W; Session 75 opens with content authoring + functional verification of Y, then Group Z (detail pages). 8 backend migrations + 1 new Edge Function + 2 Lovable prompts shipped. (1) **Six gap decisions locked at session open via interactive Q&A.** Gap 1: locked-tile = plan_tier-gated visible-but-locked; org/role/cert/account_type-gated hidden entirely. Gap 2: all Resources sort by content_type group (video→guide→article→worksheet→template) then published_at DESC + search bars on All/Coach/My Learning. Gap 3: My Learning sort by status_group (in_progress→not_started→completed) then last_engaged_at DESC. Gap 4: new `list_available_learning(p_user_id)` RPC for All Available view. Gap 5: standalone modules — full `user_module_assignments` schema + trainee self-enroll + super-admin direct-assign. Gap 6: worksheet/template = private bucket reuse (`lesson-assets`), new `content_asset_id` column on resources, server-side signed URLs via new Edge Function. Self-enrollable model: schema-now with `is_self_enrollable` boolean + `self_enroll_price_cents` (nullable, NULL=free) + `self_enroll_currency` (default 'usd'). Wipe-on-toggle-off. New entities default OFF. Backend safety net forces price NULL when not self-enrollable. Upgrade modal: "Upgrade to access this {entityName}". Routes to in-app `/pricing` (which is PricingRouter — auth'd non-corp → /settings/plan, corp → /dashboard). (2) **Migration 1 `group_y_m1_add_self_enrollable_columns_and_extend_upserts` + `..._drop_old_upsert_signatures` SHIPPED.** 3 columns × 3 tables (cert_paths, curricula, modules), backfilled clean. Extended `upsert_certification_path` (15 params), `upsert_curriculum` (17), `upsert_module` (16) — old signatures DROPPED. (3) **Migration 2 `group_y_m2_resource_content_asset_id_and_resolver_rpc` SHIPPED + 3 follow-ups.** `resources.content_asset_id` uuid FK→content_assets ON DELETE SET NULL. New `get_resource_content_asset(p_resource_id uuid)` SECDEF RPC — access-checked via grant logic, anon revoked, granted to authenticated. `upsert_resource` extended to 10 params (added p_content_asset_id). Process note: caught hardcoded `audiences=ARRAY['free']` regression in upsert_resource rewrite during sub-iteration — patched to leave NULL. (4) **Migration 3 `group_y_m3_extend_get_user_resources_with_accessibility` SHIPPED.** Added `is_accessible` boolean + `content_asset_id` per resource. Plan_tier grants: visible-but-possibly-locked. Org/role/cert grants: hidden when no match. Sort: video→guide→article→worksheet→template, then published_at DESC. (5) **Migration 4 `group_y_m4_user_module_assignments_table_and_rls` + `..._self_enroll_and_module_assign_rpcs_v2` + `..._revoke_anon_on_admin_module_rpcs` SHIPPED.** New `user_module_assignments` table — mirrors user_curriculum_assignments shape + EXCLUDE constraint preventing duplicate active rows per (user, module), 3 indexes, 4 §82-compliant RLS policies. 2 new super_admin_action_types: `module_directly_assigned`, `module_unassigned` (admin_role_management, tier2, MFA + justification required, denylist during impersonation). 5 new RPCs: `assign_module_directly`, `unassign_module` (super-admin only, anon revoked); `self_enroll_in_module`, `self_enroll_in_curriculum`, `self_enroll_in_certification_path` (authenticated). Paid path returns `payment_required` without creating assignment. Process note caught + fixed: first attempt failed on NOT NULL `description` column in super_admin_action_types — standing rule reinforced: query column list of unfamiliar table before INSERT. (6) **Migration 5 `group_y_m5_extend_get_user_learning_state` SHIPPED.** Added `last_engaged_at` (GREATEST over assigned_at + max module/content-item completion update times) per assignment. Added `status_group` ∈ {in_progress, not_started, completed}. New `module_assignments[]` array with same shape for standalone modules. (7) **Migration 6 `group_y_m6_list_available_learning_rpc` SHIPPED.** New SECDEF RPC `list_available_learning(p_user_id uuid DEFAULT NULL)` returning `cert_paths[]`, `standalone_curricula[]`, `standalone_modules[]`. Each entity carries: enrollment_status, is_accessible, is_self_enrollable, self_enroll_price_cents, self_enroll_currency. Standalone filter: NOT in certification_path_curricula / curriculum_modules. (8) **Migration 7 `group_y_m7_revise_content_field_model_per_type` SHIPPED (mid-Lovable pivot per Cole product decision).** Replaced CHECK constraint with new content-field model: guide/worksheet/template → file required when published, url_or_content not used; article/video → exactly ONE of file OR URL when published (XOR), never both; drafts → no field requirements. `upsert_resource` rewritten with explicit publish-gate validation + clear error codes (`content_asset_required_to_publish_X`, `url_or_file_required_to_publish_X`, `provide_url_or_file_not_both_to_publish_X`). Original M2 said article/guide/video must have NULL content_asset_id, worksheet/template required file. Cole's correct model: guide is file-only, article/video are file-OR-URL XOR. (9) **Migration 8 `group_y_m8_allow_ref_field_content_for_resources` SHIPPED (during Prompt B UI testing).** `request_asset_upload` RPC patched: allow ref_field IN ('thumbnail', 'content') for resource scope (was thumbnail-only). New validation: content uploads must be document asset_kind; thumbnail still must be image. New behavior: when ref_field='content' on resource, stamps `resources.content_asset_id = new asset_id` (mirrors thumbnail pointer-stamp pattern). Got "Edge Function returned non-2xx status code" during file upload UI test, diagnosed by reading `request_asset_upload` RPC body — found legacy guardrail rejecting any ref_field other than 'thumbnail' for resource scope. (10) **New Edge Function `get-resource-signed-url` v1 ACTIVE, verify_jwt:true (Class A pattern).** POST /functions/v1/get-resource-signed-url with body `{ p_resource_id }`. Server-side: JWT client calls `get_resource_content_asset` for access check → service-role client `storage.createSignedUrl(bucket, path, 900)` (bypasses storage RLS). 15-minute expiry. Response: signed_url, asset_id, bucket, path, mime_type, original_filename, size_bytes. Rationale: `lesson-assets` storage RLS only allows super_admin SELECT — client-side `supabase.storage.createSignedUrl()` would fail for every trainee. (11) **Prompt B (super-admin authoring extensions) SHIPPED + fully verified.** 4 files: `ResourceEditor.tsx` (contentAssetId state, dirty check, validation gate, 10-key payload, mapRpcError extension, conditional Content file section), `CertPathEditor.tsx` (RadioGroup import, 3 state hooks, 15-key payload, wipe-on-toggle-off, cp-* ids), `CurriculumEditor.tsx` (same pattern, 17-key payload, curr-* ids, create-mode standalone hint via existing `hasAttachmentSection && attachToCertPathId`), `ModuleEditor.tsx` (same, 16-key payload, mod-* ids, hint via `attachToCurriculumId`). Lovable's plan improvement caught: used existing `hasAttachmentSection` derived state for standalone hint instead of new query (create-mode only — RPC enforcement covers edit-mode). Accepted. UI testing PASSED — Cole exercised cert path, curriculum, module — toggle ON $49.99 → save → toggle OFF → save: Free/Paid radio conditional on toggle correct, Price input conditional on Paid correct, wipe-on-toggle-off correct (price cleared to null), round-trip persistence correct on reload. DB round-trip verification PASSED: audit log shows symmetric before/after JSON across all 3 entity types. For curricula/modules, after_value is NESTED (`after_value->'curriculum'->>'is_self_enrollable'`); for cert_path, after_value is FLAT. Real data verified `is_self_enrollable=false`, `self_enroll_price_cents=null`, `self_enroll_currency='usd'` post-wipe. (12) **Prompt A (trainee surfaces) SHIPPED + verified end-to-end.** 10+ files: `src/pages/Resources.tsx` (rewritten — Coming soon placeholders replaced, dispatches to 3 tab components), `src/components/resources/AllResourcesTab.tsx` (thin wrapper passing tab to ResourceGridTab), `src/components/resources/CoachResourcesTab.tsx` (thin wrapper), `src/components/resources/ResourceGridTab.tsx` (5.6KB shared grid + search + click routing logic — extracted per Lovable plan recommendation), `src/components/resources/MyLearningTab.tsx` (14.9KB — smart-default Enrolled/All Available toggle, status_group sort, three sections cert_paths/curricula/modules, self-enroll RPC dispatch by entity type, payment_required toast handling, UpgradeNudgeModal integration), `src/components/resources/UpgradeNudgeModal.tsx`, `src/components/resources/types.ts`, `src/pages/ResourceReader.tsx` (289 lines — fetches resource via single supabase.functions.invoke get-resource-signed-url at line 70+119, XOR field-driven routing `hasUrl = url_or_content != null; hasFile = content_asset_id != null` lines 234-235, dangerouslySetInnerHTML for article URL, VideoEmbed for video URL, file download CTA for guide/worksheet/template), `src/lib/assetUrls.ts` (31 lines — resolveThumbnailUrls helper that batches asset_id → URL lookups), `src/components/tile/Tile.tsx` (added `locked?: boolean` prop, Lock icon overlay with backdrop-blur, suppresses hover overlay when locked — additive-only, no regression to existing call sites). App.tsx route `/resources/:resourceId` wired at line 145, not behind SubscriptionGate (free users hit upgrade modals via tile-level locked flow). MyLearningTab `shouldShowEnrollButton` correctly gates on `is_self_enrollable + enrollment_status='not_enrolled' + is_accessible=true` — locked tiles never get Enroll button passed. handleTileClick locked path opens UpgradeNudgeModal; unlocked path navigates to detail route. handleEnrollClick dispatches to `self_enroll_in_certification_path/curriculum/module` by entity type, maps 5 distinct error codes (not_self_enrollable, already_assigned_active, already_has_active_certification, not_standalone, not_published) to user-readable strings, handles `payment_required` response with toast showing formatted price + "Payment flow coming soon — contact support" placeholder. Invalidates both `get_user_learning_state` and `list_available_learning` query keys on successful enroll. ResourceGridTab line 82 uses `supabase.functions.invoke("get-resource-signed-url")` not client-side createSignedUrl; line 106 routes by `resource.content_asset_id != null` not by content_type. (13) **NEW: Prompt A functional verification — BLOCKED on content authoring.** Cole authors content via super-admin UIs before functional testing can run. Content prerequisites: at least 1 published article URL-only (url_or_content set, content_asset_id null); at least 1 published article file-only (content_asset_id set, url_or_content null); at least 1 published guide (file required); at least 1 published video YouTube URL; at least 1 published video file (mp4); at least 1 published worksheet; at least 1 published template — all 7 above tagged to "All Resources" tab with broad enough access grants that super_admin test account can see them; at least 1 published resource on "Coach Resources" tab; at least 1 published, self-enrollable, free standalone curriculum; at least 1 published, self-enrollable, free cert path; at least 1 published, self-enrollable, paid (any price) cert path for payment_required toast test; at least 1 published resource with corporate_level grant to test locked overlay for super_admin without matching level. Functional test matrix once content loaded: All Resources tab content_type grouping order verified (video→guide→article→worksheet→template), search filter works, each content_type click routes correctly (URL article → reader; file article → download; etc.); Coach Resources tab same as All Resources plus empty state correct on coach-only; My Learning tab enrolled view smart-default vs all-available smart-default tested, status_group sort verified, Enroll button on self-enrollable shows correctly, free enroll succeeds + invalidates query, paid enroll surfaces toast; ResourceReader article URL renders HTML, video URL embeds YouTube, video file plays via <video>, guide/worksheet/template fallback shows Download CTA; locked tile flow at least 1 resource with no matching access grant for super_admin (set a corporate_level grant) renders locked overlay + opens upgrade modal + Upgrade button navigates to /pricing; Edge Function signed URL download succeeds for trainee, fails for unauthorized resource, 15-min expiry honored. Full payment integration deferred (likely Stripe Checkout integration with metadata indicating cert_path/curriculum/module enrollment intent + webhook handler creating the assignment row post-payment). (b) `user_curriculum_assignments` has 3 §82 violations (roles `{public}` not `{authenticated}`) — pre-existing, NOT fixed this session. Next §82 audit pass. (c) `get_lesson_block_assets` gates on super-admin only — trainees can't use; needs own resolver RPC when Group W ships (content item viewers for lesson_blocks). (d) Tile.tsx latent footgun observation: locked tile would render inline CTA if both `inlineCtaLabel` and `onInlineCtaClick` provided. MyLearningTab call site correctly gates via `shouldShowEnrollButton` which checks `is_accessible`, so theoretical only. Add defensive `&& !locked` gate to Tile.tsx inline CTA render block on next touch. (15) **Standing patterns reinforced Session 74:** §82 RLS TO {role} discipline on every new policy (4 policies clean on user_module_assignments). Read existing RPC body before any rewrite (extends §92 from Edge Functions to RPCs — caught Migration 8's request_asset_upload legacy thumbnail-only guardrail this way). Query unfamiliar table column list before INSERT (caught NOT NULL `description` on super_admin_action_types). Server-side signed URLs via SECDEF RPC + service-role Edge Function pattern when storage RLS blocks client-side createSignedUrl — formalize as §95 architecture-reference next session. get_user_resources projects both content_asset_id AND url_or_content per resource — frontend has everything for field-driven routing. When extending pre-existing table (like resources): audit defaults + CHECK constraints + RLS policies, not just ADD COLUMN — lesson reinforced from Session 70's 4-bug pattern. (16) **Sequencing locked for Session 75+:** Session 75 opens with content authoring by Cole (1-2 hours) BEFORE functional testing of Prompt A. After functional verification passes: Group Z (detail pages — cert path, curriculum, module + completion modal sequence + stubs for Restart/Review/Request Access) ships next per locked X→Y→Z→V→W order. Group V = Certification page at /certifications/<id> with Canvas API browser composition for PNG personalization (PDF generation deferred). Group W = Content item viewers (Video, External Link, lesson_blocks minimum) — requires lesson_blocks interactive widget recon first. NO movement this session on: AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine, six §82 RLS issues on `coach_disclosure_acceptances` + `user_curriculum_assignments` deferred from prior sessions. Edge Function versions at Session 74 close: **get-resource-signed-url v1 NEW** (Class A custom-auth, verify_jwt:true), `create-checkout` v56 unchanged, `customer-portal` v41 unchanged, `create-comp-coupon` v2 unchanged, `stripe-webhook` v26 unchanged, all AI authoring functions unchanged from Session 69 close.*

*v81 - Session 73 CLOSE (security audit cycle) — Full Lovable security scan cycle run after Pattern C shipped. Initial scan surfaced 2 errors + 9 warnings. Four diagnostic rounds with Lovable to triage every finding into real bug vs scanner cache vs design-intentional. Outcome: 7 backend migrations + 2 Edge Function deploys + 2 GitHub commits + 2 Lovable frontend prompts shipped to close every real bug; all false positives + design-intentional warnings marked as ignored in Lovable UI with documented rationale. Final scanner state: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 15 items in Ignored. (1) **Migration `security_session_73_error_1_drop_trainee_writes_and_82_sweep` SHIPPED.** Closed Error 1 ("Quiz Scores Manipulation"). Dropped trainee-direct UPDATE/INSERT policies on `content_item_completions`, `lesson_block_progress`, `quiz_attempts`, `written_submissions`. All writes now flow exclusively through 11 SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (`record_video_progress`, `confirm_external_link`, `submit_file_upload`, `mark_live_event_attendance`, `upsert_lesson_progress`, `upsert_lesson_block_progress`, `start_lesson_reattempt`, `complete_lesson`, plus the 3 written-submission/quiz-attempt RPCs from prior sessions). Trainees can no longer write arbitrary values; the RPCs validate and stamp the proper fields. §82 sweep on surviving policies on these tables — explicit `TO authenticated`/`TO service_role` declarations everywhere. (2) **Migration `security_session_73_error_2_lock_account_type_org_id_practitioner_flag` SHIPPED.** Closed Error 2 ("Super admin role escalation"). Rewrote `enforce_immutable_user_fields()` trigger to freeze `account_type`, `organization_id`, AND `is_practitioner_coach` (added Session 73 Pattern C) for ALL callers EXCEPT `brainwise_super_admin`. Bypass paths: super admin (legitimate elevation/promotion path), the existing GUC opt-out `app.bypass_user_immutable_check` (used by `invitation_redeem`), and `auth.uid() IS NULL` (system trigger paths). Verified: company_admin attempting UPDATE on their own row to set `account_type='corporate_employee'` raises permission denied; full_name updates still succeed. The trigger now covers all three privilege-bearing columns; prior version only covered `account_type` + `organization_id`. (3) **Migration `security_session_73_quiz_questions_82_anti_pattern_fix` SHIPPED.** §82 sweep on `quiz_questions` + `quiz_answer_options` policies. Both tables had `roles: {public}` defaulted-in on service_role policies (the exact §82 anti-pattern). DROP+CREATE all affected policies with explicit `TO service_role`. (4) **Migration `security_session_73_coach_clients_client_view_complete_columns` SHIPPED.** Closed the real bug behind Lovable's "Clients Can Read Coaches' Private Notes" finding. Rewrote the existing `coach_clients_client_view` view with 16 client-safe columns + `WHERE client_user_id = auth.uid()` gate. Explicitly EXCLUDES `coach_notes` (the leak vector), all Stripe fields (`stripe_payment_intent_id`, `stripe_session_id`, `stripe_customer_id`, etc.), refund/coupon/revoked/source columns. The view's WHERE clause IS the gate — security_definer (NOT security_invoker) was chosen because base-table policy must remain locked (see Migration 6 below). (5) **Migration `security_session_73_coach_clients_view_revoke_extra_grants` SHIPPED.** Revoked INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE from authenticated on `coach_clients_client_view`. Kept SELECT. Tightens defense-in-depth: even if base-table writability were ever accidentally added to the view by Postgres view-update rules, the explicit revoke prevents misuse. (6) **Migration `security_session_73_drop_coach_clients_leaky_client_select_policy` SHIPPED.** DROPPED the `coach_clients: clients read their own invitation` policy. This was the original leak — the policy gated SELECT on `client_user_id = auth.uid()` but applied to ALL columns including `coach_notes`. Removal means clients have NO direct base-table SELECT on `coach_clients`. All client reads route through `coach_clients_client_view` (Migration 4) which projects only the 16 safe columns. (7) **Migration `security_session_73_cohorts_82_anti_pattern_fix` SHIPPED.** §82 sweep on `cohorts` + `cohort_members` super_admin policies (same anti-pattern: `roles: {public}` defaulted-in). DROP+CREATE clean. Both tables empty in production today (Phase 5 / Group D feature not built yet); fix is forward-looking — when the cohorts feature ships, policies are already clean. (8) **Edge Function `create-checkout` v55→v56 deployed (verify_jwt:true, ACTIVE).** Three hardening additions, all preserved byte-identical from Pattern C v55 except the new validations: `PRICE_ID_ALLOWLIST = new Set<string>([5 Stripe price IDs matching src/lib/stripe.ts exactly])` validated at line 84 — rejects unknown price_ids with 400 `price_id_not_allowed` before Stripe session creation. `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` Set + `LOVABLE_PREVIEW_PATTERN` regex + `isOriginAllowed()` helper at lines 22-40 — validates Origin header before constructing `safeOrigin` used in `success_url`/`cancel_url`, returns 400 `origin_not_allowed` if mismatch. `MAX_QUANTITY = 50` enforced at line 95 — rejects out-of-bounds quantity with 400. Pattern C comp coupon logic (`get_applicable_comp_coupon` lookup + `discounts:[{coupon}]` injection + `metadata.bw_super_admin_comp` stamping) preserved verbatim. The `req.headers.get("origin") || "http://localhost:3000"` fallback the scanner flagged was removed entirely. (9) **Edge Function `customer-portal` v40→v41 deployed (verify_jwt:true, ACTIVE).** Same Origin allowlist hardening pattern applied to `return_url` construction at lines 21-37 + 63-71. Returns 400 `origin_not_allowed` before any `safeOrigin` value reaches Stripe `return_url`. (10) **GitHub commits SHIPPED Session 73 close.** Both deployed Edge Functions sync'd to repo `main`: `supabase/functions/create-checkout/index.ts` (deployed v56 source replaces prior repo version that was already drifted from runtime before Pattern C) and `supabase/functions/customer-portal/index.ts` (deployed v41 source). Resolves repo↔runtime drift discovered Round 2 of the audit when Lovable's scanner found references to lines that didn't exist in the deployed version. Cole uploaded manually via GitHub web UI (READ-ONLY MCP). Standing rule reinforced: **whenever an Edge Function is deployed via Supabase MCP, the same source MUST be uploaded to GitHub same-session to keep repo↔runtime in sync.** Pre-existing drift on these two files predated Session 73 (Pattern C v55 deploy was the first MCP deploy on `create-checkout`; the repo version dated from earlier Lovable-shipped versions). Going forward: every deploy commits same session. (11) **Lovable prompt `coach_clients_client_view` frontend swap SHIPPED.** Five client-side `.from("coach_clients")` queries swapped to `.from("coach_clients_client_view")` across 4 files: `src/pages/MyResults.tsx` (2 calls at lines ~437 + 454), `src/pages/Onboarding.tsx` (1 call at line ~42), `src/pages/PrivacySettings.tsx` (1 call at line ~116), `src/components/assessment/InstrumentSelection.tsx` (2 calls at lines ~95 + 104). Coach-side queries on `coach_clients` base table (e.g., `src/pages/coach/CoachClients.tsx`) left untouched — coaches still need full table access including their own `coach_notes`. Verified Onboarding.tsx and PrivacySettings.tsx via GitHub `get_file_contents` after Lovable ship. (12) **Lovable prompt `ai-chat + generate-report` rate limit + length cap SHIPPED.** Two Edge Function source changes in `supabase/functions/`: (a) `ai-chat/index.ts` lines 70-83 — `message.length > 4000` → HTTP 400 `message_too_long`; `conversation_history.length > 50` → HTTP 400 `conversation_history_too_long`. Pre-Anthropic-call validation prevents oversized payloads from consuming credits. (b) `generate-report/index.ts` lines 70-94 — pre-Anthropic-call rate limit via `check-ai-usage` Edge Function with `usage_type: "report_generation"`, gated by `!isInternal` so internal-secret callers (`retry-ptp-narratives`) bypass intentionally. Returns 429 when quota exceeded. Verified both in GitHub main after Lovable deploy. (13) **All security findings dispositioned (15 items total, final scanner state clean):** REAL BUGS CLOSED (5): Error 1 quiz scores manipulation (Migration 1), Error 2 super admin escalation (Migration 2), coach_notes exposure to clients (Migrations 4+5+6 + Lovable view swap), Stripe price_id not validated (Edge Function v56), Stripe redirect URLs from unvalidated origin (Edge Function v56 + v41), generate-report no rate limiting (Lovable ai-chat/generate-report prompt), ai-chat unbounded message size (Lovable ai-chat/generate-report prompt). SCANNER CACHE / FALSE POSITIVES (Lovable scanner stale snapshot after fixes shipped, marked ignored with rationale): Stripe price_id, Stripe redirect URLs, ai-chat unbounded, generate-report no rate limit, coach_clients leaky policy (all already fixed but scanner didn't re-evaluate). DESIGN-INTENTIONAL (marked ignored): Trainees cannot read lesson-assets storage directly (§84 design — signed URLs via `get_lesson_block_assets` RPC are the canonical access path), Coaches cannot read `coach_pending_bulk_batches` directly (Session 71 design — webhook reconstruction mechanism, coaches see status via `coach_clients` grouped by `stripe_payment_intent_id`), `lesson-thumbnails` bucket allows public SELECT (§84 design — public-tier promotional imagery), `coach_clients_client_view` is SECURITY DEFINER (intentional — view's WHERE clause is the gate, security_invoker would require re-adding the leaky base-table policy). DEFERRED-BY-DESIGN (logged forward, marked ignored): Cohort members cannot read cohort records (Phase 5/Group D feature not yet built, RLS already drafted clean), Lesson assets inaccessible to enrolled trainees from storage (same as §84 design pattern above), 95 SECURITY DEFINER functions with anon EXECUTE (Session 71 deferred to dedicated audit session, no live exploit because all 77 Edge Functions use service role). (14) **Build Queue items added Session 73 security audit cycle:** (a) **Post-launch refactor: replace `coach_clients_client_view` with SECURITY DEFINER RPC `get_my_coach_invitations()`.** Views with implicit security_definer behavior + WHERE-clause-as-gate work correctly but are slightly fragile to future Postgres version semantics; converting to an explicit SECURITY DEFINER RPC with documented auth check is cleaner long-term. Low priority because current view works correctly. (b) **Session 71 anon EXECUTE audit on 95 SECDEF functions.** Dedicated audit session walking each function with anon EXECUTE and deciding per-function: keep (signup/public probe), `SECURITY INVOKER` (RLS handles it), or `REVOKE EXECUTE FROM anon, PUBLIC`. No live exploit (all Edge Functions use service role); defense-in-depth concern only. Lovable's auto-fix path would likely break signup/auth flows by over-revoking — must be hand-walked. (c) **Phase 5/Group D cohort SELECT policy for enrolled members.** When cohorts feature ships, add `cohort_members can read their own cohorts` policy (currently empty tables, just service_role + super_admin policies live). (d) **Stripe `price_id` / origin pattern lessons learned doc.** Add to architecture-reference: when shipping new Edge Functions that consume external request data (Stripe API, third-party webhooks, etc.), the allowlist pattern (`new Set([known_values])` + `helperFn()` validator + 400 reject before downstream call) is the standing pattern. Already applied to `create-checkout` v56 and `customer-portal` v41 — formalize as §91 in architecture-reference next session. (e) **Repo↔runtime sync discipline.** Every Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function` MUST be followed same-session by GitHub commit of the deployed source. Pre-Session-73 drift on `create-checkout` and `customer-portal` cost ~30 minutes of Round 2 audit time to diagnose. Going forward, drift is preventable. (15) **Standing patterns from Session 73 security audit:** **Diagnostic rounds with Lovable as triage tool.** When a scanner produces N findings, the cost-efficient pattern is: Round 1 — send all findings to Lovable with explicit "diagnose only, do not write code" instruction; Lovable reports back which are real and which are scanner cache. Round 2 — Claude verifies the real ones via Supabase MCP queries + GitHub `get_file_contents`. Round 3 — fix migrations + Edge Function deploys ship for real bugs. Round 4 — re-run scanner, expect cache + lingering false positives, batch-ignore with documented rationale. Four rounds beats four sequential fix-then-rescan cycles. **View-as-gate pattern for column-level privacy on row-readable tables.** When a base table has rows that some viewers should see SOME columns of but not OTHERS, and direct base-table SELECT gating cannot project columns, the canonical pattern is: (a) drop the base-table SELECT policy for the limited-access viewer class, (b) create a SECURITY DEFINER view that projects only safe columns + WHERE clause filtering to the viewer's authorized rows, (c) revoke non-SELECT grants from authenticated on the view, (d) frontend reads through the view exclusively. Applied Session 73 to `coach_clients_client_view`. **Two-stage RLS audit pattern.** Stage 1 quick query: `SELECT tablename, policyname, roles FROM pg_policies WHERE schemaname='public' AND 'public'=ANY(roles) AND qual='true';` Surfaces every §82 anti-pattern in one query. Stage 2 per-table audit: read all policies on each high-value table (assessments, results, coach_clients, user-PII-bearing tables) and check explicit `TO role` + non-trivial qual. Stage 1 catches the common case; Stage 2 catches the subtle case. Edge Function versions at Session 73 close UPDATED for security audit: `create-checkout` v56 ACTIVE verify_jwt:true (Pattern C + security hardening), `customer-portal` v41 ACTIVE verify_jwt:true (security hardening), `create-comp-coupon` v2 ACTIVE verify_jwt:false (Pattern C, unchanged), `stripe-webhook` v26 ACTIVE verify_jwt:false (Pattern C, unchanged), `ai-chat` + `generate-report` latest Lovable deploys with new validation logic.*



*v80 - Session 73 CLOSE — Pattern C "super admin acts as practitioner coach" SHIPPED end-to-end. Standalone one-off session. Eight backend migrations + three Edge Function deploys + one Lovable prompt (6 files) + one hotfix migration + one synthetic data row. Verified live with two successful comp-paid coach invitations from super admin to test client emails — Stripe Checkout bypassed entirely (frontend short-circuits to direct DB write + email send when the comp coupon system has an applicable 100% coupon for the caller), invitation emails delivered, no real money charged, test client invitations exist in `coach_clients` ready for the clients to sign up and take their PTP assessments at $0. The frontend route guards, sidebar Coach Tools section, and `/super-admin/coupons` admin page all working as designed. One pre-existing frontend bug discovered and hotfixed in same session (My Clients roster table not filtering revoked invitations). (1) **Migration A `pattern_c_a_comp_coupons_table` SHIPPED.** New `comp_coupons` table for managing Stripe-side promotional/comp coupons via BrainWise admin UI. Columns: id, stripe_coupon_id (UNIQUE), internal_name, description, percent_off (1-100 CHECK), duration (once/repeating/forever CHECK), duration_in_months (conditional CHECK — required when repeating, must be null otherwise), max_redemptions, redeem_by (NOT NULL — every coupon expires), applicable_account_types text[], applicable_instrument_ids uuid[], created_by FK users(id), created_at, archived_at, archive_reason, notes. 4 indexes including partial `idx_comp_coupons_active WHERE archived_at IS NULL`. 2 §82-clean RLS policies (super_admin ALL TO authenticated with role gate, service_role ALL). The applicable_account_types and applicable_instrument_ids arrays default null meaning "any" — Pattern C super_admin_comp coupon uses `['brainwise_super_admin']` for account type and null for instruments (applies to all 4). (2) **Migration B `pattern_c_b_comp_coupon_rpcs_and_action_types` SHIPPED.** Two action_type whitelist rows added to `super_admin_action_types`: `comp_coupon_created` and `comp_coupon_archived` — both category=content_authoring (initial assignment), requires_mfa=false, requires_justification=true, denylist_during_impersonation=true. Three RPCs deployed: `_insert_comp_coupon_row` (internal, SECURITY DEFINER, 13-param, service-role-only EXECUTE initially per defense-in-depth — see B3 for the hotfix), `get_applicable_comp_coupon(p_caller_user_id, p_instrument_id)` (SECURITY DEFINER STABLE, returns TABLE shape with out_coupon_id/out_stripe_coupon_id/out_internal_name/out_percent_off, filters by archived_at IS NULL + redeem_by > now() + account_type match + instrument match if specified, ORDER BY percent_off DESC then created_at DESC for tiebreak, LIMIT 1), `archive_comp_coupon(p_coupon_id, p_reason)` (super-admin-gated via assert_super_admin + impersonation gate, reason min 10 chars, soft-deletes via archived_at + archive_reason, does NOT delete Stripe-side coupon). (3) **Migration B2 `pattern_c_b2_add_impersonation_gate_to_insert_comp_coupon_row` SHIPPED.** Added `PERFORM public.assert_impersonation_allows('financial_transaction')` to `_insert_comp_coupon_row` after the super-admin check. Rationale: keep impersonation enforcement at the DB layer rather than in the Edge Function, avoiding the `_shared/impersonation_gate.ts` sync gap with GitHub repo (file lives in deployed runtime only, not in repo). The gate fires regardless of how the RPC is reached. financial_transaction is in the impersonation denylist — coupon creation can't happen during any impersonation session even in act mode. (4) **Migration C `pattern_c_c_is_practitioner_coach_column` SHIPPED.** New `users.is_practitioner_coach boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false` column. Partial index `idx_users_is_practitioner_coach WHERE is_practitioner_coach = true`. Backfilled all `account_type IN ('coach', 'brainwise_super_admin')` rows to true. Verified: 5 of 5 coaches flagged, 2 of 2 super admins flagged, 0 leaks to other account types. Decouples "what is your primary account type" from "do you provide coaching services" — a brainwise_super_admin can simultaneously be a practitioner coach (Pattern C) without sharing the super admin's elevated permissions across role boundaries. The 2 super admin users both got the flag automatically per Lock 5; if the second super admin is not supposed to be a practitioner coach, manual UPDATE to false is the deliberate action (flagged for Cole's awareness). (5) **Migration D `pattern_c_d_current_user_is_practitioner_coach_helper` SHIPPED.** New `current_user_is_practitioner_coach()` SECURITY DEFINER STABLE function returning boolean. Mirrors the existing `current_user_account_type()` pattern. Returns COALESCE of users.is_practitioner_coach for auth.uid(), or false. Used inside RLS policies on coach-affordance tables so policies don't need inline subqueries against users. EXECUTE granted to authenticated + service_role. (6) **Migration E `pattern_c_e_swap_coach_rls_to_practitioner_flag` SHIPPED.** 7 RLS policies on coach-affordance tables DROP+CREATE swapped from `current_user_account_type()='coach'` to `current_user_is_practitioner_coach() = true`. Tables: `users` (coaches can read their clients), `assessments` (coaches read client assessments), `assessment_responses` (coaches read client responses), `assessment_results` (coaches read client results), `assessment_acknowledgments` (coaches read client acks), `facet_interpretations` (coaches read client interpretations), `coach_disclosure_acceptances` (coach inserts own). All 7 recreated §82-clean with explicit `TO authenticated`. Backfill from Migration C means all real coaches already have the flag true, so this swap is behavior-preserving for them. New behavior: super admin practitioner coaches now pass these policies and can see clients/assessments/results for the coach_clients rows where coach_user_id = their UUID. Pre-existing observation logged: 3 OTHER policies on `coach_disclosure_acceptances` still carry `roles: {public}` (the coach-reads-own SELECT, the service_role ALL, and the super-admin-reads-all SELECT) — these are §82 anti-pattern leftovers from pre-Session-71 work that didn't get caught in the security audit sweep. Logged as a Build Queue item for the next §82 audit pass; low severity because the policies' quals are scoped (auth.uid()/role check inside qual), not blanket TRUE. (7) **Migration F `pattern_c_f_coach_shareable_link_rpcs_practitioner_widening` SHIPPED.** Both `coach_shareable_link_coach_paid` and `coach_shareable_link_self_pay` PL/pgSQL RPCs reworked. Auth gate widened: accepts `account_type='coach'` OR `(account_type='brainwise_super_admin' AND is_practitioner_coach=true)`. Cert check bypassed entirely for super admin practitioner coaches per Lock 5 (Cole's explicit instruction "we are not gated by being certified"). For super admin practitioner coaches, allowed_instrument_ids = all 4 instruments (PTP/NAI/AIRSA/HSS). Real coach path completely unchanged (cert check still required, allowed instruments still derived from certification_type IN ('ptp_coach', 'ai_transformation_coach', 'ai_transformation_ptp_coach', 'my_brainwise_coach')). All other RPC logic preserved verbatim — email validation, name trimming, instrument validation, pricing lookup, batch row construction, FOREACH insert loop. (8) **Migration G `pattern_c_g_practitioner_coach_sync_trigger` SHIPPED.** New `_sync_is_practitioner_coach_on_account_type()` SECURITY DEFINER trigger function + 2 BEFORE triggers on users table. INSERT trigger fires when `NEW.account_type IN ('coach', 'brainwise_super_admin')`. UPDATE trigger fires when `NEW.account_type IN (...) AND OLD.account_type IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.account_type`. Auto-flips `is_practitioner_coach=true` only when transition INTO the target role happens. Does NOT auto-flip OFF when account_type transitions away — that's a permission-removal action and must be deliberate (manual UPDATE), not implicit. Found during Migration G design: `handle_new_user` trigger does NOT set `account_type` (only handles ToS/Privacy version capture + email/full_name), so a trigger fired on auth.users insert wouldn't have account_type to read. The right hook is on public.users UPDATE OF account_type. Verified via DO-block test inside transaction-with-cleanup (flipped a test individual user to coach, checked flag went true, restored). (9) **Edge Function `create-comp-coupon` v1→v2 ACTIVE, verify_jwt:false.** Initial v1 deploy used Class A custom auth (auth.getClaims) + super_admin gate + Stripe API call + RPC call via service-role client. v1 had a critical bug discovered during Cole's first attempt to create the super_admin_comp coupon: the `_insert_comp_coupon_row` RPC's internal call to `log_super_admin_action` reads `auth.uid()`, which is NULL when invoked by service-role. The audit log function raised `Authentication required` and the whole transaction rolled back, causing the Edge Function to return 500 with `db_insert_failed`. Diagnosis took 3 minutes: zero rows in `super_admin_audit_log` for `comp_coupon_created` in the failure window meant the failure happened BEFORE audit logging, narrowing to either auth/validation OR the Stripe step OR DB insert. Synthetic test call to `_insert_comp_coupon_row` directly from MCP execute_sql raised the exact `Authentication required: log_super_admin_action cannot be called without an authenticated session` error, conclusively isolating the bug. **Hotfix Migration B3 `pattern_c_b3_grant_insert_comp_coupon_row_to_authenticated` SHIPPED.** Granted EXECUTE on `_insert_comp_coupon_row` to `authenticated` role (the internal super-admin gate inside the RPC body still protects it from non-super-admin callers — defense in depth). **Edge Function v2 deployed.** Switched the RPC call from `serviceClient` to `callerClient` (which carries the caller's JWT). Now `auth.uid()` resolves to Cole's super admin UUID inside `log_super_admin_action`, audit attribution is to the human actor not service role, transaction commits cleanly. Side benefit: cleaner audit trail. After v2 deploy, Cole's coupon creation succeeded on first retry. Standing pattern lesson: any SECURITY DEFINER RPC that internally calls `log_super_admin_action` (or any other auth.uid()-dependent function) MUST be invoked via a JWT-bearing client, not service role. The Edge Function pattern in this codebase historically defaulted to service-role for cross-cutting RPC calls; the new pattern is caller-JWT for any RPC that audits. (10) **Edge Function `create-checkout` v54→v55 ACTIVE, verify_jwt:true.** Surgical edit adding `get_applicable_comp_coupon(user.id, null)` lookup before building Stripe session params. If a coupon row is returned, adds `discounts: [{ coupon: stripe_coupon_id }]` to sessionParams AND stamps `metadata.bw_super_admin_comp: "true"` + `metadata.bw_comp_coupon_internal_name: <name>` on the session metadata. The instrument_id parameter passed as null (option 1 of three discussed) means the coupon applies session-wide — Stripe coupons apply to whole sessions anyway, so a single-instrument-restricted coupon doesn't fit Stripe's model. Future per-instrument promotions would need a different lookup strategy (loop per instrument and pick best) but Pattern C launch doesn't need that. ALL existing flow preserved verbatim — auth lookup, Stripe customer find-or-create, line_items, success_url/cancel_url, all existing metadata keys (user_id, instrument_ids, checkout_type, client_email/first_name/last_name/coach_note for coach_order mode). The lookup is wrapped in try/catch so a coupon RPC error doesn't block checkout — best-effort augmentation, real coach flows unchanged when no coupon matches their account_type. (11) **Edge Function `stripe-webhook` v25→v26 ACTIVE, verify_jwt:false.** Surgical edits in BOTH `coach_order` (single-client) AND `coach_bulk_order` (Group D) branches. Read `metadata.bw_super_admin_comp === "true"` into `isSuperAdminComp` boolean at the top of `checkout.session.completed` handler. Guard `recalculateCombinedCouponForEmail` calls with `if (!isSuperAdminComp)` — skip the client-facing credit-coupon generation on $0 super-admin comp orders (no money to credit back to client). Real coach orders ALWAYS run `recalculateCombinedCouponForEmail` unchanged because `bw_super_admin_comp` metadata is never set on their sessions. All other webhook logic preserved verbatim: coach_clients insertion, assessment_purchases insertion, sendInvitationEmail (fires unconditionally on both paths — preserves Cole's hard constraint "invitation stuff stays intact"), bulk batch completion stamping, subscription tier handling. The bw_super_admin_comp metadata key is purpose-built (not a generic "comp applied" signal) so future promotional coupons for real coaches won't accidentally trigger the credit-skip behavior. (12) **Migration H `pattern_c_h_synthetic_my_brainwise_coach_cert_for_super_admin` SHIPPED.** Synthetic `coach_certifications` row inserted for Cole's super admin user: `certification_type='my_brainwise_coach'`, `status='certified'`, `enrolled_by` + `certified_by` both = Cole's UUID (self-attested), `free_assessment_uses` = `[]::jsonb`, `notes` flag the row as synthetic for future maintainers ("Pattern C synthetic certification (Session 73). Super admin acting as practitioner coach. NOT a real cert path completion. Use users.is_practitioner_coach + users.account_type to identify Pattern C super admins; do not count this row in real-coach analytics."). Discovered during end-to-end testing: the My Clients page disables the Order Assessment button when `coach_certifications` is empty for the user, regardless of `is_practitioner_coach`. Frontend cert check is separate from the RPC cert check (which Migration F already widened to bypass for super admin practitioner coaches). Two fix options considered: (A) frontend fix to teach order-button enable logic to pass for super admin practitioner coaches — clean architecturally but requires Lovable round-trip and finding every cert-check location across the FE; (B) backend fix giving Cole a synthetic certification row — single SQL insert, no frontend changes, slight pollution of coach_certifications table. Chose (B) per Cole's earlier explicit instruction "we are not gated by being certified if that means we need to be marked as certified in all then that is fine". `certification_type='my_brainwise_coach'` covers all 4 instruments per existing logic in `coach_shareable_link_*` RPCs. (13) **Frontend Lovable prompt SHIPPED.** 6 files modified/created in one prompt: `src/hooks/useUserProfile.tsx` EDIT (added `is_practitioner_coach` to UserProfile interface + supabase select), `src/lib/accountRoles.ts` EDIT (added `isPractitionerCoach` field to AccountRoleInfo, derived from profile?.is_practitioner_coach === true, included in returned object + loading-state default), `src/components/PractitionerCoachGuard.tsx` NEW (route guard checking isPractitionerCoach flag, redirects to /dashboard otherwise, mirrors RoleGuard styling), `src/App.tsx` EDIT (swapped 6 coach routes from RoleGuard allowedRoles=["coach"] to PractitionerCoachGuard: /coach/clients, /coach/order-assessment, /coach/client-results, /coach/invoices, /coach/profile, /coach/certification — intentionally left /coach/resources on RoleGuard because super admin has own Resources entry; added /super-admin/coupons route guarded by RoleGuard+SuperAdminSessionProvider), `src/components/AppSidebar.tsx` EDIT (added Ticket lucide-react import, Fragment react import, extended NavItem type with optional sectionHeader field, inserted Comp Coupons entry into superAdminNav between Resource Authoring and AI Chat, changed getNavItems signature to accept full profile object, brainwise_super_admin branch appends coachNav under "Coach Tools" sectionHeader when is_practitioner_coach is true, updated call site to pass profile not profile?.account_type, render loop emits section-header element above any item with sectionHeader set with sr-only when sidebar collapsed), `src/pages/super-admin/CompCouponsManagement.tsx` NEW (~570 lines: page header with Create Coupon CTA + Show archived toggle, table with 7 columns (Name / % Off / Duration / Applies to / Stripe ID / Expires / Status / Action), CreateCouponModal with all form fields + client-side validation + confirmation dialog summarizing values before commit + supabase.functions.invoke("create-comp-coupon") call, ArchiveCouponModal with reason field min 10 chars + supabase.rpc("archive_comp_coupon") call). All toasts via @/hooks/use-toast. React Query keyed ["comp-coupons", showArchived] reading directly from comp_coupons table (super admin RLS allows direct table SELECT). (14) **First comp coupon created end-to-end via the new UI.** internal_name="super_admin_comp", description="Pattern C: comp coupon for super-admin practitioner-coach orders.", percent_off=100, duration="once", redeem_by=now()+60 days (default), max_redemptions=null (unlimited), applicable_account_types=["brainwise_super_admin"], applicable_instrument_ids=null, reason+notes captured. Stripe coupon ID `<redacted>` issued. Comp_coupons row id `630de294-8c94-481c-bbf2-e0163d084628`. Audit log row attributed to Cole (super_admin_user_id matches), action_type=comp_coupon_created, reason captured. get_applicable_comp_coupon test confirmed: returns the row for Cole's UUID, returns empty for all 3 real coaches sampled (<redacted-email>, cbastian@mybrainwisecoach.com, testclientbwe+testcoach2@gmail.com). Negative regression verified — real coach checkout flows will NOT see this coupon. (15) **End-to-end Pattern C testing PASSED twice.** First test invite to <real-client-email-redacted> (real client prospect, Cole's contact) via single-client coach-paid path — invitation row created in coach_clients with coach_user_id=Cole's UUID, $0 assessment_purchases row created, invitation email delivered, no Stripe transaction. Initial confusion when Cole noticed assessment_purchases.amount_paid=0 and stripe_payment_intent_id=NULL — turns out the frontend has a Stripe-bypass path for $0 comp orders (separate from Pattern C's create-checkout wiring which I designed): frontend detects 100% comp coupon applies, writes coach_clients + assessment_purchases directly + calls send-email function, bypasses Stripe Checkout entirely. Functionally correct outcome, architecturally different from the path I designed for. Pattern C's create-checkout + stripe-webhook edits are NOT exercised by this frontend path. Logged for Session 74 investigation. Second test invite to testclientbwe+coupontest@gmail.com (Cole's controlled email) confirmed the flow is repeatable: same direct-write path, invitation appears in roster correctly, email delivered. After hotfix (item 16 below) the client's revoked invitation correctly hidden and only Test Coupon visible plus the client's new re-invite. (16) **Frontend hotfix SHIPPED in same session: My Clients roster shows revoked invitations.** Pre-existing bug (not Pattern C-caused, would affect every real coach who revokes a client invitation). Stat card "Pending Invitations" correctly excludes revoked rows via filter checking `revoked_at !== null`, but the `uniqueClients` derivation that builds the Client Roster table did NOT filter, producing a mismatch: stat card shows 1, table shows 2. Two-line Lovable prompt at `/home/claude/internal-docs/lovable-prompts/hotfix-revoked-filter.md`: (Edit 1) added `if (row.revoked_at !== null) continue;` at top of the uniqueClients-derivation loop in `src/pages/coach/CoachClients.tsx`; (Edit 2) extended the Level 2 assessment-detail TableBody filter from `c.client_email === selectedClientEmail` to `c.client_email === selectedClientEmail && c.revoked_at === null`. Verified working: stat card and table counts now match. Revocation is signaled exclusively by `coach_clients.revoked_at IS NOT NULL` (the `invitation_status` enum doesn't include a "revoked" value — only 'sent'/'opened'/'partially_completed'/'completed' per the CHECK constraint), so timestamp column is the only signal. (17) **Build Queue items added Session 73:** (a) Coupon expiration notification cron — daily check for active comp coupons with `redeem_by < now() + 7 days`, emails super admin to renew via /super-admin/coupons. Cole will forget to renew the 60-day super_admin_comp coupon otherwise. Post-launch. (b) Pattern C architectural reconciliation — the frontend "Stripe-bypass on $0 comp" path discovered Session 73 is functionally correct but means Pattern C's create-checkout RPC lookup + stripe-webhook isSuperAdminComp guard are not being exercised. Either the frontend path or the Pattern C path is dead code. Investigate which frontend code path super-admin coach orders are taking, document, decide whether to remove the Stripe path or keep both for different flows. (c) 3 §82 RLS issues on `coach_disclosure_acceptances` — existing `roles:{public}` policies pre-dating Session 73 security audit. Security audit pass needed. (d) Reports counting "certified coaches" need to filter `account_type != 'brainwise_super_admin'` to exclude Pattern C synthetic certification rows from real-coach analytics. (e) Pattern C analytics — super admin practitioner orders need to be distinguishable in revenue dashboards. `amount_paid=0` rows attributed to super admin should be excluded from coach-revenue summaries. (f) `assessment_purchases.instrument_id` schema quirk: column is text and sometimes stores comma-separated UUIDs (multi-instrument single order). Pre-existing, not Pattern C related. Logged for awareness only. (g) The 2 super admin users in production both got is_practitioner_coach=true via Migration C backfill. If the second super admin is NOT supposed to act as a practitioner coach, manual UPDATE to false is the right action. Flagged for Cole's awareness. (18) **Standing patterns reinforced this session:** Approval-gated workflow protocol (lock decisions explicitly before code — 5 locks at session open: Option C2 separate boolean column, isCoach literal + isPractitionerCoach parallel, PractitionerCoachGuard separate component, Stripe coupon path over bypass with comp coupons as forward-thinking infrastructure, super admin auto-flips). Backend-first discipline (Migrations A through G all shipped + verified before frontend Lovable prompt was written). Diagnose-before-prescribe (the v1→v2 Edge Function bug was diagnosed via a synthetic DB-level RPC call that conclusively isolated the failure mode to log_super_admin_action's auth.uid() check, eliminating Stripe API failure and validation as suspects — turned a "Edge Function returned 500" into a 5-minute fix). Audit log integrity (every state-changing Pattern C action attributed to Cole via callerClient pattern, not service role). Real coach flows protected (Cole's hard constraint "make sure these changes don't break any of the invitation stuff" honored — Migration F preserved real coach path verbatim; create-checkout comp lookup returns empty for non-super-admin callers; stripe-webhook guards on purpose-built metadata key not generic discount-applied signal; verified live via SELECT count(*) FROM get_applicable_comp_coupon for 3 real coaches all returning 0). Edge Function `verify_jwt` flag must be passed explicitly on every deploy (does NOT inherit) — Class A custom-auth functions stay verify_jwt:false. NO movement this session on: AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, Group Y/Z/V/W of Group C completion arc, Cole's 5 thumbnail re-uploads from Session 72 followup, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine.*



*v79 - Session 72 CLOSE — Group C completion arc opened. Tile primitive + Resources page shell SHIPPED via Lovable Group X. Five backend migrations shipped. 21 visual primitive design decisions locked. Slice analysis completed separating must-ship coach-cert end-to-end work from stubs and deferred work. Prompt order locked for the arc: X (shipped) → Y → Z → V → W. (1) **Migration A `phase72_a_module_completions_rls_to_role` SHIPPED.** §82 fix: 3 `module_completions` policies were created with `roles: {public}` (defaulting because no `TO {role}` declared). Dropped + recreated each with explicit `TO {role}`. Standing rule §82 reinforced: every new RLS policy must declare TO {role} explicitly. (2) **Migration B `phase72_b_get_user_learning_state_thumbnails_and_module_status` SHIPPED.** Extended the read RPC to surface `curriculum.thumbnail_asset_id`, `modules[].thumbnail_asset_id`, `modules[].module_completion` (status row or null), and `items[].thumbnail_asset_id`. Verified live against testcoach2 (`2f591eb3-8cad-4188-bac6-4ed1be6e3e50`) on published PTP-Coach tree. The frontend can now render thumbnails on every tier (cert path, curriculum, module, content_item) by joining `thumbnail_asset_id` → `content_assets.current_version_id` → `content_asset_versions.(bucket, path)`. (3) **Migration C `phase72_c_lesson_thumbnails_public_bucket` SHIPPED.** New public storage bucket `lesson-thumbnails` (10MB file ceiling, image MIME types only). 5 RLS policies all §82-clean: `lesson_thumbnails_public_select` (TO public — INTENTIONAL per CC6.1 classification, public-tier promotional imagery), `lesson_thumbnails_service_role_all` (TO service_role), `lesson_thumbnails_super_admin_insert/update/delete` (all TO authenticated with super admin gate + impersonation check). Architectural decision logged in architecture-reference §83: thumbnails are public-tier UI imagery, separated from content body files which remain in `lesson-assets` with super-admin-only RLS. Pattern matches Coursera/Udemy/Notion industry standard. SOC 2 CC6.1/CC6.3/CC7.2 defensible with documented classification policy (deferred until SOC 2 written policies session). (4) **Migration D `phase72_d_request_asset_upload_route_thumbnails_to_public_bucket` SHIPPED.** Updated `request_asset_upload` RPC: introduced `v_bucket` local set to `lesson-thumbnails` when `p_ref_field='thumbnail'`, else `lesson-assets`. The bucket value gets stored in `content_asset_versions.bucket` and returned in the response jsonb. All other RPC logic verbatim from prior version. Verified live with a synthetic curriculum-thumbnail-upload test (transaction rolled back so no orphans). Edge Function `request-asset-upload` requires NO redeploy — the function is a thin wrapper around the RPC. Cole's 5 existing test thumbnails do NOT get migrated; they sit orphaned in `lesson-assets` and will be auto-reaped by the existing nightly cron. When Cole re-uploads through existing super-admin authoring UIs (Resource Authoring + Content Authoring), new uploads automatically land in the new public bucket via the updated RPC. (5) **Migration E `phase72_e_resource_access_log` SHIPPED.** New `resource_access_log` table with 3 indexes (by resource_id, accessed_at DESC, user_id). All §82-clean RLS policies: service_role ALL, super_admin SELECT (TO authenticated with role gate), trainee INSERT own (TO authenticated WITH CHECK user_id=auth.uid()). No UPDATE/DELETE policies — append-only. Plus `log_resource_access(p_resource_id)` SECURITY DEFINER RPC. Group X frontend calls this via `useResourceAccessLog()` hook on resource click (fire-and-forget, errors silently swallowed). Granularity decision: one row per access event (not a counter on resources.access_count). (6) **21 visual primitive design decisions locked.** Tile composition (16:9 aspect ratio, name + summary + variant-dependent metadata), status pills (Option C — pill+icon+text: in_progress amber `--bw-amber #FFB703` + CircleDot, completed forest green `--bw-forest #2D6A4F` + CircleCheck, certified plum purple `--bw-plum #3C096C` + Award), resource tiles (content_type pill in place of status pill, no bottom metadata row), Required/Optional explicit treatment (Option B — Required filled `--bw-orange`, Optional outlined gray, one always shown on learning-tree tiles), hover overlay Option B (subtle gradient reveal with CTA hint, suppressed in detailPageMode), inline CTAs on detail-page child tiles Option C (click body drills down, click CTA fires action, current-location gets `border-l-[3px] border-l-[var(--bw-orange)]`), resource click destinations by content_type (article/guide → reader page; video → reader with embedded player; worksheet/template → download new tab; external_link → new tab), My Learning page structure (smart-default Enrolled vs All Available toggle Option B, three horizontal carousels NOT stacked grids Option B — cert paths / standalone curricula / standalone modules sorted by most-recently-engaged first), navigation Option A (drill-down full pages NOT accordion expansion, hero header per detail page, single back button per page Option B — NOT breadcrumbs), completed item CTAs Option B (Review + Restart side by side), CRITICAL: cert path restart NOT supported (curriculum/module-only) — simplifies attempts model, completion behavior per item_type (video auto on watch_pct ≥ threshold; quiz auto on quiz_passed; written submission per `written_completion_mode`; skills practice trainee-only or signoff-required; file_upload auto; external_link explicit "Mark as done"; live_event mentor/super-admin marks attendance; lesson_blocks per `lesson_completion_mode`), celebration modals Option B (collapse to highest-tier on cascaded transitions; full notification audit history preserved Option A), certification page Option C at `/certifications/<id>` (modal celebrates moment + dedicated page is permanent home, Canvas API browser composition PNG v1, PDF generation deferred), access request routing Option C (corporate users route to org_admin first; all others route to super admin), self-enroll on All Available Option B (auto-navigate to detail page after enroll), empty states role-aware messaging (super admin sees actionable Content Authoring links; others see informational message), resource access tracking full granularity (`resource_access_log` table — SHIPPED this session), instrument badges (PTP=Navy, NAI=Mustard, AIRSA=Forest, HSS=Slate — cert path tiles only, top-left), hero pattern locked Option A. (7) **Slice analysis completed.** **Must-ship slice for coach certification end-to-end:** Resources page shell + My Learning tab carousels + cert path/curriculum/module detail pages + content item viewers (Video, External Link, Lesson blocks minimum for current published tree) + Certification page + tile primitive + empty states + completion modals + back navigation + hover overlay + resource access tracking. **Stubbed at launch (frontend exists, "Coming soon" modals on click):** Restart on curricula+modules, Review mode, Self-service access requests, Org licensing model, Personalized PDF cert (PNG v1 ships), Resource completion tracking, Mentor view of mentee learning, `enrolled` status cleanup on coach_certifications. **Fully deferred:** Phase 8 Order Assessment gating by certification, Phase 6 mentor review UI, Phase 7 actor flow, AIRSA Phases 3e-8, multi-cert-path All Available catalog (PTP-Coach is only cert at launch). **Three critical forward-compat seams flagged:** (Seam 1) `coach_certifications.status` state machine — Cole locked NO cert-path restart, simplifies state machine, safe. (Seam 2) curriculum/module attempts model — future Restart schema MUST include `is_current` flag in UNIQUE constraint OR rollup chain reads must filter — flagged in Build Queue item. (Seam 3) org licensing model is additive entitlement, doesn't override existing direct-enrollment. (8) **Lovable prompt order locked: X→Y→Z→V→W.** X shipped this session (tile primitive + Resources page shell + thumbnail public URL helper + access log hook + coach sidebar unblock + `--bw-mustard` brand token). Y = My Learning tab + All Resources + Coach Resources tab content. Z = detail pages (cert path, curriculum, module) + completion modal sequence + stubs (Restart/Review/Request Access). V = Certification page at `/certifications/<id>` with Canvas personalization. W = Content item viewers (Video, External Link, Lesson blocks minimum — requires lesson_blocks interactive widget recon first). Estimated 6-8 sessions to close must-ship slice. (9) **Group X Lovable prompt SHIPPED.** 776-line prompt drafted, transport-stripped JSX issue surfaced twice (Lovable rebuilt from prop spec + comments + surviving fragments after second transport failure), reconstruction preserved all 9 of 10 behavioral details: onMouseEnter/Leave, conditional onClick/role/tabIndex/keyboard handler, `<img onError>` placeholder fallback, `e.stopPropagation()` on inline CTA, `aspect-video` 16:9 wrapper, name 1-line truncate / summary 2-line clamp, hover overlay conditional, resource variant metadata-row suppression, cn() conditional classes. One drift caught: current-location border condition was reconstructed as `detailPageMode && isCurrentLocation` instead of `isCurrentLocation` alone — Lovable fixed before ship. All `var(--bw-*)` tokens resolving correctly, hex audit clean (only `#FFFFFF` for white text on colored backgrounds). All 12 acceptance criteria passed. `/_dev/tile-preview` route kept as a visual regression-test surface for Groups Y/Z/V/W. (10) **Group X routing sweep prompt DRAFTED but not yet shipped.** Free users currently hit `/settings/plan` redirect via `SubscriptionGate`; corporate org_admin/company_admin get `AdminResources` placeholder; super admin has no Resources sidebar entry. Three surgical edits in one prompt at `/home/claude/internal-docs/lovable-prompts/prompt-group-x-routing-sweep.md` (96 lines): remove `SubscriptionGate` wrapper from `/resources` route in App.tsx, replace `AdminResources.tsx` body with `<Resources />` component import, add Resources entry to super admin sidebar nav array. Cole ships this whenever convenient. (11) **`--bw-mustard #7a5800` brand token landed.** Added to `src/index.css` `:root` block alongside other `--bw-*` tokens. Closes the open item in userMemories ("NAI Saturation alignment to Mustard `#7a5800` prompt drafted and finalized but not yet sent — opens next session") — Mustard now has both its locked hex value AND a CSS variable name live in the codebase. (12) **Build queue items added Session 72:** (a) Group Y must implement locked-tile pattern for free users — `get_user_resources` needs `is_accessible` boolean per resource; tile primitive needs `locked` prop showing lock-icon overlay + altered click behavior + soft upgrade nudge somewhere on the page (NOT a redirect to billing). Visible-but-locked is the conversion design, not hidden. (b) Pattern C super admin acts as a coach — separate one-off session, scope doc drafted at `/home/claude/internal-docs/scope-docs/super-admin-as-coach-pattern-c.md`. Pattern C means super admin is functionally a coach themselves (has own client list, can order assessments, see client results). Cole confirmed Pattern C is the desired model, not Pattern B (impersonation). (c) Restart-with-attempts schema (curriculum + module tiers; UNIQUE constraint must include `is_current` flag OR rollup chain reads must filter). (d) Self-service access requests with org-licensing-aware approval flow (substantial: schema + RPCs + workflow + audit, 2-3 sessions). (e) Org content licensing model (`org_content_licenses` table family). (f) Certification PDF generation — decide HTML print vs real PDF library before launch (Canvas API PNG v1 ships). (g) Resource completion tracking (post-launch enhancement). (h) Mentor view of mentee My Learning (Phase 6 work). (i) Per-item viewer review mode (`mode: 'active' | 'review'` parameter — Phase 5.5). (j) `enrolled` status cleanup on `coach_certifications` (dead config — remove CHECK or update docs). (k) Content item `estimated_minutes` column (post-launch — known LMS completion driver). (l) Public-bucket thumbnails + private-bucket content body files SOC 2 classification doc (when SOC 2 policies written). (m) When extending signed-URL pattern to content body files (Phase 5.5 viewers), revisit expiry per-asset-kind — thumbnails 12h fine, videos and PDFs should be 1-2h to limit leak window. (n) Content authoring UI sweep for super-admin docs on private/internal cert paths (currently public thumbnails would leak existence — revisit when adding internal training). NO movement this session on: AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine. Edge Function versions unchanged from Session 71 close. **Standing patterns reinforced this session:** §82 RLS TO {role} discipline on every new policy (5 new policies this session, all clean); §83 NEW — per-tier asset classification (thumbnails = public-tier separate bucket, content bodies = private-tier with super-admin-only RLS); Lovable transport-failure handling (when JSX strips twice in a row, accept reconstruction with explicit audit list rather than ping-pong). The `lesson-blocks-content-schema.md` doc remains unwritten (carried forward from Session 70).*

*v78 - Session 71 CLOSE — Security audit session. Seven findings surfaced (one ERROR + six WARN-class via the Supabase advisor and Lovable's security scanner); five shipped this session, two deferred with documented rationale. Final advisor state at session close: 304 lints, ZERO ERROR, all WARN. No Group C feature work this session. Sessions 70 (Phase 5/9 backend + Resource Authoring UI) and the one-off D-series session (Your Assessment Responses 2+/2- + coach-client split-pair fixes) are the prior entries below. Session 72 opens on the Group C completion sequence; see `group-c-completion-sequence.md` for the locked order and `phase-5-9-scope-delta.md` for the remaining 5/9 frontend work. (1) **Finding 1 — 27 tables with anon-readable `roles: {public}` policies SHIPPED FIXED.** Every `service role full access` policy on the Group C / notifications tables had been created with `qual: 'true'` and no explicit `TO service_role`, defaulting `roles` to `{public}`. Postgres OR's RLS policies, so one permissive `USING (true) WITH CHECK (true)` policy beat every restrictive one. Anon SET LOCAL probe live-confirmed exposure: 41 rows from `impersonation_sessions`, 57 from `super_admin_action_types`, 17 from `notification_types_catalog`, 12 from `assessment_acknowledgments` (PII), 4 from `user_notifications` (PII), plus smaller exposures across 22 more tables. Migration `security_audit_round1_findings_1_2_3` dropped + recreated each policy `FOR ALL TO service_role`. Tables touched (27): assessment_acknowledgments, certification_path_curricula, certification_paths, coach_disclosure_acceptances, coach_disclosure_versions, coach_mentor_assignments, cohort_members, cohorts, content_item_completions, content_items, curricula, curriculum_modules, impersonation_sessions, lesson_block_progress, module_completions, modules, notification_types_catalog, quiz_answer_options, quiz_attempts, quiz_questions, resource_access_grants, resource_tabs, super_admin_action_types, user_curriculum_assignments, user_notification_preferences, user_notifications, written_submissions. (2) **Finding 1b — 4 published-content policies also `roles: {public}` SHIPPED FIXED.** During Finding 1 verification, discovered four `authenticated read published` policies on `content_items`, `curricula`, `curriculum_modules`, `modules` also had `roles: {public}` so anon could SELECT published curriculum (3+1+3+1 rows leaked). Migration `security_audit_finding_1b_published_content_anon_leak` dropped+recreated `FOR SELECT TO authenticated`. (3) **Finding 2 — `org_users_public` view SECURITY DEFINER SHIPPED FIXED.** Pre-existing Supabase advisor ERROR-class warning. View had `security_invoker=false` explicitly. Verified zero in-codebase consumers via Lovable grep (only types.ts generator noise). `ALTER VIEW ... SET (security_invoker=true)` bundled in the Finding 1 migration. (4) **Finding 3 — 7 SECURITY INVOKER functions with mutable search_path SHIPPED FIXED.** `airsa_skills_set_updated_at`, `_ai_authoring_conversations_touch_updated_at`, `_asset_kind_size_ceiling`, `_asset_kind_mime_allowed`, `_asset_extract_ext`, `_asset_active_ref_count`, `impersonation_denylist_categories`. `ALTER FUNCTION ... SET search_path = public, pg_temp` for each, bundled in the Finding 1 migration. (5) **Finding 6 — per-instrument visibility model gaps SHIPPED FIXED** (after product Q&A and Lovable diagnostic round 3.5). The docs say PTP is shareable via toggles, AIRSA is dual-rater-visible to supervisors and admins, NAI and HSS are individual-only. The RLS on `assessments`, `assessment_acknowledgments`, `assessment_results`, and `assessment_responses` was instrument-blind for the corporate-admin, coach, and permission-gated org paths. Lovable's deep dive corrected the initial framing — `assessment_results` is instrument-blind for two paths (coach + permission-gated org admin), so scores leak through those paths too, not just metadata. Cole's product answers locked Session 71: Q1 = B (completion metadata fair game for admins, no change to admin policies on assessments/acks). Q2 = single-toggle PTP-only PrivacySettings UI confirmed (defense-in-depth: add `instrument_id='INST-001'` to the permission-gated org access policy). Q3 = A (coach RLS stays instrument-blind, upstream gating via CERT_TYPE_TO_INSTRUMENTS in CoachClients.tsx is sufficient). Q4 = supervisor AIRSA metadata visibility is acceptable (nice-to-have, add a supervisor-AIRSA policy on `assessments` so future supervisor UIs that query parent metadata just work). Migration `security_audit_finding_6_per_instrument_visibility` (round 3.5 — after Lovable caught that the inline `SELECT id FROM users WHERE supervisor_user_id=auth.uid()` subquery returns nothing for non-admin supervisors because `users` RLS only gives them `users: read own row`): two changes, one transaction. Change 1: dropped and recreated `assessment_results: permission-gated org access` with `instrument_id='INST-001'` AND `current_user_account_type() = ANY(ARRAY['company_admin','org_admin','brainwise_super_admin'])` AT TOP LEVEL (planner short-circuits per Lovable's cosmetic recommendation) before the EXISTS-against-permissions. Change 2: new policy `assessments: supervisors read direct reports AIRSA` using the existing `public.get_my_direct_reports()` SECURITY DEFINER STABLE helper (search_path=public already pinned, already filters `deactivated_at IS NULL` on reports). Verified via simulated JWT for `a1d4fc3f-d635-49ae-96d4-81b0e64864c6` (demo-csuite-1 in Test Corp, supervises 2 reports): supervisor sees 9 INST-003 rows (3 self+manager rows × 2 reports = correct), zero INST-001/INST-002/INST-004 rows for those reports. Working as designed. (6) **Finding 7 — `ai-authoring-temp` storage bucket anon-readable SHIPPED FIXED.** Caught by Lovable's security scanner re-scan post-Finding-1 (not surfaced by Supabase advisor because the advisor doesn't lint Storage policies). `ai_authoring_temp_service_role_all` policy on `storage.objects` had `roles: {public}` and qual only checked `bucket_id` — same exact anti-pattern as Finding 1, just on Storage. Anon could SELECT every object's row in the AI-authoring temp bucket (1 object visible to anon at session start — a real `MODULE 1 - Bias.docx` Cole had uploaded). Migration `security_audit_finding_7_ai_authoring_temp_storage_anon_leak` dropped+recreated `FOR ALL TO service_role`. Verified anon now returns zero. Swept other storage policies for the same anti-pattern: clean (no others). (7) **Finding 4 — 95 SECURITY DEFINER functions with anon EXECUTE — DEFERRED to its own audit session.** 207 SECDEF functions total, 95 anon-executable including admin-grade ones (`grant_certification`, `log_super_admin_action`, `export_audit_events`, etc.). No live exploit because all 77 edge functions use service role (verified via list_edge_functions — none use the anon key). Defense-in-depth concern only. Lovable's auto-fix would likely break signup/auth flows by over-revoking. Tagged for a dedicated audit session that walks each function with anon EXECUTE and decides per-function: keep (signup/public probe), `SECURITY INVOKER` (RLS handles it), or `REVOKE EXECUTE FROM anon, PUBLIC`. (8) **Finding 5 — `pg_trgm` extension in public schema — DEFERRED indefinitely.** Cosmetic. Moving extensions can break dependent indexes. (9) **Lovable security scanner additional warnings reviewed:** "Email delivery logs PII (super admin only)" = ignore by design (PII access for super admin = founders, audited via super_admin_audit_log). "Enrolled trainees cannot read lesson assets from storage" = defer; the signed-URL path via `get_lesson_block_assets` RPC bypasses RLS for trainee asset reads, no direct storage.objects access from trainee. "Coaches cannot read their own bulk batch records" = ignore by design (`coach_pending_bulk_batches` is the webhook reconstruction mechanism between checkout and stripe-webhook fanning out into `coach_clients` rows; coaches see receipts and per-client status via `CoachInvoices.tsx` reading `coach_clients` grouped by `stripe_payment_intent_id`, and Stripe emails the bulk receipt directly at payment time — verified end-to-end Session 71). "Cohort members cannot read cohort records" + "Cohort members cannot read cohort membership records" = NEW BUILD QUEUE ITEM — cohorts feature is being built next, lock the access model when the feature ships. (10) **Build queue items added Session 71:** (a) Finding 4 dedicated audit session for the 95 anon-EXECUTE SECDEF functions; (b) cohort access model design + RLS to be locked when cohorts feature ships (cohort members likely need to see their cohort name + peers list — confirm at design time); (c) the `permissions` table's `viewer_organization_id` column is dormant infrastructure — no code writes it today; either wire `share_ptp_with_company_admin` to write it OR delete the column post-launch (cleanup, not a security issue, the toggle works via `peer_ptp_visible()` + `sharing_preferences` already). NO movement this session on: AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign across six surfaces, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine. Edge Function versions at Session 71 close: unchanged from D-series session (generate-all-facets v3, generate-facet-interpretations v41, calculate-scores v50, retry-ptp-narratives v2, request-asset-upload v4, draft-lesson-block v15, expand-lesson-from-outline v13, scaffold-lesson v6, scaffold-lesson-outline v11).*

*v77 - One-off D-series + coach-client bug fix session (between Sessions 71-72) — no handoff generated, Group C Phase 5/9 continues next session. All 6 existing PTP assessment results backfilled (cplummer professional 47/47, personal 42/42, both×2 89/89; evazquez professional 47/47, personal 42/42). Reset + regenerated all sections manually; `narrative_status` set to `ready`.
