*v76 - Session 70 CLOSE — Group C Phase 5 backend + Phase 9 backend + Resource Authoring UI all SHIPPED + VERIFIED. 19 migrations, one Edge Function deploy (request-asset-upload v4), one Lovable-built UI (6 files). Recon (G10, G11) complete. (1) Path A recon executed at session open + Cole re-framed Phase 5 and Phase 9 scope mid-session — full detail in phase-5-9-scope-delta.md. The re-frame: the trainee learning UI does NOT replace /certifications (it lives inside the Resources tab as the "My Learning" tab); the Resources tab is THREE data-driven tabs (My Learning / All Resources / Coach Resources) not five categories; resource visibility is an additive multi-dimensional gating system not a `resources.audiences` array match; there is no resource authoring UI and `resources` is empty, so a super-admin authoring UI must precede the Resources tab frontend; the visual treatment is a hard requirement and the SAME tile primitive must serve both the Resources tab and the trainee learning tree. (2) **Phase 5 backend — Migration Groups 1-5, DONE + VERIFIED.** Group 1 (3 migrations): `module_completions` table + the 4-tier rollup trigger chain (`content_item_completions` status→completed → `_rollup_content_item_to_module` → `_rollup_module_to_curriculum` → `_rollup_curriculum_to_cert_path` → flips `coach_certifications.status='certified'`, certified_by=NULL system-granted), fires module_completed / curriculum_completed / certification_granted notifications on the transition moments, every function idempotent on TG_OP + transitioned-INTO-completed + parent-not-already-done. Group 2 (2 migrations): the four missing content-item completion writers — `record_video_progress` (watch_pct never regresses, completes at video_completion_threshold_pct), `confirm_external_link`, `submit_file_upload` (auto-completes on upload, validates size), `mark_live_event_attendance` (mentor/super-admin ONLY, not trainee-callable) — plus the `live_event_attendance_marked` action_type whitelist row. Group 3 (1 migration): four lesson RPCs — `upsert_lesson_progress`, `upsert_lesson_block_progress`, `start_lesson_reattempt` (full-lesson re-attempts supported, prior lesson_block_progress retained by attempt_number), `complete_lesson` (trusts the viewer's completion judgment). Group 4 (1 migration): `get_cert_path_detail` read RPC — the cert-path-rooted companion to `get_user_learning_state`, for Phase 5.2. Group 5: NOT a migration — an operational fixture-setup PERSISTED to production. Published the test tree (PTP VILT 1 curriculum + Test Module C), attached PTP VILT 1 to testcoach2's existing in-progress ptp_coach cert, walked real completions through the Group 2 writers, verified the full rollup chain landed (module → curriculum → cert all `completed`/`certified` on persisted data). STANDING FIXTURE: testcoach2 is now `certified` ptp_coach — Phase 5 frontend not-started/in-progress testing needs a different fixture (testclientbwe+branding, clean individual account, is available). (3) **Phase 9 backend — 7 migrations, DONE + VERIFIED.** `resource_tabs` lookup table (data-driven orderable tab model, 3 seeded: my_learning [is_learning_tree=true], all_resources, coach_resources [is_coach_only=true]); extended `resources` table (resource_tab_id, summary, thumbnail_asset_id, is_published, created_by/at, updated_at, archived_at); relaxed the legacy `audiences`/`content_type` NOT-NULL constraints that blocked new-model inserts; `resource_access_grants` polymorphic additive gating table (6 grant types: organization / account_type / plan_tier / corporate_level / coach_certification / all_coaches, grants OR'd, shape-consistency CHECK per type); `get_user_resources` gating evaluator RPC (returns the tab list + the published resources the user can see via additive OR, super admin sees all, coach_resources only to coaches+super admin); 4 resource action_types; 3 authoring RPCs (`upsert_resource`, `archive_resource`, `set_resource_access_grants` — all assert_super_admin + content_authoring impersonation gate + reason>=10 + audit log). (4) **Asset-pipeline `resource` parent mode — F1-F4, DONE + VERIFIED.** The Resource Authoring UI needs to upload a thumbnail for a `resources` row but the asset pipeline had no `resource` parent. F1: `content_asset_refs.resource_id` column + FK + 6-way `exactly_one_parent` CHECK (was 5-way) + indexes. F2/F2b: `create_asset_ref` extended with `p_resource_id`, old overload dropped. F3: `request_asset_upload` extended with a full `resource` parent mode (now 15-arg), old 14-arg overload dropped, path convention `resource/<resource_id>/<asset_id>.<ext>`, 7/7 functional tests passed. Edge Function `request-asset-upload` deployed **v4 ACTIVE, verify_jwt:true** (two-line change). KNOWN SYNC GAP: the asset Edge Functions + `_shared/impersonation_gate.ts` exist in the deployed runtime but NOT in the GitHub repo (repo `_shared/` has only errors.ts + secrets.ts) — Cole supplied the deploy bundle via the Dashboard Download button because this session's Supabase MCP had no `get_edge_function` tool. (5) **Resource Authoring UI (G8) — BUILT via Lovable + TESTED, all 6 files verified in `main`.** NEW `_resourceShared.tsx` (constants — CONTENT_TYPE_OPTIONS corrected pre-build to the real CHECK values `article/video/guide/worksheet/template`, the original prompt's `pdf/link/reference_library` guess would have failed `resources_content_type_check`), NEW `ResourceEditor.tsx` (~26.6KB, on the Phase 4 ModuleEditor pattern + a grants sub-section with its own reason field + save button since grants are a distinct audited action), NEW `AdminResourceAuthoring.tsx` (flat list grouped by tab, single Promise.all useQuery, learning-tree tabs filtered from the picker), EDIT `App.tsx` (route `/super-admin/resources`), EDIT `AppSidebar.tsx` ("Resource Authoring" nav entry), EDIT `FileUploadField.tsx` (resourceId prop threaded through 6 spots). **3 bug-fix migrations found during end-to-end UI testing:** `phase9_e2b_fix_upsert_resource_null_id` (`upsert_resource` create path passed explicit `p_id=null` overriding the column `gen_random_uuid()` default — fixed with COALESCE); `phase9_b3_resources_super_admin_rls` (`resources` RLS had only the legacy audience-overlap SELECT policy; new-model rows have audiences=null so super admin couldn't read their own saved rows — added super-admin ALL policy; decision: regular users read through `get_user_resources` SECURITY DEFINER, not direct-table SELECT); `phase9_f4_asset_upload_archive_prior_ref_and_link_thumbnail` (two fixes to `request_asset_upload` parented-upload block — Fix 1 all 6 modes: archive any existing active ref for the same (parent, ref_field) before inserting a new one, fixes orphaned-ref accumulation; Fix 2 / Option C, 5 of 6 modes excluding lesson_block: when ref_field='thumbnail' also set the parent row's thumbnail_asset_id, fixes the thumbnail not being linked until editor Save — no Edge Function redeploy needed, all logic in the RPC). End-to-end test PASSED: test resource created → read/displayed → thumbnails uploaded (F1-F4 chain works) → 2 grants set → archived (soft-delete + unpublish + audit entry, all verified). (6) **RECURRING LESSON — extending a pre-existing table needs a FULL audit of defaults + CHECK constraints + RLS policies, not just ADD COLUMN.** Extending `resources` caused FOUR separate bugs this session, all the same root: `phase9_b2` (legacy NOT-NULL columns blocked inserts), `phase9_e2b` (explicit null overrode a column default), `phase9_b3` (RLS gap), plus the content_type CHECK guess caught pre-build. Greenfield tables (`module_completions`) didn't have this hazard. Standing rule: before extending an established table, audit its full DDL — `\d+`, `pg_get_constraintdef` for CHECKs, `pg_policies` for RLS. (7) Tooling-capability lesson: Claude CAN read/write Postgres functions via `pg_get_functiondef`+`execute_sql` (they live in the DB), CAN write Edge Functions via `deploy_edge_function`, CANNOT read the deployed Edge Function runtime (this session's MCP had no `get_edge_function`). When an Edge Function has uncommitted `_shared/` deps, ask Cole for the Dashboard Download-button bundle up front. (8) **Bucket-two status — where Session 70 ends.** Item 1 (resource authoring UI) DONE. Item 2 (seed real resources via the UI) NOT DONE — Cole's work, unblocked. Item 3 (`lesson-blocks-content-schema.md`) NOT DONE — the real config JSON for all 18 `lesson_blocks` block types was pulled via execute_sql but the doc is not written. Item 4 (unified visual primitive design-lock) NOT DONE. Item 5 (Phase 9 Resources tab frontend) NOT DONE — backend ready, depends on item 4. Item 6 (Phase 5 structural navigator) NOT DONE — backend ready, depends on item 4. Item 7 (Phase 5 content-item players, 8 of them) NOT DONE — the largest remaining item; the `lesson_blocks` player needs an interactive-widget recon done first, and that recon is unstarted. **Items 3-7 do NOT fully close Group C Phase 5 + Phase 9.** Even with 3-7 done, these remain: AIRSA Phases 3e-8 (a separate Group C track entirely), the interactive-widget recon (a prerequisite buried inside item 7), the `create_asset_ref` library-pick orphan path (F4 fixed only the upload path through `request_asset_upload` — the library-pick path through `create_asset_ref` lacks the archive-prior-ref + link-thumbnail logic; logged here as a Build Queue item), and item 2 (Cole's). 3-7 closes the trainee-facing and resources-facing FRONTEND for the two phases — not the whole of Group C. (9) Build Queue items added Session 70: `create_asset_ref` library-pick path needs F4's archive-prior-ref + link-thumbnail logic (only the upload path got it); `lesson-blocks-content-schema.md` content-format contract doc still unwritten; the Edge-Function-source-not-in-repo sync gap (asset functions + `_shared/impersonation_gate.ts` live only in the deployed runtime); SCORM/portability schema work (noted, not scoped). (10) NO movement this session on: AIRSA Phases 3e-8, SOC 2 written policies, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign across six surfaces, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine, the three [POST-LAUNCH] items from v75 (flashcards renderer reset on AI Refine, Refine textarea voice dictation, Lovable preview-in-new-tab). *v75 - Session 69 CLOSE — All four interactive block types SHIPPED end-to-end (flashcards from Session 67/68, card_sort + scenario + knowledge_check shipped this session) plus four backend hot-fixes resolving AI Refine bugs across all interactive types. (1) Lovable prompts shipped serially per §75: card_sort (session-69-card-sort.md, 1462 lines, verified working with PTP True/False example), scenario (session-70-scenario.md, 1401 lines, shipped after stripping markdown formatting to fit Lovable's ~48k input cap), knowledge_check 4a (session-71, 44,629 chars — adds block + MC + multi_select + true_false), knowledge_check 4b (session-72, 28,408 chars — adds fill_in_blank + match), knowledge_check 4c (session-73, 33,417 chars — adds ranking + timeline). User confirmed "all three prompts worked" on knowledge_check sub-prompts. (2) Edge Function hot-fix progression — draft-lesson-block v12→v13→v14→v15 + expand-lesson-from-outline v11→v12→v13. **v12→v13:** MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS raised 3000→8000 (matches scaffold-lesson v6 and expand-lesson-from-outline v11). Root cause: 10-moment scenario refines (and other high-density generates like 5-question knowledge_check or 20-card flashcards) easily exceed 3000 output tokens; Anthropic returned max_tokens-truncated JSON; JSON.parse threw; 502 ai_output_unparseable. 3000 was sized for single-instruction refines on simple blocks (text, heading); interactive blocks were added Sessions 65-66 without re-tuning the cap. **v13→v14 + v11→v12 parity:** Added explicit "EVERY markdown-typed field MUST be populated with non-empty Markdown STRING" directive to system prompt Critical rules section. Root cause: AI was returning structurally-valid output for interactive blocks with type-discriminator fields (choices/blanks/pairs) populated but the rich-text fields (prompt_markdown, explanation_markdown, setup_markdown, outcome_markdown) emitted as missing or non-string, causing transformConfigForCanvas to fall through to empty TipTap docs via mdToTipTap(""). Confirmed via lesson_blocks JSONB inspection — first AI Refine on freshly-inserted knowledge_check produced 3 questions with proper choices arrays but every prompt_markdown was `{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}`. **v14→v15 + v12→v13 parity:** Tightened directive to INLINE markdown ONLY (bold, italic, links). Root cause: v14 told AI to populate markdown fields with "real content" — AI interpreted broadly and started emitting `## headings` inside prompt_markdown for ranking and timeline question types. Since mdToTipTap parses only inline marks + paragraphs + hard breaks, heading syntax rendered as literal '##' text in trainee view (visible '## Order the arc of...' and '## What matters most...' before the actual prompts). New v15 directive explicitly enumerates forbidden block-level markdown (no #/##/###, no -/* bullets, no 1./2./3. numbered, no > blockquotes, no ``` code fences, no --- horizontal rules) and explains the failure mode in-prompt. All deploys preserved verify_jwt:false (Class A custom-auth pattern); architecture-reference §61 block parity preserved across both functions. (3) FRONTEND HOT-FIX shipped via Lovable prompt diagnosing AI Refine `lesson_context` bug: BlockEditorPane.tsx handleRefine and IterationModal.tsx full_block branch were BOTH concatenating `JSON.stringify(block.config)` with " --- Change request: " + textarea text into `author_prompt`, sending nothing in `lesson_context`. The AI received its own existing config as the instruction; the user's textarea text was buried after a 5-10KB JSON blob. Diagnosed via super_admin_audit_log query — the author_prompt_excerpt for a failed scenario refine was just the JSON config dump starting with `{"title":null,"intro_markdown":null,"moments":[...]`. Lovable diagnostic prompt run first (no code changes — Lovable reported back the exact concatenation pattern in both files), then fix prompt shipped: send `refineText.trim()` alone as `author_prompt`, send `JSON.stringify(block.config)` as `lesson_context` (the Edge Function's DraftRequest interface already supports it; system prompt formatting wraps it as "Surrounding lesson context for cohesion"). User-confirmed fix worked. Architecture-reference §77 added: AI Refine sends user instruction in `author_prompt` and existing block config in `lesson_context`, never concatenated. (4) Build queue items added (all [POST-LAUNCH]): **Flashcards renderer state reset on AI Refine** — after AI Refine on existing flashcards block, canvas preview shows all cards in reviewed/locked state until Save → reload. Suspected root cause: FlashcardsRender trainee-state object initialized on mount and keyed off per-card client_id; when AI Refine swaps the cards array to a new set, reset effect either doesn't fire or falls through to "completed" default. Fix candidate: mirror pattern in scenario and card_sort renderers — useEffect resetting per-card state when joined client_id list changes. ~5 LOC, single file (BlockRenderer.tsx FlashcardsRender). Not blocking launch (Save fixes it). **Refine textarea voice dictation** — no mic icon for voice input on either BlockEditorPane Refine textarea or IterationModal Refine textarea. Browser Web Speech API would cover it. **Lovable preview-in-new-tab spins forever** — Lovable platform issue, not application code. New-tab preview hangs on spinner; in-editor iframe works. Likely auth-cookie or publish-deploy. Not actionable from Supabase/code side. (5) Standing patterns reinforced: §61 block parity discipline (always update draft-lesson-block AND expand-lesson-from-outline in matching surgical-edit pairs for system prompt changes — three deploy pairs this session v13→v14→v15 and v11→v12→v13); §75 batch-recon-batch-design-lock-serial-Lovable (recon and design-lock once for interactive blocks, ship serially with verify cycles between each); approve→verify→fix-what-breaks (per Lovable Credit Conservation Protocol — don't burn cycles on speculative pre-emption; AI Refine bugs surfaced during real use, not from anticipating them); when adding a new block type with structured JSON output across many sub-items, audit AI Edge Function's MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS for headroom before shipping (added to architecture-reference §78). (6) Group C v1 Phase 4 (lesson block authoring) is now COMPLETE for the full 18-block v1 catalog: text, heading, divider, image, video_embed, quote, list, callout, embed_audio, stat_callout, statement_a_b, accordion, tabs, button_stack, flashcards, card_sort, scenario, knowledge_check. Frontend whitelist matches Edge Function ALLOWED_BLOCK_TYPES matches lesson_block_types registry. §61 Concern A fully closed for v1 catalog. (7) Session 70 opens with Group C Phase 5 (trainee learning UI) — the destination is locked, the path is not yet picked. Two options to evaluate at session open: **(Path A — recon first)** ~20 min Supabase-only schema inventory before any Lovable spend: trainee_progress / module_completion / cert_path_grants table existence + state + write paths, interactive block completion data persistence (sessionStorage vs server-side trainee state), /certifications route current behavior, cert path grant mechanism end-to-end. After recon, design-lock Phase 5 into sub-phases (5.1 My Learning landing, 5.2 cert path detail, 5.3 curriculum detail, 5.4 module detail, 5.5 per-item viewers, 5.6 cert grant) and ship serially per §75. **(Path B — start with Phase 5.1)** read-only My Learning landing first; if data isn't there to display, pivot to backend work. Faster start, slight risk of wasted cycle. Recommend Path A. Phase 5 acceptance criterion: "trainee can complete a full cert path from invitation to certification grant via the UI alone." (8) Group E confirmed done by user. No outstanding security warnings. Path forward after Phase 5: per the Session 62 close sequence — Phase 6 mentor review UI, Phase 7 actor flow, Phase 8 Order Assessment gating, Phase 9 Resources tab redesign, Phase 10 polish. Then Group D OR Group A based on customer feedback, then Groups A and B last. (9) NO movement this session on: SOC 2 written policies (deferred until feature-complete), Action-Oriented Voice Redesign across six surfaces (top post-launch priority), pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine. (10) Edge Function versions at Session 69 close: **draft-lesson-block v15** (INLINE-only markdown directive), **expand-lesson-from-outline v13** (INLINE-only markdown directive parity), scaffold-lesson v6 (unchanged from Session 67 silent bump), scaffold-lesson-outline v11 (unchanged from Session 66).*

AI can already generate all three types; canvas just can't render them yet because there's no BlockRenderer case, no block-form file, no CSS section. **Backend is NOT a blocker for any of the three remaining block types.** (3) Canonical recent template patterns locked from FlashcardsBlockForm + FlashcardsRender for reuse: (a) dnd-kit imports + PointerSensor activationConstraint distance:4 + SortableContext + verticalListSortingStrategy + arrayMove; (b) SortableItemRow inner component with useSortable + GripVertical drag handle as separate button + transform CSS.Transform.toString + opacity 0.5-when-dragging; (c) module-level MIN/MAX constants and MAX_CAPTION_LENGTH-style limits; (d) parent handleDragEnd/handleItemChange/handleDelete-with-guard/handleAdd-with-guard/handleGatingChange preserving sibling state in onConfigChange; (e) min/max hint paragraphs in text-xs text-muted-foreground; (f) gating checkbox in rounded-md border bg-muted/20 p-3 block at form bottom; (g) FileUploadField refField for nested-array items uses `<block_type>.<array_name>.${item.client_id}.<field>` literal — NOT array index — survives reorder (Session 67 pattern, NOT yet documented in Session 67 closeout but baked into all three remaining types via this recon doc); (h) BlockRenderer sub-component pattern with typed config + module-level lookup-table-and-helper-function + render signature accepting (items, urlMap, mode, blockClientId) + sessionStorage hydration-and-persist gated on `mode === "trainee" && typeof window !== "undefined"` + empty state JSX + buttons-inside-clickable-cards using e.stopPropagation + tabIndex 0 + onKeyDown for keyboard accessibility; (i) CSS namespace `bw-<blocktype>-shell/progress/controls/done` appended via `=== Session NN: <block_type> ===` comment marker, no risk of conflicts with existing block-type sections. (4) Per-block-type design questions resolved or queued (full detail in interactive-blocks-frontend-recon.md): **card_sort** locked all 5 design questions during recon — author form uses vertical SortableContext for reorder + dropdown for correct_bucket_id assignment (NOT drag-between-containers in form), trainee renderer is drag-between-containers via dnd-kit useDroppable+useDraggable, 4-or-fewer horizontal row of buckets at top + holding area below, no per-bucket color, mobile stacks below 640px, "Check my answers" shows per-card green/red border + correct-bucket tooltip on wrong + retry by returning wrong cards to holding area. **scenario** locked 5 of 5 — shadcn Dialog primitive for modal outcome reveal (Radix Portal + focus trap + Esc + Enter dismiss already wired; backdrop click does NOT dismiss because user must read outcome), RadioGroup at top of moment for prompt_type switch following ButtonStackBlockForm precedent with warn-before-destroying-content guard, reflection response stored ONLY in completion_data not in author form (v1 Phase 5 concern), per-moment small badge showing prompt_type + complete/needs-setup state, per-moment image rendered above setup_markdown when asset_id present matching flashcards front-image pattern. **knowledge_check** decisions: split into 3 sub-prompts per Session 65 design (A: MC+multi_select+true_false sharing `choices[]`; B: fill_in_blank+match sharing text-input-grading; C: ranking+timeline sharing drag-to-order), one form file with per-question sub-component dispatched by question_type (pattern bigger than ButtonStackBlockForm's action_type switch — 7 branches), dnd-kit verticalListSortingStrategy for ranking + horizontalListSortingStrategy for timeline, match question UI custom (click-left-then-right pair-linking, lines drawn between linked pairs — no shadcn primitive), timeline v1 = ordered placement on horizontal axis NOT date-precision (locked Session 65), gating_required default-TRUE pre-checked in form with helper text. (5) Per-block-type defaultConfig shapes verbatim-ready for blockTypeMeta.ts (card_sort 2 buckets + 4 cards seeded, scenario 1 moment with 2 MC choices seeded, knowledge_check 1 MC question with 2 empty choices seeded + `gating_required: true`). Lucide icon recommendations: card_sort → LayoutGrid, scenario → GitBranch, knowledge_check → HelpCircle. (6) Estimated Lovable prompt sizes from recon analysis: card_sort ~500-800 LOC single prompt (similar to flashcards 6c); scenario ~800-1200 LOC single prompt (modal adds complexity but stays in one prompt); knowledge_check 3 sequential prompts (4a MC family ~600 LOC, 4b FITB+match ~700 LOC, 4c ranking+timeline ~600 LOC). Realistic estimate from Session 69 to all three block types fully shipped: 5 sessions (69=card_sort, 70=scenario, 71+72+73=knowledge_check sub-prompts). (7) Stretch concern logged: AI Refine button wiring across the three new block types. flashcards has AI Refine via existing draft-lesson-block panel; the three new types inherit "for free" because backend supports them via v12/v11. The Lovable prompts SHOULD include the AI Refine button in the same location as flashcards. Verify after each ship — there's a real chance the FE's context-aware Refine pattern (which surfaced the Session 67 author_prompt cap bug) doesn't have parity across all block types yet. Missing AI Refine button in form footer = missing FE wiring. (8) Session 69 opener path simplified by this recon dump: open by reading `docs/interactive-blocks-frontend-recon.md` in full → spot-check no drift via `curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cbastianBWE/brainwise-blueprint/main/src/components/super-admin/lesson-blocks/blockTypeMeta.ts | head -40` → pick card_sort → write Lovable prompt directly (template patterns are pre-locked in the recon doc) → ship → verify. No design-from-scratch needed. Skip the redundant recon work. (9) NO movement this session on: Group E/C/D/A/B deployment sequencing, six Lovable security warnings, action-oriented voice redesign, SOC 2 policies, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal, Clarity Engine, post-launch Refine-config-context refactor (FE moving from `author_prompt`→`lesson_context`) — those carry forward unchanged from v73. (10) Mid-session decision discipline reinforced: pushed back on "do backend for all three at once" framing (Cole proposed) because the work was already shipped Session 66 — verified via execute_sql + list_edge_functions before agreeing to recon-only approach; then pushed back on "ship all three frontends this session" because realistic Lovable prompt verify cycles make that infeasible (each block type is its own session arc, knowledge_check is 3 sessions). Landing point: recon for all three at once (cheap and high-leverage; files overlap), design-lock for all three at once (also cheap once recon is in hand), Lovable prompts one block type per session (the actual cost driver). New standing pattern: "do the recon and design-lock work once for a batch of related block types; serialize the Lovable-prompt-write-and-verify per block type." Applies any time multiple block types share frontend touchpoints (which is always — every block type touches blockTypeMeta + BlockRenderer + BlockEditorPane + lesson-blocks.css + a new block-form file).*

*v73 - Session 67 CLOSE — Flashcards FRONT-image flip backend + AI grounding fix + per-card background color support + author_prompt cap hot-fix. Five things shipped in order: (1) draft-lesson-block v9→v10 — flashcards image moved BACK→FRONT per locked design C1; fields `back_image_asset_id`→`front_image_asset_id` and `back_caption`→`front_caption`; 5 surgical edits in BLOCK_SCHEMAS + rules + transformConfigForCanvas; end-to-end regression test PASSED on test fixture 32e0e966-4cb8-4e8b-abf8-5617de346f59. Schema discoveries logged: content_assets uses `uploaded_by` + `is_library_asset`; content_asset_versions uses `bucket`/`path`/`size_bytes`/`original_filename`; content_asset_refs has module_id/curriculum_id/certification_path_id columns. (2) Lovable Prompt 6c-frontend-flashcards SHIPPED — 829-line prompt applied by Lovable, 5 files committed to main, all verified via curl from raw.githubusercontent.com. Two cosmetic findings (non-blocking): `gatingRequired` prop declared but unused (Phase 5 future wiring), "All cards reviewed" text duplicated in progress+done panel. (3) Issue A AI grounding fix — `ai_authoring_context` row id `10908e38-3947-4b4c-b0df-c9fd65ccb98a` (context_name=`platform_overview`) UPDATEd in place. Prior body listed instruments inline with INST-00X codes only and glossed NAI wrong. New body 2015 chars (was 986) includes canonical full names pulled directly from `public.instruments` table: PTP=Personal Threat Profile, NAI=Neuroscience Adoption Index, AIRSA=AI Readiness Skills Assessment, HSS=Habit Stabilization Scorecard. Five verification checks passed. ai_authoring_context schema has no `updated_at` column and no version-bumps in prior rows — in-place UPDATE matches table convention. AI Edge Functions read this row at runtime — NO redeploy needed. (4) Issue C per-card background color support — draft-lesson-block v10→v11 and expand-lesson-from-outline v10→v11 deployed. Six surgical edits each: header changelog, BRAND_COLORS Set<string> module constant with 8 locked brand hex values, BLOCK_SCHEMAS/BLOCK_SCHEMA_HINTS flashcards schema gains `"background_color": null` per-card, rules text documents auto-pair behavior, transformConfigForCanvas flashcards branch passes through `c.background_color` validated as `BRAND_COLORS.has(c.background_color) ? c.background_color : null`, system prompt rule "AI emits null always — author picks post-generation; renderer auto-pairs text color". expand v11 also fixed stale `v9` header from prior v10 deploy. Block parity §61 preserved. Locked auto-pair lookup table (FLASHCARD_TEXT_COLOR_FOR_BG): null+Sand→Navy text #021F36, Navy/Orange/Teal/Mustard/Slate/Purple/Forest→White text #FFFFFF. (5) CRITICAL hot-fix draft-lesson-block v11→v12 — `AUTHOR_PROMPT_MAX_CHARS` raised 4000→50000. User reported Refine returning 400 immediately after v11 deploy. Diagnosis path: synthetic pg_net request to deployed function returned `401 invalid_jwt` cleanly (function healthy, no v11 regression); audit log query for `ai_authoring_draft_generated` in last 30 min returned ZERO rows (failing pre-audit-write step); last successful flashcards Refine was 17:33:45 UTC, ~40 min BEFORE v11 deploy at 18:13 UTC; `author_prompt_excerpt` from that successful row started with `{"cards":[{"client_id":"d5df7722...` revealing that **frontend sends existing block config JSON-stringified as `author_prompt` for context-aware Refine**; active flashcards blocks were 4790 + 7851 chars; v10/v11 validation `if (body.author_prompt.length > 4000) return jsonError(400, "author_prompt_too_long")` was the rejection gate. **Pre-existing fragility, NOT v11 regression** — cap was sized for single-block instructions and broke when FE switched to embedding config-as-prompt. Fix: deploy v12 with `AUTHOR_PROMPT_MAX_CHARS = 50000` extracted to named constant; all other v11 logic preserved verbatim. Deploy timestamp updated_at 1778696302284. Status ACTIVE, verify_jwt:false, sha b603eaa0b14cb24d359947b9aa5eb1038c3ecebc2796c06e489b4f9c0b0e4608. User confirmed Refine working end-to-end after v12. Pushed back on user's "you broke it" framing with audit-log evidence — the 40-minute gap between last successful call and v11 deploy was the clincher; v11 didn't introduce the bug, the block grew past 4000 chars in between. Final Edge Function versions at Session 67 close: draft-lesson-block v12, expand-lesson-from-outline v11, scaffold-lesson-outline v11 (unchanged), scaffold-lesson v5 (unchanged). (6) Lovable Prompt 6d DRAFTED but NOT yet sent — `/mnt/user-data/outputs/lovable-prompts/prompt-6d-flashcards-color-and-centering.md` (348 lines, 15.4KB). 4 files modified, no new dependencies. Reuses existing BrandColorSwatch component (at src/components/super-admin/lesson-blocks/BrandColorSwatch.tsx; supports palette="full" + allowDefault + value/onChange API; exports BRAND_SWATCH_COLORS 8 saturated and BRAND_TINT_COLORS 8 pastel). Changes: blockTypeMeta.ts defaultConfig cards seed background_color:null; FlashcardsBlockForm.tsx Card type + BrandColorSwatch import + per-card picker after Back editor + handleAdd seed; BlockRenderer.tsx FlashcardConfig type extended + FLASHCARD_TEXT_COLOR_FOR_BG lookup const + getFlashcardTextColorForBg() helper + face JSX with inline {backgroundColor, color} style; lesson-blocks.css `.bw-flashcards-face` align-items stretch→center + text-align center, `.bw-flashcards-face-body` font-size 1.5rem + color: inherit + cascade rules for p/li/strong/em, `.bw-flashcards-front-caption` color #6D6875→inherit + opacity 0.75. 12 acceptance criteria including legacy compat (legacy flashcards blocks without `background_color` field still render with Default/Navy-on-Sand behavior). Session 68 opens with: ship 6d. (7) Pending Session 68 work prioritized: (Immediate) Ship Lovable Prompt 6d and verify against 12 acceptance criteria; (Post-launch structural fix) refactor FE to stop embedding existing block config in `author_prompt` — the `lesson_context` field already exists in DraftRequest schema for this purpose; Refine should move config from prompt→lesson_context; this is the correct structural fix, v12's raised cap is a workaround; logged as post-launch Build Queue item; (Housekeeping) stale draft row on test fixture (1 row in lesson_block_drafts with 20 blocks including 1 flashcards block, content_item_id=32e0e966-4cb8-4e8b-abf8-5617de346f59, author_id=super admin — Cole's discretion); (Already queued, no change) AIRSA Phase 3e-8 with BUG-1 isAIRSA bundled, Group E deployment readiness, six Lovable security warnings deferred from Session 36, Phase 4.3/4.4/final publish, Action-Oriented Voice Redesign across six surfaces, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine (not yet built), Phase 5 trainee renderer carry-forward (queued behind interactive-block frontend batch). Realistic estimate from Session 68 to all four block types fully shipped: 3-5 more sessions, one block type per Lovable prompt. (8) Key learnings Session 67: (a) `public.instruments` is canonical source of truth for instrument abbreviation→full name mapping — NOT user memory, NOT any markdown doc; (b) `ai_authoring_context` rows UPDATEd in place despite having a `version` column (table convention is in-place edits, no historical row preservation, no `updated_at` column); (c) right first question on a "function broke" report is "what does the response body actually say" — not "what did I change last"; in this case `{"error":"author_prompt_too_long"}` + audit-log size math made the cause obvious within a few queries; the v11-deploy-as-suspect framing was disproved within one audit-log timestamp query; (d) **MCP `Supabase:get_logs` for `edge-function` is BROKEN in this project** — analytics endpoint returns `INVALID_ARGUMENT: User specified reservation projects/supabase-analytics-ext-queries/locations/EU/reservations/queries-short-12hr is not found`; don't waste a tool call on it, use `pg_net` synthetic HTTP requests + audit-log `after_value->>'edge_function'` filter instead; pg_net pattern: `SELECT net.http_post(url, headers, body) AS request_id;` then separate `SELECT pg_sleep(2); SELECT status_code, content::text FROM net._http_response WHERE id = <id>;` (pg_net is async); (e) `super_admin_audit_log.after_value` (NOT `after_json` as I tried first) — column list verified: id, super_admin_user_id, action_type, company_id, affected_user_id, session_id, detail, created_at, ip_address, user_agent, reason, before_value, after_value, mode, expires_at, ended_at, end_reason; (f) `list_edge_functions` returns are STALE within a single conversation — after a `deploy_edge_function` call, subsequent `list_edge_functions` may still show the old version; use `get_edge_function` against specific slug for fresh state; (g) named-constant extraction trick reinforced — when raising a magic number, extract to a named module-level constant first (`const AUTHOR_PROMPT_MAX_CHARS = 50000`), reference from the validation gate; makes the new value greppable, intent self-documenting, future tuning a one-line change. (9) Standing code-style patterns unchanged from Session 66 (PL/pgSQL structure, Edge Function structure, migration discipline, per-function role boundaries, JSONB schema invariants). Block parity §61 reinforced: any changes to a block_type config schema must touch BOTH draft-lesson-block AND expand-lesson-from-outline in matching surgical-edit pairs to preserve canvas-output byte-equivalence. NO movement this session on: Group E/C/D/A/B deployment sequencing, six Lovable security warnings, action-oriented voice redesign, SOC 2 policies, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal, Clarity Engine — those carry forward unchanged from v72.*

*v72 - Session 66 CLOSE — Backend SHIPPED for the four new v1 interactive block types (flashcards, card_sort, scenario, knowledge_check). Four migrations + four Edge Function deploys, all verified end-to-end. (1) Migration A `update_lesson_block_types_for_v1_interactive_batch`: UPDATEd three stale rows (flashcards / scenario / knowledge_check descriptions rewritten to match the locked Session 65 design), INSERTed card_sort row (`category='interactive', is_interactive=true, is_scored=false, is_v1_active=true`). Registry now at 18 rows total, 4 target rows verified. (2) Migration B `create_walk_block_config_for_asset_refs_helper`: created `public._walk_block_config_for_asset_refs(p_block_type text, p_config jsonb) RETURNS TABLE(out_ref_field text, out_asset_id uuid)`, IMMUTABLE, `SECURITY DEFINER`, `SET search_path TO 'public', 'pg_temp'`. Handles three patterns: legacy single-asset (image/video_embed/embed_audio emit one pair with `ref_field='<block_type>_asset'` preserving prior naming so existing rows still match on rebind), nested arrays (flashcards.cards[N].back_image_asset_id, card_sort.cards[N].image_asset_id, scenario.moments[N].setup_image_asset_id emit N≥0 pairs with `ref_field='<block_type>.<array>[<idx>].<field>'`), and zero-rows-for-no-asset-blocks (knowledge_check/text/heading/etc.) — unified call site, no rejection. 11 isolated walker tests passed via execute_sql (synthetic configs for each pattern + null/empty edge cases). (3) Migration C `rework_replace_lesson_blocks_for_nested_asset_refs`: reworked the 200-line SECURITY DEFINER RPC to use the walker. Two changes vs prior version — (a) up-front validation loop now enumerates ALL asset_id references (top-level or nested) and verifies each points to an active `content_asset`, replacing the single-field read of `v_block->'config'->>'asset_id'`; (b) insert-and-rebind loop now calls walker per block, applies B-2 rebind logic per `(ref_field, asset_id)` pair — match by `(asset_id, content_item_id, lesson_block_id IS NULL, archived_at IS NULL)` IGNORING ref_field, then UPDATE both `lesson_block_id` AND `ref_field` together on rebind. B-2 was chosen over B-1 (match by ref_field literal) because cleaner data on reorders — no orphan refs left behind when nested indices shift. Session 60 ordering preserved: rebind/insert BEFORE cascade helper. `_cascade_archive_asset_refs_for_lesson_blocks` itself unchanged (operates on lesson_block_id not ref_field — verified verbatim). Audit log counters (`asset_refs_created`, `asset_refs_rebound`) sum across nested refs per block. Three regression scenarios passed end-to-end against test fixture `32e0e966-4cb8-4e8b-abf8-5617de346f59` (19 blocks, mixed non-asset types) — Test A no-op resave of 19 blocks zero assets returned archived 19 inserted 19 zero refs touched (confirms the 14 non-asset block types unaffected); Test B-Phase-1 added image block referencing synthetic asset `aaaaaaaa-6666-6666-6666-aaaaaaaaaaaa` returned archived 19 inserted 20 1 ref created (`ref_field='image_asset'`, lesson_block-scoped, asset stayed active); Test B-Phase-2 replaced image block with flashcards block sharing the same asset on card 0 returned archived 20 inserted 20 1 ref created at `ref_field='flashcards.cards[0].back_image_asset_id'` 0 rebound cascade archived 1 prior ref 0 assets archived — asset survived because new ref created BEFORE cascade ran, Session 60 ordering held; cleanup save removed the flashcards block and cascade correctly auto-archived the synthetic asset once zero active refs remained. Fixture restored to original 19 blocks, synthetic asset archived, zero junk left in production data. (4) Migration D `rework_get_lesson_block_assets_for_nested_asset_refs`: replaced single-field `lb.config->>'asset_id'` projection with `CROSS JOIN LATERAL public._walk_block_config_for_asset_refs(lb.block_type, lb.config) w` in the saved_asset_ids CTE. Same signature, RETURNS TABLE shape, auth checks (no impersonation gate — it's a read-only signed-URL resolver), active-status filters on content_assets and content_asset_versions, UNION with `p_extra_asset_ids`. Verified clean fixture returns 0 rows correctly. (5) Edge Function deploys: scaffold-lesson-outline v9→v11 (silent Lovable bump to v10 then Session 66 deploy to v11; ALLOWED_BLOCK_TYPES extended to 18 types — 4 new interactives + 14 existing; block-type intent guide bullets added for each new type describing when AI should propose them at outline stage; no schema hints — outline emits outline shape only `{block_type, summary_one_line, learning_objective_fragment}`), scaffold-lesson v3→v5 (silent bump to v4 then deploy to v5; ALLOWED_BLOCK_TYPES extended — card_sort added, three others were already listed but with badly stale schema hints; full BLOCK_SCHEMA_HINTS rewrite for all 4 new types matching locked design — markdown-shape emit since this is the legacy one-shot scaffolder NOT canvas-final-TipTap; removed the hard-recommended knowledge_check-at-end-of-every-lesson advice — now describes when each interactive type is appropriate so author intent leads), expand-lesson-from-outline v7→v9 (silent bump to v8 then deploy to v9; ALLOWED_BLOCK_TYPES + BLOCK_SCHEMA_HINTS + transformConfigForCanvas all extended for 4 new types; schema hints document `gating_required` defaults — false for flashcards/card_sort/scenario, true for knowledge_check; transformConfigForCanvas branches: flashcards (front/back md→TipTap, back_image_asset_id passes through, back_caption trimmed-or-null), card_sort (buckets/cards client_id assigned-if-missing, correct_bucket_id validated against bucket client_ids and dropped to null if dangling, content md→TipTap), scenario (linear moments per spec, prompt_type switch between multiple_choice and reflection branches, intro_markdown md→TipTap, choice outcome_markdown md→TipTap, reflection outcome_markdown md→TipTap), knowledge_check (7 question_types with per-type field handling — choices for MC/multi_select/true_false; blanks for fill_in_blank; pairs for match; items for ranking; events for timeline; prompt_markdown and explanation_markdown md→TipTap; gating_required defaults TRUE)), draft-lesson-block v8→v9 (BLOCK_SCHEMAS extended for 4 new types; transformConfigForCanvas branches IDENTICAL to expand's — intentional consistency across canvas-final-shape paths so a block drafted via draft-lesson-block and one expanded via expand-lesson-from-outline produce byte-equivalent canvas configs). All four deployed with `verify_jwt:false` (custom auth via getClaims inside each handler — standard Class A pattern). All four anon-probed clean (`HTTP 401 missing_bearer_token`). _shared/ modules byte-identical across the four deploys (`impersonation_gate.ts`, `markdown_to_tiptap.ts`, `length_guidance.ts`). (6) Standing patterns committed to in Session 66 and documented in session-66-to-67.md handoff (locked for cross-session consistency going forward): PL/pgSQL function structure (SECURITY DEFINER, search_path public+pg_temp, declare-vars-first auth-header order auth.uid→assert_super_admin→assert_impersonation_allows→validate args→business logic→audit log→jsonb_build_object return, `v_` prefix on vars, `_underscore` prefix on internal helpers, `out_` prefix on RETURNS TABLE columns to avoid OUT-parameter-shadows-column-reference trap, snake_case error codes with ERRCODE); Edge Function structure (header changelog comment with session ref, jsr→npm@2.57.2→_shared/ import order, fixed serve handler order OPTIONS→method→auth header→token→env→getClaims→service/caller clients→super-admin check→impersonation gate→body parse→validation→business logic→audit via callerClient→response, `jsonError(status,code)` and `jsonResponse(status,body)`, outer try/catch with `console.error` stable label and 500 fallback); migrations (one logical change per migration, snake_case names, DDL via apply_migration verification via execute_sql); per-AI-Edge-Function role boundaries (ai-authoring-chat block-type-agnostic conversational, scaffold-lesson-outline outline shape only, scaffold-lesson markdown-shape one-shot, expand+draft canvas-final TipTap via transformConfigForCanvas); JSONB schema invariants (every interactive block has top-level gating_required boolean, array items use client_id strings not indices, optional fields null not absent, unknown enum values fall through to safe default — Session 65 pattern in button_stack). (7) Version drift note: handoff predicted v10/v4/v8/v9 but live ended at v11/v5/v9/v9 due to silent Lovable redeploy bumps on outline and scaffold before Session 66 deploys landed (documented Lovable behavior, not a bug). (8) Phase 5 doc updates needed BEFORE Phase 5 work resumes (queued not done): phase-5-lesson-progress-carry-forward.md §1.2 "who writes to lesson_block_progress" list needs card_sort added; §1.2 completion_data shapes table needs locked Session 65 shapes (flashcards `{cards_completed:[], cards_review_count:{}}`, card_sort `{final_score_pct:100, attempts_count:N, incorrect_cards_history:[]}`, scenario `{moments_submitted:{client_id:{type,choice_id or text}}}`, knowledge_check `{answered:{q_id:{type,selected/filled,attempts}}}`); §4 gating_required defaults table needs card_sort row with value false. Edits should happen before any Phase 5 writer code reads completion_data shape. (9) Frontend recon preserved at /home/claude/recon/ for Session 67 — AccordionBlockForm.tsx + TabsBlockForm.tsx + ButtonStackBlockForm.tsx (canonical templates for array-of-items block forms, identical @dnd-kit + SortableItem inner component pattern), AddBlockPopover.tsx (iterates IN_SCOPE_BLOCK_TYPES, no changes needed once meta extended), blockTypeMeta.ts (BlockType union + BLOCK_TYPE_META record + IN_SCOPE_BLOCK_TYPES array all need extending for 4 new types), lesson-blocks.css (confirmed greenfield — no 3D-flip, drag-drop, or modal-overlay styles exist for the 4 new types). (10) Session 67 opens with two valid paths: (Option A — Phase 3+ frontend kickoff) start with flashcards form Lovable prompt (simplest — renderer + form + meta seed + CSS 3D flip), then card_sort (adds dnd-kit drag-to-bucket which is more complex because cards drag between containers not just reorder within one), then scenario (modal overlay outcome reveal + MC-vs-reflection per-moment switch), then knowledge_check split into 2-3 Lovable prompts by question-type family (4a MC+multi_select+true_false → 4b FITB+match → 4c ranking+timeline). (Option B — pick up a different queued item) Action-Oriented Voice Redesign, Group E deployment readiness, pricing-reads refactor, six security warnings from Lovable scan, etc. — and treat the block-type frontend batch as a multi-session arc to return to. No backend blocks either choice. Phase 5 trainee renderer carry-forward stays queued behind whichever frontend path is picked. Deferred unchanged from v71: Group E/C/D/A/B deployment sequencing, six Lovable security warnings, action-oriented voice redesign, SOC 2 policies, pricing-reads refactor, corporate contract renewal schema change, Clarity Engine. NO movement this session on any of those. Updated sub-prompt sequence: 6c-backend ✅ → 6c-frontend-flashcards → 6c-frontend-card_sort → 6c-frontend-scenario → 6c-frontend-knowledge_check (×2-3 prompts).
